project_goal.value: Desktop application for CNC operators to migrate legacy proprietary CAM G-code programs to ISO 6983 format with validation and dry-run simulation before upload to shop-floor controllers
project_goal.rationale: Core business need to standardize CNC programs across legacy proprietary CAM systems

target_users.value: CNC machine operators and manufacturing engineers managing G-code programs on shop-floor workstations
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery sessions with plant operations

project_class.rationale: Standalone Windows desktop application running on shop-floor workstations without cloud dependency

capabilities.rationale: Electron UI, embedded Python processing engine, SQLite persistence, and controller upload integrations

complexity_level.rationale: G-code parsing is domain-complex but scale is local single-user workstation

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect G-code migration could cause machine damage; validation and simulation mitigate this

data_sensitivity.rationale: G-code programs are proprietary manufacturing IP but not personal or financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Application runs offline on workstation, no internet connectivity required or permitted

access_model.value: Single-user local authentication with custom JWT; role separation between operators and engineers
access_model.rationale: Workstation-local auth sufficient for shop-floor deployment model

feature_scope.value: G-code import and parsing, proprietary command translation, ISO 6983 export, validation reports, dry-run simulation with cycle time estimates, controller upload, tool library management, machine profile configuration
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for production-ready migration workflow

mvp_scope.value: Import legacy G-code, parse and flag unsupported commands, generate migration report, export ISO G-code, basic machine profile selection
mvp_scope.rationale: Core import-validate-export loop is minimum viable for pilot deployment

security_baseline.value: Local JWT authentication, encrypted SQLite database, read-only file system access for controller upload directories, audit log of all program migrations and uploads
security_baseline.rationale: Internal tooling on air-gapped workstation; local encryption and audit trail sufficient

frontend_stack.value: Electron 29 with React 18 and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform desktop with web tech familiar to the team; Electron enables Python backend integration via IPC

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI running as embedded local server
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem has excellent G-code parsing libraries; FastAPI provides clean IPC over localhost HTTP

data_platform.value: SQLite via SQLAlchemy for program history, machine profiles, and migration logs
data_platform.rationale: Single-file database perfect for offline desktop deployment; zero ops overhead

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Windows workstation on shop floor, installed via MSI package
hosting_target.rationale: Air-gapped shop-floor environment; packaged installer for IT-managed deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: Migration job history retained locally for 24 months; operators can export archives; no cloud sync
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Shop-floor workstation storage limits drive retention window

future_scope.value: Multi-machine batch migration, network-shared program library, integration with ERP work order system
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap items pending shop-floor network upgrade

constraints.value: Must run offline on Windows 10/11, no external network dependencies, MSI installer under 200 MB, target workstations have 4 GB RAM
constraints.rationale: Air-gapped shop floor; IT limits installer size for managed deployment

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for process metrics on workstation, structured file-based logging for migration audit trail
observability_baseline.rationale: Local monitoring sufficient for desktop tool; audit logs satisfy ISO 6983 change-control requirements

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with 2-week sprints; desktop releases via MSI with changelog
execution_preference.rationale: Small team; frequent releases allow rapid operator feedback

project.goal: Migrate legacy CAM G-code to ISO 6983 with validation and dry-run simulation
project.target_users: CNC operators and manufacturing engineers

frontend.state: Zustand for local state, React Query for IPC calls

backend.framework: FastAPI embedded local server on port 8765
backend.parsing: pygcode + custom translation tables for proprietary dialects

security.auth: Custom JWT stored in OS keychain
security.access: Read-only mount for controller upload directories

reasoner_reviews.product: The migration workflow is well-scoped for MVP but previous rounds exposed a gap in the validation report UX — operators need clear human-readable error summaries rather than raw G-code line references. Dry-run simulation must display cycle time diffs between legacy and ISO programs side by side.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Embedding a FastAPI process inside Electron via PyInstaller is architecturally sound but IPC reliability has been a recurring concern across prior rounds. The Python subprocess lifecycle must be managed explicitly: startup timeout, health check ping, and graceful shutdown on Electron quit. SQLCipher adds encryption without swapping the SQLite driver.
reasoner_reviews.security: Local JWT is appropriate for air-gapped workstation but token expiry and keychain storage implementation were flagged as incomplete in round 2. The audit log table must be append-only (no DELETE permissions for the app user) and include program hash to detect post-migration tampering.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: MSI size constraint of 200 MB is tight given PyInstaller bundling of numpy and scipy for simulation. Previous round showed the bundle at 240 MB. Dependency pruning and Python slim base are needed to meet the constraint.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Validation report section remains thin across rounds 1 and 2. The data model is missing a validation_errors child table linked to migration_jobs. The simulation engine design lacks detail on how axis limit checks are parameterized from machine_profiles.

backend.service_design: Embedded FastAPI server on localhost:8765 managing four domain modules: parser (proprietary dialect ingestion), translator (command mapping tables), simulator (kinematic dry-run engine), and uploader (controller file transfer).
backend.api_patterns: REST over localhost with no auth (loopback only). Endpoints: POST /parse, POST /translate, POST /simulate, POST /upload, GET /jobs, GET /machines.
backend.failure_handling: Subprocess manager in Electron main process polls /healthz every 2 seconds; if three consecutive failures, restarts Python process and notifies renderer via IPC event. FastAPI returns structured error payloads with G-code line references and human-readable descriptions.
backend.scaling_notes: Single-user desktop; no horizontal scaling needed. Simulation of programs over 10,000 lines runs in a background thread to keep UI responsive. Progress reported via SSE stream to renderer.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT signed with HS256, stored in Windows Credential Manager via keytar. Token expires after 8-hour shift. Refresh requires operator PIN re-entry.
security.authorization_model: Two roles: operator (import, validate, dry-run) and engineer (all operations including controller upload and machine profile edit). Role encoded in JWT claims checked by FastAPI dependency.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: migrations_audit table with INSERT-only grant for app DB user. Records: program_id, operator_id, source_dialect, target_format, validation_pass, upload_target, action_timestamp, program_sha256. Log exported to CSV on demand for ISO 6983 change-control submissions.
security.security_risks: Risk: PyInstaller bundle may include unused packages expanding attack surface. Mitigation: pip-audit in CI to scan bundled dependencies for CVEs.

data.entities: gcode_programs (id, filename, source_dialect, raw_content_path, iso_content_path, program_sha256, imported_at), machine_profiles (id, controller_brand, model, max_axis_travel_xyz, spindle_rpm_max, tool_change_type, upload_protocol), migration_jobs (id, program_id, machine_profile_id, status ENUM pending/validating/simulated/uploaded/failed, validation_pass, cycle_time_legacy_ms, cycle_time_iso_ms, migrated_at), validation_errors (id, job_id, line_number, error_code, description, severity ENUM warning/error/fatal), tool_libraries (id, machine_profile_id, tool_number, diameter_mm, length_mm, material), controller_targets (id, machine_profile_id, upload_protocol ENUM ftp/sftp/serial/manual, endpoint_config_json).
data.storage_design: SQLite encrypted with SQLCipher using AES-256. WAL mode for write performance. G-code file content stored on filesystem at configured program library path; DB holds metadata and file references only.
data.retention_and_deletion: Migration job records and validation reports retained 24 months via Alembic-managed TTL cleanup job on application startup. Raw G-code files archived to a user-configured backup path before deletion.
data.data_risks: Risk: SQLCipher key derived from user password — key loss means data loss. Mitigation: key escrow to a plant IT-managed file, encrypted with IT public key.

devops.deployment_topology: Single-workstation deployment. PyInstaller bundles Python 3.12 runtime and all dependencies into a self-contained directory. Electron Builder packages Electron + bundled Python into MSI installer. IT deploys via SCCM or manual install.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (ruff, eslint), unit tests (pytest for parser/translator/simulator, Jest for renderer), PyInstaller build, Electron Builder packaging, MSI artifact upload. Release gate: all tests green and bundle size under 200 MB checked by CI step.
devops.observability_stack: Structured JSON logs written to %APPDATA%/gcode-migrator/logs/ with daily rotation. Prometheus client in FastAPI exposing /metrics on localhost:8765. Grafana Agent scrapes and writes to local Prometheus instance for live process monitoring during development and staging.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: PyInstaller bundle size exceeds 200 MB due to scipy/numpy. Mitigation: exclude unused scipy sub-modules in pyinstaller spec, use numpy wheels without BLAS extras. Target is 180 MB with 10% safety margin.

frontend.app_structure: Electron 29 main process handles file I/O, G-code parsing, and controller upload via IPC; renderer hosts React SPA with TypeScript for all UI views
frontend.pages_and_flows: Import View (file picker), Translation Preview (diff view), Validation Report, Dry-Run Simulation (cycle time estimate), Export and Upload, Tool Library Manager, Machine Profile Configuration
frontend.state_management: Redux Toolkit: slices for activeFile, translationResult, validationReport, simulationState, machineProfiles; RTK Query for controller API calls
frontend.performance_notes: G-code parsing runs in Electron worker thread via ipcMain bridge to keep renderer responsive; large files (>10 MB) streamed line by line
frontend.frontend_risks: Uploading wrong G-code to production CNC controllers causes physical damage; dry-run confirmation modal must require explicit user acknowledgment before upload

ISS-001.title: Validation report lacks human-readable error descriptions
ISS-001.detail: The validation_errors table and UI only surface raw G-code line numbers without translating error codes to operator-readable descriptions. Operators cannot act on migration reports without manually looking up error codes.

Partial fix: descriptions added to API response but UI still shows raw codes

ISS-002.title: PyInstaller bundle exceeds 200 MB size constraint

scipy added for simulation increased to 240 MB

ISS-003.title: Simulation engine axis limit parameterization not specified
ISS-003.detail: The dry-run simulation section does not explain how machine_profiles axis travel limits are read and enforced during kinematic simulation. This gap means the simulation could pass programs that would trigger hardware limit switches.

history.note: Simulation engine described at high level only
Still no detail on axis limit enforcement logic

target_output.title: CNC G-code Migration Desktop Tool
target_output.executive_summary: An Electron 29 + Python 3.12 desktop application for Windows shop-floor workstations that imports legacy proprietary CAM G-code programs, parses dialect-specific commands using pygcode and custom translation tables, validates output against ISO 6983, runs a kinematic dry-run simulation against configurable machine profiles, and uploads verified programs to shop-floor CNC controllers. All migration history is stored in an encrypted SQLite database with an append-only audit log for change-control compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron main process spawns a PyInstaller-bundled FastAPI server on localhost:8765 at startup. The renderer process (React 18) communicates with FastAPI exclusively over loopback HTTP. Electron main monitors the Python subprocess health via /healthz polling every 2 seconds and restarts it on failure. The translation engine is a pure Python module with dialect-specific command mapping tables for Fanuc, Siemens, Heidenhain, and Mazak proprietary extensions. The kinematic simulator reads machine_profiles axis travel limits and spindle envelope to detect violations before ISO G-code is accepted for controller upload.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop shell: Electron 29. Frontend: React 18 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Zustand, React Query. Backend: Python 3.12 FastAPI on localhost:8765. G-code parsing: pygcode 0.2 with custom dialect extension modules. Simulation: numpy-based trapezoidal motion profile (no scipy). Database: SQLite via SQLAlchemy with SQLCipher AES-256. Packaging: PyInstaller 6 + Electron Builder producing MSI. CI: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Prometheus client + Grafana Agent (dev/staging).
target_output.functional_feature_map: G-code Import: drag-and-drop or file picker for .nc/.mpf/.tap files; dialect auto-detected from file header tokens. Validation Report: per-line error listing joined to validation_error_codes descriptions and resolution hints, grouped by severity (fatal/error/warning), exportable to PDF. Dry-Run Simulation: kinematic replay against selected machine_profile showing cycle time estimate, axis utilization heatmap, and limit violation markers. ISO Export: writes validated ISO 6983 program to user-specified path with SHA-256 checksum. Controller Upload: protocol-specific transfer (FTP, SFTP, serial RS-232) to configured controller_targets. Tool Library Manager: per-machine tool number, diameter, length catalog synced to simulation.
target_output.system_components: Electron main process (subprocess lifecycle, IPC bridge, OS keychain via keytar). React renderer (migration wizard, report viewer, simulation visualizer). FastAPI server (parser module, translator module, simulator module, uploader module). SQLite/SQLCipher database (program library, machine profiles, migration history, audit log). PyInstaller bundle (Python 3.12 runtime, FastAPI, pygcode, numpy, SQLAlchemy, SQLCipher). MSI installer package.
target_output.workflows: CAM Import and Migration: operator opens legacy .mpf file; parser module tokenizes Siemens dialect commands; translator maps non-ISO tokens against Siemens-to-ISO lookup table; unsupported tokens are recorded in validation_errors with error_code and line reference; validation_error_codes lookup joins to provide description ('G75: Siemens thread cutting cycle has no direct ISO equivalent — manually replace with G33 sequence') and resolution_hint; operator reviews collapsible error panel, acknowledges warnings, and exports ISO G-code if no fatal errors remain. Dry-Run Simulation: operator selects machine_profile (e.g., Fanuc 0i-MD, X-travel 650 mm, Y-travel 500 mm, Z-travel 450 mm, spindle 12000 RPM); simulator replays ISO program in forward-kinematic mode computing axis positions at each block; axis_limit_check() compares cumulative axis displacement against max_axis_travel_xyz from machine_profile and raises LimitViolationError if exceeded; cycle time is computed from feed rates and rapid traverse rates per machine_profile; results displayed as Gantt-style timeline with axis utilization bars.
target_output.data_model: gcode_programs (id INTEGER PK, filename TEXT, source_dialect ENUM fanuc/siemens/heidenhain/mazak/unknown, raw_content_path TEXT, iso_content_path TEXT, program_sha256 TEXT, imported_at DATETIME). machine_profiles (id INTEGER PK, controller_brand TEXT, model TEXT, max_axis_travel_x_mm REAL, max_axis_travel_y_mm REAL, max_axis_travel_z_mm REAL, spindle_rpm_max INTEGER, tool_change_type ENUM automatic/manual, upload_protocol ENUM ftp/sftp/serial/manual). migration_jobs (id INTEGER PK, program_id FK, machine_profile_id FK, status ENUM pending/validating/simulated/uploaded/failed, validation_pass BOOLEAN, cycle_time_legacy_ms INTEGER, cycle_time_iso_ms INTEGER, migrated_at DATETIME). validation_errors (id INTEGER PK, job_id FK, line_number INTEGER, error_code TEXT, severity ENUM warning/error/fatal). validation_error_codes (error_code TEXT PK, description TEXT, resolution_hint TEXT, severity ENUM warning/error/fatal, source_dialect TEXT). tool_libraries (id INTEGER PK, machine_profile_id FK, tool_number INTEGER, diameter_mm REAL, length_mm REAL, material TEXT). controller_targets (id INTEGER PK, machine_profile_id FK, upload_protocol TEXT, endpoint_config_json TEXT). migrations_audit (id INTEGER PK, program_id FK, operator_id INTEGER, action TEXT, upload_target TEXT, program_sha256 TEXT, action_timestamp DATETIME).
target_output.api_design: REST over localhost:8765. POST /parse {file_path, dialect_hint} -> {program_id, detected_dialect, line_count}. POST /translate {program_id} -> {job_id, status}. GET /jobs/{job_id}/validation -> {errors: [{line_number, error_code, description, resolution_hint, severity}]}. POST /simulate {job_id, machine_profile_id} -> {cycle_time_ms, limit_violations: [{axis, position_mm, limit_mm}], axis_utilization_pct}. POST /upload {job_id, controller_target_id} -> {status, transferred_bytes}. GET /machines -> [{id, controller_brand, model}]. GET /healthz -> {status: 'ok'}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT (HS256, 8-hour expiry) stored in Windows Credential Manager via keytar. Two roles: operator (import/validate/simulate) and engineer (all operations including upload and machine profile edit) encoded in JWT claims validated by FastAPI dependency. SQLite encrypted with SQLCipher AES-256; key derived from user passphrase with PBKDF2 (100,000 iterations). migrations_audit table has INSERT-only grant for app DB user — no UPDATE or DELETE. Audit records include program_sha256 to detect post-migration file tampering. pip-audit runs in GitHub Actions CI to scan bundled Python dependencies for CVEs before MSI packaging.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: PyInstaller bundles Python 3.12 with FastAPI, pygcode, numpy, SQLAlchemy, and SQLCipher into a self-contained directory. scipy excluded via PyInstaller spec hidden imports exclusion list — confirmed bundle target 175-180 MB. Electron Builder wraps Electron + PyInstaller bundle into MSI with NSIS installer script. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: ruff lint, eslint, pytest (parser/translator/simulator unit tests), Jest (renderer), PyInstaller build, Electron Builder MSI, bundle size gate (fail if MSI > 185 MB), artifact upload. Deployment: IT pushes MSI via SCCM to registered shop-floor workstations. Auto-updater disabled for air-gapped environment; updates delivered as new MSI via SCCM.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON logs written to %APPDATA%/gcode-migrator/logs/app-YYYY-MM-DD.log with 30-day rotation. FastAPI exposes Prometheus /metrics on localhost:8765 including: migrations_total counter, validation_errors_total by severity, simulation_duration_ms histogram, upload_success_total and upload_failure_total. Grafana Agent scrapes metrics and writes to local Prometheus during development and staging. Production workstations write only to local log files; no remote telemetry.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost — fully offline desktop application. Windows workstation hardware cost is plant IT responsibility. GitHub Actions CI usage is minimal (MSI build <15 min per run). No scaling considerations beyond single-user workstation performance; large G-code programs (>10,000 lines) processed in background thread with progress SSE.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (completed): G-code import, dialect detection, translation engine for Fanuc and Siemens dialects, validation report with error codes, ISO export, SQLite schema, JWT auth, MSI packaging. Phase 2 (current round): Human-readable validation error descriptions via error_codes lookup table, bundle size optimization to meet 200 MB constraint, kinematic dry-run simulation with axis limit enforcement, controller upload via FTP/SFTP. Phase 3 (roadmap): Heidenhain and Mazak dialect support, batch migration for program libraries, network shared program repository, ERP work order integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff for linting, black for formatting, pytest with 85% coverage gate for parser, translator, and simulator modules. TypeScript: ESLint + Prettier, Jest for renderer logic, no any types in parser response interfaces. IPC contract (FastAPI schema) treated as first-class API surface — OpenAPI spec generated and committed to repo. Dialect translation tables stored as versioned JSON files in repo, not hardcoded. ADR documents for each new dialect addition.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Incorrect translation table mapping causes valid ISO G-code to be semantically wrong for a specific controller model. Mitigation: per-controller integration test suite with reference programs and expected outputs. Risk: SQLCipher key loss on workstation failure means migration history unrecoverable. Mitigation: PBKDF2 key derivation from user passphrase plus IT-managed key escrow file. Tradeoff: PyInstaller + Electron bundle creates a large installer but eliminates Python environment setup on shop-floor workstations. Tradeoff: numpy-only kinematic simulation avoids scipy bundle bloat but limits simulation accuracy to trapezoidal motion profiles without jerk-limited smoothing.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed pygcode as primary G-code parser with custom extension modules for proprietary dialects. Selected SQLCipher over plaintext SQLite for program IP protection. Chose localhost HTTP (FastAPI) over Electron IPC serialization for clean Python-JS boundary. Confirmed numpy-only simulation to meet bundle size constraint — scipy excluded.

metadata.notes: CNC G-code migration desktop tool, round 3 revision closing bundle size and validation UX issues.

profile.domain: Predictive Bearing Failure Detection Research Prototype

project_goal.value: Research prototype benchmarking real-time vibration signal processing algorithms for predictive bearing failure detection on high-speed spindles, comparing FFT, wavelet decomposition, and CNN-based approaches under varying load profiles
project_goal.rationale: Core research objective to identify best algorithm for production deployment

target_users.value: Research engineers and data scientists analyzing spindle bearing health on manufacturing test rigs
target_users.rationale: Internal research team only; no external users

project_class.rationale: Experimental benchmarking system without production deployment requirements

capabilities.rationale: Signal processing backend, ClickHouse time-series storage, CNN model inference, analytics dashboards

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time signal processing with parallel algorithm pipelines and GPU inference at research scale

risk_level.rationale: Research prototype on internal test rig; no production systems affected

data_sensitivity.rationale: Vibration telemetry and bearing failure events are internal research data, not personal or financial

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible only within research lab network; no public or partner access

access_model.value: Custom JWT with researcher and admin roles; single shared lab deployment
access_model.rationale: Internal lab tool; lightweight auth appropriate for research context

feature_scope.value: Vibration signal ingestion, configurable batch size preprocessing, FFT pipeline, wavelet decomposition pipeline, CNN inference pipeline, side-by-side latency and F1 benchmarking, benchmark result aggregation across load profiles, Grafana dashboards, failure event labeling interface
feature_scope.rationale: Full benchmark coverage across three algorithm families under load variation

mvp_scope.value: FFT and wavelet pipelines with side-by-side latency benchmarking, ClickHouse ingestion, basic Grafana dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: CNN pipeline added after baseline algorithms are validated

security_baseline.value: JWT authentication for API access, HTTPS within lab network, no external egress, structured audit log for experiment runs
security_baseline.rationale: Internal research tool; lightweight security baseline appropriate

frontend_stack.value: None - headless API with Grafana for visualization
frontend_stack.rationale: No custom frontend; Grafana dashboards provide all analytics visualization

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for benchmark orchestration and ingestion API
backend_stack.rationale: Python has best-in-class signal processing (numpy, scipy, PyWavelets) and ML (PyTorch) ecosystem

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for time-series vibration samples and benchmark results; Redis 7 for pipeline state and inter-process coordination
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse columnar storage delivers the query performance needed for billion-row vibration sample analysis

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Linux server in research lab with NVIDIA GPU for CNN inference
hosting_target.rationale: GPU acceleration required for CNN training and inference; cloud GPU cost unjustified for research prototype

privacy_retention_policy.value: Vibration samples retained for duration of research project (estimated 18 months); failure_events retained permanently for labeled dataset
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Research datasets must be preserved for reproducibility; raw samples can be purged after paper publication

future_scope.value: Production deployment of winning algorithm as real-time edge inference on spindle controller, integration with CMMS for maintenance scheduling
future_scope.rationale: Prototype findings feed production deployment decision

constraints.value: Must run on bare-metal Linux with single NVIDIA A100 GPU; no cloud APIs; all inference local; Redis for pipeline state
constraints.rationale: Air-gapped lab network; cloud egress not permitted; GPU already procured

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for system metrics, custom benchmark_runs table in ClickHouse for experiment telemetry
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard research lab monitoring stack; experiment results queryable via SQL

execution_preference.value: Iterative research cycles with weekly experiment milestones; no fixed sprint cadence
execution_preference.rationale: Research work requires flexibility for hypothesis-driven iteration

llm_integration.value: Local PyTorch CNN model (ResNet-18 adapted for 1D vibration spectra) for bearing failure classification; no external LLM APIs; model weights stored in local model registry
llm_integration.rationale: CNN-based approach is one of three competing algorithms; local inference required for air-gapped environment

project.goal: Benchmark FFT, wavelet, and CNN algorithms for bearing failure detection under load variation
project.target_users: Research engineers and data scientists

backend.framework: FastAPI with async background tasks for pipeline execution
backend.signal_processing: numpy, scipy.signal for FFT, PyWavelets for wavelet decomposition, PyTorch for CNN

security.auth: Custom JWT

data.db: ClickHouse for vibration samples and benchmark results
data.cache: Redis 7 for pipeline state and job coordination

reasoner_reviews.product: The benchmark framework is well-scoped for comparing three algorithm families. The key gap in prior rounds was insufficient detail on how F1 scores are computed - specifically how ground-truth bearing failure labels are associated with vibration windows for precision-recall calculation. This must be clarified in the data model and workflows sections.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The architecture of three parallel algorithm pipelines feeding a unified benchmark result store is sound. Redis pipeline state management for long-running benchmark jobs is appropriate. The ClickHouse schema for vibration_samples needs a partition key on sensor_id and sample_timestamp to avoid full-table scans on benchmark queries. Previous rounds lacked this detail.
reasoner_reviews.security: Low-risk internal prototype. JWT and HTTPS within lab network are appropriate. The main concern from round 2 was that experiment reproducibility requires locking algorithm_configs at benchmark run time - a mutable config could silently change results between runs.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Bare-metal A100 GPU constraint is well-managed. CNN inference pipeline should pre-load model weights at startup rather than per-request to avoid inference latency spikes. PyTorch model warm-up was flagged as missing in round 2.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 improved the ClickHouse schema and pipeline design. The benchmark result aggregation across load profiles is still described too generically - it should specify which statistical aggregates are computed (mean latency, p95 latency, precision, recall, F1 at threshold 0.5) and how load_profiles are varied in the test fixture.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with four domain modules: ingestion (sensor data intake), pipeline_runner (parallel FFT/wavelet/CNN execution), benchmark_aggregator (cross-load-profile result computation), model_registry (CNN weight versioning).
backend.api_patterns: REST API. POST /ingest/batch {sensor_id, samples[]}. POST /benchmarks/run {load_profile_id, algorithm_ids[], batch_size}. GET /benchmarks/{run_id}/results. GET /benchmarks/{run_id}/metrics. GET /algorithms. POST /models/load {model_version}.
backend.failure_handling: Long-running benchmark jobs submitted as FastAPI background tasks tracked in Redis with job_id. If pipeline step fails, Redis job state set to failed with error detail. Client polls GET /benchmarks/{run_id}/status for completion.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly GitLab CI scheduled job triggers automated benchmark sweep across all load_profiles and algorithm combinations, storing results to ClickHouse for trending analysis.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT signed HS256, 24-hour expiry appropriate for research use. Researcher and admin roles. Admin required for model_registry write and ClickHouse table management.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: experiment_audit table in ClickHouse recording run_id, algorithm_configs snapshot (JSON), load_profile_id, initiated_by, started_at, completed_at. Config snapshot ensures reproducibility - algorithm parameters frozen at run initiation.
security.security_risks: Risk: mutable algorithm_config between benchmark runs corrupts comparative results. Mitigation: algorithm_configs snapshot stored immutably at run start; config changes create new config version ID.

data.entities: spindle_sensors (id, location, spindle_model, sample_rate_hz, installed_at), vibration_samples (sensor_id, sample_timestamp, channel ENUM x/y/z, amplitude_g FLOAT, partition by sensor_id YYYYMM), benchmark_runs (id UUID, load_profile_id, algorithm_ids[], batch_size, status, initiated_by, started_at, completed_at), algorithm_configs (id UUID, algorithm_type ENUM fft/wavelet/cnn, params_json, version, created_at), failure_events (id UUID, sensor_id, detected_at, confirmed_at, failure_mode ENUM spalling/fatigue_crack/lubrication_failure, labeled_by), load_profiles (id UUID, description, spindle_rpm, radial_load_n, duration_s, created_at), model_versions (id UUID, algorithm_type, weights_path, training_dataset_hash, val_f1, created_at).
data.storage_design: ClickHouse: vibration_samples table partitioned by toYYYYMM(sample_timestamp) and clustered by sensor_id. MergeTree engine. benchmark_runs and algorithm_configs in separate ClickHouse database for benchmark metadata. Redis: benchmark job state hashes keyed by run_id.
data.data_risks: Risk: vibration_samples table grows unboundedly. Mitigation: TTL policy drops raw samples older than 18 months; failure_event windows retained permanently via separate materialized view.

devops.deployment_topology: Single bare-metal Linux server. FastAPI app in Docker container with NVIDIA Container Toolkit for GPU passthrough. ClickHouse in Docker. Redis in Docker. Grafana + Prometheus in Docker Compose.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: ruff lint, pytest unit tests for signal processing modules, docker build, docker push to local registry, deploy to lab server via SSH. Nightly scheduled pipeline runs automated benchmark sweep.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics (benchmark_runs_total, inference_latency_ms histogram by algorithm, pipeline_queue_depth). Grafana dashboards: algorithm comparison (F1 vs latency scatter), load profile heatmaps, ClickHouse query latency. Custom benchmark_runs ClickHouse table queryable via Grafana ClickHouse plugin.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: A100 GPU thermal throttling under sustained CNN inference. Mitigation: Prometheus nvidia_gpu_temperature alert at 80C; benchmark runner throttles inference batch rate if temperature exceeded.

ISS-001.detail: The benchmark_runs results schema does not specify how F1 scores are computed - which labeling window size aligns vibration samples to failure_events, what classification threshold is used, and how precision/recall are calculated from streaming data.

benchmark_results table added but F1 calculation logic still absent

ISS-002.detail: Round 2 noted that algorithm_configs can be mutated between benchmark runs, invalidating comparative results. No snapshot mechanism was implemented.

ISS-003.title: CNN model warm-up not addressed in inference pipeline
ISS-003.detail: PyTorch model weights loaded per-request in round 2, causing inference latency spikes. Resolved in round 3 by specifying startup model pre-load.

history.note: Per-request model load causes cold-start spikes
Model pre-loaded at FastAPI startup lifecycle hook

Implement algorithm_configs immutability by snapshotting params_json at benchmark run creation and storing as immutable JSON in benchmark_runs table
Pre-load PyTorch CNN model weights at FastAPI startup using lifespan context manager

previous_plan.title: Spindle Bearing Failure Detection Benchmark - Round 2 Plan
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python FastAPI benchmark framework comparing FFT, wavelet, and CNN algorithms for bearing failure detection with ClickHouse storage and Grafana visualization.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI orchestrates parallel algorithm pipelines with Redis job state. ClickHouse stores vibration_samples partitioned by sensor and time.
previous_plan.data_model: spindle_sensors, vibration_samples (partitioned), benchmark_runs, algorithm_configs, failure_events, load_profiles, model_versions.
previous_plan.workflows: Batch ingestion from sensor buffers; parallel pipeline execution per algorithm; benchmark result collection; Grafana visualization.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT, HTTPS within lab, experiment audit log in ClickHouse with config snapshots.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must resolve ISS-001 by fully specifying the F1 computation methodology: 500ms pre-failure labeling window aligned to failure_events.confirmed_at, binary threshold at 0.5, per-window true/false positive counting in a benchmark_results child table. ISS-002 gets closed by adding algorithm_config_snapshot_json to benchmark_runs schema and populating it at run creation. ISS-003 (CNN warm-up) is already resolved. The plan quality target is moderate - most sections are solid but some may be slightly thinner due to research prototype scope.

fix_report.action_taken: Added benchmark_results table with columns: run_id, algorithm_type, window_start, window_end, prediction_score, threshold, true_positive, false_positive, false_negative. Specified F1 computation: 500ms labeling window preceding failure_events.confirmed_at; prediction considered TP if score >= 0.5 and window overlaps failure event; FP if score >= 0.5 with no overlapping failure event; FN if failure event has no overlapping positive prediction. Aggregate F1 = 2*precision*recall / (precision+recall) computed across all windows per benchmark run per algorithm.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Benchmark results are statistically valid and publishable; researchers can compare algorithm F1 scores with defined methodology

fix_report.action_taken: Added algorithm_config_snapshot_json TEXT column to benchmark_runs table, populated at POST /benchmarks/run with deepcopy of algorithm_configs.params_json at request time. algorithm_configs records marked immutable after first use (is_locked=true) to prevent post-hoc modification.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Algorithm parameters frozen at benchmark run creation; comparative results across runs are reproducible

target_output.title: Spindle Bearing Failure Detection Benchmark Prototype
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 FastAPI research prototype on a bare-metal Linux server with NVIDIA A100 GPU that ingests high-frequency vibration timeseries from spindle sensors, executes parallel FFT, wavelet decomposition, and CNN classification pipelines with configurable batch sizes, and computes side-by-side F1 scores and latency benchmarks across load profiles. Results are stored in ClickHouse and visualized in Grafana dashboards to identify the optimal algorithm for production spindle bearing failure detection.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI application with four domain modules (ingestion, pipeline_runner, benchmark_aggregator, model_registry) runs in a Docker container with NVIDIA Container Toolkit for GPU passthrough. Redis 7 tracks long-running benchmark job state. ClickHouse stores raw vibration_samples in a partitioned MergeTree table and benchmark_results in a separate metadata database. PyTorch CNN model weights are pre-loaded into GPU memory at FastAPI startup via lifespan context manager to eliminate cold-start inference latency. Three parallel algorithm pipelines (FFT via numpy.fft, wavelet via PyWavelets db4, CNN via ResNet-18 adapted for 1D vibration spectra) execute concurrently within each benchmark run.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI with async background tasks. Signal processing: numpy (FFT), scipy.signal (bandpass filtering), PyWavelets (db4 wavelet decomposition). ML inference: PyTorch 2.3 with ResNet-18 1D adaptation, CUDA 12.3 on NVIDIA A100. Time-series DB: ClickHouse 24.3 with MergeTree. Cache/state: Redis 7. Containerization: Docker with NVIDIA Container Toolkit. CI: GitLab CI. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Vibration Ingestion: POST /ingest/batch accepts sensor_id and amplitude arrays, writes to ClickHouse vibration_samples. Algorithm Pipeline Execution: POST /benchmarks/run launches three parallel background tasks per run, each processing the requested batch through FFT, wavelet, or CNN pipeline. Benchmark Result Collection: per-window classification scores, TP/FP/FN counts, and F1 aggregates stored in benchmark_results. Load Profile Sweep: scheduler runs benchmark across all load_profiles for comprehensive comparison. Grafana Visualization: F1 vs latency scatter, per-load-profile precision-recall curves, algorithm latency histograms. Failure Event Labeling: POST /failure-events to record confirmed bearing failures with failure mode and confirmation timestamp.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI application container (ingestion, pipeline_runner, benchmark_aggregator, model_registry modules). ClickHouse container (vibration_samples, benchmark_runs, benchmark_results, failure_events, load_profiles tables). Redis container (benchmark job state hashes). Grafana + Prometheus containers (algorithm comparison dashboards, GPU health monitoring). NVIDIA A100 GPU (CNN inference, shared via Container Toolkit passthrough).
target_output.workflows: Benchmark Run Execution: researcher POSTs /benchmarks/run with load_profile_id, algorithm_ids, and batch_size; API creates benchmark_run record with algorithm_config_snapshot_json populated from current algorithm_configs; three async background tasks launched in parallel - FFT task applies bandpass filter (200-10000 Hz) and computes power spectral density per 100ms window; wavelet task applies db4 3-level decomposition and extracts energy coefficients per sub-band; CNN task pre-processes spectrogram and runs ResNet-18 inference on A100; each pipeline emits prediction_score per window to benchmark_results with window timestamps. F1 Evaluation: after pipeline tasks complete, benchmark_aggregator joins benchmark_results windows to failure_events where window_end is within 500ms before confirmed_at; windows with prediction_score >= 0.5 overlapping a failure_event are counted as TP; windows with score >= 0.5 and no overlapping failure_event are FP; failure_events with no overlapping positive prediction are FN; aggregate F1 computed per algorithm and stored to benchmark_runs.
target_output.data_model: spindle_sensors (id UUID, location TEXT, spindle_model TEXT, sample_rate_hz INTEGER, installed_at DATETIME). vibration_samples (sensor_id UUID, sample_timestamp DateTime64(3), channel ENUM x/y/z, amplitude_g Float32) ENGINE=MergeTree PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(sample_timestamp) ORDER BY (sensor_id, sample_timestamp) TTL sample_timestamp + INTERVAL 18 MONTH. benchmark_runs (id UUID, load_profile_id UUID, algorithm_ids Array(String), batch_size INTEGER, algorithm_config_snapshot_json TEXT, status ENUM running/completed/failed, f1_fft Float32, f1_wavelet Float32, f1_cnn Float32, initiated_by TEXT, started_at DATETIME, completed_at DATETIME). benchmark_results (run_id UUID, algorithm_type ENUM fft/wavelet/cnn, window_start DateTime64(3), window_end DateTime64(3), prediction_score Float32, threshold Float32, true_positive UInt8, false_positive UInt8, false_negative UInt8) ENGINE=MergeTree ORDER BY (run_id, algorithm_type, window_start). algorithm_configs (id UUID, algorithm_type TEXT, params_json TEXT, version INTEGER, is_locked Boolean, created_at DATETIME). failure_events (id UUID, sensor_id UUID, detected_at DATETIME, confirmed_at DATETIME, failure_mode ENUM spalling/fatigue_crack/lubrication_failure, labeled_by TEXT). load_profiles (id UUID, description TEXT, spindle_rpm INTEGER, radial_load_n FLOAT, duration_s INTEGER). model_versions (id UUID, algorithm_type TEXT, weights_path TEXT, training_dataset_hash TEXT, val_f1 Float32, created_at DATETIME).
target_output.api_design: POST /ingest/batch {sensor_id, channel, samples: [{timestamp, amplitude_g}]} -> {ingested_count}. POST /benchmarks/run {load_profile_id, algorithm_ids, batch_size} -> {run_id, status}. GET /benchmarks/{run_id}/status -> {status, progress_pct}. GET /benchmarks/{run_id}/results -> {f1_fft, f1_wavelet, f1_cnn, latency_p95_ms by algorithm}. POST /failure-events {sensor_id, detected_at, confirmed_at, failure_mode, labeled_by} -> {id}. GET /load-profiles -> [{id, description, spindle_rpm, radial_load_n}]. POST /models/load {model_version_id} -> {status}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT (HS256, 24-hour expiry) with researcher and admin roles. Admin role required for POST /models/load and ClickHouse DDL operations. HTTPS within lab network via self-signed certificate. algorithm_configs marked is_locked=true after first benchmark use - subsequent modification returns 409 Conflict. experiment_audit table in ClickHouse records run_id, algorithm_config_snapshot_json, load_profile_id, initiated_by, started_at, completed_at for research reproducibility. No external egress from lab network.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Docker Compose on bare-metal Linux: fastapi container (Python 3.12, NVIDIA Container Toolkit GPU passthrough), clickhouse container (volume-mounted /var/lib/clickhouse), redis container, grafana container, prometheus container. GitLab CI: ruff lint, pytest (signal processing unit tests, F1 computation logic, ClickHouse schema tests), docker build, push to local registry, deploy to lab server via SSH deploy key. Nightly scheduled CI job: automated benchmark sweep across all load_profiles and algorithm combinations writing results to ClickHouse trending table. No auto-update; manual deploy via CI manual trigger.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics: benchmark_runs_total counter by algorithm, inference_latency_ms histogram by algorithm_type (p50/p95/p99), pipeline_queue_depth gauge, gpu_inference_batch_size histogram. Grafana dashboards: Algorithm Comparison (F1 score by algorithm and load_profile scatter), Latency Analysis (p95 latency histograms per algorithm), GPU Health (A100 temperature, utilization, memory usage via nvidia-smi exporter). Alert: A100 temperature > 80C triggers Prometheus alert and benchmark runner reduces CNN batch rate by 50%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost - bare-metal server already provisioned. ClickHouse storage estimate: 1 kHz vibration at 3 channels, 4 bytes/sample yields ~10 GB/month per sensor for 18-month retention. A100 GPU amortized cost included in lab infrastructure. GitLab CI compute uses lab-internal runners.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (completed): vibration ingestion API, FFT and wavelet pipelines, ClickHouse schema, basic Grafana dashboard, benchmark_runs table. Phase 2 (prior rounds): CNN pipeline with ResNet-18 1D adaptation, A100 GPU inference, Redis job state, F1 computation (partially resolved). Phase 3 (current round): F1 methodology finalized with benchmark_results table and 500ms labeling window, algorithm_config immutability via is_locked and snapshot_json, PyTorch model pre-load at startup, load_profile sweep CI job. Phase 4 (future): Production edge deployment of winning algorithm on spindle controller.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff + black, pytest with 90% coverage gate for signal processing modules (FFT, wavelet, F1 computation). ClickHouse schema changes via migration scripts in repo with sequential version numbering. Algorithm params stored in algorithm_configs table, not hardcoded. Benchmark results must be reproducible: algorithm_config_snapshot_json and data window checksums logged. Research findings documented in /docs/experiments/ as Jupyter notebooks committed to repo.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: F1 scores sensitive to labeling window size (500ms) - different window choices change which algorithm wins. Mitigation: run sensitivity analysis across 100ms, 250ms, 500ms, 1000ms windows and report all results. Risk: ClickHouse vibration_samples grows beyond lab server disk capacity. Mitigation: 18-month TTL and disk usage Prometheus alert at 80% capacity. Tradeoff: ResNet-18 adapted for 1D spectra vs purpose-built 1D CNN - faster iteration with ResNet-18 but potentially suboptimal architecture; acceptable for prototype phase. Tradeoff: parallel background tasks vs sequential execution - parallel maximizes throughput but complicates failure isolation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed 500ms pre-failure labeling window for F1 computation based on domain expert input on bearing failure progression timescales. Selected PyWavelets db4 wavelet basis per literature review. Confirmed ClickHouse over TimescaleDB for vibration_samples query performance at billion-row scale. CNN warm-up resolved via FastAPI lifespan pre-load.

metadata.notes: Spindle bearing failure detection benchmark prototype, round 3 closing F1 methodology and algorithm config immutability.

profile.domain: Manufacturing Execution System (MES) for Discrete Parts Plant

project_goal.value: Fullstack manufacturing execution system for a discrete parts plant tracking work orders, WIP inventory, machine utilization, and quality inspection results in real time across multiple production lines
project_goal.rationale: Core operational requirement to replace paper-based shop-floor tracking and siloed PLM/ERP data

target_users.value: Production schedulers, shop-floor operators, quality engineers, shift supervisors, and plant managers across multiple production lines
target_users.rationale: All plant roles interact with MES for work order execution and reporting

project_class.rationale: Full UI for shop-floor terminals and management dashboards backed by Java Spring Boot API on AKS

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, realtime, admin_panel, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Real-time WIP tracking, batch quality reports, admin configuration, analytics dashboards all required

complexity_level.rationale: Multiple production lines, high-frequency machine event streams, cross-line analytics, concurrent shop-floor terminal load

risk_level.rationale: Production disruption from incorrect work order routing or false quality signals has direct revenue and safety impact

data_sensitivity.rationale: Work order routing, BOM structures, and process parameters are trade secrets; quality records are subject to IATF 16949 audit

external_exposure.rationale: MES is accessible only from plant network and VPN; no public internet exposure

access_model.value: Azure AD (Entra ID) SSO with RBAC: operator, quality_engineer, supervisor, scheduler, admin roles; MFA enforced for all roles
access_model.rationale: IATF 16949 requires traceable user identity for all quality-critical actions; Azure AD aligns with corporate identity strategy

feature_scope.value: Work order creation and release, operation sequence routing, shop-floor terminal for operation completions, WIP lot tracking, real-time machine utilization dashboards, end-of-line quality inspection with dimensional measurements and defect coding, shift logs, nightly batch yield reports, admin panel for BOM and routing configuration
feature_scope.rationale: Full MES feature set required for plant-wide deployment

mvp_scope.value: Work order release and operation completion reporting on two pilot production lines, WIP lot tracking, basic machine utilization dashboard, quality inspection recording
mvp_scope.rationale: Two-line pilot validates core MES loop before plant-wide rollout

security_baseline.value: Azure AD MFA, TLS 1.3, AKS network policies, PostgreSQL encryption at rest, row-level security for production line data isolation, audit log for all quality-critical mutations
security_baseline.rationale: IATF 16949 and SOC 2 Type II require traceable access and data integrity controls

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for fast terminal load times on shop-floor hardware; React ecosystem familiar to team

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2
backend_stack.rationale: Enterprise Java ecosystem; Spring Boot provides production-grade messaging, transaction management, and Actuator observability

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7 for event caching and real-time state
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL handles relational MES data with row-level security; Redis provides sub-millisecond WIP lot position reads for shop-floor terminals

hosting_target.value: AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service) in plant Azure subscription
hosting_target.rationale: Kubernetes enables rolling deployments without production line downtime; Azure aligns with corporate cloud strategy

privacy_retention_policy.value: Work order and quality records retained 10 years per IATF 16949; shift logs retained 3 years; machine event streams retained 90 days hot then archived to Azure Blob Storage
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Automotive supply chain quality records retention requirements

future_scope.value: ERP (SAP) integration for work order creation and material consumption, predictive OEE analytics, MES SaaS multi-tenant offering
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap contingent on single-plant pilot success

constraints.value: Shop-floor terminals are Windows 10 thin clients; Next.js UI must load in under 3 seconds on 100 Mbps LAN; no internet egress from plant network; AKS cluster shared with other plant applications
constraints.rationale: Plant IT constraints on terminal hardware and network topology

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed tracing, structured JSON logging, Datadog dashboards for OEE and quality SLA metrics, PagerDuty for critical production alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; OEE dashboards required by plant management

execution_preference.value: Agile with 2-week sprints; deployments gated on staging validation; rollback procedure documented
execution_preference.rationale: Production system requires validated deployment gates

compliance_context.value: ISO 9001 quality management, IATF 16949 for automotive supply chain traceability, SOC 2 Type II for SaaS deployment path
compliance_context.rationale: Automotive customers require IATF 16949 compliance; SaaS path requires SOC 2

project.goal: Real-time MES for discrete parts plant tracking work orders, WIP, machine utilization, and quality inspection
project.target_users: Production schedulers, operators, quality engineers, supervisors, plant managers

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with custom shop-floor component library
frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for terminal session state
frontend.realtime: WebSocket subscriptions for WIP lot position and machine status updates

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3.2 with Spring Data JPA, Spring WebSocket, Spring Batch
backend.api: REST with OpenAPI 3.1 and WebSocket for real-time events

security.auth: Azure AD Entra ID with OIDC and MFA
security.authorization: Spring Security RBAC with row-level filtering by production_line_id

data.cache: Redis 7 for WIP lot positions and machine status
data.archive: Azure Blob Storage for machine event stream archives

reasoner_reviews.product: The MES scope is comprehensive and well-aligned to the discrete parts manufacturing workflow. Rounds 1 and 2 established the core work order and WIP tracking architecture. The outstanding gap is the quality inspection data model - specifically how dimensional measurement tolerances and defect codes are structured per part number, and how first-pass yield is computed from inspection_results for the nightly batch report.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot with WebSocket and Redis for real-time WIP lot positions is the right architectural pattern. The concern from round 2 is that the Spring Batch nightly yield job design lacks detail on how it aggregates inspection_results per work_order and production_line to compute first-pass yield percentages and flag lines breaching the quality SLA threshold. This is the core analytics requirement.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD RBAC with row-level security on production_line_id is correctly designed. The IATF 16949 traceability requirement means every quality inspection mutation must be logged with user identity, timestamp, and previous value. The audit_events table design in round 2 was incomplete - it logged actions but not the before/after values for dimensional measurement corrections.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Next.js SSR on AKS with Redis-backed WIP state should meet the 3-second terminal load constraint. The shop-floor WebSocket subscriptions need Redis Pub/Sub fanout to handle concurrent terminal connections across two production lines without Spring instance memory pressure.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 produced strong architecture and data model sections but the batch_jobs section describing the nightly yield report remains thin. The quality SLA threshold configuration (what FPY% triggers a supervisor alert) is not specified in either the data model or the admin panel feature map.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot application with domain packages: work_orders (release, routing, completion), wip (lot tracking, position events), quality (inspection recording, defect coding), analytics (OEE computation, yield aggregation), admin (BOM, routing, line configuration).
backend.api_patterns: REST over HTTPS with OpenAPI 3.1. WebSocket (STOMP over SockJS) for real-time WIP lot position and machine status events. Spring Batch jobs for nightly yield aggregation. All mutations return operation_id for audit correlation.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly Spring Batch job: reads inspection_results for the shift window, computes first-pass yield per work_order and production_line_id, compares against quality_sla_thresholds table, writes yield_reports records, triggers PagerDuty alert via Datadog if FPY below threshold. Job runs at 23:30 local time with configurable window.
backend.failure_handling: Optimistic locking on wip_lots to prevent concurrent operation completion conflicts. Circuit breaker on Redis WIP cache reads with PostgreSQL fallback. Spring Batch restart from last checkpoint on job failure.

security.auth_design: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with Spring Security OAuth2 resource server. JWT claims include user roles and production_line_ids. MFA enforced via Conditional Access policy in Azure AD.
security.authorization_model: Row-level security in PostgreSQL: operators and quality engineers see only records for their assigned production_line_ids. Supervisors see their shift scope. Schedulers and admins have plant-wide access. Spring Security @PreAuthorize annotations enforce role checks at service layer.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table records entity_type, entity_id, action, user_id, user_name, before_json, after_json, timestamp for all quality-critical mutations (inspection result create/update, defect record create, work order status change). Append-only table with no UPDATE permission for app role.
security.security_risks: Risk: Shared AKS namespace with other plant applications. Mitigation: AKS NetworkPolicy restricts egress to PostgreSQL and Redis namespaces only; PodSecurityAdmission enforces non-root containers.

data.entities: work_orders (id UUID PK, part_number, quantity, route_id FK, production_line_id FK, status ENUM planned/released/in_progress/completed/on_hold, released_at, completed_at). operations (id UUID PK, work_order_id FK, route_step_id FK, machine_resource_id FK, status ENUM pending/active/completed, started_at, completed_at, operator_id). wip_lots (id UUID PK, work_order_id FK, quantity, current_operation_id FK, position_updated_at). machine_resources (id UUID PK, production_line_id FK, machine_code, description, oee_target_pct). inspection_results (id UUID PK, work_order_id FK, lot_id FK, inspector_id, inspected_at, overall_pass BOOLEAN, measurement_json JSONB, defect_codes TEXT[]). defect_records (id UUID PK, inspection_result_id FK, defect_code, description, severity ENUM minor/major/critical, disposition ENUM rework/scrap/use_as_is). shift_logs (id UUID PK, production_line_id FK, shift ENUM morning/afternoon/night, operator_ids TEXT[], notes, shift_start, shift_end). yield_reports (id UUID PK, production_line_id FK, work_order_id FK, report_date, total_inspected, first_pass_count, fpy_pct, below_sla BOOLEAN). quality_sla_thresholds (production_line_id UUID FK, part_number TEXT, min_fpy_pct FLOAT, effective_from DATE).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security policies on production_line_id. Redis 7 hashes for wip_lot current position (key: lot:{id}, TTL 8 hours). Azure Blob Storage for machine event stream archives (Parquet format, partitioned by date and machine_code). Flyway for schema migrations.
data.schema_notes: measurement_json stores dimensional measurement values per feature code (e.g., {"DIM-01": 25.03, "DIM-02": 12.97}); tolerance limits stored in part_inspection_plans table joined at report time. This avoids schema changes when inspection plans are updated.
data.data_risks: Risk: measurement_json JSONB makes tolerance validation application-side only. Mitigation: part_inspection_plans defines nominal and tolerance per feature_code; Spring validation layer enforces before write. Risk: 10-year retention for IATF compliance on hot PostgreSQL. Mitigation: archive records older than 2 years to Azure Blob Parquet via scheduled export job.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster with namespaces: mes-prod, mes-staging. Next.js deployed as Deployment with 3 replicas; Spring Boot as Deployment with 5 replicas and HPA on CPU 70%. PostgreSQL on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (HA pair). Redis on Azure Cache for Redis Premium tier. Ingress via Azure Application Gateway with WAF.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, SpotBugs static analysis, unit + integration tests (JUnit + Testcontainers for PostgreSQL and Redis), Docker build and push to Azure Container Registry, Helm chart deploy to mes-staging, automated smoke tests, manual gate for production deploy. Flyway migration runs as Kubernetes init container before Spring Boot startup.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with Java ddtrace agent (distributed tracing across REST and WebSocket handlers). Structured JSON logging via Logback to Datadog Log Management. Custom Datadog dashboards: OEE per machine, FPY per production line, work order throughput rate, WebSocket connection count. PagerDuty P1 alerts: FPY below SLA, Spring Boot pod crash loop, PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: Flyway migration failure during rolling deployment leaves schema in mixed state. Mitigation: backward-compatible migrations only (additive); Blue/Green deployment for breaking schema changes.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with route groups: (terminal) for shop-floor operator views, (supervisor) for shift dashboards, (scheduler) for work order management, (quality) for inspection entry and reports, (admin) for configuration.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Terminal: /terminal/operations - shows pending operations for the logged-in operator's machine; tap to start/complete with timestamp. Scheduler: /scheduler/work-orders - create, release, monitor work orders with Gantt view. Quality: /quality/inspect/[lot_id] - guided inspection form with dimensional inputs per part_inspection_plan. Supervisor: /supervisor/dashboard - live OEE gauges and WIP lot positions per line. Admin: /admin/routes, /admin/bom, /admin/quality-sla.
frontend.state_management: React Query for REST data fetching with 30-second stale time for dashboards. Zustand for terminal session state (current operator, current machine). useWebSocket hook subscribes to STOMP topics for WIP lot position updates and machine status changes.
frontend.frontend_risks: Risk: WebSocket reconnection on flaky shop-floor LAN. Mitigation: SockJS fallback with exponential backoff; stale indicator shown in UI when connection lost > 10 seconds.

ISS-001.title: Nightly yield batch job design missing FPY computation logic
ISS-001.detail: The Spring Batch nightly yield report job has been described at a high level in both prior rounds but the actual step configuration - reading inspection_results, joining to quality_sla_thresholds, computing FPY per production_line_id and work_order, and writing yield_reports - is not specified. Without this, the core analytics requirement is unimplemented.

history.note: Batch job mentioned but not designed
Job described as nightly but computation steps still absent

ISS-002.title: Audit log missing before/after values for quality mutations
ISS-002.detail: IATF 16949 requires full traceability of inspection record changes. The audit_events table in round 2 logs action and user_id but not the before_json and after_json values for inspection_result updates, making the audit trail insufficient for automotive quality audits.

history.note: Audit log designed without change capture
audit_events table added but missing before/after value capture

ISS-003.title: Quality SLA threshold configuration not in admin panel feature map
ISS-003.detail: The quality_sla_thresholds table exists in the data model but there is no admin UI for configuring FPY thresholds per production_line_id and part_number. Plant quality engineers cannot manage SLA thresholds without direct database access.

history.note: Table exists but admin UI not described
