previous_plan.title: MES Discrete Parts Plant - Round 2 Plan
previous_plan.executive_summary: Java 21 Spring Boot MES on AKS with Next.js 14 shop-floor terminals, PostgreSQL row-level security, Redis WIP tracking, and Spring Batch nightly quality reports.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Spring Boot REST + WebSocket API on AKS serving Next.js terminals. Redis Pub/Sub for real-time WIP lot position fanout. PostgreSQL with RLS for production line data isolation.
previous_plan.data_model: work_orders, operations, wip_lots, machine_resources, inspection_results, defect_records, shift_logs, quality_sla_thresholds. audit_events with action/user_id logging.
previous_plan.workflows: Work order release triggers operation sequence generation. Shop-floor terminal completion events update wip_lots and push WebSocket updates. Nightly Spring Batch job aggregates FPY.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC, Spring Security RBAC, row-level security by production_line_id, audit_events table, IATF 16949 traceability.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 addresses two high-severity IATF 16949 compliance gaps. ISS-001: I will fully specify the Spring Batch FPY job with named reader, processor, and writer steps including the FPY formula and Datadog alert trigger. ISS-002: I will add before_json/after_json to audit_events and specify the Spring Data JPA @EntityListeners implementation. ISS-003: I will add the /admin/quality-sla page to the functional feature map. This is a strong-quality plan with all sections domain-specific to discrete parts MES.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Spring Batch FPY job with three named steps: (1) InspectionResultsReader - JdbcCursorItemReader reading inspection_results WHERE inspected_at BETWEEN shift_window_start AND shift_window_end grouped by production_line_id and work_order_id; (2) FPYComputationProcessor - computes first_pass_count as COUNT(*) WHERE overall_pass=true, fpy_pct = first_pass_count / total_inspected * 100, joins quality_sla_thresholds by production_line_id and part_number; (3) YieldReportWriter - JdbcBatchItemWriter inserting yield_reports rows, publishing Datadog event 'mes.quality.fpy_below_sla' with production_line_id and fpy_pct if below_sla=true.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Spring Batch FPY job is implementable from spec; nightly quality SLA alerting unblocked

fix_report.action_taken: Added before_json JSONB and after_json JSONB columns to audit_events table. Implemented via Spring Data JPA @EntityListeners with AuditingEntityListener on InspectionResult, DefectRecord, and WorkOrder entities. Before state captured in @PreUpdate by serializing entity to JSON; after state captured in @PostUpdate. Enables full change history for IATF 16949 inspection record audit queries.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Automotive quality auditors can reconstruct full change history for any inspection record; IATF 16949 audit trail requirement satisfied

target_output.title: Manufacturing Execution System - Discrete Parts Plant
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale fullstack MES built with Next.js 14 and Java 21 Spring Boot on Azure AKS, backed by PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security and Redis 7 for real-time WIP tracking. The system manages work order release through ISO-routed operation sequences, tracks WIP lot positions across production lines in real time via WebSocket, captures dimensional inspection results with full change-history audit logging for IATF 16949 traceability, and generates nightly first-pass yield reports via Spring Batch with PagerDuty alerting when quality SLA thresholds are breached.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier AKS deployment: Next.js 14 SSR frontend served from 3 replicas with Azure Application Gateway ingress, Java 21 Spring Boot API with 5 replicas and CPU-based HPA, and Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server in HA configuration. Real-time WIP lot position events flow from operation completion REST calls through Spring WebSocket STOMP broker backed by Redis Pub/Sub, allowing all shop-floor terminals subscribed to a production line topic to receive instantaneous lot position updates. PostgreSQL row-level security policies restrict data access by production_line_id based on Azure AD JWT claims. Spring Batch nightly jobs run as Kubernetes CronJobs computing first-pass yield per production line and comparing against configurable quality_sla_thresholds.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, SockJS with STOMP for WebSocket. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.2, Spring Data JPA, Spring WebSocket, Spring Batch, Spring Security OAuth2. Database: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server (HA), Flyway migrations, row-level security. Cache: Azure Cache for Redis Premium (Redis 7) for WIP lot hashes and Pub/Sub. Infrastructure: AKS with Helm, Azure Container Registry, Azure Application Gateway with WAF. Observability: Datadog APM (ddtrace Java agent), Logback JSON, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Work Order Management: schedulers create work orders with part_number, quantity, and route assignment; release triggers auto-generation of operation sequences per route steps. Shop-Floor Terminal: operator views pending operations for their machine, taps Start and Complete with timestamp capture, WebSocket push updates WIP lot position on all dashboards immediately. WIP Tracking Dashboard: real-time lot position map per production line with in-progress lot count, machine utilization bars, and queue depth per operation. Quality Inspection: guided form per part_inspection_plan with dimensional input per feature code, overall pass/fail determination, and defect code capture for failures. Supervisor Shift Dashboard: live OEE per machine, FPY trend, open defects by severity, shift log entry. Nightly Yield Report: Spring Batch FPY computation per production line and work order with SLA breach alerting. Admin Panel: BOM management, route configuration, machine resource setup, quality SLA threshold CRUD at /admin/quality-sla.
target_output.system_components: Next.js frontend pods (SSR, shop-floor terminal and management views). Spring Boot API pods (REST endpoints, WebSocket STOMP broker, Spring Batch scheduler). Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server (primary + standby HA). Azure Cache for Redis Premium (WIP lot hashes, STOMP message broker backend). Azure Application Gateway with WAF (HTTPS ingress, SSL termination). Azure Container Registry (image storage). Azure AD Entra ID (OIDC authentication, Conditional Access MFA). AKS CronJob pods (nightly FPY Spring Batch jobs). Datadog Agent daemonset (APM, metrics, logs).
target_output.workflows: Work Order Release and Operation Routing: production scheduler POSTs /api/v1/work-orders with part_number, quantity, and route_id; Spring service generates operations records for each route step linked to the assigned machine_resource; wip_lots record created with status 'pending_first_op'; work order status set to 'released'; real-time dashboard subscription receives 'work_order_released' STOMP event. Shop-Floor Operation Completion: operator on terminal POSTs /api/v1/operations/{id}/complete; service validates operator is assigned to machine_resource, updates operation status to 'completed' with timestamp, advances wip_lot current_operation_id to next route step, publishes Redis Pub/Sub message to production_line:{id} topic; all terminals subscribed to that production line receive WebSocket push updating lot position display. End-of-Line Inspection and Defect Capture: quality engineer on terminal navigates to /quality/inspect/{lot_id}; form pre-populated with dimensional feature codes from part_inspection_plans for the lot's part_number; engineer enters measurements, Spring validator checks each against nominal +/- tolerance; if any feature out-of-tolerance, overall_pass=false and defect code form displayed; POST /api/v1/inspections creates inspection_result with measurement_json and triggers @PostUpdate audit capture. Nightly FPY Batch Job: CronJob triggers at 23:30; InspectionResultsReader queries inspection_results for the day's shift window grouped by production_line_id; FPYComputationProcessor computes fpy_pct = first_pass_count / total_inspected * 100 and joins quality_sla_thresholds; YieldReportWriter inserts yield_reports rows and POSTs Datadog event 'mes.quality.fpy_below_sla' with production_line_id and fpy_pct if below_sla=true, triggering PagerDuty P2 alert to shift supervisor.
target_output.data_model: work_orders (id UUID PK, part_number TEXT, quantity INTEGER, route_id UUID FK, production_line_id UUID FK, status ENUM planned/released/in_progress/completed/on_hold, released_by UUID, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). operations (id UUID PK, work_order_id UUID FK, route_step_id UUID FK, machine_resource_id UUID FK, status ENUM pending/active/completed, operator_id UUID, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). wip_lots (id UUID PK, work_order_id UUID FK, quantity INTEGER, current_operation_id UUID FK, position_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). machine_resources (id UUID PK, production_line_id UUID FK, machine_code TEXT UNIQUE, description TEXT, oee_target_pct FLOAT). inspection_results (id UUID PK, work_order_id UUID FK, lot_id UUID FK, inspector_id UUID, inspected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, overall_pass BOOLEAN, measurement_json JSONB, defect_codes TEXT[]). defect_records (id UUID PK, inspection_result_id UUID FK, defect_code TEXT, description TEXT, severity ENUM minor/major/critical, disposition ENUM rework/scrap/use_as_is). shift_logs (id UUID PK, production_line_id UUID FK, shift ENUM morning/afternoon/night, operator_ids UUID[], notes TEXT, shift_start TIMESTAMPTZ, shift_end TIMESTAMPTZ). yield_reports (id UUID PK, production_line_id UUID FK, work_order_id UUID FK, report_date DATE, total_inspected INTEGER, first_pass_count INTEGER, fpy_pct FLOAT, below_sla BOOLEAN). quality_sla_thresholds (production_line_id UUID FK, part_number TEXT, min_fpy_pct FLOAT, effective_from DATE, PRIMARY KEY (production_line_id, part_number, effective_from)). audit_events (id UUID PK, entity_type TEXT, entity_id UUID, action ENUM create/update/delete, user_id UUID, user_name TEXT, before_json JSONB, after_json JSONB, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ). bill_of_materials (id UUID PK, part_number TEXT, revision TEXT, component_part_number TEXT, quantity FLOAT, unit TEXT).
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1 with Spring Security JWT filter. POST /work-orders (release), GET /work-orders?line_id=&status= (list), GET /work-orders/{id}/operations (operation sequence). POST /operations/{id}/start, POST /operations/{id}/complete (terminal completion). GET /wip/lots?line_id= (lot positions). POST /inspections (record quality result), GET /inspections?work_order_id= (history). GET /quality/reports?date=&line_id= (yield reports). CRUD /admin/routes, /admin/machines, /admin/quality-sla. WebSocket: STOMP connect at /ws, subscribe to /topic/line/{production_line_id} for real-time lot position and machine status events.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with Conditional Access MFA policy enforced for all roles. Spring Security RBAC: @PreAuthorize annotations on service methods per role (operator, quality_engineer, supervisor, scheduler, admin). PostgreSQL row-level security policies: CREATE POLICY line_isolation ON work_orders USING (production_line_id = current_setting('app.current_line_id')::uuid). audit_events table: before_json and after_json populated via Spring Data JPA @EntityListeners on InspectionResult, DefectRecord, WorkOrder entities; table has INSERT-only grant for app role. AKS NetworkPolicy restricts Spring Boot egress to PostgreSQL namespace and Redis namespace. WAF rules on Application Gateway block SQLi and XSS. IATF 16949 compliance: full change history on inspection records; 10-year retention enforced via PostgreSQL archive export job.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS namespaces: mes-prod and mes-staging. Spring Boot Deployment: 5 replicas, HPA min 3 max 10 on CPU 70%, liveness probe /actuator/health, readiness probe /actuator/ready. Next.js Deployment: 3 replicas, HPA min 2 max 6 on CPU 60%. Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server: General Purpose 8 vCores HA pair, automated backups 35-day retention. Azure Cache for Redis Premium P2: 13 GB, zone redundant. Flyway migrations run as Kubernetes init container before Spring Boot starts. Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, SpotBugs, JUnit + Testcontainers integration tests, Docker build, push to ACR, Helm upgrade to mes-staging, automated Selenium smoke tests (work order release, operation completion, inspection recording), manual approval gate for mes-prod. Spring Batch FPY jobs as AKS CronJob (cron: '30 23 * * *') with completions:1 and backoffLimit:2.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: Java ddtrace agent as init container, distributed traces across REST, WebSocket, and Batch job spans. Structured JSON via Logback with correlation_id, production_line_id, and user_id MDC fields forwarded to Datadog Log Management. Custom Datadog dashboards: OEE per machine (availability x performance x quality), FPY trend per production line vs SLA threshold, work order throughput (orders completed per hour), WebSocket connection count per AKS pod. PagerDuty P1: Spring Boot pod crash loop, PostgreSQL connection pool >80%; PagerDuty P2: FPY below SLA threshold (triggered by Batch job via Datadog event), operation queue depth >50 lots on any machine.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server GP 8 vCores HA ~$800/month. Azure Cache for Redis P2 ~$300/month. AKS node pool (3x D4s_v3 baseline + HPA headroom) ~$600/month. Azure Application Gateway Standard v2 ~$150/month. Total estimated ~$2,000/month for two production lines. Plant-wide scale (10 lines, 500 concurrent terminal sessions): HPA scales Spring Boot to 10 replicas; Redis Premium scales to P3 (26 GB) for higher Pub/Sub throughput; PostgreSQL scales to 16 vCores.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (delivered): work order creation and release, operation sequence generation for two pilot lines, shop-floor terminal operation completion, WIP lot tracking, basic Grafana-equivalent dashboard via Datadog. Phase 2 (prior rounds): quality inspection recording, defect code capture, real-time WebSocket WIP dashboard, IATF 16949 audit log design. Phase 3 (current round): nightly FPY Spring Batch job with SLA alerting, audit_events before/after change capture for IATF compliance, /admin/quality-sla configuration UI, row-level security on all entities, AKS CronJob for FPY batch. Phase 4 (roadmap): SAP integration for work order ingestion, predictive OEE analytics, SOC 2 Type II controls for SaaS deployment.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java: Checkstyle + SpotBugs in CI, JUnit 5 + Testcontainers for integration tests with real PostgreSQL and Redis, 80% coverage gate on service layer. TypeScript: ESLint strict + Prettier, Jest for React Query hooks, Cypress E2E for operation completion and inspection flows. Flyway migrations: additive only (no DROP or ALTER COLUMN); breaking changes via Blue/Green deploy. All quality-critical REST mutations return audit_event_id in response body for traceability. ADR documents for route algorithm, FPY formula, and IATF retention policy decisions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Operation completion event storm on shift change (all operators completing simultaneously) overloads Redis Pub/Sub fanout. Mitigation: Spring WebSocket broker uses Redis Pub/Sub with per-line topic partitioning; horizontal scaling of Spring pods absorbs burst. Risk: Flyway migration during rolling deployment causes schema version mismatch between old and new Spring Boot pods. Mitigation: backward-compatible migrations only; Blue/Green for structural changes. Tradeoff: monolithic Spring Boot vs microservices - chose monolith for MVP speed with clear domain package boundaries for future service extraction. Tradeoff: JSONB for measurement_json vs normalized dimensional tables - JSONB chosen to avoid schema changes when inspection plans change, at the cost of application-side validation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Spring Batch over Apache Spark for FPY aggregation - batch job processes thousands of records per shift, not millions; Spring Batch Testcontainers integration testing is simpler. Confirmed Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server over self-managed PostgreSQL on AKS for HA and automated patching. Confirmed STOMP over raw WebSocket for message routing by production line topic. IATF 16949 10-year retention confirmed with PostgreSQL-to-Azure-Blob archive export at 2-year mark.

metadata.notes: MES for discrete parts plant, round 3 closing IATF 16949 audit trail and FPY batch job design gaps.

profile.domain: Injection Molding Defect Prediction AI System

project_goal.value: AI system ingesting terabytes of sensor telemetry from injection molding machines to predict part defect probability before ejection, enabling press operators to adjust process parameters mid-cycle
project_goal.rationale: Core business objective to reduce scrap rate in injection molding production

target_users.value: Press operators receiving real-time defect probability scores and process parameter adjustment recommendations on machine HMI displays
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of prediction output are machine operators on the shop floor

project_class.rationale: Core value is ML model inference pipeline for real-time defect prediction, not a generic backend or data platform

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, analytics, batch_jobs, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Real-time inference pipeline, ClickHouse time-series storage, LSTM model, analytics dashboards, weekly retraining batch jobs

complexity_level.rationale: Terabytes of high-frequency sensor telemetry, sub-200ms inference SLA, GPU-based model training, IATF 16949 traceability

risk_level.rationale: False negatives allow defective parts to reach customers; incorrect process adjustments could damage molds

data_sensitivity.rationale: Process parameters and defect patterns are trade secrets; IATF 16949 traceability records are audit-sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: System operates on plant network only; no internet exposure permitted

access_model.value: AWS Cognito with press operator and data scientist roles; MFA for data scientist admin access
access_model.rationale: Cognito provides managed auth for ECS Fargate deployment; operator role read-only for inference results

feature_scope.value: High-frequency sensor data ingestion (temperature, pressure, screw position) per molding cycle, feature pipeline computing cycle-level aggregates, LSTM model inference scoring defect probability within 200ms of cycle completion, process parameter adjustment recommendations, weekly model retraining batch job with canary deployment, defect prediction audit trail for IATF traceability, analytics dashboards for defect rate trends
feature_scope.rationale: Full production system requirements from engineering and quality teams

mvp_scope.value: Sensor ingestion for two press machines, feature pipeline, LSTM inference with defect probability output, basic cycle dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Two-machine pilot validates inference pipeline before fleet-wide deployment

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA for admin roles, TLS 1.3, VPC with no public subnets, ClickHouse encrypted at rest, audit trail for all model version promotions and defect predictions for IATF traceability
security_baseline.rationale: High risk and confidential data mandate strong access controls and complete audit trail

frontend_stack.value: None - headless system; inference results pushed to machine HMI via MQTT and to internal Grafana dashboards
frontend_stack.rationale: No custom web UI; HMI displays are machine-integrated; Grafana for analytics

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for ingestion and inference APIs
backend_stack.rationale: Python ML ecosystem (PyTorch, numpy, pandas) is required for LSTM model training and inference

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for sensor telemetry and cycle-level features; Redis 7 for real-time cycle state and inference result caching
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse columnar engine handles terabyte-scale sensor telemetry with fast analytical queries; Redis provides sub-millisecond cycle state reads

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for inference and ingestion services; AWS Batch for model retraining on GPU instances
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate for managed containerized inference; AWS Batch with GPU instances for weekly retraining without persistent GPU cost

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sensor telemetry retained 90 days hot in ClickHouse then archived to S3; defect predictions and model versions retained 5 years for IATF 16949 traceability
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Automotive quality traceability requirements for part production records

future_scope.value: Fleet-wide deployment across all 50 press machines, integration with ERP for scrap cost reporting, multi-plant model federation
future_scope.rationale: Two-machine pilot success gates fleet deployment

constraints.value: Inference must complete within 200ms of cycle completion signal; ClickHouse on self-managed EC2 due to no ClickHouse Cloud in VPC; no public internet egress from plant VPC; AWS Batch GPU for retraining only
constraints.rationale: 200ms SLA driven by operator reaction time window; ClickHouse cloud limitation; security egress restriction

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for service metrics; custom defect_predictions ClickHouse table queryable for IATF audit; model performance metrics (precision, recall, F1) tracked per model_version
observability_baseline.rationale: Prometheus standard for containerized services; ClickHouse for prediction audit trail

execution_preference.value: Iterative ML development cycles; model retraining gated on validation F1 improvement before canary promotion
execution_preference.rationale: ML systems require validation-gated promotion to avoid regressing inference quality

llm_integration.value: Local LSTM model (PyTorch) trained on cycle-level sensor aggregates for defect probability regression; no external LLM APIs; model weights versioned in S3 model registry
llm_integration.rationale: LSTM chosen for temporal dependency modeling of injection cycle pressure and temperature sequences; local inference required for VPC isolation

compliance_context.value: IATF 16949 traceability for all defect predictions and model version promotions linked to part production records
compliance_context.rationale: Automotive supply chain quality standard requires full traceability from part to prediction to model version

project.goal: Real-time defect probability prediction for injection molding cycles with process parameter adjustment recommendations
project.target_users: Press operators and data scientists

backend.framework: FastAPI for ingestion and inference APIs; PyTorch for LSTM model

security.auth: AWS Cognito with MFA for admin
security.vpc: Private subnets only, no public egress

data.db: ClickHouse on EC2 for sensor telemetry and features
data.cache: Redis 7 for cycle state
data.archive: S3 for telemetry archive and model registry

reasoner_reviews.product: The defect prediction system addresses a high-value scrap reduction use case. Round 1 established the ingestion pipeline and LSTM model architecture. The critical gap is that the feature pipeline is not specified - what cycle-level aggregates are computed from raw sensor channels (temperature, pressure, screw position), what window sizes are used, and how features are normalized before LSTM input. Without this, the inference pipeline cannot be implemented.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The ECS Fargate + ClickHouse architecture is appropriate for the inference latency requirement. The 200ms SLA requires that feature computation completes within ~50ms, leaving ~150ms for LSTM inference. Redis cycle state caching for in-progress cycles is necessary to avoid ClickHouse query latency on the hot path. The round 1 plan described the feature pipeline vaguely as 'cycle-level aggregates' without specifying which aggregates.
reasoner_reviews.security: Cognito MFA for admin roles and VPC isolation are correct. IATF 16949 requires that every defect_prediction record is linked to the model_version that produced it. The defect_predictions table in round 1 lacks model_version_id foreign key, making audit trail incomplete.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 200ms inference SLA is achievable if the LSTM sequence length is kept short (20-50 timesteps) and the model is pre-loaded at container startup. AWS Batch GPU retraining constraint acknowledged. ClickHouse on EC2 within VPC avoids egress. The round 1 data model does not specify the ClickHouse partitioning strategy for sensor_telemetry, which is critical for query performance at terabyte scale.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three modules: ingestion (POST /cycles/telemetry for sensor data intake), feature_pipeline (cycle-level aggregate computation on cycle completion signal), inference (LSTM model scoring per completed cycle).
backend.api_patterns: POST /cycles/telemetry {machine_id, cycle_id, channel, readings[{t, value}]}. POST /cycles/{cycle_id}/score (triggers feature computation and inference). GET /predictions/{cycle_id} (retrieve defect probability and recommendations). GET /models/active (current model version metadata).
backend.failure_handling: Inference timeout at 180ms with fallback to last-cycle moving average if LSTM inference exceeds threshold. Redis cycle state cleared on cycle completion to prevent stale data. ECS Fargate task health check on /healthz with 30s interval.
backend.scaling_notes: ECS Fargate auto-scaling on CPU 70% target. Each Fargate task pre-loads LSTM model weights at startup. Concurrent cycles from multiple machines handled by async FastAPI handlers with asyncio task pool.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with press operator and data_scientist groups. MFA enforced via Cognito user pool MFA settings for data_scientist group. Operator group: read-only GET /predictions. Data scientist group: full access including model promotion endpoints.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: defect_predictions table includes model_version_id FK for IATF traceability. model_version_promotions table records who promoted each model version, when, and what validation F1 it achieved. Append-only audit records in ClickHouse for immutability.
security.security_risks: Risk: LSTM model weights exfiltration via S3 model registry. Mitigation: S3 bucket policy restricts GetObject to ECS task IAM role; bucket versioning enabled; access logs to CloudTrail.

data.entities: press_machines (id, plant, press_tonnage, material_type, mold_id), molding_cycles (id, machine_id, cycle_start, cycle_end, shot_weight_g, cycle_time_ms), sensor_channels (id, machine_id, channel_type ENUM temperature/pressure/screw_position, unit, sample_rate_hz), sensor_telemetry (machine_id, cycle_id, channel_type, timestamp_us, value FLOAT, partitioned by machine_id and cycle_date), process_parameters (id, machine_id, cycle_id, barrel_temp_zone1_c, barrel_temp_zone2_c, injection_pressure_bar, hold_pressure_bar, screw_speed_rpm, cooling_time_s), defect_predictions (id, cycle_id, machine_id, model_version_id FK, defect_probability FLOAT, predicted_at, recommendation_json), part_classifications (id, cycle_id, actual_defective BOOLEAN, classified_at, classified_by), model_versions (id, version_tag, weights_s3_key, training_dataset_hash, val_f1, val_precision, val_recall, promoted_at, promoted_by, status ENUM challenger/canary/champion).
data.storage_design: ClickHouse sensor_telemetry: MergeTree partitioned by toYYYYMMDD(timestamp_us) ORDER BY (machine_id, cycle_id, channel_type, timestamp_us). TTL 90 days for hot data with S3 tiering. defect_predictions and model_versions in ClickHouse for append-only audit trail. Redis: cycle_state hashes keyed by cycle_id with TTL 60s.
data.data_risks: Risk: ClickHouse partition per day creates thousands of parts at terabyte scale. Mitigation: partition by toYYYYMM for monthly partitions; rely on machine_id ORDER BY for query filtering.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: ingestion-service task (2 vCPU, 4 GB), inference-service task (4 vCPU, 8 GB, pre-loaded LSTM weights). AWS Batch compute environment: GPU instance (g4dn.xlarge) for weekly LSTM retraining. ClickHouse on EC2 r6i.4xlarge within VPC. Redis on ElastiCache.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, pytest (feature pipeline unit tests, inference latency tests), docker build, push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Model promotion: data scientist triggers /models/promote endpoint after validation; canary at 10% traffic for 24 hours before full promotion.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics: inference_latency_ms histogram (p50/p95/p99), feature_pipeline_duration_ms, defect_predictions_total by machine, model_version gauge. Grafana dashboards: per-machine defect rate trend, model F1 over versions, inference latency heatmap by hour.

ISS-001.title: Feature pipeline aggregate specification missing
ISS-001.detail: The round 1 plan describes cycle-level feature computation as computing 'aggregates' but does not specify which aggregates are computed per sensor channel (e.g., mean, max, std dev of injection pressure over the fill phase window; peak temperature per zone), what time windows are used, or how features are normalized before LSTM input. Without this, the feature pipeline cannot be implemented.

history.note: Feature pipeline described as aggregate computation without specifying which aggregates

ISS-002.title: defect_predictions missing model_version_id for IATF traceability
ISS-002.detail: The IATF 16949 compliance context requires that every defect prediction is traceable to the model version that produced it. The round 1 defect_predictions table does not include model_version_id, making automotive audit trail incomplete.

previous_plan.title: Injection Molding Defect Prediction AI System - Round 1 Plan
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python FastAPI AI system on ECS Fargate ingesting injection molding sensor telemetry, computing cycle-level features, and scoring LSTM defect probability within 200ms.

previous_plan.data_model: press_machines, molding_cycles, sensor_telemetry (ClickHouse), process_parameters, defect_predictions (without model_version_id), part_classifications, model_versions.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must close both high-severity issues. ISS-001: I will specify the full feature vector with named aggregates per sensor channel (injection pressure mean/max/std over fill phase, barrel temperature means, screw position at peak pressure) and document z-score normalization using training set statistics. ISS-002: I will add model_version_id to defect_predictions and describe how the champion model version is looked up at inference time from Redis cache. Plan quality target is weak - sections will address the critical issues but some areas remain thinner than a strong plan.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined the cycle-level feature vector: for each molding cycle, the feature pipeline computes 8 aggregates: injection_pressure_fill_mean_bar (mean pressure over 0-2s fill phase), injection_pressure_fill_max_bar (peak injection pressure), injection_pressure_fill_std (standard deviation indicating instability), barrel_zone1_temp_mean_c, barrel_zone2_temp_mean_c, screw_position_at_peak_pressure_mm (screw position when injection pressure peaks), cooling_time_deviation_s (actual vs setpoint cooling time), shot_weight_delta_g (deviation from nominal shot weight). Features normalized per channel using z-score with mean/std from training set stored in feature_normalization_params ClickHouse table. LSTM input sequence is last 20 cycles of this 8-dimensional feature vector.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Feature pipeline is implementable; LSTM input dimensionality and normalization are fully specified

fix_report.action_taken: Added model_version_id UUID to defect_predictions table as NOT NULL FK to model_versions. At inference time, FastAPI inference service reads champion model_version_id from Redis key 'champion_model_version_id' (updated on every model promotion). Defect prediction record written with model_version_id populated. model_version_promotions audit table records promoter_user_id, promoted_at, from_version_id, to_version_id, validation_f1 for IATF traceability.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every defect prediction traceable to exact model version; IATF 16949 audit chain complete from part to prediction to model

target_output.title: Injection Molding Defect Prediction AI System
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 FastAPI AI system on AWS ECS Fargate that ingests high-frequency sensor telemetry (temperature, pressure, screw position) from injection molding presses, computes an 8-aggregate cycle-level feature vector, and scores a PyTorch LSTM model within 200ms of cycle completion to emit a defect probability score to press operator HMIs. Weekly AWS Batch GPU retraining promotes challenger models via canary deployment when validation F1 improves. All predictions are IATF 16949-traceable to the model version that produced them.
target_output.architecture_overview: ECS Fargate cluster runs two task definitions: ingestion-service (receives sensor telemetry via POST and writes to ClickHouse) and inference-service (triggered on cycle completion signal, computes 8 aggregate features from ClickHouse, looks up champion LSTM model version from Redis, runs PyTorch inference, writes defect_prediction with model_version_id). ClickHouse on EC2 stores terabyte-scale sensor telemetry partitioned by month per machine. Redis holds in-progress cycle state (keyed by cycle_id, TTL 60s) and champion_model_version_id for low-latency lookup at inference time. AWS Batch with g4dn.xlarge GPU instance runs weekly LSTM retraining job pulling labeled part_classifications from ClickHouse.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI with asyncio. ML: PyTorch 2.3 LSTM, numpy for feature aggregation. Time-series: ClickHouse 24.3 on EC2 r6i.4xlarge (MergeTree partitioned by month). Cache: Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Inference hosting: ECS Fargate (4 vCPU, 8 GB per inference task). Retraining: AWS Batch g4dn.xlarge. Model registry: S3 with versioned weights objects. CI: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Sensor Ingestion: POST /cycles/telemetry receives temperature, pressure, and screw position readings per cycle_id; stored to ClickHouse sensor_telemetry partitioned by month. Feature Pipeline: on cycle completion signal, computes 8 aggregates per cycle (injection_pressure_fill_mean_bar, injection_pressure_fill_max_bar, injection_pressure_fill_std, barrel_zone1_temp_mean_c, barrel_zone2_temp_mean_c, screw_position_at_peak_pressure_mm, cooling_time_deviation_s, shot_weight_delta_g); z-score normalized per channel. LSTM Inference: champion model scores 20-cycle sequence; defect_probability emitted within 200ms; recommendation_json generated from process_parameters deviation analysis. HMI Push: MQTT message to machine HMI with defect_probability and parameter adjustment hints. Weekly Retraining: AWS Batch job pulls new labeled part_classifications, trains challenger LSTM, validates against holdout set, promotes to canary if F1 improves. Analytics: Grafana dashboards for defect rate by machine, model F1 over versions.
target_output.system_components: ingestion-service ECS task (sensor telemetry write to ClickHouse). inference-service ECS task (feature computation, LSTM inference, defect_prediction write, MQTT publish). ClickHouse EC2 cluster (sensor_telemetry, cycle_features, defect_predictions, model_versions). ElastiCache Redis (cycle_state, champion_model_version_id). AWS Batch GPU environment (LSTM retraining job). S3 model registry (versioned PyTorch weights). Grafana + Prometheus (inference metrics dashboards).
target_output.workflows: Real-Time Inference Cycle: press controller signals cycle completion via POST /cycles/{cycle_id}/score; inference-service fetches sensor_telemetry for cycle_id from ClickHouse (query on machine_id + cycle_id index, <20ms); computes 8 feature aggregates using numpy (fill phase window 0-2000ms for pressure channels, full cycle for temperature); z-score normalization applied per channel using feature_normalization_params lookup from ClickHouse; retrieves last 20 completed cycles for LSTM input sequence from Redis cycle_features cache; runs PyTorch LSTM forward pass on CPU (Fargate; GPU inference not warranted at single-cycle latency); writes defect_prediction record with model_version_id from Redis champion key; publishes MQTT message to machine/{machine_id}/defect_score topic within 200ms SLA. Weekly Retraining: AWS Batch job triggered by EventBridge schedule; pulls inspection_results and part_classifications for the past 7 days from ClickHouse; preprocesses using same feature pipeline; trains LSTM for 50 epochs on GPU; evaluates on holdout set; if val_f1 > champion_val_f1 + 0.02, uploads weights to S3 and inserts model_versions record with status=canary; canary handles 10% of inference traffic for 24 hours; data scientist reviews Grafana canary metrics and triggers /models/promote to update champion_model_version_id in Redis.
target_output.data_model: press_machines (id UUID PK, plant_code TEXT, press_tonnage_t INTEGER, material_type TEXT, mold_id TEXT, installed_at DATETIME). molding_cycles (id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK, cycle_start DateTime64(3), cycle_end DateTime64(3), shot_weight_g FLOAT, cycle_time_ms INTEGER) ENGINE=MergeTree PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(cycle_start). sensor_telemetry (machine_id UUID, cycle_id UUID, channel_type ENUM temperature/pressure/screw_position, timestamp_us UInt64, value Float32) ENGINE=MergeTree PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(toDateTime(timestamp_us/1000000)) ORDER BY (machine_id, cycle_id, channel_type, timestamp_us) TTL toDateTime(timestamp_us/1000000) + INTERVAL 90 DAY. cycle_features (cycle_id UUID PK, machine_id UUID, injection_pressure_fill_mean_bar Float32, injection_pressure_fill_max_bar Float32, injection_pressure_fill_std Float32, barrel_zone1_temp_mean_c Float32, barrel_zone2_temp_mean_c Float32, screw_position_at_peak_pressure_mm Float32, cooling_time_deviation_s Float32, shot_weight_delta_g Float32, computed_at DateTime). defect_predictions (id UUID PK, cycle_id UUID FK, machine_id UUID, model_version_id UUID FK NOT NULL, defect_probability Float32, predicted_at DateTime, recommendation_json TEXT). part_classifications (id UUID PK, cycle_id UUID FK, actual_defective Boolean, classified_at DateTime, classified_by TEXT). model_versions (id UUID PK, version_tag TEXT, weights_s3_key TEXT, training_dataset_hash TEXT, val_f1 Float32, val_precision Float32, val_recall Float32, promoted_at DateTime, promoted_by TEXT, status ENUM challenger/canary/champion). feature_normalization_params (channel_name TEXT PK, mean Float64, std Float64, updated_at DateTime). model_version_promotions (id UUID PK, from_version_id UUID, to_version_id UUID, promoter_user_id TEXT, promoted_at DateTime, validation_f1 Float32).
target_output.api_design: POST /cycles/telemetry {machine_id, cycle_id, channel_type, readings:[{timestamp_us, value}]} -> {accepted_count}. POST /cycles/{cycle_id}/score -> {prediction_id, defect_probability, recommendation_json, model_version_id, latency_ms}. GET /predictions/{cycle_id} -> {defect_probability, model_version_id, predicted_at}. GET /models/active -> {champion_version_id, val_f1, promoted_at}. POST /models/promote {version_id} -> {status} (data_scientist role). GET /healthz -> {status, model_loaded}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pool with press_operator group (GET /predictions only) and data_scientist group (full access including /models/promote). MFA via Cognito user pool TOTP requirement for data_scientist group. TLS 1.3 on all endpoints. VPC with private subnets only; no internet egress. S3 model registry: bucket policy restricts GetObject to ECS task IAM role; S3 access logs to CloudTrail. defect_predictions table includes model_version_id NOT NULL ensuring every prediction is traceable. model_version_promotions table append-only for IATF 16949 audit trail of model governance. IATF 16949 query: SELECT dp.id, dp.defect_probability, dp.predicted_at, mv.version_tag, mv.val_f1 FROM defect_predictions dp JOIN model_versions mv ON dp.model_version_id = mv.id WHERE dp.cycle_id = {cycle_id}.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster: ingestion-service (2 vCPU 4 GB, auto-scaling 2-8 tasks on SQS queue depth), inference-service (4 vCPU 8 GB, auto-scaling 2-10 tasks on CPU 70%, pre-loads LSTM weights at container startup via lifespan hook). ClickHouse on EC2 r6i.4xlarge in private subnet with EBS gp3 2 TB volume. ElastiCache Redis r6g.large. S3 bucket for model weights with versioning and lifecycle to Glacier after 2 years. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, pytest (feature aggregation unit tests, inference latency integration tests against mock ClickHouse), docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. AWS Batch compute environment: on-demand g4dn.xlarge, triggered by EventBridge weekly schedule.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics: inference_latency_ms histogram (p50/p95/p99 target p95 < 180ms), feature_pipeline_duration_ms, defect_predictions_total by machine_id, sla_breach_total (when latency > 200ms). Grafana dashboards: per-machine defect_probability 24h rolling average, model F1 over version history, inference latency heatmap, champion vs canary prediction score distribution. Alert: p95 inference latency > 180ms triggers PagerDuty P2; canary defect_rate deviation > 5% from champion triggers automatic canary rollback.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate inference tasks at baseline 2 tasks ~$150/month. ClickHouse EC2 r6i.4xlarge ~$700/month. ElastiCache r6g.large ~$150/month. AWS Batch g4dn.xlarge on-demand weekly retraining ~$20/month (2h job). Total ~$1,100/month for two-machine pilot. Fleet scale (50 machines): ClickHouse scales vertically to r6i.16xlarge; inference tasks scale to 20; estimated ~$3,500/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (completed): sensor telemetry ingestion, ClickHouse schema, basic LSTM model with placeholder features, ECS Fargate deployment, cycle completion trigger. Phase 2 (current round): Feature vector specification (8 named aggregates, z-score normalization), model_version_id on defect_predictions, IATF traceability audit tables, canary deployment workflow, Grafana dashboards. Phase 3 (roadmap): Fleet-wide deployment to 50 machines, ERP scrap cost integration, multi-machine model federation research.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff + black, pytest with 90% coverage for feature_pipeline and inference modules. Feature aggregation logic is pure Python/numpy with unit tests per channel type. Model validation script run in GitHub Actions on every PR touching LSTM training code. ClickHouse schema migrations via numbered SQL scripts in repo applied manually; no ORM migration framework for ClickHouse. LSTM sequence length and feature vector changes are breaking changes requiring model retraining - documented in ADR.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: LSTM inference on CPU in Fargate may not meet 200ms SLA for longer sequence lengths. Mitigation: sequence length capped at 20 cycles; inference benchmarked in CI with 95th percentile assertion < 150ms. Risk: ClickHouse EC2 single instance is SPOF. Mitigation: daily EBS snapshots; read replica for analytics queries. Tradeoff: LSTM over transformer for temporal dependency - LSTM has lower inference latency at the cost of longer-range dependency capture; acceptable for 20-cycle window. Tradeoff: cycle_features materialization in ClickHouse vs compute-on-demand - materialized features add storage overhead but reduce p95 inference latency by ~40ms.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed z-score normalization over min-max scaling to handle outlier injection pressure spikes that would saturate min-max range. Confirmed 20-cycle LSTM sequence length as optimal balance of historical context and inference latency. Confirmed cycle_features materialization to meet 200ms SLA. model_version_id FK confirmed as NOT NULL to enforce IATF traceability at database level.

metadata.notes: Injection molding defect prediction AI system, round 2 addressing feature pipeline spec and IATF traceability gap.

project_goal.value: OT/IT convergence infrastructure project deploying a secure industrial DMZ with MQTT brokers and Kafka clusters to bridge PLC sensor data from the plant floor to cloud analytics in real time without exposing control systems to the enterprise network
project_goal.rationale: Core infrastructure objective to enable cloud analytics on PLC data while maintaining OT network isolation

target_users.value: OT engineers managing PLC assets, IT infrastructure engineers operating the DMZ and cloud connectivity, security team owning firewall and certificate policies
target_users.rationale: Three distinct stakeholder groups with different access and operational responsibilities

project_class.rationale: Core deliverable is network topology, Kubernetes manifests, Terraform modules, and certificate infrastructure - not an application

capabilities.rationale: Go edge gateway services, InfluxDB time-series storage, real-time MQTT-to-Kafka bridging, GitLab CI IaC, PLC integration

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-zone network segmentation, mTLS certificate management, on-premises Kubernetes, AWS Transit Gateway integration, IEC 62443 compliance

risk_level.rationale: Misconfigured firewall rules or certificate failures could expose PLC control systems to enterprise network or internet, risking plant safety

data_sensitivity.rationale: PLC telemetry reveals production process parameters and machine states that are trade-secret; DMZ topology is security-sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: OT zone, DMZ, and IT zone are all internal; AWS cloud analytics access restricted to corporate VPN via Transit Gateway

access_model.value: Keycloak with OT engineer, IT engineer, and security admin roles; mTLS mutual certificate authentication for all service-to-service communication across zone boundaries
access_model.rationale: IEC 62443 requires mutual authentication for all communications crossing security zone boundaries; Keycloak provides OIDC for human operator access

feature_scope.value: MQTT broker cluster in OT zone (EMQX), edge gateway services (Go) with mTLS authentication and protocol translation, DMZ Kafka cluster for IT zone delivery, InfluxDB for hot time-series data, S3 Glacier archive, firewall rules as Terraform code, certificate authority hierarchy (root CA, OT intermediate CA, IT intermediate CA), GitLab CI pipeline for topology change validation with security team approval gate
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for production OT/IT convergence infrastructure

mvp_scope.value: EMQX MQTT broker in OT zone for 10 PLCs, single edge gateway with mTLS to DMZ Kafka, InfluxDB ingestion, firewall rules Terraform module, basic GitLab CI topology validation pipeline
mvp_scope.rationale: Ten-PLC pilot validates the OT-to-DMZ data flow and certificate lifecycle before full plant deployment

security_baseline.value: IEC 62443 zone and conduit model enforced via firewall rules; mTLS with mutual certificate authentication on all cross-zone channels; certificate rotation automated via cert-manager; no unencrypted traffic allowed in DMZ; all topology changes require security team approval in GitLab CI before apply
security_baseline.rationale: IEC 62443 and NIST CSF critical infrastructure requirements

frontend_stack.value: None - infrastructure project; no web UI; operational visibility via Grafana dashboards and GitLab CI pipeline status
frontend_stack.rationale: Infrastructure project; no application frontend required

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 custom edge gateway services for MQTT ingestion, protocol translation, and Kafka publishing
backend_stack.rationale: Go delivers low-latency, low-memory-footprint services appropriate for edge gateway hardware constraints; no framework overhead

data_platform.value: InfluxDB 2.7 for hot time-series PLC telemetry (30-day retention); S3 via AWS Transit Gateway for cold archive (Parquet, indefinite retention)
data_platform.rationale: InfluxDB time-series native for PLC sensor data with flux query support; S3 for cost-effective long-term archive

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes on-premises for OT zone and DMZ zone components; AWS Transit Gateway for cloud connectivity; AWS MSK for cloud Kafka; InfluxDB on-premises in IT zone
hosting_target.rationale: OT zone components must be on-premises for network isolation; AWS MSK for cloud analytics consumers

privacy_retention_policy.value: PLC telemetry retained 30 days hot in InfluxDB; archived indefinitely to S3 in Parquet format for process analytics; no personal data in telemetry stream
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Process telemetry is not personal data; retention driven by analytics and root cause analysis requirements

future_scope.value: Expansion to full plant (200 PLCs), cloud analytics dashboards via AWS MSK consumers, OT asset management portal, integration with CMMS for predictive maintenance
future_scope.rationale: Pilot success gates plant-wide OT/IT convergence program

constraints.value: OT zone Kubernetes nodes have no internet access; all container images must be pulled from internal Harbor registry; AWS connectivity via Transit Gateway only; certificate chain must be offline-signable for OT zone (no ACME HTTP challenge); on-premises Kubernetes nodes are bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04
constraints.rationale: Air-gapped OT zone requires offline cert management and internal image registry

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for message throughput, edge gateway latency, Kafka consumer lag, and mTLS certificate expiry monitoring; GitLab CI pipeline status as deployment audit trail
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard infrastructure monitoring stack; certificate expiry alerting is safety-critical for OT connectivity

execution_preference.value: Infrastructure-as-code first; all changes via GitLab CI with automated validation and security team approval; no manual kubectl or Terraform applies in production
execution_preference.rationale: IaC-first approach is mandatory for IEC 62443 change control traceability

compliance_context.value: IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standard (zone and conduit model, security levels SL-2 for DMZ, SL-3 for OT zone), NIST CSF Protect and Detect functions for critical infrastructure
compliance_context.rationale: Industrial cybersecurity standard for OT/IT convergence in critical manufacturing infrastructure

project.goal: Secure OT/IT DMZ bridging PLC MQTT telemetry to cloud analytics via Kafka without exposing OT control systems
project.target_users: OT engineers, IT engineers, security team

backend.framework: Custom edge gateway services with no framework
backend.protocols: MQTT 5.0 inbound from PLCs, Kafka outbound to IT zone

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC for human access; mTLS for service-to-service
security.zones: IEC 62443 zone and conduit model; firewall rules as Terraform
security.certificates: Internal CA hierarchy; cert-manager for automated rotation

data.streaming: AWS MSK for cloud analytics consumers

devops.hosting: Bare-metal Kubernetes on-premises + AWS Transit Gateway

constraints.network: OT zone air-gapped; no internet access

reasoner_reviews.product: The OT/IT convergence infrastructure scope is well-defined for the 10-PLC pilot. The IEC 62443 zone and conduit model maps cleanly to EMQX in OT zone, Go edge gateway in DMZ, and Kafka/InfluxDB in IT zone. The key design decision is how the edge gateway authenticates to both OT MQTT and IT Kafka using mTLS with separate intermediate CAs per zone.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The three-zone architecture (OT zone with PLCs and EMQX, DMZ with edge gateways and Kafka bridge, IT zone with InfluxDB and AWS Transit Gateway) is the correct IEC 62443 segmentation pattern. Go edge gateways are the right choice for low-latency protocol translation. The certificate authority hierarchy needs to be explicitly designed: root CA (offline), OT intermediate CA (signs EMQX broker and PLC client certs), DMZ intermediate CA (signs edge gateway certs), IT intermediate CA (signs Kafka broker and consumer certs). Cross-zone conduits use mTLS with client certs from the source zone's intermediate CA verified by the destination zone's trust anchor.
reasoner_reviews.security: IEC 62443 SL-2 for DMZ and SL-3 for OT zone require mutual authentication on all conduits, no unnecessary services in any zone, and audit logging for all topology changes. The GitLab CI approval gate for firewall rule changes is essential. cert-manager cannot use ACME HTTP challenge in air-gapped OT zone - must use offline-signed certificates with automated rotation via cert-manager Vault issuer or manual renewal workflow. Certificate expiry monitoring in Prometheus is safety-critical.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Bare-metal Kubernetes on-premises means no managed load balancers. MetalLB for on-premises LoadBalancer service type. Harbor internal registry for all container images including EMQX, Go edge gateway, InfluxDB. AWS Transit Gateway VPN attachment for cloud connectivity. The transit gateway routing tables must be explicitly specified to prevent OT zone subnets from being routable to internet.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The EMQX broker configuration for handling PLC MQTT connections with mTLS needs specificity: listener configuration for TLS port 8883, client certificate validation against the OT intermediate CA, and MQTT topic-based access control (ACL) per PLC asset_id. The Kafka topic naming convention for PLC data and the edge gateway routing logic from MQTT topic to Kafka topic should be specified.

backend.service_design: Go edge gateway binary with three concurrently running goroutines: MQTT subscriber (connects to EMQX in OT zone via mTLS on port 8883), message transformer (translates MQTT payload to Avro-encoded Kafka message with PLC metadata enrichment), Kafka producer (publishes to DMZ Kafka topic with mTLS client authentication).
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP API - pure message pipeline. MQTT topic subscription pattern: plc/{asset_id}/telemetry/#. Kafka topic naming: ot.plc.{asset_id}.telemetry. Avro schema registry in DMZ for message schema versioning.
backend.failure_handling: MQTT reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s) on broker disconnect. Kafka producer with idempotent producer config and retries=3. Local disk buffer (badger KV store) for telemetry messages during Kafka unavailability up to 1 GB; replay on reconnect. Dead letter topic ot.plc.dlq for malformed payloads.
backend.scaling_notes: Single edge gateway handles 10 PLCs at 100 Hz each = 1000 msg/s; well within single Go process capacity. Scale to 200 PLCs requires 2-3 edge gateway replicas with Kubernetes Deployment and anti-affinity for node spread.

security.auth_design: Three-tier CA hierarchy: root CA (offline HSM), OT intermediate CA (signs EMQX broker cert and PLC client certs, valid 2 years), DMZ intermediate CA (signs edge gateway certs, valid 1 year), IT intermediate CA (signs Kafka broker and InfluxDB certs, valid 1 year). cert-manager installed in DMZ and IT Kubernetes clusters with Vault issuer for automated rotation. OT zone certificates renewed manually via offline CSR workflow (air-gapped constraint).
security.authorization_model: EMQX ACL: PLCs can only publish to plc/{their_asset_id}/telemetry/# (topic-based ACL enforced by EMQX against CN in client cert matching asset_id). Edge gateway Kafka producer has produce-only permission on ot.plc.* topics. IT zone Kafka consumers have consume-only permission on ot.plc.* and read access to InfluxDB.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All GitLab CI Terraform apply runs logged with user, commit SHA, changed resources, and approval chain. EMQX audit log for all client connect/disconnect events with certificate CN. Kubernetes audit log enabled on all clusters. Firewall rule change events logged to central syslog.
security.security_risks: Risk: cert-manager cannot auto-renew OT zone certificates (air-gapped). Mitigation: Prometheus alert at 30-day cert expiry; manual offline renewal procedure documented in runbook. Risk: Transit Gateway route table misconfiguration allows OT subnet routing to internet. Mitigation: Terraform explicit deny route for 0.0.0.0/0 from OT subnets; automated connectivity smoke test in CI verifying no internet path from OT node.

data.entities: plc_assets (id UUID, asset_code TEXT UNIQUE, description TEXT, ot_zone_subnet TEXT, mqtt_topic_prefix TEXT, installed_at DATETIME). mqtt_topics (id UUID, plc_asset_id FK, topic_pattern TEXT, payload_schema_version TEXT, sample_rate_hz INTEGER). kafka_topics (topic_name TEXT PK, source_plc_pattern TEXT, partition_count INTEGER, replication_factor INTEGER, retention_ms BIGINT). data_flows (id UUID, source_zone ENUM ot/dmz/it/cloud, destination_zone ENUM ot/dmz/it/cloud, protocol TEXT, authentication_method TEXT, firewall_rule_id TEXT). firewall_rules (id TEXT PK, source_cidr TEXT, destination_cidr TEXT, port INTEGER, protocol TEXT, action ENUM allow/deny, zone_conduit TEXT, last_changed_at DATETIME). certificate_authorities (id UUID, name TEXT, zone ENUM ot/dmz/it, issuer_ca_id UUID FK, cert_pem TEXT, valid_from DATETIME, valid_until DATETIME). edge_gateways (id UUID, hostname TEXT, zone ENUM dmz, kubernetes_node TEXT, current_cert_cn TEXT, cert_expiry DATETIME).
data.storage_design: InfluxDB 2.7 on-premises in IT zone: bucket 'plc-telemetry' with 30-day retention. Tag keys: asset_id, channel_type. Field keys: value (float). S3 bucket via Transit Gateway: Parquet files partitioned by year/month/asset_id written by nightly InfluxDB-to-S3 export job. Kafka DMZ cluster: 3 brokers, 12 partitions for ot.plc.* topics, 7-day retention.
data.retention_and_deletion: InfluxDB 30-day TTL automatic. S3 Parquet: Intelligent-Tiering, no deletion (process telemetry archive). Kafka: 7-day retention window for IT consumers with replay capability.
data.data_risks: Risk: InfluxDB single-node SPOF. Mitigation: daily backup to S3; InfluxDB OSS replication to standby node. Risk: Kafka DMZ broker loss causes edge gateway backpressure. Mitigation: local badger buffer provides 1 GB offline buffering; Kafka 3-broker replication factor 3.

devops.deployment_topology: Three Kubernetes clusters: ot-cluster (bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04, air-gapped, runs EMQX and PLC connectivity pods), dmz-cluster (bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04, DMZ network, runs edge gateway pods and Kafka), it-cluster (bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04, IT network, runs InfluxDB and Prometheus/Grafana). AWS side: Transit Gateway with VPN attachment to on-premises IT network; MSK for cloud analytics consumers. All images pulled from internal Harbor registry.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline stages: validate (terraform validate, tflint, helm lint), diff (terraform plan output committed as artifact), security-approval (manual gate requiring security team member approval), apply (terraform apply in target environment), smoke-test (connectivity verification: MQTT publish from OT test node -> Kafka consumer read in IT zone, verifying end-to-end data flow). Firewall rule changes require explicit security_team GitLab group approval. cert-manager certificate requests automatically applied via Helm chart updates in CI.
devops.rollback_strategy: Terraform state stored in GitLab-managed Terraform state backend. Rollback by reverting Git commit and re-running CI pipeline to terraform apply previous state. Firewall rollback requires security team approval. EMQX configuration rollback via Helm chart version pin.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: Terraform state lock contention if multiple engineers run CI simultaneously. Mitigation: GitLab state backend with locking enabled. Risk: cert-manager Vault issuer unavailable in DMZ causes certificate renewal failure. Mitigation: Prometheus alert at 30-day cert expiry; manual renewal procedure as fallback.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 1 infrastructure project for OT/IT DMZ convergence. Key architectural decisions: three-zone Kubernetes topology (OT, DMZ, IT) with IEC 62443 zone and conduit model; three-tier CA hierarchy (root offline, OT intermediate, DMZ intermediate, IT intermediate) with cert-manager for DMZ and IT rotation and manual offline renewal for air-gapped OT zone; Go edge gateway as the cross-zone protocol bridge with mTLS on both EMQX and Kafka sides; Terraform IaC with GitLab CI security approval gate for all firewall changes. The plan quality target is strong with all sections domain-specific to industrial OT/IT infrastructure.
target_output.title: OT/IT Convergence Industrial DMZ Infrastructure
target_output.executive_summary: An infrastructure project deploying a three-zone IEC 62443-compliant industrial DMZ that bridges PLC sensor telemetry from an air-gapped OT zone through a DMZ Kafka cluster to InfluxDB in the IT zone and AWS analytics via Transit Gateway. EMQX MQTT brokers in the OT zone receive PLC telemetry; Go edge gateway services in the DMZ authenticate to both zones via mTLS with zone-specific intermediate CA certificates, translate MQTT payloads to Avro Kafka messages, and publish to ot.plc.* Kafka topics. All network topology and firewall rules are managed as Terraform IaC with GitLab CI validation and mandatory security team approval before apply.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three security zones following IEC 62443 zone and conduit model. OT Zone (SL-3): PLCs publish MQTT 5.0 telemetry to EMQX broker cluster on port 8883 with mTLS; PLC client certificates signed by OT intermediate CA. DMZ Zone (SL-2): Go edge gateway pods on bare-metal Kubernetes subscribe to EMQX and produce to local Kafka cluster using mTLS with DMZ intermediate CA; acts as the cross-zone conduit with strict ACL enforcement. IT Zone: DMZ Kafka brokers replicate to IT Kafka; InfluxDB 2.7 consumes telemetry topics; AWS Transit Gateway VPN attachment forwards to MSK for cloud analytics. Certificate authority hierarchy: offline root CA issues OT, DMZ, and IT intermediate CAs; cert-manager automates certificate rotation in DMZ and IT Kubernetes clusters; OT zone certificates renewed via offline CSR workflow due to air-gap constraint. All firewall rules defined in Terraform with conduit-specific security group policies enforcing zone boundaries.
target_output.technology_stack: Edge gateway: Go 1.22 custom binary (Paho MQTT client, Confluent Kafka client, badger KV for local buffer). MQTT broker: EMQX 5.3 cluster (3 nodes) in OT zone. Message bus: Kafka 3.7 (3-broker cluster in DMZ, AWS MSK in cloud). Time-series: InfluxDB 2.7 on-premises in IT zone. Schema registry: Confluent Schema Registry in DMZ for Avro schemas. Certificate management: cert-manager 1.14 with Vault PKI issuer in DMZ and IT zones. IaC: Terraform 1.7, Helm 3.14, Kustomize. Container registry: Harbor 2.10 (internal). CI/CD: GitLab CI 17.x. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus with EMQX exporter, Kafka JMX exporter, cert-manager expiry metrics. Network: MetalLB for on-premises LoadBalancer, AWS Transit Gateway for cloud connectivity.
target_output.functional_feature_map: OT Zone MQTT Ingestion: EMQX 5-node cluster handles up to 10,000 concurrent PLC connections; TLS port 8883 with mTLS enforced; ACL rules restrict each PLC to publish only to plc/{asset_id}/telemetry/#. Edge Gateway Protocol Bridge: Go binary subscribes to plc/+/telemetry/# on EMQX; enriches message with asset metadata from plc_assets lookup; encodes to Avro using schema registry; publishes to ot.plc.{asset_id}.telemetry Kafka topic. Local Buffer: badger KV disk buffer absorbs up to 1 GB of telemetry during Kafka downtime with ordered replay. InfluxDB Ingestion: Kafka Connect InfluxDB sink connector writes tag-keyed measurements per channel_type. S3 Archive: nightly InfluxDB-to-S3 export job writes Parquet files partitioned by year/month/asset_id. Topology Change Pipeline: GitLab CI runs terraform plan on PR, requires security team approval, applies, then runs connectivity smoke test. Certificate Lifecycle: cert-manager auto-renews DMZ and IT certs at 2/3 of validity; Prometheus alerts at 30-day and 7-day expiry for OT certs requiring manual renewal.
target_output.system_components: EMQX cluster (3 nodes in OT zone, ports 8883 mTLS, 18083 dashboard). Edge gateway Deployment (DMZ Kubernetes, 2 replicas, anti-affinity for node spread). Kafka cluster (3 brokers in DMZ, topics ot.plc.* with 12 partitions). Confluent Schema Registry (DMZ). InfluxDB 2.7 node (IT zone, plc-telemetry bucket). InfluxDB Kafka Connect sink (IT zone). AWS MSK cluster (cloud, mirrors ot.plc.* from DMZ via MirrorMaker 2). AWS Transit Gateway (VPN attachment to on-premises IT network). Harbor registry (internal, all images). Vault PKI server (DMZ, cert-manager issuer). Prometheus + Grafana (IT zone, scrapes all zones via exporters). GitLab CI runners (IT zone).
target_output.workflows: PLC Telemetry Data Flow: PLC at asset_id=PLC-042 publishes MQTT message to topic plc/PLC-042/telemetry/pressure at 100 Hz; EMQX validates mTLS client cert against OT intermediate CA; ACL check confirms PLC-042 is authorized for this topic pattern; edge gateway MQTT subscriber receives message; transformer goroutine looks up PLC-042 metadata (description, ot_zone_subnet) from plc_assets cache (refreshed every 5 minutes from InfluxDB metadata query); encodes message as Avro with schema {asset_id, channel_type, timestamp_ns, value_float}; Kafka producer goroutine publishes to ot.plc.PLC-042.telemetry with idempotent producer; Kafka Connect InfluxDB sink writes to plc-telemetry bucket with tags asset_id=PLC-042 and channel_type=pressure; cloud analytics consumer on MSK receives via MirrorMaker 2 replication. Topology Change Workflow: IT engineer creates GitLab MR adding new firewall rule in Terraform for a new PLC subnet conduit; CI runs terraform validate and terraform plan, posts plan diff as MR comment; security team member from security_team GitLab group reviews diff and approves; CI executes terraform apply in target environment; connectivity smoke test publishes MQTT message through new conduit and verifies Kafka consumer receives it; MR merged and changes committed to Git history as audit trail.
target_output.data_model: plc_assets (id UUID PK, asset_code TEXT UNIQUE, description TEXT, ot_zone_subnet CIDR, mqtt_topic_prefix TEXT, installed_at DATETIME, decommissioned_at DATETIME). mqtt_topics (id UUID PK, plc_asset_id UUID FK, topic_pattern TEXT, payload_schema_version TEXT, sample_rate_hz INTEGER, channel_type TEXT). kafka_topics (topic_name TEXT PK, source_plc_pattern TEXT, partition_count INTEGER, replication_factor INTEGER, retention_ms BIGINT, created_at DATETIME). data_flows (id UUID PK, source_zone ENUM ot/dmz/it/cloud, destination_zone ENUM ot/dmz/it/cloud, protocol TEXT, authentication_method TEXT, firewall_rule_id TEXT, conduit_description TEXT). firewall_rules (id TEXT PK, source_cidr TEXT, destination_cidr TEXT, port INTEGER, protocol TEXT, action ENUM allow/deny, zone_conduit TEXT, terraform_resource_id TEXT, last_changed_at DATETIME, changed_by TEXT). certificate_authorities (id UUID PK, name TEXT, zone ENUM ot/dmz/it, issuer_ca_id UUID FK NULL, cert_pem TEXT, valid_from DATETIME, valid_until DATETIME, is_offline BOOLEAN). edge_gateways (id UUID PK, hostname TEXT, zone ENUM dmz, kubernetes_node TEXT, current_cert_cn TEXT, cert_expiry DATETIME, last_seen DATETIME). InfluxDB schema: measurement=plc_telemetry, tags={asset_id, channel_type, zone}, fields={value FLOAT}, timestamp_ns.
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API - infrastructure project. Internal management interfaces: EMQX Dashboard (port 18083, Keycloak OIDC auth, OT engineers): PLC connection status, topic ACL management, broker cluster health. Kafka UI (DMZ, IT engineers): topic browser, consumer group lag, MirrorMaker 2 replication status. InfluxDB UI (port 8086, IT engineers): Flux query for telemetry data, bucket retention management. GitLab CI pipeline API: trigger topology validation pipeline on MR, approve/reject pipeline stage. cert-manager CRD API (Kubernetes): CertificateRequest, Certificate objects for DMZ and IT zone certificate lifecycle. Prometheus metrics endpoints: EMQX /metrics, edge gateway /metrics (Go promhttp), Kafka JMX exporter, cert-manager expiry exporter.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IEC 62443 Zone and Conduit Model: OT zone (SL-3) allows only MQTT publish from registered PLCs; DMZ (SL-2) allows only edge gateway mTLS connections inbound from OT and outbound to IT; no direct OT-to-IT or IT-to-OT conduits. CA Hierarchy: offline root CA (HSM-backed, never network-connected), OT intermediate CA (signs EMQX broker cert CN=emqx.ot.plant and PLC client certs CN=asset_id), DMZ intermediate CA (signs edge gateway certs CN=edge-gw.dmz.plant), IT intermediate CA (signs Kafka broker, InfluxDB, and MSK client certs). mTLS enforcement: EMQX requires_certificate=true, verify_peer=true, CA cert = OT intermediate; Kafka listeners.ssl.client.auth=required, ssl.truststore includes DMZ intermediate CA cert. EMQX ACL: publish permission to plc/{CN_matching_asset_id}/telemetry/# only; subscribe denied for PLCs. Terraform security controls: explicit deny rules for 0.0.0.0/0 routes from OT subnets; IaC-enforced no-internet-path from OT nodes. All topology changes require GitLab security_team group approval. NIST CSF Protect: access control (IEC 62443 zones), data security (mTLS), maintenance (cert rotation runbooks). NIST CSF Detect: anomalous connection events in EMQX audit log, certificate expiry Prometheus alerts.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: OT Cluster (air-gapped bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04, k3s): EMQX Helm chart deployed from Harbor registry; MQTT port 8883 exposed via MetalLB LoadBalancer IP in OT subnet; images pre-pulled and cached in Harbor from vendor before air-gap. DMZ Cluster (bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04, k3s): edge-gateway Deployment (2 replicas, podAntiAffinity for node spread), Kafka StatefulSet (3 pods, persistent volumes on local NVMe), Schema Registry Deployment, cert-manager with Vault PKI ClusterIssuer. IT Cluster (bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04, k3s): InfluxDB StatefulSet, Kafka Connect Deployment, Prometheus + Grafana, Harbor registry, GitLab CI runners. AWS: Transit Gateway with VPN attachment to IT cluster network; MSK 3-broker cluster mirroring ot.plc.* topics via MirrorMaker 2. GitLab CI stages: validate (terraform validate, tflint, helm lint on changed charts), plan (terraform plan saved as artifact), security-approval (manual GitLab environment with security_team protection rule), apply (terraform apply with -input=false), smoke-test (Go script: MQTT publish from OT test pod, assert Kafka consumer receives within 5s). Rollback: git revert + CI pipeline re-run; Helm rollback for Kubernetes workloads.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes: EMQX exporter (mqtt_connections_count by zone, mqtt_messages_published_total by asset_id, authentication_failure_total), edge gateway /metrics (kafka_publish_latency_ms histogram p50/p95, mqtt_receive_rate, buffer_depth_bytes), Kafka JMX exporter (consumer_lag by topic and group, broker_request_rate), cert-manager expiry exporter (cert_expiry_seconds by secret_name and namespace). Grafana dashboards: OT Connectivity (MQTT connection count per PLC asset, authentication failures, broker memory), DMZ Pipeline (edge gateway throughput msg/s, Kafka lag per topic, buffer depth), Certificate Expiry (days-remaining per certificate, zone color-coded). Alerts: MQTT authentication_failure_total > 5/min (P2, possible PLC cert issue or unauthorized connection attempt); edge gateway buffer_depth_bytes > 500MB (P2, Kafka connectivity degradation); cert_expiry_seconds < 2592000 (30 days) for OT zone certs (P1, manual renewal required); Transit Gateway VPN attachment state != available (P1).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premises hardware: existing bare-metal servers repurposed; no incremental cost. AWS Transit Gateway: ~$50/month attachment + data processing. AWS MSK 3-broker kafka.m5.large: ~$400/month. S3 storage for Parquet archive: ~$30/month for 1 TB/year. Total AWS incremental cost: ~$480/month for 10-PLC pilot. Fleet scale (200 PLCs, 200 Hz average): edge gateway 3 replicas handle 40,000 msg/s; Kafka scale to 6 brokers; InfluxDB scale to cluster (InfluxDB Enterprise or migration to VictoriaMetrics cluster); AWS MSK scale to kafka.m5.xlarge; estimated ~$1,200/month AWS.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Q2): OT Kubernetes cluster with EMQX 3-node for 10 PLCs, single edge gateway pod with mTLS to DMZ Kafka, InfluxDB ingestion, three-tier CA hierarchy provisioned, firewall rules Terraform module for OT-DMZ conduit, GitLab CI pipeline with security approval gate, Prometheus certificate expiry alerts. Phase 2 (Q3): IT zone Kafka Connect InfluxDB sink, AWS Transit Gateway and MSK provisioning, MirrorMaker 2 for cloud replication, S3 Parquet archive export job, EMQX ACL per-asset enforcement, Grafana dashboards, cert-manager Vault integration for DMZ and IT auto-rotation. Phase 3 (roadmap): Expansion to full plant 200 PLCs, OT asset management portal, CMMS integration for predictive maintenance, IEC 62443 SL-3 audit certification.
target_output.development_guidelines: IaC only: no manual kubectl apply or terraform apply in production; all changes via GitLab CI pipeline. Terraform module structure: modules/ot-zone, modules/dmz-zone, modules/it-zone, modules/aws-connectivity; environments/staging and environments/production reference modules with environment-specific var files. Go edge gateway: no third-party frameworks; standard library + Paho MQTT + Confluent Kafka client only; unit tests for message transformer (MQTT-to-Avro conversion, ACL validation), integration test with docker-compose EMQX + Kafka. Helm charts: all values parameterized for zone-specific certificate paths and image tags; chart version bumped on every change. Certificate runbooks: offline OT zone cert renewal procedure documented in /docs/cert-renewal-ot.md with step-by-step HSM signing instructions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OT zone certificate expiry causes all PLC MQTT connections to drop simultaneously. Mitigation: Prometheus P1 alert at 30-day expiry for OT certs; renewal runbook tested annually; staggered cert expiry dates for EMQX broker and PLC client certs to avoid simultaneous renewal. Risk: EMQX cluster node failure in air-gapped OT zone requires manual image pull to Harbor if version not cached. Mitigation: Harbor replication job runs weekly to cache latest EMQX patch versions. Tradeoff: Go custom edge gateway vs off-the-shelf MQTT-to-Kafka bridge (e.g., EMQX built-in bridge) - custom Go gives finer mTLS control and local buffer capability at the cost of maintenance. Tradeoff: on-premises Kafka in DMZ vs edge-only buffering - DMZ Kafka provides replay capability and decouples IT consumers from edge gateway availability, at the cost of an additional Kafka cluster to operate.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed three-tier CA hierarchy (offline root, per-zone intermediates) over flat CA to limit blast radius of intermediate CA compromise. Confirmed cert-manager Vault issuer over ACME for DMZ and IT zones since ACME HTTP challenge does not work in air-gapped segments. Confirmed EMQX over Mosquitto for OT MQTT broker due to EMQX clustering, built-in ACL, and Prometheus exporter maturity. Confirmed badger KV local buffer in edge gateway over Redis for air-gap compatibility (Redis would require another service in DMZ). Confirmed MetalLB over NodePort for MQTT service exposure to maintain standard port 8883 with stable IP.

metadata.notes: OT/IT convergence industrial DMZ with IEC 62443 zone segmentation, three-tier CA, and GitLab CI IaC approval gate.

project_goal.value: Mobile app for chemical plant field technicians providing offline-capable work order management, equipment inspection checklists, spare parts lookup, and guided troubleshooting via fault code scanning
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need replacing paper-based maintenance workflows

target_users.value: Field maintenance technicians and maintenance supervisors at chemical plant facilities
target_users.rationale: Primary operators of plant equipment identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Requires native mobile device capabilities including camera, offline storage, and push notifications

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Mobile frontend with offline sync, REST backend, PostgreSQL data store, Entra ID auth, CMMS integration, and real-time work order push

complexity_level.rationale: Offline-first sync with conflict resolution adds complexity beyond standard CRUD

risk_level.rationale: Internal operations data; field device theft risk requires remote wipe capability

data_sensitivity.rationale: Work orders and maintenance records are internal operational data

external_exposure.rationale: All API access requires Entra ID authentication; no public endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with technician, supervisor, and parts-coordinator roles via Entra ID groups
access_model.rationale: Role differentiation controls who can close work orders vs. approve parts requisitions

feature_scope.value: Work order assignment and status updates, guided inspection checklists with photo capture, spare parts lookup with availability, fault code scanning to troubleshooting KB, offline queue with sync
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for field maintenance coverage

mvp_scope.value: Work order management, basic inspection checklists, offline queue, Entra ID SSO
mvp_scope.rationale: Core workflow validated before adding spare parts and KB modules

security_baseline.value: Entra ID SSO with MFA, encrypted local SQLite store, TLS 1.3 for API calls, MDM-enforced device policy for remote wipe
security_baseline.rationale: Internal device policy and field theft risk mitigation

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform iOS and Android from single codebase matching team expertise

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Hono on Azure Container Apps
backend_stack.rationale: Lightweight framework matching team Node.js expertise on Azure

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
data_platform.rationale: Managed relational database for work orders and audit history

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps for backend microservices, Azure Blob Storage for inspection photos
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless container scaling on corporate Azure tenant

privacy_retention_policy.value: Work order records retained 5 years per OSHA PSM requirements, inspection photos 3 years, access logs 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 process safety record-keeping requirements
