future_scope.value: Predictive maintenance alerts via IoT sensor integration, SAP PM integration for work order origination
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after field validation of core workflow

constraints.value: Must function fully offline for up to 8 hours, devices managed via Microsoft Intune MDM, backend must reside in corporate Azure tenant in West Europe region
constraints.rationale: Chemical plant process areas have no cellular or Wi-Fi coverage

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM for backend services, React Native Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting, Azure Monitor for container health
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standards on Azure

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with React Native and backend teams aligned; MVP in 12 weeks
execution_preference.rationale: Agile delivery matching plant maintenance turnaround schedule

project.goal: Replace paper-based field maintenance workflows with offline-capable mobile app
project.target_users: Chemical plant field technicians and maintenance supervisors

frontend.state: Redux Toolkit with RTK Query, WatermelonDB for offline SQLite sync

backend.framework: Hono with Zod validation

security.auth: Entra ID with MSAL React Native SDK, MFA via Conditional Access
security.device: Intune MDM app protection policies with selective wipe

data.local: WatermelonDB SQLite with AES-256 SQLCipher on device
data.cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for parts inventory snapshot

devops.environments: dev, staging, production in West Europe

open_questions.cmms_integration: SAP PM or Maximo as source of work orders TBD for Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: Work order management and offline checklist completion are the critical path for field technician adoption. Photo capture during inspections is a high-value differentiator over paper forms. Spare parts lookup with live availability will reduce technician round-trips to the warehouse significantly.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: WatermelonDB is the correct choice for React Native offline-first sync given its observable queries and conflict resolution primitives. The Hono backend should expose a delta-sync endpoint returning only records changed since a client-provided timestamp to minimize payload over cellular. Fault code scanning via camera QR/barcode requires careful permission handling on both iOS and Android.
reasoner_reviews.security: Entra ID Conditional Access with Intune compliance check ensures only enrolled devices obtain tokens. Local SQLite encryption via SQLCipher within WatermelonDB prevents data exfiltration if a device is lost in a hazardous area. Remote selective wipe via Intune MDM removes app data without full device factory reset.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 8-hour offline window is achievable by syncing full work order payloads and checklist templates on each connection. Azure West Europe data residency matches corporate policy. The 12-week MVP timeline is achievable if spare parts lookup and KB modules are deferred to Phase 2.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The sync conflict resolution strategy needs explicit definition before development starts. If two supervisors update the same work order status offline simultaneously, the merge policy must be documented and tested. Recommend last-write-wins with server timestamp and supervisor notification on detected conflict.

backend.service_design: Three Container Apps: work-order-service (CRUD and status transitions), sync-service (delta sync, conflict resolution, idempotent push endpoint keyed on client UUID), kb-service (fault code lookup and troubleshooting procedures). Shared Redis cache for parts inventory snapshots refreshed every 15 minutes from ERP export.
backend.api_patterns: GET /api/v1/work-orders?assigned_to={tech_id}&updated_since={iso_ts} for delta sync payload. POST /api/v1/sync/push for offline queue upload with idempotency keys. GET /api/v1/fault-codes/{code}/procedures for KB lookup. POST /api/v1/inspections/{id}/photo-upload-url returns pre-signed Blob Storage URL.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly Azure Container Apps job archives closed work orders older than 90 days to PostgreSQL cold partition and refreshes parts inventory cache from ERP CSV export file.
backend.failure_handling: Idempotent sync push endpoint prevents duplicate submissions on retry. Circuit breaker on ERP integration with fallback to stale Redis cache. 503 responses from kb-service degrade gracefully to cached WatermelonDB procedures.

frontend.app_structure: React Native with feature-based folder structure: /work-orders, /inspections, /parts, /troubleshooting, /sync. WatermelonDB models co-located with feature modules.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Bottom tab: Work Orders (list, detail, status transitions), Inspections (checklist with photo capture and signature pad), Parts (barcode scan, availability), Troubleshooting (fault code scan, procedure steps), Sync Status (pending queue count, last sync time).
frontend.state_management: WatermelonDB observable queries drive list and checklist screens reactively. Redux Toolkit manages global auth state and sync queue metadata. RTK Query for live availability checks when online.
frontend.performance_notes: Lazy-load checklist templates on first use and persist in WatermelonDB. Compress inspection photos to JPEG 85% quality before upload. Paginate work order list to 50 items with infinite scroll.

security.auth_design: MSAL React Native acquires Entra ID access tokens scoped to Container App audience. Tokens stored in iOS Keychain and Android Keystore. Conditional Access enforces Intune device compliance check before token issuance.
security.authorization_model: Backend validates Entra ID JWT on every request. Role claims (technician, supervisor, parts_coordinator) extracted from token and enforced at Hono route middleware. Technicians can only read and update their own assigned work orders.
security.secrets_management: Backend environment variables injected via Azure Container Apps secrets linked to Azure Key Vault. No credentials in container images or source repositories.

data.entities: work_orders (id UUID, asset_id FK, wo_type ENUM corrective/preventive/emergency, status ENUM open/in_progress/pending_parts/closed, assigned_technician_id, priority, server_updated_at), inspection_submissions (id UUID, work_order_id FK, checklist_template_id FK, responses_json, photos_blob_keys TEXT[], synced_at), equipment_assets (id UUID, plant_area, equipment_tag UNIQUE, criticality ENUM a/b/c), spare_parts (id UUID, part_number UNIQUE, qty_on_hand, warehouse_location, last_refreshed_at), fault_codes (id UUID, code UNIQUE, procedure_steps_json, related_part_numbers TEXT[]).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for work orders, inspection submissions, and asset master. Azure Blob Storage for inspection photos with CDN URL returned in API. Redis 7 for parts inventory snapshot with 15-minute TTL.
data.retention_and_deletion: Work orders and inspection submissions retained 5 years. Photos moved to Azure Blob Cool tier after 1 year, deleted at 3 years via lifecycle policy. Sync logs retained 1 year.

devops.deployment_topology: Three Azure Container Apps in West Europe region: work-order-service, sync-service, kb-service. PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with zone-redundant standby. Azure Cache for Redis in same region.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: PR pipeline runs ESLint, Jest unit tests, Zod schema validation. Merge to main builds Docker image, pushes to Azure Container Registry, rolling deploys to staging. Manual approval gate for production promotion.
devops.observability_stack: New Relic APM agent in each Container App. Structured JSON logs to Azure Monitor Log Analytics. Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash telemetry. Custom dashboards: sync success rate, work order close time, offline queue depth per device.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an offline-first React Native app for chemical plant field technicians. The central challenge is reliable 8-hour offline operation with delta-sync conflict resolution. WatermelonDB with SQLCipher handles local persistence. The Hono backend uses a delta-sync pattern returning records updated_since a client timestamp to minimize payload over narrow-band cellular. Three Container Apps separate work order CRUD, sync logic, and KB lookup for independent scaling.
target_output.title: Chemical Plant Field Maintenance Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first React Native application for field maintenance technicians at a chemical plant, built on Node.js 22 with Hono deployed to Azure Container Apps with PostgreSQL 16. The app enables technicians to manage work orders, complete guided inspection checklists with photo capture, scan fault codes to retrieve troubleshooting procedures, and look up spare parts availability while operating for up to 8 hours without network connectivity. Data syncs via a delta-sync endpoint when connectivity is restored with last-write-wins conflict resolution.
target_output.architecture_overview: Offline-first mobile architecture with React Native client using WatermelonDB (SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite) as the local data layer and three Hono microservices on Azure Container Apps handling work order CRUD, delta sync with conflict resolution, and fault code KB lookup respectively. Field devices authenticate via Entra ID Conditional Access requiring Intune MDM compliance before token issuance. Inspection photos upload directly to Azure Blob Storage via pre-signed URLs bypassing the backend for large binary payloads. Redis 7 caches parts inventory snapshots refreshed from ERP exports every 15 minutes. All backend services reside in Azure West Europe for corporate data residency compliance.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native with TypeScript, WatermelonDB with SQLCipher for offline sync, MSAL React Native for Entra ID SSO, React Navigation v6, Redux Toolkit. Backend: Node.js 22, Hono with Zod validation on Azure Container Apps. Data: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server with zone-redundant standby. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis. Storage: Azure Blob Storage for inspection photos with lifecycle policies. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines with Container Registry. Monitoring: New Relic APM, Azure Monitor Log Analytics, Firebase Crashlytics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Work Order Management: receive assigned corrective and preventive work orders, update status through open/in_progress/pending_parts/closed states, log repair outcomes with notes. Guided Inspection Checklists: step-by-step templates per equipment type with mandatory photo capture at flagged steps and digital signature on completion. Fault Code Troubleshooting: scan QR/barcode on control panel to retrieve ordered resolution steps and related spare parts from kb-service. Spare Parts Lookup: search by part number or equipment tag to view warehouse availability from Redis-cached ERP snapshot. Offline Queue: visual pending sync count with automatic sync on reconnect and conflict notification.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Apps (West Europe): work-order-service for CRUD and status transitions, sync-service for delta sync endpoint and idempotent push with conflict resolution, kb-service for fault code lookup and troubleshooting procedures. PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with zone-redundant standby. Azure Cache for Redis 7 for parts inventory snapshot at 15-minute TTL. Azure Blob Storage for inspection photos with CDN. Azure Container Registry. New Relic APM sidecars per container. Intune-managed field devices running React Native with WatermelonDB encrypted local store.
target_output.workflows: Work Order Completion: supervisor assigns corrective work order in CMMS stub; work-order-service creates record in PostgreSQL; technician device pulls delta payload on next sync. Technician opens work order offline, navigates to equipment by asset tag, selects pre-cached inspection checklist template in WatermelonDB, completes steps with required photos saved to device local storage. On reconnection, sync-service POST /api/v1/sync/push receives work order status update and inspection submission with client UUID idempotency key; server validates timestamps, resolves conflicts with last-write-wins on server_updated_at, persists to PostgreSQL, triggers photo upload via pre-signed Blob URL. Fault Code Troubleshooting: technician scans QR code on control panel with device camera; React Native camera module decodes fault code string; app queries kb-service GET /api/v1/fault-codes/{code}/procedures (also cached offline in WatermelonDB); displays ordered resolution steps with safety precautions and required tools; surfaces related spare part numbers for warehouse pickup confirmation.
target_output.data_model: work_orders (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, wo_type ENUM corrective/preventive/emergency, status ENUM open/in_progress/pending_parts/closed, assigned_technician_id, priority ENUM 1-4, description, created_at, updated_at, closed_at, server_updated_at for sync conflict resolution). inspection_submissions (id UUID PK, work_order_id FK, checklist_template_id FK, technician_id, responses_json JSONB, photos_blob_keys TEXT[], submitted_at, synced_at). equipment_assets (id UUID PK, plant_area VARCHAR, equipment_tag VARCHAR UNIQUE, asset_type, criticality ENUM a/b/c, manufacturer, model_number). spare_parts (id UUID PK, part_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, description, uom, warehouse_location, qty_on_hand INT, last_refreshed_at). fault_codes (id UUID PK, code VARCHAR UNIQUE, equipment_type, description, procedure_steps_json JSONB, related_part_numbers TEXT[]). checklist_templates (id UUID PK, equipment_type, version INT, steps_json JSONB, required_photo_step_ids TEXT[]).
target_output.api_design: RESTful JSON at /api/v1. GET /api/v1/work-orders?assigned_to={tech_id}&updated_since={iso_ts} returns delta payload for device sync. POST /api/v1/sync/push accepts array of offline mutations with client_uuid idempotency keys, returns conflict summary. GET /api/v1/fault-codes/{code}/procedures returns JSON procedure steps and related_part_numbers. GET /api/v1/spare-parts?q={part_number} returns availability from Redis cache. POST /api/v1/inspections/{work_order_id}/photo-upload-url returns pre-signed Azure Blob Storage URL. POST /api/v1/inspections/{work_order_id}/submit persists completed checklist. All endpoints require Bearer Entra ID JWT validated at Container Apps ingress.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Entra ID Conditional Access policy requires Intune-compliant device enrollment and MFA before token issuance. MSAL tokens stored in iOS Keychain and Android Keystore, never in AsyncStorage. WatermelonDB SQLite encrypted with SQLCipher AES-256 key derived from device hardware-backed keystore. Backend validates Entra ID JWT audience and role claims on every Hono route. Intune app protection policy enables selective wipe of app container data on device loss without full device factory reset. Azure Key Vault stores backend secrets injected as Container Apps environment variables. TLS 1.3 enforced on all API and Blob Storage endpoints. OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM record retention enforced by 5-year PostgreSQL retention and 3-year photo lifecycle policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps in West Europe: work-order-service, sync-service, kb-service each with min 1 max 5 replicas, HTTP request queue-based auto-scaling. PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with zone-redundant standby and daily automated backups with 35-day point-in-time restore. Azure DevOps Pipelines: PR pipeline runs ESLint, Jest unit tests, and Zod contract validation; merge to main builds Docker image, pushes to Azure Container Registry, rolling-deploys to staging Container App with health probe gate; manual approval gate for production promotion. React Native builds via Azure DevOps with Fastlane code signing and distribution via Intune Company Portal. Rollback: Container Apps traffic-split to prior revision within 2 minutes. Nightly archive job moves closed work orders older than 90 days to cold partition.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM agent in each Container App capturing transaction traces, error rates, and external call latency. Structured JSON logs to Azure Monitor Log Analytics with fields: technician_id, work_order_id, sync_batch_size, conflict_count, duration_ms. Firebase Crashlytics captures React Native JS and native crash traces with device model and OS version. Custom New Relic dashboards track sync success rate (target >99%), offline queue depth per device, work order close time by priority, parts cache hit rate. PagerDuty alert when sync error rate exceeds 2% or kb-service p99 latency exceeds 1 second.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800-1200/month for 200 concurrent field devices. Azure Container Apps consumption plan with scale-to-zero for kb-service during off-shift hours. PostgreSQL Flexible Server burstable B4ms for MVP, upgradeable to General Purpose D4s at >500 technicians. Azure Blob Storage LRS for photos at $0.018/GB. Redis Cache Basic C1 for parts snapshot. Cost review trigger when active technician count exceeds 300.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Entra ID SSO with Intune enrollment, work order list and status updates with offline queue and delta sync, 3 standard inspection checklist templates with photo capture, Azure Container Apps and PostgreSQL setup, CI/CD pipeline to Intune Company Portal. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Fault code QR/barcode scanner with kb-service, spare parts lookup with Redis-cached ERP snapshot, supervisor assignment dashboard, parts requisition workflow. Phase 3 (6 weeks): SAP PM integration for automated work order origination, predictive maintenance push notifications, advanced analytics for MTTR and inspection compliance rate, conflict audit reporting for supervisors.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in React Native and Hono services. ESLint with react-native community config and Prettier enforced in CI. Jest unit tests for sync conflict resolution logic and Hono route handlers at 75% coverage gate. WatermelonDB schema migrations versioned and smoke-tested before each release build. Detox E2E tests for work order completion and checklist submission flows on physical iOS and Android devices in CI. ADRs required for any change to sync protocol. kb-service reads only from its own data partition; no cross-service direct database access.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB sync conflict on concurrent offline edits to the same work order by two supervisors. Mitigation: last-write-wins on server_updated_at with push notification listing changed fields sent to both supervisors on conflict detection. Risk: SQLCipher key loss on device factory reset making local data unrecoverable. Mitigation: sync-service persists all data server-side before confirming sync success; local WatermelonDB store is treated as a cache, not a system of record. Tradeoff: three separate Container Apps over a monolith increases deployment surface but allows kb-service to scale independently during fault code lookup peaks at shift start. Tradeoff: WatermelonDB over raw SQLite chosen for observable query reactivity and built-in sync primitives at the cost of steeper onboarding for React Native developers unfamiliar with the library.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected WatermelonDB over PouchDB for offline sync due to better React Native performance benchmarks and native SQLCipher encryption support. Confirmed Azure Blob pre-signed URLs for photo upload to avoid routing binary data through Container Apps ingress. Resolved conflict strategy as last-write-wins on server_updated_at with supervisor notification on conflict. SAP PM CMMS integration deferred to Phase 3 pending API access agreement with ERP team.

metadata.notes: Offline-first mobile maintenance app for chemical plant with WatermelonDB delta-sync and Intune MDM device management.

project_goal.value: Supplier quality management portal for automotive OEM engineers to submit material certification requests, enable suppliers to upload test reports and COAs, and run automated rules engine validation against engineering specifications before granting part approval
project_goal.rationale: Replaces email-based PPAP submission process with auditable digital workflow

target_users.value: OEM quality engineers creating approval requests, external automotive suppliers uploading certification documents, and quality managers reviewing conformance decisions
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user populations with different access levels and portal views

project_class.rationale: OEM-facing Next.js portal plus REST API consumed by ERP integration and supplier portal

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, admin_panel, public_api, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Complex portal with supplier-facing front end, rules engine backend, ERP integration, and nightly batch conformance re-evaluation

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-party portal with rules engine, digital signing, ERP REST integration, and PPAP documentation workflow

risk_level.rationale: Confidential engineering drawings and supplier trade data exchanged between competing OEM and supplier organizations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Material specifications, engineering drawings, and supplier test data are commercially sensitive intellectual property

external_exposure.rationale: Supplier portal accessed by external partner organizations via invite-only accounts; mTLS required for ERP integration

access_model.value: RBAC with oem_engineer, supplier_contact, quality_manager, and system_admin roles; supplier access scoped to their own approval requests only
access_model.rationale: Suppliers must not see other suppliers' submissions or OEM-internal engineering notes

feature_scope.value: Material approval request creation with drawing attachment, supplier document upload portal (test reports, COA), automated conformance rules engine for composition and mechanical properties, digital approval signatures, deviation request workflow, REST API for ERP integration, admin panel for spec management
feature_scope.rationale: Full IATF 16949 PPAP digital workflow

mvp_scope.value: Approval request creation, supplier document upload, manual OEM review and approve/reject, ERP approval status push via REST
mvp_scope.rationale: Manual review validates workflow before automated rules engine layer

security_baseline.value: Cognito with MFA for OEM and supplier users, mTLS for ERP REST integration, S3 server-side encryption for document storage, row-level security in PostgreSQL, TLS 1.3
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential trade data and external partner access require layered security controls

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript and App Router
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for document-heavy pages, React Server Components for admin views

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI on AWS ECS Fargate
backend_stack.rationale: Python rules engine with Pydantic validation and existing ML team expertise

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Amazon RDS with Multi-AZ and row-level security
data_platform.rationale: Row-level security enforces supplier data isolation at database level

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1, S3 for document storage, CloudFront for portal CDN
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with S3 document archival on corporate AWS account

privacy_retention_policy.value: Approval records and PPAP documents retained 15 years per IATF audit requirements, EU supplier contact data deleted within 30 days of account termination per GDPR Art.17
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: IATF 16949 traceability and GDPR data subject rights obligations

future_scope.value: AI-assisted specification authoring, supplier scorecard analytics, integration with OEM PLM system for automatic drawing versioning
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 after rules engine stabilization

constraints.value: Must support 500+ supplier organizations each with up to 10 users, ERP integration via mTLS REST with existing SAP S4/HANA, GDPR compliance for EU supplier contacts
constraints.rationale: OEM supplier base scale and existing ERP architecture

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed tracing across FastAPI services, CloudWatch for ECS task logs, PagerDuty alerts for rules engine failures and ERP integration errors
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard on AWS

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; OEM engineer review at end of each sprint; suppliers onboarded in Phase 2 pilot with 10 partner organizations
execution_preference.rationale: Phased supplier onboarding to validate portal UX before broad rollout

compliance_context.value: IATF 16949 supplier approval process and PPAP Level 3 documentation requirements; GDPR for EU supplier contact data with DPA required; mTLS mandated for all ERP API endpoints
compliance_context.rationale: Automotive quality standard and EU data protection obligations

project.goal: Digital PPAP portal replacing email-based supplier certification workflow
project.target_users: OEM quality engineers, external automotive suppliers, quality managers
project.timeline: MVP 14 weeks, pilot with 10 suppliers at week 18

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with Radix UI primitives
frontend.state: TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for approval wizard local state

backend.framework: FastAPI with Pydantic v2
backend.api: RESTful JSON at /api/v1, OpenAPI 3.1 spec published at /docs

security.auth: Cognito User Pools with MFA, hosted UI for supplier self-service signup
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for browser, mTLS for ERP endpoint

data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache for rules engine result cache and session store
data.storage: S3 for COA and test report PDFs with KMS encryption

constraints.gdpr: EU supplier contact deletion within 30 days of termination

open_questions.rules_engine_versioning: How to version conformance rules when engineering specs are revised mid-open-request

reasoner_reviews.product: The automated conformance rules engine is the highest-value feature differentiating this portal from shared-drive alternatives. Chemical composition tolerance checks and mechanical property validation rules must be configurable by OEM engineers without deployments. The deviation request workflow for non-conforming submissions is critical for supplier retention.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with Pydantic v2 is well-suited for the rules engine given Pydantic's model validation capabilities. PostgreSQL row-level security is the correct mechanism to enforce supplier data isolation; application-level filtering alone is insufficient for confidential IP. The public ERP API endpoint must use a separate Fargate task with mTLS listener distinct from the browser-facing API.
reasoner_reviews.security: Row-level security policies must be audited with pg_audit to confirm suppliers cannot access other organizations' records even via UNION injection. The digital approval signature using AWS KMS ensures non-repudiation: the KMS key ID, signing algorithm, and approver Cognito sub are stored alongside each conformance decision for audit.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 500 supplier organizations at 10 users each (5000 external users) is within Cognito User Pool limits. mTLS for SAP S4/HANA requires ACM Private CA certificate issuance for the ERP integration endpoint. GDPR deletion within 30 days of account termination must be implemented as a scheduled Lambda triggered on account deactivation event.

backend.service_design: Three Fargate services: portal-api (approval requests, document management, user management), rules-engine-service (conformance evaluation against versioned spec rules), erp-integration-service (mTLS REST endpoint publishing approval decisions to SAP S4/HANA). Rules engine reads spec definitions from PostgreSQL and evaluates uploaded COA values against tolerance bands.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/v1/approval-requests creates request linked to material_spec_id and engineering_drawing_id. POST /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/documents presigns S3 upload URL for supplier COA/test report. POST /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/submit triggers async rules engine evaluation via SQS. GET /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/conformance-result returns evaluation detail. PATCH /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/decision for manual OEM approve/reject with KMS signature. mTLS endpoint: POST /erp/v1/part-approvals for SAP callback.
backend.failure_handling: Rules engine evaluation is async via SQS; portal shows 'evaluation in progress' status. Dead-letter queue captures failed evaluations with CloudWatch alert. mTLS ERP endpoint uses retry with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts before marking decision as pending_erp_sync.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly EventBridge-triggered Lambda re-evaluates all open approval requests against latest published spec versions; sends email digest to OEM engineers listing requests affected by spec revision. Weekly GDPR deletion job processes deactivated EU supplier accounts older than 30 days.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with route groups: (oem) for engineer and quality manager views, (supplier) for partner document upload portal, (admin) for spec management. Server Components for read-heavy document list pages, Client Components for multi-step approval wizard.
frontend.pages_and_flows: OEM: New Approval Request wizard (select material spec, attach drawing, set submission deadline), Pending Review queue with conformance result detail, Approval Decision page with KMS signature confirmation. Supplier: portal invite landing, Document Upload (drag-drop COA and test reports with file type validation), Submission status tracker. Admin: Spec Rule Editor for tolerance band configuration, Supplier Organization management.
frontend.state_management: TanStack Query caches approval request lists and conformance results. Zustand manages multi-step wizard state for new approval request creation. Next.js server actions for document upload presign requests.

security.auth_design: Cognito User Pools with two user pool clients: oem_client (internal, Entra ID federation option) and supplier_client (external, email-based with MFA required). Hosted UI for supplier self-service account creation on portal invite link.
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL RLS policies: oem_engineer and quality_manager see all rows; supplier_contact policy enforces WHERE supplier_org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id'). FastAPI middleware sets session variable on each request from JWT claims.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: pg_audit extension logs all SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE on approval_requests and conformance_decisions tables. CloudTrail captures S3 document access. KMS sign operations logged to CloudTrail with approver Cognito sub.
security.security_risks: Risk: supplier user enumeration via invite email flow. Mitigation: invite tokens are single-use UUID, expired after 72 hours, with no user existence hints on invalid token response.

data.entities: approval_requests (id UUID, material_spec_id FK, engineering_drawing_id FK, supplier_org_id FK, status ENUM draft/submitted/under_review/conforming/non_conforming/approved/rejected/deviation_requested, submitted_at, decided_at), suppliers (id UUID, org_name, country_code, vat_number, gdpr_region BOOL, deactivated_at), material_specs (id UUID, spec_number, version INT, element_rules_json JSONB, mechanical_rules_json JSONB, published_at), test_reports (id UUID, approval_request_id FK, s3_key, content_type, uploaded_by), certificates_of_analysis (id UUID, approval_request_id FK, s3_key, parsed_values_json JSONB), conformance_decisions (id UUID, approval_request_id FK, evaluation_version INT, result ENUM conforming/non_conforming, deviation_notes, approver_id, kms_signature, decided_at), audit_trails (id UUID, entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_id, actor_org, ip_address, occurred_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for all structured data. S3 with KMS SSE-S3 for COA PDFs and test reports. Redis 7 ElastiCache for rules engine result cache (keyed by approval_request_id + spec_version) with 1-hour TTL.
data.retention_and_deletion: Approval records and PPAP documents retained 15 years in PostgreSQL and S3. EU supplier contact PII (name, email) soft-deleted within 30 days of deactivation_at via scheduled Lambda, approval request records anonymized but preserved for IATF traceability.

devops.deployment_topology: Three ECS Fargate services in us-east-1: portal-api (public ALB with WAF), rules-engine-service (internal ALB), erp-integration-service (NLB with mTLS, private subnet only). RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL 16. ElastiCache Redis 7. SQS queue for async rules evaluation.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: PR runs pytest with 85% coverage gate, mypy type checking, Bandit SAST scan, and Trivy container image scan. Merge to main builds and pushes to ECR, deploys to staging via ECS rolling update with health check gate. Production deployment requires pull request approval from security team member.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with FastAPI auto-instrumentation and distributed trace propagation through SQS. Custom metrics: rules_engine_evaluation_duration_ms, erp_sync_success_rate, approval_cycle_time_days. PagerDuty alert for ERP sync failures and rules engine DLQ messages.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS service update rollback to previous task definition revision on health check failure. RDS point-in-time restore with 35-day window. S3 versioning enabled for spec rule JSON files with explicit version on each approval record.

ISS-001.title: PostgreSQL RLS policy not validated for injection bypass
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described RLS conceptually but did not specify the policy predicate or how the app.current_org_id session variable is set. Risk of privilege escalation if middleware is bypassed.

history.note: Policy predicate missing from data section

ISS-002.title: mTLS ERP endpoint not isolated from browser-facing ALB
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan placed all three Fargate services behind a single ALB. The mTLS ERP integration endpoint must be on a separate NLB in a private subnet to prevent browser clients from reaching it.

history.note: Deployment topology lacked ERP endpoint isolation

ISS-003.title: GDPR deletion job timeline and anonymization strategy undefined
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned GDPR deletion but did not specify which fields are deleted vs. anonymized, the trigger mechanism, or how IATF traceability is preserved after PII removal.

history.note: Retention policy section lacked deletion mechanics

focus_issues.title: mTLS ERP endpoint not isolated from browser-facing ALB
focus_issues.detail: The mTLS ERP integration endpoint must use a separate NLB in a private subnet, not share the public ALB used by browser clients.

focus_issues.title: GDPR deletion job timeline and anonymization strategy undefined
focus_issues.detail: Specify field-level deletion vs. anonymization, trigger mechanism on account deactivation, and IATF traceability preservation approach.

latest_recommendations: Isolate the mTLS ERP endpoint on a private NLB separate from the public ALB used by the supplier portal
Define GDPR field-level deletion mechanics: delete name/email, anonymize approver_id in conformance_decisions, preserve approval_request records for IATF 15-year traceability

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues including one high-severity topology gap

previous_audits.summary: Strong domain coverage and rules engine design. Security topology has a critical gap: mTLS ERP endpoint shares ALB with browser portal. GDPR mechanics are conceptual only.
strengths: Rules engine design with SQS async evaluation is well-reasoned
KMS digital signature for non-repudiation on approval decisions is correct
PostgreSQL RLS conceptual design is sound
concerns: mTLS ERP endpoint must not share public ALB with browser clients
GDPR deletion does not specify field-level mechanics or IATF traceability preservation
blocking_issues: mTLS endpoint on shared public ALB violates network isolation requirement for partner-facing API
recommendations: Deploy erp-integration-service behind a private NLB in a private subnet; browser-facing portal-api keeps the public ALB with WAF

Specify: on account deactivation, delete supplier name/email/phone within 30 days; anonymize approver_id in audit records; preserve approval_request records with null supplier contact fields for 15-year IATF retention

issue_updates.title: PostgreSQL RLS policy predicate undefined
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan specifies FastAPI middleware sets app.current_org_id from JWT claims before each query; RLS policy predicate confirmed in data section

issue_updates.title: mTLS ERP endpoint not isolated
issue_updates.detail: Deployment topology in round 1 showed all services behind single ALB

previous_plan.title: Automotive Supplier Quality Portal v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A fullstack PPAP portal for automotive OEM and supplier quality management built on Next.js 14 and FastAPI on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL 16 and Cognito authentication.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture with Next.js SSR portal, FastAPI backend services on ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL 16 with RLS for supplier data isolation. Rules engine evaluates COA values against tolerance bands asynchronously via SQS.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA for all users. PostgreSQL RLS for supplier isolation. KMS signatures on approval decisions. GDPR deletion within 30 days. IATF 15-year retention.

best_plan.title: Automotive Supplier Quality Portal v1
best_plan.executive_summary: A fullstack PPAP portal for automotive OEM and supplier quality management built on Next.js 14 and FastAPI on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL 16 and Cognito authentication.
best_plan.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture with Next.js SSR portal, FastAPI backend services on ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL 16 with RLS for supplier data isolation. Rules engine evaluates COA values against tolerance bands asynchronously via SQS.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA for all users. PostgreSQL RLS for supplier isolation. KMS signatures on approval decisions. GDPR deletion within 30 days. IATF 15-year retention.
best_plan.deployment_and_operations: Three Fargate services behind a single ALB with WAF. mTLS configured on the ERP endpoint path. GitHub Actions CI with pytest and Bandit.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revision round 2 addressing two unresolved issues: mTLS ERP endpoint isolation on private NLB and GDPR field-level deletion mechanics with IATF traceability preservation. The deployment topology now separates erp-integration-service behind a private NLB in a private subnet, completely decoupled from the public ALB. GDPR mechanics are specified: supplier name/email deleted within 30 days of deactivation, approval_request records retained with null contact fields for 15-year IATF traceability.

fix_report.action_taken: Deployment topology updated: erp-integration-service moved to private NLB in private subnet; portal-api and rules-engine-service remain behind public ALB with WAF. Security section updated to describe separate mTLS termination on NLB listener.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Browser clients cannot reach mTLS ERP endpoint; SAP S4/HANA connects directly to private NLB using mutual certificate authentication

fix_report.action_taken: Data model and privacy_retention_policy sections updated with field-level GDPR mechanics: on account deactivation EventBridge rule triggers Lambda that nullifies supplier name, email, phone, and vat_number after 30 days; conformance_decisions approver_id replaced with anonymized hash; approval_request and COA records preserved with supplier_org_id for IATF traceability.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR Art.17 deletion completed within 30-day SLA while preserving IATF 16949 15-year traceability on approval records

target_output.title: Automotive Supplier Quality Portal
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack PPAP supplier quality portal for automotive OEM engineers and external supplier organizations, built on Next.js 14 and FastAPI on AWS ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL 16 RLS and Cognito. Engineers create material certification requests linked to engineering drawings and specs; suppliers upload COA PDFs and test reports via an invite-only partner portal; an automated rules engine validates chemical composition tolerances and mechanical property requirements asynchronously via SQS. Non-conforming submissions receive machine-generated deviation notes; approved parts generate KMS-signed conformance decisions published to SAP S4/HANA via a private mTLS NLB endpoint.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier fullstack architecture: Next.js 14 App Router portal (Server Components for document lists, Client Components for multi-step approval wizard) served via CloudFront. Two ECS Fargate services behind a public ALB with WAF Rules: portal-api handles approval requests and document management; rules-engine-service processes conformance evaluation jobs from SQS. A third Fargate service, erp-integration-service, sits behind a private NLB in a private subnet with mTLS termination, exclusively handling outbound conformance decision publication to SAP S4/HANA. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ enforces supplier data isolation via row-level security policies. S3 with KMS SSE stores COA PDFs and test reports. Cognito User Pools serve two distinct client apps: an OEM internal client and a supplier external client with invite-based MFA enrollment.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, TanStack Query v5, Zustand for wizard state. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI with Pydantic v2, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, asyncpg. Async evaluation: SQS FIFO queue with rules-engine-service consumer. Data: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with pg_audit extension, Redis 7 ElastiCache for rules cache. Storage: S3 with KMS SSE-S3. Auth: Cognito User Pools with hosted UI. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, public ALB with WAF, private NLB for ERP mTLS. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Approval Request Lifecycle: OEM engineer creates material approval request selecting material_spec version and attaching engineering drawing; system assigns request to relevant supplier org via supplier_org_id lookup; supplier receives email notification with time-limited portal invite URL. Supplier Document Upload: supplier logs into invite-linked account, uploads COA PDF and test reports via presigned S3 URL, submits for evaluation. Automated Conformance Evaluation: rules-engine-service dequeues submission, extracts parsed_values_json from COA, evaluates each element against element_rules_json tolerance bands and mechanical_rules_json property ranges, writes conformance_decisions record with per-element pass/fail detail. Deviation Request Workflow: non-conforming submissions return to supplier with element-level failure notes; supplier submits deviation request with engineering justification; OEM quality manager approves or rejects deviation. ERP Approval Publication: approved conformance decisions are signed with KMS and published to SAP S4/HANA via erp-integration-service NLB endpoint for supplier qualification status update. Admin Spec Management: quality engineers edit tolerance band rules in material_specs without deployments.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront CDN for Next.js static assets. Public ALB with AWS WAF (OWASP rule group + rate limiting) routing to portal-api and rules-engine-service ECS Fargate tasks. Private NLB with mTLS listener routing exclusively to erp-integration-service Fargate tasks in private subnet. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with pg_audit and row-level security. Redis 7 ElastiCache for rules engine result cache. SQS FIFO queue for async evaluation jobs with dead-letter queue. S3 with KMS SSE-S3 for document storage and versioning. Cognito User Pools (OEM client + supplier client). EventBridge rules for GDPR deletion Lambda trigger and nightly spec re-evaluation batch job. ACM Private CA for mTLS certificate issuance for SAP S4/HANA integration.
target_output.workflows: Material Approval Submission: OEM engineer opens New Approval Request wizard, selects material_spec_id and version, uploads engineering_drawing PDF via presigned S3 URL, sets submission deadline, and publishes request. Supplier org admin receives invite email with 72-hour single-use token; creates account with Cognito hosted UI and MFA enrollment; uploads COA PDF and test reports to presigned S3 keys. Submission triggers SQS message with approval_request_id. Rules Engine Evaluation: rules-engine-service dequeues message, fetches COA parsed_values_json, loads material_spec element_rules_json and mechanical_rules_json, evaluates each element against min/max tolerance bands, writes conformance_decisions record with per-element result and deviation_notes for failures, updates approval_request status to conforming or non_conforming. Conforming requests trigger KMS sign operation on decision payload; signed decision published to SAP S4/HANA via POST /erp/v1/part-approvals on private NLB with client certificate. SAP returns part qualification status acknowledgment stored in audit_trails.
target_output.data_model: approval_requests (id UUID PK, material_spec_id FK, material_spec_version INT, engineering_drawing_id FK, supplier_org_id FK, status ENUM draft/submitted/under_review/conforming/non_conforming/approved/rejected/deviation_requested, submitted_at, decided_at, erp_sync_status ENUM pending/synced/failed). suppliers (id UUID PK, org_name VARCHAR, country_code CHAR(2), gdpr_region BOOL, vat_number VARCHAR, deactivated_at TIMESTAMP, gdpr_deleted_at TIMESTAMP). material_specs (id UUID PK, spec_number VARCHAR, version INT, element_rules_json JSONB, mechanical_rules_json JSONB, published_by UUID, published_at). test_reports (id UUID PK, approval_request_id FK, s3_key, content_type). certificates_of_analysis (id UUID PK, approval_request_id FK, s3_key, parsed_values_json JSONB). conformance_decisions (id UUID PK, approval_request_id FK, evaluation_spec_version INT, result ENUM conforming/non_conforming, element_results_json JSONB, deviation_notes TEXT, approver_id UUID, approver_id_hash VARCHAR -- used after GDPR anonymization, kms_key_id, kms_signature, decided_at). audit_trails (id UUID PK, entity_type, entity_id UUID, action, actor_id UUID, actor_org_id UUID, ip_address INET, occurred_at).
target_output.api_design: RESTful JSON at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 at /docs. POST /api/v1/approval-requests creates request with material_spec_id and drawing upload URL. GET /api/v1/approval-requests?status=submitted&supplier_org_id={id} lists pending reviews for OEM. POST /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/documents returns presigned S3 upload URL for COA or test report. POST /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/submit enqueues SQS evaluation job. GET /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/conformance-result returns element-level evaluation detail. PATCH /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/decision accepts approve/reject with KMS-signed payload. POST /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/deviation-request for supplier deviation submission. Admin: PUT /api/v1/material-specs/{id}/rules for tolerance band updates. ERP mTLS endpoint (private NLB only): POST /erp/v1/part-approvals publishes signed conformance decision to SAP S4/HANA.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito OEM client uses SAML federation option for internal SSO; supplier client uses email-based hosted UI with TOTP MFA mandatory. PostgreSQL RLS policy on approval_requests: USING (supplier_org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id')::uuid) for supplier_contact role; FastAPI auth middleware sets pg session variable from JWT sub claim on every request. pg_audit logs all SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE on approval_requests, conformance_decisions, and material_specs to CloudWatch. KMS sign operation on approval decision payload with key ID, signing algorithm, and approver Cognito sub stored in conformance_decisions for non-repudiation. erp-integration-service on private NLB with mTLS listener; ACM Private CA issues client certificate to SAP S4/HANA; certificate CN validated in FastAPI middleware. IATF 16949: approval records retained 15 years; spec rules versioned with immutable S3 object lock. GDPR Art.17: EventBridge rule triggers Lambda 30 days after deactivated_at, nullifies supplier name/email/phone/vat_number, hashes approver_id in conformance_decisions to SHA-256 hex, preserves approval_request and COA records with supplier_org_id for traceability. WAF OWASP Core Rule Set v3.2 on public ALB. TLS 1.3 enforced on all listeners.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate in us-east-1: portal-api (min 2 max 8 tasks, CPU 30% target scaling) and rules-engine-service (min 1 max 4 tasks, SQS queue depth scaling) behind public ALB with WAF. erp-integration-service (min 1 max 2 tasks) behind private NLB in private subnet with mTLS listener on port 8443; security group allows inbound only from SAP S4/HANA NAT IP range. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with 35-day PITR and daily snapshots. GitHub Actions: PR runs pytest 85% coverage, mypy, Bandit, Trivy image scan; merge to main builds and pushes to ECR, rolling ECS deploy to staging; production requires security team PR approval. EventBridge scheduled rules: nightly spec re-evaluation at 02:00 UTC, GDPR deletion Lambda triggered on account deactivation event. Secrets Manager for Cognito client secret, RDS password, and KMS key alias with 90-day rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with FastAPI and SQLAlchemy auto-instrumentation; distributed trace context propagated through SQS message attributes to rules-engine-service. Custom Datadog metrics: rules_engine_queue_depth, evaluation_duration_p99_ms, erp_sync_success_rate, approval_cycle_time_days. CloudWatch Logs Insights for pg_audit query logs. PagerDuty critical alerts: ERP sync failure rate >5% in 15-minute window, rules engine DLQ message count >0, RDS replication lag >30s. Weekly Datadog dashboard review of approval cycle time and supplier submission error rate for product team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2200/month for 500 supplier orgs with 50 concurrent approval evaluations. ECS Fargate spot for rules-engine-service during off-peak hours saves ~30%. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for COA documents after 30 days, Glacier Deep Archive after 3 years for 15-year retention at minimal cost. Redis cache reduces PostgreSQL query load for repeated conformance result fetches. Budget alert at $2800/month for review.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks): Cognito OEM and supplier user pools, approval request creation with drawing upload, supplier document upload via presigned S3 URLs, manual OEM review with approve/reject, KMS signature on decisions, mTLS ERP endpoint publishing to SAP S4/HANA stub, GDPR deletion Lambda skeleton, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Automated rules engine with SQS async evaluation, per-element conformance result UI, deviation request workflow, admin spec rule editor, pilot with 10 supplier organizations, pg_audit enabled. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Nightly spec re-evaluation batch job, full Datadog APM instrumentation, supplier scorecard analytics, SAML federation for OEM internal SSO, GDPR deletion Lambda production hardening.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. Pydantic v2 models for all API request and response shapes; no dict responses without Pydantic model. pytest with 85% coverage gate enforced in CI; async test fixtures for FastAPI routes using httpx.AsyncClient. Bandit SAST and Trivy container scan required on every PR. RLS policy changes require security team review before merge. Rules engine tolerance evaluation logic tested with golden test set of 50 known COA fixtures covering boundary and off-nominal cases. No direct S3 URL construction in application code; always via presign helper with expiry validation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: rules engine false-positive rejection of valid COA due to parsed_values_json extraction errors from non-standard PDF layouts. Mitigation: manual override path for OEM engineer with audit log entry; PDF parsing confidence score stored alongside parsed values. Risk: mTLS certificate expiry causing ERP sync outage. Mitigation: ACM Private CA auto-renewal with 30-day expiry alert to DevOps team. Tradeoff: SQS async evaluation over synchronous rules check adds latency but decouples portal from rules engine failures and allows retry without re-upload. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL RLS over application-level tenant filter chosen for defense-in-depth; application bug cannot bypass database-level isolation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed separate private NLB for ERP mTLS endpoint to satisfy network isolation requirement. Resolved GDPR deletion: field-level nullification of contact PII with approver_id hash replacement preserves IATF traceability on approval records. Rules engine spec versioning: evaluation records store the spec version at time of evaluation, enabling historical re-audit. Supplier invite token expiry set to 72 hours with single-use enforcement.

metadata.notes: Automotive PPAP portal revision round 2 fixing mTLS endpoint isolation and GDPR deletion mechanics; IATF 16949 and GDPR compliance.

project_goal.value: Shift handover and production reporting web application for steel mill operators enabling structured end-of-shift reporting, equipment anomaly logging, and KPI dashboard review, replacing paper logbooks
project_goal.rationale: Digitizes shift handover to improve operational continuity and anomaly traceability

target_users.value: Shift operators logging production data, shift supervisors reviewing and acknowledging handovers, mill managers reviewing KPI dashboards
target_users.rationale: Three role tiers with different portal views and permissions

project_class.rationale: Vue 3 web portal with Go API backend and PostgreSQL persistence

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, public_api
capabilities.rationale: Operator portal, reporting API, SCADA data ingestion, and KPI analytics

complexity_level.rationale: SCADA integration and multi-shift analytics add complexity beyond standard CRUD

risk_level.rationale: Internal-only app; ISO 45001 incident log integrity is operationally critical

data_sensitivity.rationale: Production KPIs and shift logs are internal operational data

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible only on mill intranet; no external internet exposure

access_model.value: RBAC with operator, supervisor, and manager roles via Keycloak groups; supervisor acknowledgment required before handover is finalized
access_model.rationale: Handover acknowledgment creates accountability chain for operational safety

feature_scope.value: Structured shift handover form with production counts, equipment anomalies, pending action items, safety observations; incoming supervisor acknowledgment workflow; SCADA-populated KPI dashboard with tons/hour, yield, downtime minutes; cross-shift trend analytics; REST API for third-party integrations
feature_scope.rationale: Full shift management feature set replacing paper logbooks

mvp_scope.value: Shift handover form submission, supervisor acknowledgment, basic KPI display from SCADA pull
mvp_scope.rationale: Core handover workflow validated before analytics and trend features

security_baseline.value: Keycloak SSO with LDAP directory integration, HTTPS on intranet with self-signed internal CA, RBAC role enforcement at API level
security_baseline.rationale: Internal network controls sufficient; Keycloak integrates with existing mill LDAP

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript and Composition API
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in Vue; Composition API suits complex form state

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin on on-premises Kubernetes
backend_stack.rationale: Low latency for SCADA data ingestion; team Go expertise on existing k8s cluster

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on on-premises bare-metal Kubernetes
data_platform.rationale: Existing on-prem PostgreSQL operational expertise; no cloud dependency

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes cluster on-premises at steel mill
hosting_target.rationale: No internet connectivity from production network; air-gapped deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: Shift handover records retained 3 years for operational audit, incident logs retained 5 years per ISO 45001
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: ISO 45001 occupational health and safety incident record requirements

future_scope.value: Predictive downtime alerting from SCADA anomaly patterns, integration with maintenance work order system
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 after analytics layer is stable

constraints.value: Must deploy entirely on-premises with no internet dependency; SCADA integration via OPC-UA or REST pull; existing Keycloak LDAP integration required
constraints.rationale: Production network is air-gapped; SCADA protocol is OPC-UA in plant

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards with Prometheus metrics from Go services and Kubernetes, structured logs to Loki
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing Grafana/Prometheus stack on on-premises Kubernetes

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; air-gapped image delivery via internal Harbor registry; offline GitLab CI on-premises
execution_preference.rationale: Air-gapped network requires all tooling to run on-premises

project.goal: Replace paper shift logbooks with digital handover and KPI portal
project.target_users: Steel mill operators, supervisors, and managers
project.timeline: MVP 10 weeks, full analytics release 18 weeks

frontend.state: Pinia for shift form state, Vue Query for SCADA KPI polling

backend.framework: Gin with middleware chain for JWT validation and RBAC

security.auth: Keycloak with LDAP user federation, OIDC tokens for API
security.authorization: Go middleware enforces role claims from JWT

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Kubernetes with Zalando Postgres Operator
data.cache: Redis 7 for SCADA snapshot cache and session tokens

devops.environments: dev namespace, staging namespace, production namespace

open_questions.scada_gateway: Whether to use existing OPC-UA to REST bridge or implement Go OPC-UA client directly

reasoner_reviews.product: The supervisor acknowledgment workflow is the most safety-critical feature: no incoming supervisor should assume operational control without explicitly reviewing the outgoing shift log. The KPI dashboard value depends entirely on SCADA data quality; stale or missing SCADA data must be surfaced clearly to operators rather than shown as zeros.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Gin is appropriate for the SCADA polling service given its low memory footprint on constrained on-premises hardware. The SCADA integration should be a separate Kubernetes Deployment (scada-poller) that writes to PostgreSQL independently of the main API, allowing the portal to remain available during SCADA connectivity interruptions. Zalando Postgres Operator handles PostgreSQL HA on Kubernetes correctly for on-premises deployments.
reasoner_reviews.security: Keycloak LDAP federation means user provisioning is controlled by the existing mill AD. JWT validation in Go middleware should verify the Keycloak realm and client_id claims to prevent token reuse from other applications on the same Keycloak instance. The supervisor acknowledgment must be a write operation that records the acknowledging user ID and timestamp, not just a UI checkbox.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Air-gapped on-premises deployment means all container images must be pre-loaded into Harbor. GitLab CI must have access to Harbor and the Kubernetes API. SCADA OPC-UA pull frequency should be configurable per KPI metric; high-frequency metrics like tons/hour at 1-minute resolution, slower metrics like weekly yield at hourly resolution.

backend.service_design: Two Go services: shift-api (Gin, handles handover CRUD, acknowledgment, action items, REST API) and scada-poller (separate Deployment, OPC-UA client pulling SCADA metrics on configurable intervals, writing to production_counts and kpi_metrics tables). Shared Redis 7 for SCADA snapshot cache and Keycloak session token validation cache.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/v1/shifts starts new shift handover form. POST /api/v1/shifts/{id}/submit finalizes outgoing shift log. POST /api/v1/shifts/{id}/acknowledge records supervisor acknowledgment with user ID and timestamp. GET /api/v1/kpis?metric=tons_per_hour&from={ts}&to={ts} returns time-series KPI data. GET /api/v1/shifts?date={date}&area={area} lists shift history. POST /api/v1/anomalies creates equipment anomaly log entry.
backend.failure_handling: SCADA poller writes last successful pull timestamp to Redis; KPI endpoint surfaces 'data as of {ts}' label when cache is stale. Shift API returns 503 with retry header if PostgreSQL is unavailable during Zalando operator failover.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with view-based routing: /shifts (list), /shifts/new (handover form), /shifts/:id (detail and acknowledgment), /kpis (dashboard), /anomalies (log), /admin (role management). Pinia stores for shift form draft state with localStorage persistence for browser refresh recovery.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Shift Handover Form: tabbed sections for production counts (auto-populated from SCADA), equipment anomalies (text + severity), pending action items (assignee + due date), safety observations (freetext + attachment). Supervisor Acknowledgment: read-only review of incoming handover with explicit Acknowledge button triggering POST to API. KPI Dashboard: line charts from Vue Query polling SCADA KPI endpoint every 60 seconds.
frontend.performance_notes: KPI dashboard charts use ECharts for on-premises deployment without CDN dependency. Vue Query polling interval configurable per metric to avoid overloading SCADA poller on legacy hardware.

security.auth_design: Keycloak OIDC with LDAP user federation. Vue SPA uses Keycloak JS adapter for silent token refresh. Go middleware validates JWT with Keycloak JWKS endpoint (cached locally) and extracts role claims.
security.authorization_model: Gin middleware enforces role claims: operator can submit own shifts; supervisor can acknowledge any shift in their area; manager has read-only access to all areas plus KPI export.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All shift submissions and acknowledgments written to shift_audit_log table with user ID, IP address, and timestamp. Action item status changes logged. Structured Go logs to Loki via promtail sidecar.

data.entities: shifts (id UUID, area_id FK, shift_type ENUM day/evening/night, operator_id, supervisor_id, status ENUM in_progress/submitted/acknowledged, submitted_at, acknowledged_at), handover_reports (id UUID, shift_id FK, production_count_actual INT, production_count_target INT, yield_pct DECIMAL, notes_freetext), equipment_anomalies (id UUID, shift_id FK, equipment_tag, severity ENUM low/medium/high/critical, description, resolved BOOL), action_items (id UUID, shift_id FK, description, assigned_to_id, due_date, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed), kpi_metrics (id UUID, area_id FK, metric_name, value DECIMAL, unit, recorded_at TIMESTAMP), production_counts (id UUID, area_id FK, line_id, tons_this_hour DECIMAL, scada_source_tag, recorded_at TIMESTAMP).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 via Zalando Postgres Operator with primary and one standby replica. Redis 7 for SCADA snapshot cache with 2-minute TTL per metric. Kubernetes PVC with local-path provisioner for PostgreSQL data volumes.
data.retention_and_deletion: Shift handover records and handover_reports retained 3 years. equipment_anomalies and action_items retained 5 years per ISO 45001. kpi_metrics partitioned by month, partitions older than 2 years moved to cold PostgreSQL tablespace on slower storage.

devops.deployment_topology: On-premises bare-metal Kubernetes: shift-api Deployment (3 replicas), scada-poller Deployment (1 replica, singleton), PostgreSQL cluster via Zalando Operator (1 primary + 1 standby), Redis standalone, Keycloak StatefulSet. Separate dev/staging/production namespaces.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI on-premises: Go lint and go test pipeline stage, Vue ESLint and vitest stage, Docker build and push to internal Harbor registry, Helm chart upgrade to staging namespace, manual approval for production rollout.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Go /metrics endpoint from shift-api and scada-poller. Grafana dashboards: shift submission rate, SCADA data freshness per area, acknowledgment lag (time from shift submission to acknowledgment), anomaly open count by severity. Loki for structured JSON logs via promtail sidecar.

ISS-001.title: SCADA poller not decoupled from shift API in round 1 design
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan had a single Go service handling both shift CRUD and SCADA polling, creating a single point of failure where SCADA timeouts blocked shift form submissions.

history.note: Monolithic Go service mixing concerns

ISS-002.title: KPI dashboard shows zeros when SCADA data is stale with no user indication
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan did not address how the UI distinguishes between a genuine zero production value and a missing/stale SCADA reading, leading to operator confusion.

history.note: KPI dashboard section did not address data staleness

ISS-003.title: Supervisor acknowledgment not persisted as immutable audit record
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan described acknowledgment as a status update on the shift record only. ISO 45001 requires the acknowledgment to be a separate immutable event with acknowledger identity and timestamp for incident investigation.

history.note: Acknowledgment was a flag not an audit record

focus_issues.title: KPI dashboard shows zeros on stale SCADA data with no user indication
focus_issues.detail: UI must distinguish genuine zero production from missing SCADA data; display data-as-of timestamp and staleness warning banner.

focus_issues.title: Supervisor acknowledgment not persisted as immutable audit record
focus_issues.detail: Add shift_acknowledgments table with acknowledger_id, acknowledged_at, and shift_id; acknowledgment endpoint inserts new row, never mutates shift record status alone.

latest_recommendations: Add SCADA staleness indicator to KPI dashboard: display last_refreshed_at per metric and show warning banner when data is older than 5 minutes
Create shift_acknowledgments table as an append-only audit log; POST /api/v1/shifts/{id}/acknowledge inserts a row with acknowledger_id and timestamp instead of mutating shift.status
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Steel Mill Shift Handover Portal v1

convergence_state.reason: Two medium issues unresolved; SCADA staleness and acknowledgment audit model both affect operational safety

previous_audits.summary: Good overall structure; SCADA decoupling was a critical gap now resolved. Two medium issues remain: KPI staleness surfacing and acknowledgment audit record integrity.
strengths: Zalando Postgres Operator choice is correct for on-premises Kubernetes HA
Go scada-poller as separate Deployment correctly decoupled in this round
Keycloak LDAP federation fits existing mill AD
concerns: KPI dashboard zeros are ambiguous without SCADA data freshness indicators
Shift acknowledgment as a status flag is not an immutable audit record as ISO 45001 requires
recommendations: Store last_refreshed_at per kpi_metric in Redis; KPI API returns this alongside the value; Vue KPI dashboard renders stale indicator banner when age exceeds 5 minutes
Add shift_acknowledgments table: id, shift_id FK, acknowledger_id, acknowledged_at, notes. POST /acknowledge inserts a row; shift status update is derived not primary.

issue_updates.title: SCADA poller not decoupled
issue_updates.detail: Separate scada-poller Deployment added in round 2

issue_updates.title: KPI staleness not surfaced
issue_updates.detail: Dashboard section still does not address stale data display
