best_plan.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA with Go Gin API and SCADA polling service on bare-metal Kubernetes. PostgreSQL 16 for shift data. Keycloak LDAP for authentication.
best_plan.data_model: shifts, handover_reports, equipment_anomalies, action_items, kpi_metrics. Acknowledgment stored as status flag on shift record.
best_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI with Harbor registry. Go lint, Vue ESLint, Helm chart deploy to Kubernetes namespaces. Grafana/Prometheus monitoring.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revision round 2 for steel mill shift handover portal, addressing SCADA data staleness surfacing and supervisor acknowledgment audit record integrity. The KPI API now returns last_refreshed_at per metric from Redis and the Vue dashboard renders a staleness warning banner for readings older than 5 minutes. The shift_acknowledgments table replaces the status-flag acknowledgment approach with an append-only audit record capturing acknowledger identity and timestamp for ISO 45001 compliance.

fix_report.action_taken: scada-poller now writes last_refreshed_at timestamp alongside each metric value in Redis. KPI API GET /api/v1/kpis response includes data_as_of ISO timestamp per metric. Vue KPI dashboard renders amber staleness banner and grays metric values when data_as_of is older than 5 minutes, distinguishing stale readings from genuine zero production.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Operators see clear staleness indicator preventing misinterpretation of stale zero values as real production stops

fix_report.action_taken: shift_acknowledgments table added as append-only audit record with shift_id FK, acknowledger_id, acknowledged_at, notes. POST /api/v1/shifts/{id}/acknowledge inserts new row; shift.acknowledged_at is derived from shift_acknowledgments.min(acknowledged_at) for display only. ISO 45001 incident investigation can query shift_acknowledgments directly.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Supervisor acknowledgment creates an immutable timestamped record with acknowledger identity meeting ISO 45001 audit requirements

target_output.title: Steel Mill Shift Handover and Production Reporting Portal
target_output.executive_summary: A Vue 3 and Go Gin web application for steel mill operators and supervisors to conduct structured shift handovers, log equipment anomalies, and review SCADA-populated KPI dashboards, deployed entirely on-premises on bare-metal Kubernetes. Outgoing operators complete tabbed handover forms covering production counts auto-populated from SCADA, equipment anomaly entries, and pending action items; incoming supervisors review and acknowledge the handover via an immutable acknowledgment audit record. KPI dashboards display tons per hour, yield, and downtime with explicit SCADA data freshness indicators.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two Go services on on-premises Kubernetes: shift-api (Gin, handles handover form CRUD, supervisor acknowledgment, action items, REST API for analytics consumers) and scada-poller (singleton Deployment, OPC-UA client pulling SCADA metrics on configurable intervals, writing to PostgreSQL and Redis snapshot cache). Vue 3 SPA served as Nginx static Deployment behind internal Kubernetes Ingress. Keycloak StatefulSet with LDAP federation authenticates all users; Go JWT middleware enforces RBAC claims on each route. PostgreSQL 16 via Zalando Postgres Operator provides primary-standby HA. Redis 7 holds SCADA metric snapshots with last_refreshed_at metadata. Grafana/Prometheus/Loki stack already running on the cluster collects all service observability.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3 with TypeScript, Composition API, Pinia, Vue Query, Tailwind CSS, ECharts for charts (no CDN, bundled). Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL, go-redis/v9. SCADA Integration: OPC-UA Go client (gopcua/opcua) in scada-poller. Auth: Keycloak with LDAP, OIDC tokens. Data: PostgreSQL 16 via Zalando Postgres Operator. Cache: Redis 7 standalone. Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, Loki, promtail. CI/CD: GitLab CI on-premises with Harbor container registry. Deployment: Helm charts on bare-metal Kubernetes.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Shift Handover Form: tabbed sections for production counts (auto-populated from SCADA, editable override), equipment anomalies (equipment_tag, severity, description), pending action items (assignee, due date, carry-forward from previous shift), safety observations (freetext, photo attachment). Supervisor Acknowledgment: read-only handover review with explicit Acknowledge button creating immutable shift_acknowledgments record. KPI Dashboard: real-time line charts for tons/hour, yield percentage, downtime minutes per area; SCADA data freshness indicator showing last_refreshed_at and amber staleness banner when data age exceeds 5 minutes. Anomaly Log: filterable list of equipment anomalies across shifts by area, severity, and date range. Action Item Tracker: cross-shift action item list with open/in_progress/closed status and overdue highlighting. Public REST API for analytics system consumers.
target_output.system_components: Vue 3 SPA served from Nginx Deployment behind Kubernetes Ingress with internal CA TLS termination. shift-api Go Gin service (3 replicas) with HPA on CPU 70%. scada-poller Go service (1 replica, singleton via leader election or Deployment with replicas=1). PostgreSQL 16 cluster via Zalando Postgres Operator: 1 primary + 1 standby with synchronous replication. Redis 7 standalone Deployment for SCADA snapshot cache and Keycloak session token cache. Keycloak StatefulSet with LDAP user federation to mill Active Directory. Prometheus scraping shift-api and scada-poller /metrics. Grafana for operational dashboards and Loki for log aggregation via promtail sidecars. Harbor internal container registry for air-gapped image management.
target_output.workflows: Shift Handover Submission: outgoing operator opens /shifts/new, reviews SCADA-populated production counts for their area (scada-poller has written latest values to PostgreSQL and Redis within the last polling interval), adds equipment anomaly entries and action items, attaches safety observations, and submits handover form via POST /api/v1/shifts/{id}/submit. Incoming supervisor opens shift notification, reviews all sections on read-only handover detail page, clicks Acknowledge which calls POST /api/v1/shifts/{id}/acknowledge, inserting a shift_acknowledgments row with acknowledger_id from JWT sub claim, current timestamp, and optional notes. Shift transitions to acknowledged status derived from shift_acknowledgments existence. KPI Dashboard Refresh: Vue Query polls GET /api/v1/kpis?metric=tons_per_hour&area={id}&from={ts} every 60 seconds; API returns value and data_as_of timestamp from Redis snapshot; Vue component computes age delta; if age exceeds 300 seconds, dashboard renders amber SCADA DATA STALE banner with last known timestamp rather than showing potentially misleading zeros.
target_output.data_model: shifts (id UUID PK, area_id FK, shift_type ENUM day/evening/night, operator_id UUID, status ENUM in_progress/submitted/acknowledged, submitted_at, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMP -- derived from shift_acknowledgments). handover_reports (id UUID PK, shift_id FK, production_count_actual INT, production_count_target INT, production_count_scada_populated BOOL, yield_pct DECIMAL, notes_freetext TEXT). equipment_anomalies (id UUID PK, shift_id FK, equipment_tag VARCHAR, severity ENUM low/medium/high/critical, description TEXT, resolved BOOL, resolved_at TIMESTAMP). action_items (id UUID PK, shift_id FK, description TEXT, assigned_to_id UUID, due_date DATE, carried_forward_from_shift_id UUID, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed). shift_acknowledgments (id UUID PK, shift_id FK, acknowledger_id UUID, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, notes TEXT) -- append-only, no updates. kpi_metrics (id UUID PK, area_id FK, metric_name VARCHAR, value DECIMAL, unit VARCHAR, scada_source_tag VARCHAR, recorded_at TIMESTAMP) partitioned by month. production_counts (id UUID PK, area_id FK, line_id UUID, tons_this_hour DECIMAL, scada_source_tag VARCHAR, recorded_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: RESTful JSON at /api/v1. POST /api/v1/shifts creates new shift handover for current operator. POST /api/v1/shifts/{id}/submit finalizes outgoing log and notifies incoming supervisor. POST /api/v1/shifts/{id}/acknowledge inserts shift_acknowledgments row (supervisor role required). GET /api/v1/shifts?area={id}&date={date} lists shifts for supervisor review. POST /api/v1/anomalies creates equipment anomaly entry linked to open shift. GET /api/v1/action-items?status=open&area={id} returns open action items for incoming supervisor briefing. GET /api/v1/kpis?metric={name}&area={id}&from={ts}&to={ts} returns time-series array with value and data_as_of per point. GET /api/v1/analytics/shifts/summary?from={ts}&to={ts} for manager export. All endpoints require Keycloak JWT Bearer token; role claims enforced in Gin middleware.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak 24 with LDAP user federation to mill Active Directory; operators and supervisors provisioned via AD group membership mapped to Keycloak roles. OIDC access tokens with 15-minute expiry; Vue Keycloak JS adapter performs silent refresh. Go Gin middleware validates JWT signature against Keycloak JWKS endpoint (JWKS cached in-process with 1-hour refresh). Role enforcement: operator can POST shifts and anomalies for own area only; supervisor can acknowledge and read all shifts in assigned areas; manager read-only with export permission. shift_acknowledgments table has no UPDATE or DELETE grants; enforced via PostgreSQL role REVOKE. Structured audit log in Loki for all shift submissions and acknowledgments including user ID and IP. ISO 45001 incident log retention enforced by 5-year PostgreSQL partition retention policy. Internal CA TLS for all Kubernetes Ingress routes; no plaintext HTTP. All images pre-loaded into Harbor registry; no external registry pulls in air-gapped environment.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes cluster with namespaces: shift-handover-dev, shift-handover-staging, shift-handover-production. Helm chart per service with values files per namespace. GitLab CI on-premises: Go vet/staticcheck/go test, Vue ESLint/vitest, Docker build and push to Harbor, helm upgrade --install to staging namespace with readiness probe gate, manual approval job for production promotion. Zalando Postgres Operator manages PostgreSQL HA with synchronous replication to standby and daily pg_basebackup to NFS share. scada-poller deployed as single-replica Deployment; pod disruption budget prevents concurrent unavailability with shift-api. Redis standalone with AOF persistence. Keycloak StatefulSet with PostgreSQL backend using a separate Zalando cluster. All ConfigMaps and Secrets managed via Helm values with Sealed Secrets for sensitive fields. Rolling update strategy with maxSurge=1 for zero-downtime deployments.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes shift-api /metrics (request rate, latency histograms, PostgreSQL connection pool, acknowledgment lag gauge) and scada-poller /metrics (poll success rate, OPC-UA latency, last_successful_poll_timestamp per area). Grafana dashboards: shift submission rate by area, SCADA data freshness heatmap (green/amber/red per metric per area), acknowledgment lag histogram, equipment anomaly open count by severity. Loki aggregates structured JSON logs from shift-api and scada-poller via promtail sidecars; alert rules on scada-poller OPC-UA error rate >10% in 5-minute window and acknowledgment gap >45 minutes after shift submission.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premises hardware already provisioned; marginal cost is engineering time and additional Kubernetes worker capacity if needed. Current cluster handles 3 shift areas with 15 concurrent operators comfortably on 2 application nodes. Scaling to 10 areas requires HPA to scale shift-api beyond 3 replicas and PostgreSQL vertical sizing on primary node. kpi_metrics table partitioning by month prevents unbounded query scan growth as historical data accumulates.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Keycloak LDAP integration, shift handover form with production counts and anomaly entry, shift_acknowledgments table and acknowledgment endpoint, SCADA poller for tons/hour with Redis snapshot and staleness metadata, KPI dashboard with staleness banner, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Action item tracker with carry-forward from previous shift, equipment anomaly trend analytics, multi-area supervisor view, REST API for analytics consumers, Grafana operational dashboards for shift metrics. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Predictive downtime alert integration with SCADA anomaly patterns, mobile-responsive Vue layout for tablet use on plant floor, manager export reports for ISO 45001 audit, maintenance work order system integration link.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint (errcheck, staticcheck, gosec enabled). All PostgreSQL interactions via pgx/v5 with prepared statements; no raw string interpolation in queries. JWT middleware must verify both signature and realm claim to prevent cross-application token reuse. Go unit tests for shift state machine transitions and SCADA metric parsing at 80% coverage gate. Vue component tests with vitest for KPI staleness banner rendering logic covering edge cases (null value, age exactly at threshold). Air-gapped image builds: all base images pinned to digest in Dockerfile FROM lines and pre-loaded in Harbor. Helm chart changes require staging deployment validation before production merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OPC-UA connectivity interruption causes SCADA poller to stop updating Redis, leading to stale KPI data without operator awareness. Mitigation: staleness banner and last_refreshed_at display on dashboard; Prometheus alert on poller error rate. Risk: Zalando Postgres Operator failover during peak shift submission window causes brief API unavailability. Mitigation: Gin middleware returns 503 with Retry-After header; Vue Query retry logic retries submission up to 3 times with 5-second backoff. Tradeoff: Redis standalone over cluster chosen for operational simplicity on constrained on-premises hardware; Redis AOF persistence reduces data loss risk on pod restart. Tradeoff: shift_acknowledgments as append-only table over status flag adds a join on read but provides immutable audit record required for ISO 45001 compliance.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed OPC-UA Go client (gopcua/opcua) over building a REST bridge to avoid an additional service dependency. Resolved: scada-poller writes last_refreshed_at to Redis alongside metric value, enabling API to return data freshness metadata. Shift acknowledgment model changed from status flag to separate shift_acknowledgments table for audit immutability. SCADA polling frequency set to 1-minute intervals for tons/hour and downtime, 15-minute for yield, configurable per metric in ConfigMap.

metadata.notes: Steel mill shift handover portal revision 2 fixing SCADA staleness surfacing and ISO 45001 acknowledgment audit record; fully on-premises air-gapped deployment.

project_goal.value: ML pipeline to train and deploy computer vision models for automated surface defect detection on painted automotive body panels, classifying scratches, dents, and paint runs at end-of-line inspection using high-resolution line-scan cameras with sub-500ms inference latency
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual visual inspection with consistent automated detection to reduce escape rate

target_users.value: Quality engineers reviewing defect detection results, MLOps engineers managing model versions, line supervisors monitoring false positive rates and escape rates
target_users.rationale: Three operational roles interacting with pipeline outputs and model management

project_class.rationale: Core value is ML model training, versioning, and real-time inference rather than a standard CRUD application

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, analytics, batch_jobs, devops
capabilities.rationale: Inference API backend, image dataset storage, computer vision ML, detection analytics, weekly MLOps retraining batch, and GPU-enabled Kubernetes DevOps

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-500ms inference on GPU nodes, line-scan camera throughput of 200 panels/hour, weekly retraining pipeline on 100k+ image dataset

risk_level.rationale: Model escape rate directly impacts customer quality escapes; confidential OEM panel design images must not leave controlled environment

data_sensitivity.rationale: High-resolution body panel images reveal OEM panel geometry and surface finish specifications that are proprietary

external_exposure.rationale: Inference endpoint and training pipeline are internal only; no external access to model or image data

access_model.value: RBAC with mlops_engineer, quality_engineer, and line_supervisor roles; model promotion requires mlops_engineer approval
access_model.rationale: Model lifecycle operations must be gated behind MLOps engineer authorization

feature_scope.value: Real-time inference API serving YOLOv8 model for defect detection, weekly MLOps retraining pipeline with annotation ingestion, model registry with blue-green promotion, false positive review workflow, defect analytics dashboard, camera calibration management
feature_scope.rationale: Full MLOps lifecycle from data to production model management

mvp_scope.value: Inference API with current best model, basic defect result storage, false positive review queue
mvp_scope.rationale: Production inference must be running before retraining pipeline investment

security_baseline.value: Cognito for internal user auth, S3 bucket policies restricting image access to EKS node IAM roles only, VPC-only endpoints, KMS encryption for S3 image data at rest
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential image data must not be accessible from internet or non-authorized EKS workloads

frontend_stack.value: None — no browser frontend; defect results surfaced via API to line-side display terminals
frontend_stack.rationale: Line display terminals run their own HMI software consuming the defect result API

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI on EKS GPU node pools
backend_stack.rationale: PyTorch and Ultralytics YOLOv8 ecosystem requires Python; FastAPI for async inference API

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for inference results and annotations, S3 with KMS for image storage
data_platform.rationale: Document store fits variable-schema defect annotation objects; S3 for large image binary storage

hosting_target.value: AWS EKS with GPU node pools (g4dn.xlarge) for inference, CPU node pool for training orchestration
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Kubernetes with GPU node auto-scaling for inference load

privacy_retention_policy.value: Panel images retained 90 days for annotation review, archived to S3 Glacier after 90 days, deleted at 18 months; inference results retained 3 years for IATF traceability; model artifacts retained indefinitely
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: IATF 16949 quality record traceability and storage cost management

future_scope.value: Extension to weld seam inspection and glass fit inspection stations, active learning loop for annotation prioritization
future_scope.rationale: Other end-of-line stations identified for automation after surface defect pipeline is stable

constraints.value: Sub-500ms inference latency from image receipt to result, GPU node pool limited to 4 g4dn.xlarge nodes, all image data must remain in AWS us-east-1 VPC, retraining pipeline must complete within 6-hour weekly maintenance window
constraints.rationale: Line cycle time and OEM data sovereignty requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for FastAPI inference service, custom metrics for inference latency p99, false positive rate, model version in production, EKS GPU utilization
observability_baseline.rationale: Production ML quality metrics require domain-specific observability beyond standard APM

execution_preference.value: MLOps iteration cycles: inference MVP in 6 weeks, first retraining pipeline in 10 weeks, blue-green promotion in 14 weeks
execution_preference.rationale: Staged MLOps maturity progression

compliance_context.value: IATF 16949 quality management system traceability: each inference result must be traceable to panel VIN, inspection station, model version, and timestamp; OEM customer-specific requirements mandate audit trail for false positives reviewed and overridden
compliance_context.rationale: Automotive quality standard traceability requirements for end-of-line inspection records

llm_integration.value: YOLOv8 custom-trained computer vision model for defect classification (scratch, dent, paint_run, good); model served via TorchServe with batch inference disabled for latency compliance; not a generative LLM but listed as ai_llm capability for CV model training and serving
llm_integration.rationale: Custom CV model training and inference is the core technical capability of this ai_system

project.goal: Automated computer vision defect detection pipeline for painted automotive body panels at end-of-line inspection
project.target_users: Quality engineers, MLOps engineers, line supervisors
project.timeline: Inference MVP 6 weeks, full MLOps pipeline 14 weeks

backend.framework: FastAPI async with TorchServe model server

security.auth: Cognito for quality engineer and MLOps dashboards
security.storage: S3 VPC endpoint, KMS SSE-S3 for images

data.db: MongoDB 7 for inference results, annotations, model versions
data.storage: S3 with KMS for panel images
data.cache: Redis 7 for inference result cache and false positive queue

devops.hosting: EKS with GPU g4dn.xlarge node pool

open_questions.annotation_tool: Whether to use CVAT or Label Studio for defect annotation

reasoner_reviews.product: The false positive review workflow is critical for sustained production trust. If quality engineers cannot easily review and override false positives, they will disable the system. The defect analytics dashboard showing escape rate trends and false positive rate by defect class is needed for line supervisor confidence in the model.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: TorchServe batch inference disabled is correct for sub-500ms single-image latency. YOLOv8 on g4dn.xlarge (T4 GPU) achieves roughly 50-80ms per 4K image with CUDA acceleration, leaving headroom under the 500ms budget for network and preprocessing. The weekly retraining pipeline must use a separate CPU node pool to avoid evicting inference pods from GPU nodes. Canary model deployment is safer than full blue-green swap for production inference given recall sensitivity.
reasoner_reviews.security: S3 bucket policy should restrict GetObject to the EKS inference node IAM role only, not the broader account. KMS key policy should restrict Decrypt to the same IAM role. MongoDB Atlas or self-managed should be evaluated; self-managed on EKS avoids data leaving the VPC.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 6-hour retraining window with 100k images is feasible on a p3.2xlarge training node (V100 GPU) using mixed-precision training. If the dataset grows beyond 500k images, the window will need to be extended or data sampling introduced. Model artifacts must be versioned in S3 with immutable object versioning for IATF traceability.

backend.service_design: Three EKS Deployments: inference-api (FastAPI, async image preprocessing and TorchServe gRPC call, result persistence to MongoDB), torchserve-deployment (TorchServe serving YOLOv8 .pt model on GPU node via nodeSelector), retraining-orchestrator (Kubernetes CronJob triggering weekly MLOps pipeline: annotation pull, dataset assembly, training job submission, evaluation, model registry update).
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/v1/inspect accepts multipart image upload, returns defect_bounding_boxes JSON with class, confidence, and bounding box coordinates within 500ms. GET /api/v1/results/{panel_vin} returns full inspection history for a panel. POST /api/v1/false-positive-reviews/{result_id} records quality engineer override. GET /api/v1/models returns model registry with version, precision, recall, promoted_at. POST /api/v1/models/{version}/promote triggers blue-green model swap.
backend.failure_handling: TorchServe health probe in FastAPI middleware; if TorchServe returns 503, inference-api returns 503 to line terminal with retry-after 5 seconds. Failed MongoDB writes retry 3 times; if all fail, result written to Redis dead-letter list and alerting triggered.

data.entities: inference_results (panel_vin, inspection_station_id, model_version, defects: [{class, confidence, bbox_x, bbox_y, bbox_w, bbox_h}], overall_result ENUM pass/fail, processed_at, image_s3_key, reviewed_by, review_decision ENUM confirmed/false_positive, reviewed_at), defect_annotations (annotation_id, image_s3_key, annotator_id, defect_class ENUM scratch/dent/paint_run/good, bbox, annotation_source ENUM manual/model_assisted, created_at), model_versions (version_id, s3_artifact_key, precision, recall, f1, test_set_size, trained_at, promoted_at, retired_at), camera_calibrations (station_id, calibration_matrix_json, distortion_coeffs_json, calibrated_at, calibrated_by).
data.storage_design: MongoDB 7 self-managed on EKS for inference_results, defect_annotations, model_versions, camera_calibrations. S3 with KMS SSE-S3 for panel images (primary) and model artifacts. Redis 7 for recent inference result cache (panel_vin key, 10-minute TTL) and false positive review queue.
data.retention_and_deletion: Panel images: 90 days in S3 Standard, transition to Glacier at 90 days, expire at 18 months via S3 lifecycle. Inference results: 3 years in MongoDB. Model artifacts: indefinite retention in S3 with versioning enabled.

devops.deployment_topology: EKS cluster in us-east-1 with two node groups: gpu-nodes (g4dn.xlarge, min 2 max 4, for inference-api and torchserve pods via nodeSelector gpu=true) and cpu-nodes (m5.2xlarge, min 2 max 6, for retraining CronJob and MongoDB). VPC-only S3 and ECR endpoints. No public subnets for workload nodes.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: push to main runs pytest, mypy, Bandit SAST, builds Docker image, pushes to ECR. Model promotion is manual: MLOps engineer triggers workflow_dispatch action that runs canary deployment to 20% of inference traffic, evaluates live precision/recall for 30 minutes, then full promotion or automatic rollback.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on FastAPI. Custom Datadog metrics via DogStatsD: inference_latency_p99_ms (target <500), false_positive_rate_7d (target <5%), escape_rate_7d, model_precision, model_recall, gpu_utilization_pct. PagerDuty alert: p99 latency >500ms, false positive rate spike >10% in 1-hour window.
devops.cost_controls: GPU node group scales to 0 during planned production shutdowns (weekends). Spot instances for CPU retraining jobs. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for image archive after 30 days.

security.auth_design: Cognito User Pool for quality engineer and MLOps engineer portal access. Line terminal cameras authenticate via IAM role attached to on-premises gateway EC2 instance using STS AssumeRole.
security.secrets_management: MongoDB credentials in AWS Secrets Manager. TorchServe model server config in EKS Secrets. KMS key policy restricts Decrypt to inference-api EKS pod IAM role via IRSA.
security.security_risks: Risk: panel image data exfiltration if S3 bucket policy is misconfigured. Mitigation: S3 bucket policy with explicit Deny for s3:GetObject unless aws:SourceVpc matches VPC ID; CloudTrail S3 data events enabled for all bucket access.

ISS-001.title: TorchServe cold-start latency violates sub-500ms requirement
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1-2 did not address model warm-up; TorchServe cold-start on g4dn.xlarge is 3-8 seconds, violating the line latency budget.

history.note: Pre-warmed TorchServe pod added with startup readiness probe and min replicas=1

ISS-002.title: Retraining pipeline evaluation gates not defined
ISS-002.detail: Rounds 1-3 plan describes a weekly retraining pipeline but does not specify the precision/recall thresholds that must be met before model promotion is permitted. Without gates, a degraded model could replace production.

ISS-003.title: IATF 16949 traceability link between inference result and panel VIN not enforced
ISS-003.detail: Inference API accepts image without mandatory panel_vin parameter; results stored without VIN link break IATF audit trail requirement.

ISS-004.title: Camera calibration drift not detected between weekly maintenance cycles
ISS-004.detail: Calibration drift between scheduled recalibrations can cause systematic false positives on one station without detection until next maintenance window.

focus_issues.title: Retraining pipeline evaluation gates not defined
focus_issues.detail: Specify minimum precision and recall thresholds per defect class required for model promotion; blocking gate prevents degraded model from replacing production.

focus_issues.title: IATF traceability link between inference result and panel VIN not enforced
focus_issues.detail: panel_vin must be a required field in the inference API request; MongoDB schema must enforce its presence.

latest_recommendations: Define per-class precision/recall gates in model_versions collection: minimum scratch_precision=0.90, dent_recall=0.88, paint_run_precision=0.85; retraining pipeline fails promotion if any gate is not met
Make panel_vin a required field in POST /api/v1/inspect request body with 400 response if absent; add panel_vin as required indexed field in MongoDB inference_results collection
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Automotive Body Panel Defect Detection Pipeline v3

convergence_state.reason: ISS-002 high-severity has persisted 3 rounds without resolution; score trending down from 7.1 to 6.8

previous_audits.summary: TorchServe cold-start addressed. Retraining evaluation gates and IATF VIN traceability remain undefined. Camera calibration drift added as low issue.

MongoDB schema for inference results is domain-appropriate
concerns: No quantitative gate for model promotion decision
panel_vin optional in inference API breaks IATF traceability
recommendations: Define per-class precision/recall thresholds in model registry as promotion gates
Enforce panel_vin as required parameter in inference request

POST /api/v1/inspect must return HTTP 400 if panel_vin is absent or null; MongoDB index on panel_vin ensures fast VIN-based audit query
