previous_plan.title: Automotive Body Panel Defect Detection Pipeline v3
previous_plan.executive_summary: YOLOv8 inference pipeline on EKS GPU nodes with FastAPI, weekly MLOps retraining CronJob, MongoDB inference results, and S3 image storage with KMS encryption.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI inference-api calls TorchServe gRPC on GPU nodes. Pre-warm readiness probe ensures TorchServe is ready before pod accepts traffic. Weekly retraining CronJob assembles annotated dataset, trains YOLOv8, writes model artifact to S3. Manual promotion workflow triggers canary swap.
previous_plan.api_design: POST /api/v1/inspect accepts image, returns defect bounding boxes. GET /api/v1/results/{panel_vin}. panel_vin is optional in current design.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: EKS with gpu-nodes and cpu-nodes node groups. GitHub Actions CI. Datadog APM with custom inference latency metrics.

best_plan.title: Automotive Body Panel Defect Detection Pipeline v3
best_plan.executive_summary: YOLOv8 inference pipeline on EKS GPU nodes with FastAPI, weekly MLOps retraining CronJob, MongoDB inference results, and S3 image storage with KMS encryption.
best_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI inference-api calls TorchServe gRPC on GPU nodes. Pre-warm readiness probe ensures TorchServe is ready before pod accepts traffic. Weekly retraining CronJob assembles annotated dataset, trains YOLOv8, writes model artifact to S3. Manual promotion workflow triggers canary swap.
best_plan.api_design: POST /api/v1/inspect accepts image, returns defect bounding boxes. GET /api/v1/results/{panel_vin}. panel_vin is optional in current design.
best_plan.deployment_and_operations: EKS with gpu-nodes and cpu-nodes node groups. GitHub Actions CI. Datadog APM with custom inference latency metrics.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 revision addressing the two persistent issues: ISS-002 (evaluation gates for model promotion) and ISS-003 (panel_vin enforcement). Adding explicit per-class precision/recall thresholds to the model_versions collection and retraining pipeline evaluation step. Making panel_vin a required field in the inference API with HTTP 400 enforcement and MongoDB index for IATF VIN-based audit queries.

fix_report.action_taken: model_versions collection updated with quality_gates subdocument: {scratch_precision_min: 0.90, dent_recall_min: 0.88, paint_run_precision_min: 0.85, overall_f1_min: 0.87}. Retraining CronJob evaluation step evaluates new model against 2000-image golden test set; if any gate fails, job writes failed status to model_versions, sends PagerDuty alert, and does not proceed to canary promotion. Promotion workflow reads quality_gates pass status before permitting canary traffic split.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Degraded model cannot replace production model; MLOps engineer receives alert with per-class evaluation scores for manual investigation

fix_report.action_taken: POST /api/v1/inspect Pydantic request model updated: panel_vin is required str field with VIN17 regex validator; returns HTTP 422 if absent or malformed. MongoDB inference_results collection enforces panel_vin as required field via schema validation. Index added on panel_vin for fast GET /api/v1/results/{panel_vin} IATF audit queries.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every inspection record is linkable to a specific vehicle VIN for IATF 16949 traceability audit; no orphaned inference results

target_output.title: Automotive Body Panel Defect Detection ML Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A computer vision ML pipeline deploying YOLOv8 on AWS EKS GPU nodes to detect scratches, dents, and paint runs on automotive body panels at end-of-line inspection. FastAPI inference-api receives line-scan camera images with mandatory panel VIN, calls TorchServe on pre-warmed GPU pods, and returns defect bounding boxes with class and confidence within 500ms. A weekly Kubernetes CronJob retrains the model on newly annotated images, evaluates per-class precision/recall against quality gates, and promotes via canary deployment only when all gates pass. Inference results are stored in MongoDB with panel VIN linkage for IATF 16949 traceability.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI inference-api Deployment on EKS GPU nodes receives images from line-side camera gateways, preprocesses for lens distortion correction, and calls TorchServe gRPC (also on GPU nodes, pre-warmed with readiness probe). Defect bounding boxes are persisted to MongoDB with panel VIN and model version for IATF traceability. A weekly Kubernetes CronJob orchestrates retraining: pulls newly confirmed annotations from MongoDB, assembles augmented dataset on EFS, submits PyTorch training job to CPU training node, evaluates against 2000-image golden test set for per-class quality gates, writes model artifact to S3 with version metadata, then triggers canary promotion workflow via GitHub Actions if all gates pass. Quality engineers access false positive review queue via FastAPI endpoints. All workloads run in VPC-only subnets with S3 and ECR VPC endpoints; no public internet egress for image data.
target_output.technology_stack: Model: YOLOv8 (Ultralytics) custom-trained for scratch/dent/paint_run/good classification. Serving: TorchServe on g4dn.xlarge EKS GPU nodes with gRPC handler. Inference API: Python 3.12 FastAPI with Pydantic v2 async. Image preprocessing: OpenCV for lens distortion correction and JPEG decode. Data: MongoDB 7 self-managed on EKS for inference results, annotations, and model versions. Storage: S3 with KMS SSE-S3 for panel images and model artifacts. Cache: Redis 7 for inference result cache and false positive review queue. Annotation: Label Studio on EKS CPU node. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with ECR push and EKS kubectl apply. Monitoring: Datadog APM, DogStatsD custom metrics, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Real-time Inference: line-scan camera gateway sends POST /api/v1/inspect with panel image and VIN; inference-api preprocesses, runs TorchServe YOLOv8, returns defect list with class, confidence, bounding box coordinates within 500ms; result stored in MongoDB. False Positive Review: quality engineer browses inference_results flagged as fail, views annotated image with bounding box overlay, marks individual detections as confirmed or false_positive; overrides stored for retraining annotation ingestion. Weekly Retraining: CronJob pulls annotations added since last training run, augments dataset, trains YOLOv8 on CPU training node, evaluates per-class quality gates, promotes to canary if gates pass, full promotion after 30-minute canary evaluation. Model Registry: model_versions collection tracks version, artifact S3 key, per-class precision/recall, quality_gates evaluation result, promotion and retirement timestamps. Camera Calibration Management: MLOps engineers upload new calibration matrices per station; inference-api loads calibration from MongoDB on startup and applies distortion correction.
target_output.system_components: EKS GPU node group (g4dn.xlarge, min 2 max 4): inference-api Deployment (2 replicas, HPA on custom inference queue depth), TorchServe Deployment (2 replicas with GPU nodeSelector, pre-warmed, readiness probe on model health endpoint). EKS CPU node group (m5.2xlarge): retraining-orchestrator CronJob, MongoDB StatefulSet (primary + 1 replica), Label Studio Deployment, Redis 7 StatefulSet. S3 buckets: panel-images-bucket (KMS encrypted, VPC endpoint only, lifecycle to Glacier at 90 days), model-artifacts-bucket (versioning enabled, immutable via S3 Object Lock on promoted artifacts). EFS for training dataset assembly. Cognito User Pool for quality engineer and MLOps engineer portal access. GitHub Actions for CI and model promotion workflow dispatch.
target_output.workflows: Defect Inspection: camera gateway at end-of-line station captures 4096x1024 pixel JPEG image of panel, sends POST /api/v1/inspect with multipart image and panel_vin (VIN17 required). FastAPI validates VIN format (Pydantic regex), applies OpenCV distortion correction using station calibration matrix loaded from MongoDB, sends preprocessed image to TorchServe via gRPC. TorchServe returns YOLOv8 inference result: list of detected objects with class ENUM scratch/dent/paint_run, confidence float, and bounding box XYWH coordinates. inference-api writes inference_result document to MongoDB with panel_vin, inspection_station_id, model_version, defects array, overall_result ENUM pass/fail, processed_at timestamp, and image_s3_key. Response returns to camera gateway within 500ms budget. Weekly Model Retraining: CronJob triggers Saturday 02:00 UTC. Step 1: pull defect_annotations created since last training run from MongoDB. Step 2: assemble Ultralytics YOLOv8 dataset YAML on EFS with augmentation (horizontal flip, brightness jitter, mosaic). Step 3: submit training job to CPU training pod (YOLOv8l, 100 epochs, mixed precision). Step 4: evaluate trained model against golden test set of 2000 labeled images; compute per-class precision and recall. Step 5: compare against quality_gates thresholds in current model_versions document. If any gate fails, write evaluation_failed status, send PagerDuty alert with per-class scores, halt pipeline. If all gates pass, write new model_versions document with artifact S3 key and evaluation scores. Step 6: trigger GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch for canary deployment (20% traffic split for 30 minutes). Step 7: automated canary evaluation compares live false_positive_rate against baseline; if within 2% delta, promote to 100%; otherwise rollback to previous model version.
target_output.data_model: inference_results (MongoDB collection): {panel_vin: str required VIN17, inspection_station_id: str, model_version: str, defects: [{class: ENUM scratch/dent/paint_run/good, confidence: float, bbox_x: float, bbox_y: float, bbox_w: float, bbox_h: float}], overall_result: ENUM pass/fail, processed_at: ISODate, image_s3_key: str, reviewed_by: str nullable, review_decision: ENUM confirmed/false_positive nullable, reviewed_at: ISODate nullable}. Index: panel_vin (for IATF VIN audit), processed_at (for time-range queries). defect_annotations: {image_s3_key: str, panel_vin: str, annotator_id: str, defect_class: ENUM, bbox: XYWH object, annotation_source: ENUM manual/model_assisted, created_at: ISODate}. model_versions: {version_id: str, s3_artifact_key: str, precision_by_class: {scratch: float, dent: float, paint_run: float}, recall_by_class: {scratch: float, dent: float, paint_run: float}, quality_gates: {scratch_precision_min: 0.90, dent_recall_min: 0.88, paint_run_precision_min: 0.85, overall_f1_min: 0.87}, gates_passed: bool, trained_at: ISODate, promoted_at: ISODate nullable, retired_at: ISODate nullable}. camera_calibrations: {station_id: str UNIQUE, calibration_matrix_json: str, distortion_coeffs_json: str, calibrated_at: ISODate, calibrated_by: str}.
target_output.api_design: All endpoints VPC-internal only; no public ALB. POST /api/v1/inspect: multipart/form-data with image file and panel_vin (required, VIN17 regex validated, HTTP 422 if absent or malformed); returns {result_id, panel_vin, defects: [], overall_result, model_version, processed_at} within 500ms SLA. GET /api/v1/results/{panel_vin}: returns all inspection records for VIN for IATF audit queries; indexed on panel_vin. POST /api/v1/false-positive-reviews/{result_id}: quality engineer marks specific defect bounding boxes as false_positive; stores reviewer Cognito sub and timestamp. GET /api/v1/models: lists model_versions with evaluation scores and gates_passed status. POST /api/v1/models/{version_id}/promote: MLOps engineer triggers canary promotion workflow; validates gates_passed=true before accepting. GET /api/v1/calibrations/{station_id}: returns current calibration matrix for station. PUT /api/v1/calibrations/{station_id}: MLOps engineer uploads new calibration matrix JSON.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito User Pool for quality engineer and MLOps engineer API access; JWT validated in FastAPI dependency. Camera gateway authenticates via IAM role attached to on-premises gateway EC2 instance using IRSA-equivalent STS AssumeRole for inference endpoint. S3 bucket policy: explicit Deny s3:GetObject unless aws:SourceVpc equals VPC ID; inference-api pod IAM role via IRSA has s3:PutObject to panel-images-bucket and s3:GetObject to model-artifacts-bucket. KMS key policy restricts Decrypt to inference-api IRSA role only. MongoDB authentication via Secrets Manager credential injection; TLS between FastAPI and MongoDB via mongo+srv URI. IATF 16949 traceability: panel_vin enforced as required field in POST /api/v1/inspect with VIN17 regex validation; MongoDB collection-level schema validation enforces panel_vin required; inference results permanently linked to VIN, model version, inspection station, and timestamp. False positive review overrides stored with reviewer identity and timestamp for OEM customer audit trail. CloudTrail S3 data events enabled on panel-images-bucket for all access logging.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: EKS cluster in us-east-1: gpu-nodes (g4dn.xlarge, min 2 max 4 with Cluster Autoscaler, Karpenter or CAS), cpu-nodes (m5.2xlarge, min 2 max 6). TorchServe Deployment: nodeSelector gpu=true, resources.limits.nvidia.com/gpu=1, startup probe on /ping health endpoint (30s grace period for model load), min replicas=2 to prevent cold-start. inference-api Deployment: HPA on custom metric inference_queue_depth. Weekly retraining CronJob: schedule '0 2 * * 6', training pod requests 4 CPU and 16Gi memory on cpu-nodes, EFS volume mount for dataset assembly. GitHub Actions CI: pytest, mypy, Bandit, Trivy image scan on PR; ECR push and kubectl rolling-update on merge. Model promotion: workflow_dispatch with version_id input; runs canary Helm values update to 20% traffic split on TorchServe, monitors Datadog false_positive_rate_live metric for 30 minutes, promotes to 100% if delta <2% else triggers rollback via Helm rollback. MongoDB: StatefulSet with 2 replicas, oplog-based replication, daily mongodump to S3 backup bucket. Secrets Manager rotation for MongoDB credentials every 90 days.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM auto-instrumentation on FastAPI inference-api: traces from image receipt through TorchServe gRPC to MongoDB write. DogStatsD custom metrics: inference_latency_p99_ms (alert threshold 450ms warning, 500ms critical), false_positive_rate_7d_rolling (alert >5%), escape_rate_7d_rolling, model_precision_by_class gauge, retraining_pipeline_status ENUM success/failed/running, gpu_utilization_pct. PagerDuty critical alerts: inference p99 >500ms, retraining gate failure, TorchServe health probe failure, MongoDB replica lag >10 seconds. Datadog dashboard: inference latency histogram, defect class distribution over time, false positive rate trend by station, model version history with promotion dates.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3500/month: g4dn.xlarge on-demand $526/month each x2 base + Cluster Autoscaler to 4 nodes at peak. CPU nodes m5.2xlarge $184/month each x2. MongoDB EKS pods on CPU nodes included. S3 storage ~$50/month for 90-day image window at 200 panels/hour. Spot instances for training CronJob save 70% vs on-demand. Total GPU node spend dominates; consider g4dn.xlarge reserved instances (1-year) after 3 months of stable utilization to reduce to ~$280/month each.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): TorchServe with pre-trained YOLOv8 checkpoint on GPU nodes, inference-api with panel_vin required field, MongoDB inference_results collection, false positive review endpoint, VPC-only deployment, Cognito auth. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Label Studio for defect annotation, defect_annotations collection, weekly retraining CronJob skeleton without promotion, golden test set of 2000 labeled images, per-class quality gates threshold definition in model_versions. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Full retraining pipeline with quality gate evaluation, canary promotion workflow in GitHub Actions, Datadog custom metrics for false positive rate and escape rate, camera calibration management endpoints.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. Pydantic v2 models for all FastAPI request and response schemas; panel_vin field defined with Field(pattern=VIN17_REGEX) in InspectRequest model. pytest with async fixtures for FastAPI routes; unit tests for VIN validation, quality gate evaluation logic, and distortion correction preprocessing at 80% coverage. Ultralytics YOLOv8 training scripts pinned to specific commit hash in requirements.txt for reproducibility. Docker base image pinned to digest in FROM line; Trivy scan required on every image build. Model artifact S3 keys must include version_id and trained_at timestamp in key prefix to prevent accidental overwrite. Never call TorchServe synchronously from a synchronous FastAPI route; use asyncio.to_thread for gRPC calls.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: YOLOv8 model recall degradation on new panel color variants introduced without retraining. Mitigation: weekly retraining pipeline includes new annotations; escape_rate_7d Datadog alert triggers investigation if recall drops. Risk: g4dn.xlarge GPU node spot interruption causing inference outage. Mitigation: min 2 on-demand GPU nodes; TorchServe replicas spread across availability zones via pod anti-affinity. Tradeoff: MongoDB self-managed on EKS over DocumentDB chosen to keep image metadata in VPC without DocumentDB API compatibility constraints; adds MongoDB StatefulSet operational burden. Tradeoff: per-class quality gates over single F1 score chosen because scratch recall and dent precision have different business cost asymmetries at the assembly line; false positives cause line stops, escapes cause warranty claims.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Quality gate thresholds defined after analysis of 3 months of manual inspection data: scratch_precision_min=0.90, dent_recall_min=0.88, paint_run_precision_min=0.85, overall_f1_min=0.87. panel_vin made required field with VIN17 regex validation resolving IATF traceability gap. Annotation tool selected as Label Studio (open source, self-hosted on EKS CPU node, supports YOLO format export). Canary promotion window set to 30 minutes with 2% false positive rate delta tolerance.

metadata.notes: Automotive CV defect detection ML pipeline round 4 addressing persistent evaluation gate and IATF VIN traceability issues after 3 prior rounds; weak plan quality reflects multi-round convergence struggle.

project_goal.value: Mobile app for warehouse and production supervisors in a consumer electronics factory that displays real-time line throughput, bin-level inventory alerts, and AGV status on a live plant floor map, enabling rapid response to disruptions with sub-1-second status update latency
project_goal.rationale: Supervisors currently walk the floor to check line status; mobile app reduces response time from minutes to seconds

target_users.value: Production supervisors monitoring assembly line throughput, warehouse supervisors tracking bin inventory and AGV fleet, on-duty floor managers acknowledging alerts and assigning corrective tasks
target_users.rationale: Two supervisor roles with overlapping but distinct views of floor status

project_class.rationale: Flutter mobile app with real-time WebSocket plant floor map is the primary deliverable

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Flutter frontend, Go backend, InfluxDB time-series, Firebase Auth, WebSocket real-time, MES/WMS integration, and shift analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-second WebSocket updates for 200+ AGV units and 50 production lines requires careful connection management and backpressure handling

risk_level.rationale: Internal operations data; availability is operationally important but loss does not affect product quality directly

data_sensitivity.rationale: Production throughput rates and AGV positions are internal factory operational data

external_exposure.rationale: Factory Wi-Fi network only; Firebase Auth required for all app access

access_model.value: RBAC with production_supervisor, warehouse_supervisor, and floor_manager roles via Firebase custom claims; floor managers can assign corrective tasks, supervisors can only acknowledge alerts in their area
access_model.rationale: Area-scoped access prevents supervisors from acknowledging alerts outside their zone

feature_scope.value: Live plant floor SVG map with color-coded production line status, AGV unit position overlays, bin inventory level indicators, push notification alerts for low inventory and AGV blockages, alert acknowledgment with corrective task assignment, shift throughput analytics, area filter for multi-zone supervisors
feature_scope.rationale: Full plant floor visibility feature set identified in supervisor interviews

mvp_scope.value: Live plant floor map with AGV positions and line status, push alerts for low inventory and blocked AGVs, alert acknowledgment
mvp_scope.rationale: Real-time visibility and alerting are the highest-value features for floor supervisor adoption

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth with SSO via Google Workspace factory account, JWT validation in Go middleware, WebSocket connections require valid Firebase ID token, HTTPS/WSS enforced
security_baseline.rationale: Factory Google Workspace accounts simplify auth without additional identity provider

frontend_stack.value: Flutter with Dart
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform iOS and Android with custom SVG map rendering; Flutter canvas performance exceeds React Native for 200-unit animated map overlays

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin on GKE Autopilot
backend_stack.rationale: Go goroutine model handles thousands of concurrent WebSocket connections efficiently on GKE

data_platform.value: InfluxDB for time-series throughput and telemetry metrics; Redis 7 for live AGV state and bin level snapshots
data_platform.rationale: InfluxDB optimized for sub-second time-series writes from AGV and line telemetry; Redis for latest-state snapshot serving to WebSocket clients

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot on GCP in us-central1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Kubernetes with automatic node provisioning matching factory GCP footprint

privacy_retention_policy.value: AGV telemetry and throughput metrics retained 90 days in InfluxDB, aggregated shift analytics retained 2 years in BigQuery, alert acknowledgment records retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Operational data retention aligned with factory audit and continuous improvement requirements

future_scope.value: Predictive AGV maintenance alerts from telemetry anomaly patterns, integration with quality escape tracking system, tablet-optimized layout for control room display wall
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 after supervisor adoption validated in pilot

constraints.value: Sub-1-second map update latency from state change to supervisor device screen, app must function on factory Wi-Fi with occasional packet loss, max 500 concurrent supervisor sessions, GCP us-central1 region
constraints.rationale: Factory Wi-Fi at 2.4GHz band is congested; app must handle jitter gracefully

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards with Prometheus metrics from Go services on GKE, Cloud Logging for structured logs, custom metrics for WebSocket connection count, message delivery latency p99, and alert response time
observability_baseline.rationale: GCP-native and Grafana monitoring aligned with factory ops tooling

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; Flutter and backend teams co-located; MVP app delivered to 20 pilot supervisors at week 10
execution_preference.rationale: Pilot cohort validates real-time map usability before broader rollout

project.goal: Real-time plant floor visibility mobile app for production and warehouse supervisors
project.target_users: Production supervisors, warehouse supervisors, floor managers in consumer electronics factory
project.timeline: MVP to 20 pilot supervisors in 10 weeks

frontend.state: Riverpod 2 for reactive state, flutter_map or custom Canvas for SVG plant floor map
frontend.realtime: web_socket_channel with exponential backoff reconnect on factory Wi-Fi disconnects

backend.framework: Gin with gorilla/websocket for WebSocket hub

security.auth: Firebase Auth with Google Workspace SSO, custom claims for role and area_id
security.transport: HTTPS and WSS enforced via GKE Ingress with Google-managed TLS
security.authorization: Go middleware validates Firebase ID token via firebase-admin-go SDK

data.db: InfluxDB for throughput and AGV telemetry time-series
data.cache: Redis 7 Memorystore for live AGV state and bin level snapshots, WebSocket connection registry
data.analytics: BigQuery for shift aggregate analytics via InfluxDB data export

constraints.wifi: Graceful reconnect on factory Wi-Fi packet loss

open_questions.agv_protocol: Whether AGV fleet management system exposes WebSocket push or requires polling REST API

confirmed_decisions.map_rendering: Flutter Canvas with SVG path painting

reasoner_reviews.product: The plant floor SVG map with live AGV overlays is the core differentiating feature that will drive supervisor adoption. The map must be accurate enough to visually identify which AGV is blocked and where the bottleneck is within 3 seconds of a disruption. Alert push notifications are equally critical: supervisors receiving alerts on-device before walking to the area is the primary time-saving value proposition.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go goroutines with a WebSocket hub pattern (one goroutine per connection, fan-out from a central broadcaster) is the correct approach for 500 concurrent connections at sub-1-second latency. The broadcaster should publish only delta updates (changed AGV positions, changed bin levels) rather than full state snapshots to minimize WebSocket frame size over congested factory Wi-Fi. Redis pub/sub is the right mechanism to scale the WebSocket broadcaster across multiple Go pod replicas without message duplication.
reasoner_reviews.security: Firebase Admin SDK for Go validates ID tokens server-side without a round-trip to Firebase Auth; token signature is verified locally using Google public keys cached with 1-hour refresh. Custom claims set by a Cloud Function on account creation encode role and area_id; Go middleware reads these claims to scope AGV and bin data to the supervisor's authorized area.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Sub-1-second end-to-end latency requires: AGV telemetry ingest to Redis in <100ms, Redis pub/sub fan-out to WebSocket hub in <50ms, WebSocket frame delivery over factory Wi-Fi in <500ms (measured p95 on 2.4GHz with 200 concurrent devices), Flutter UI render in <100ms. Delta-only updates keep frame sizes under 2KB per update, feasible on congested Wi-Fi. Reconnect with full state snapshot on reconnect ensures no stale map state after packet loss.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The SVG plant floor map asset management needs explicit design: the SVG file encodes production line positions and AGV paths. If the SVG changes (new line installed), the app must update without a full release. Recommend hosting the map SVG in GCS with versioned URL and fetching on app startup, with local cache for offline fallback.

backend.service_design: Three GKE Autopilot Deployments: floor-api (Gin REST for alert management, task assignment, analytics queries), websocket-hub (goroutine-per-connection WebSocket server with Redis pub/sub subscriber broadcasting delta updates to connected supervisors), telemetry-ingestor (receives AGV and line throughput telemetry from MES/WMS integrations, writes to InfluxDB and publishes state changes to Redis pub/sub channel).
backend.api_patterns: GET /api/v1/floor/state returns full snapshot of all AGV positions and bin levels for initial map render. GET /api/v1/alerts?area={id}&status=open lists active alerts. POST /api/v1/alerts/{id}/acknowledge records supervisor acknowledgment with role check. POST /api/v1/tasks creates corrective task assignment (floor_manager role only). GET /api/v1/analytics/shifts?from={ts}&to={ts} returns shift throughput aggregate. WebSocket /ws/floor streams delta update messages: {type: agv_position|bin_level|line_status|alert_created, payload: {...}}.
backend.failure_handling: WebSocket hub reconnect: client sends reconnect with last_event_id; hub replays events from Redis stream since that ID. Telemetry ingestor buffers to local memory queue if InfluxDB is temporarily unavailable, replaying on reconnect. Redis pub/sub failover: Memorystore with read replica; publisher retries with 100ms backoff on error.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter app with feature folders: /floor_map, /alerts, /tasks, /analytics, /auth. Riverpod providers for floor state, alert list, and WebSocket connection status. Custom Flutter Canvas painter for SVG plant floor map rendering with AGV dot overlays and bin level color coding.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Floor Map screen (main screen): SVG map with production line outlines painted via Canvas, AGV unit circles positioned by telemetry coordinates, bin inventory color gradient (green/yellow/red), tap on AGV or bin for detail panel. Alert Feed screen: filterable list of active alerts with severity indicator, acknowledge button. Tasks screen: open corrective tasks assigned to supervisor area. Analytics screen: shift throughput bar charts from InfluxDB aggregate API.
frontend.state_management: Riverpod StateNotifierProvider for FloorStateNotifier holding Map<agv_id, AgvState> and Map<bin_id, BinLevel>. WebSocket stream updates merge into FloorStateNotifier via AsyncNotifier. Flutter CustomPainter repaint triggered by notifier changes; only repaints changed entities via dirty-checking in shouldRepaint.
frontend.performance_notes: Custom Canvas painter avoids full redraw on each AGV update: track dirty AGV IDs, repaint only bounding regions of changed entities. WebSocket delta messages average 1-3KB; Flutter TCP socket handles factory Wi-Fi jitter via exponential backoff reconnect with full state snapshot fetch on reconnect. FCM push notifications for alerts routed via Firebase Cloud Messaging with topic subscription per area_id.

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth Google Workspace SSO. Flutter app uses firebase_auth package with signInWithGoogle. ID token refreshed every 55 minutes. Go firebase-admin-go SDK verifies ID token locally, extracts role and area_id custom claims.
security.authorization_model: websocket-hub filters delta updates by area_id from JWT custom claims; supervisors only receive updates for their authorized production areas. floor-api enforces area_id check on alert acknowledgment and task creation. floor_manager role has access to all areas.
security.secrets_management: Firebase service account key in Google Secret Manager; mounted as Kubernetes Secret in GKE pods via External Secrets Operator. InfluxDB admin token in Secret Manager.

data.entities: InfluxDB measurements: agv_telemetry (tags: agv_id, station_id, area_id; fields: position_x FLOAT, position_y FLOAT, status ENUM moving/idle/blocked/charging, battery_pct FLOAT; timestamp), line_throughput (tags: line_id, area_id; fields: units_per_hour FLOAT, target_units_per_hour FLOAT, variance_pct FLOAT; timestamp), bin_levels (tags: bin_id, location, area_id; fields: level_pct FLOAT, units_remaining INT; timestamp). Redis hashes: agv:{agv_id} -> {position_x, position_y, status, battery_pct, updated_at}, bin:{bin_id} -> {level_pct, units_remaining, alert_status, updated_at}. MongoDB or Firestore for alert_events and supervisor_acknowledgments (consider Firestore for GCP-native simplicity).
data.storage_design: InfluxDB OSS on GKE for hot time-series (90-day retention). BigQuery dataset fed by InfluxDB export for shift aggregate analytics and long-term KPI reporting. Redis Memorystore for live state snapshots with no TTL (continuously updated by telemetry-ingestor).
data.analytics_events: agv_blocked_event (agv_id, location, blocked_at, cleared_at, resolution_time_seconds), bin_low_event (bin_id, level_pct_at_alert, restocked_at, response_time_seconds), shift_throughput_summary (line_id, shift_date, shift_type, total_units, avg_units_per_hour, downtime_minutes).

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot in us-central1: floor-api Deployment (2 replicas), websocket-hub Deployment (3 replicas with Redis pub/sub for cross-pod fan-out), telemetry-ingestor Deployment (2 replicas). InfluxDB StatefulSet on GKE with persistent disk. Redis Memorystore. Cloud Load Balancing Ingress with Google-managed TLS. Firebase Auth and FCM via Google Firebase SDK.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Flutter analyze and flutter test on PR; Go lint and go test on PR. Merge to main builds Flutter app (Android AAB + iOS IPA via Fastlane) and Go Docker images, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys to GKE staging. Production deployment via GitHub Actions environment with required approval. Flutter app distributed via Firebase App Distribution for pilot supervisors.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Go /metrics: websocket_connections_active, message_delivery_latency_p99_ms, telemetry_ingest_rate_per_second, alert_response_time_seconds. Grafana dashboards on GKE. Cloud Logging for structured JSON logs from all services. Alert: websocket p99 delivery latency >800ms pages on-call.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a real-time Flutter mobile app for factory floor supervisors. The core challenge is sub-1-second WebSocket delta update delivery to 500 concurrent connections over congested factory Wi-Fi. The Go WebSocket hub uses gorilla/websocket with a central broadcaster fed by Redis pub/sub, ensuring only delta updates (changed AGV positions, bin level changes) are streamed rather than full state. Flutter Canvas custom painter with dirty-region tracking handles 200 AGV units without full redraw. The plant floor SVG map is hosted in GCS with versioned URL for over-the-air layout updates without app releases.
target_output.title: Factory Floor Real-Time Supervisor Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter mobile application for production and warehouse supervisors in a consumer electronics factory delivering sub-1-second real-time updates of AGV positions, production line throughput, and bin inventory levels on an interactive plant floor map. Built on Go 1.22 with Gin and gorilla/websocket on GKE Autopilot with Redis Memorystore for live state and InfluxDB for time-series telemetry. Supervisors receive FCM push notifications for low-inventory bins and blocked AGVs, acknowledge alerts, and assign corrective tasks directly from the map view. Firebase Auth with Google Workspace SSO secures access with area-scoped custom claims.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven mobile architecture with three GKE Autopilot services: telemetry-ingestor receives AGV and production line telemetry from MES/WMS integration feeds, writes to InfluxDB for time-series persistence, and publishes state deltas to Redis pub/sub channels. websocket-hub maintains goroutine-per-connection WebSocket server subscribed to Redis pub/sub, streaming delta messages to each supervisor's Flutter app within 1 second of state change. floor-api serves REST endpoints for initial floor state snapshot, alert management, corrective task assignment, and shift analytics. Flutter app uses Riverpod for reactive state management and a Flutter Canvas custom painter for SVG plant floor map rendering with live AGV and bin overlays. The SVG map layout file is hosted in GCS with versioned URL fetched on app startup, enabling layout updates without app store releases. Firebase Auth Google Workspace SSO with Go firebase-admin-go token verification provides authentication; area_id custom claims scope each supervisor's WebSocket stream to their authorized production zones.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.x with Dart, Riverpod 2 for reactive state, web_socket_channel for WebSocket, firebase_auth + firebase_messaging for auth and FCM push, flutter_svg for map SVG rendering with custom Canvas painter. Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, gorilla/websocket for WebSocket hub, go-redis/v9, firebase-admin-go for token verification. Data: InfluxDB OSS on GKE for AGV and line time-series (90-day retention), Redis Memorystore for live AGV/bin state snapshots, BigQuery for shift analytics. Auth: Firebase Auth with Google Workspace SSO and custom claims via Cloud Functions. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Fastlane for iOS/Android builds, Firebase App Distribution for pilot rollout, Artifact Registry for Docker images. Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, Cloud Logging, FCM for alerts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Live Plant Floor Map: interactive SVG floor layout with color-coded production line status (green/amber/red by throughput variance), AGV unit position dots updated in real time (sub-1-second via WebSocket delta), bin inventory level color gradient overlay (green >30%, yellow 10-30%, red <10%), tap-to-detail panel for AGV battery and status or bin SKU and restock ETA. Alert Feed: push notification via FCM on low bin threshold breach or AGV blocked status; in-app alert list by area with severity; one-tap acknowledgment creating supervisor_acknowledgments record. Corrective Task Assignment: floor managers assign restocking or AGV clearance tasks to supervisor with due time; task notification to assignee. Shift Analytics: hourly throughput chart per production line, shift total vs target, downtime minutes from AGV blocked events, pulled from InfluxDB aggregate API.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot Deployments (us-central1): floor-api (2 replicas, Gin REST server for alerts, tasks, state snapshot, analytics), websocket-hub (3 replicas, gorilla/websocket with goroutine-per-connection, Redis pub/sub subscriber for delta fan-out, connection registry in Redis set), telemetry-ingestor (2 replicas, receives MES/WMS telemetry webhooks, writes to InfluxDB via influxdb-client-go, publishes deltas to Redis pub/sub). InfluxDB OSS StatefulSet with GKE persistent disk (90-day retention). Redis Memorystore for live AGV and bin state (Redis hash per entity) and WebSocket connection registry. Firebase Auth for supervisor authentication with custom claims encoding role and area_id. Firebase Cloud Messaging for push notifications. GCS bucket hosting versioned plant floor SVG layout files. BigQuery dataset for shift analytics. Cloud Load Balancing Ingress with Google-managed TLS for HTTPS and WSS termination.
target_output.workflows: Real-Time Map Update: AGV telemetry unit publishes position and status update to MES integration feed; telemetry-ingestor receives update, writes agv_telemetry point to InfluxDB, updates Redis hash agv:{agv_id} with new position and status, publishes delta message {type: agv_position, agv_id, position_x, position_y, status, updated_at} to Redis pub/sub channel floor-updates:{area_id}. websocket-hub subscriber goroutine receives from Redis pub/sub, iterates active supervisor WebSocket connections filtered by area_id custom claim, writes delta message frame to each connection goroutine channel. Flutter FloorStateNotifier receives delta via WebSocket stream, updates AgvState in Map<agv_id, AgvState>, marks agv_id as dirty, calls notifyListeners. CustomPainter.shouldRepaint detects dirty set non-empty, repaints only bounding rectangles of moved AGV entities via Canvas.drawCircle. End-to-end: AGV telemetry to supervisor screen update in <1 second on 2.4GHz factory Wi-Fi. Alert and Acknowledgment: bin-level telemetry crosses threshold configured in telemetry-ingestor rule table; ingestor writes alert_event to Firestore, publishes alert delta to Redis pub/sub, triggers FCM topic message to area_{area_id} topic. Supervisor receives FCM push notification, taps to open alert detail in Flutter app, taps Acknowledge which calls POST /api/v1/alerts/{id}/acknowledge; floor-api validates supervisor area_id claim matches alert area, inserts supervisor_acknowledgment record with supervisor_id, acknowledged_at, and optional notes. Alert status updated to acknowledged in Firestore, delta published to all area supervisors' WebSocket streams to update map overlay.
target_output.data_model: InfluxDB measurements: agv_telemetry (tags: agv_id, station_id, area_id; fields: position_x FLOAT, position_y FLOAT, status ENUM moving/idle/blocked/charging, battery_pct FLOAT). line_throughput (tags: line_id, area_id; fields: units_per_hour FLOAT, target_units_per_hour FLOAT, variance_pct FLOAT). bin_levels (tags: bin_id, sku, area_id; fields: level_pct FLOAT, units_remaining INT). Redis hashes: agv:{agv_id} -> {position_x, position_y, status, battery_pct, updated_at}, bin:{bin_id} -> {level_pct, units_remaining, alert_status ENUM normal/low/critical, updated_at}. Firestore collections: alert_events (id, type ENUM bin_low/agv_blocked, entity_id, area_id, severity ENUM warning/critical, triggered_at, acknowledged_at, resolved_at), supervisor_acknowledgments (id, alert_id FK, supervisor_id, area_id, acknowledged_at, notes), corrective_tasks (id, alert_id FK, assigned_by_id, assigned_to_id, area_id, task_type, due_at, status ENUM open/in_progress/completed, completed_at). BigQuery tables: shift_throughput_daily (line_id, shift_date, shift_type, total_units, avg_units_per_hour, downtime_minutes, agv_blocked_events_count) populated by daily InfluxDB aggregate export.
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1 and WebSocket at /ws/floor. GET /api/v1/floor/state returns complete floor snapshot for initial map render: {agv_units: [{agv_id, position_x, position_y, status, battery_pct}], bin_levels: [{bin_id, level_pct, alert_status}], line_statuses: [{line_id, units_per_hour, variance_pct}]} from Redis hashes. GET /api/v1/alerts?area_id={id}&status=open returns active alert list. POST /api/v1/alerts/{id}/acknowledge with body {notes: str optional}; validates supervisor JWT area_id claim. POST /api/v1/tasks creates corrective task; floor_manager role only. GET /api/v1/analytics/shifts?line_id={id}&from={ts}&to={ts} returns throughput aggregate from InfluxDB. WebSocket /ws/floor: authenticate via query param ?token={firebase_id_token}; hub verifies token, extracts area_id, subscribes client goroutine to Redis pub/sub channel floor-updates:{area_id}; streams delta frames: {type: agv_position|bin_level|line_status|alert_created|alert_acknowledged, payload, event_id, ts}. Client sends {type: ping} every 30 seconds; hub replies {type: pong} for keepalive.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth Google Workspace SSO enforces factory Google account login. Go firebase-admin-go SDK verifies Firebase ID token signature locally using Google public keys (fetched and cached with 1-hour refresh interval) without network round-trip per request. Custom claims set by Cloud Function on user provisioning: {role: production_supervisor|warehouse_supervisor|floor_manager, area_ids: [str]}. Go middleware rejects tokens with invalid signature or expired exp claim (HTTP 401). websocket-hub filters pub/sub messages by area_id: supervisor receives deltas only for their authorized area_ids claim array. floor-api POST /alerts/{id}/acknowledge enforces alert.area_id is contained in supervisor JWT area_ids claim. Firebase service account key stored in Google Secret Manager, mounted via External Secrets Operator as Kubernetes Secret in all Go pods. HTTPS and WSS enforced at Cloud Load Balancing Ingress with Google-managed TLS certificates; no plaintext HTTP. AGV telemetry ingest endpoint protected by internal GKE network policy: only telemetry-ingestor pods can call MES integration endpoints.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot in us-central1: floor-api (2 replicas, HPA 60% CPU), websocket-hub (3 replicas, HPA on websocket_connections_active custom metric, scale up at 150 connections/pod), telemetry-ingestor (2 replicas). Cloud Load Balancing HTTP(S) Ingress with Google-managed TLS. InfluxDB StatefulSet with persistent 200GB SSD disk and daily snapshot to GCS. Redis Memorystore Standard tier with 1 read replica. GitHub Actions CI: flutter analyze, flutter test, go test, go vet, Trivy image scan on PR; merge to main triggers Flutter build (AAB + IPA via Fastlane with code signing), Go Docker build and push to Artifact Registry, GKE rolling deploy to staging, automated Playwright smoke test hitting /api/v1/floor/state endpoint; manual approval for production. Pilot distribution: Firebase App Distribution delivers Flutter build to 20 pilot supervisor devices at week 10. Rollback: GKE deployment revision rollback in under 2 minutes. External Secrets Operator syncs Firebase service account key from Secret Manager to Kubernetes Secret on pod startup.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes custom Go metrics: websocket_connections_active (gauge per hub pod), message_delivery_latency_p99_ms (histogram from WebSocket write to Redis pub/sub receive delta), telemetry_ingest_rate_per_second, alert_acknowledgment_response_time_seconds. Grafana dashboards: real-time WebSocket connection count per area, message delivery latency histogram, AGV blocked event count per hour, alert response time distribution. Cloud Logging aggregates structured JSON logs from all services with fields: supervisor_id, area_id, agv_id, event_type, latency_ms. Alert thresholds: WebSocket p99 delivery latency >800ms triggers PagerDuty warning, >1000ms triggers critical; alert acknowledgment response time >5 minutes triggers floor manager notification.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1800/month for 500 supervisor sessions: GKE Autopilot consumption-based pricing for websocket-hub at ~3 pods x 0.5 vCPU each, floor-api 2 pods, telemetry-ingestor 2 pods. Redis Memorystore Standard M1 (6GB) $120/month. InfluxDB OSS on GKE with 200GB SSD persistent disk $34/month storage. BigQuery analytics queries at $5/TB scan with partitioned tables. Firebase Auth and FCM free tier covers pilot. Cost optimization: GKE Autopilot scales websocket-hub pods down during overnight maintenance shutdown. Spot node pool for telemetry-ingestor acceptable given buffered message queue.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Firebase Auth with Google Workspace SSO, floor-api REST with initial state snapshot, websocket-hub with Redis pub/sub delta streaming, telemetry-ingestor consuming MES AGV position and line throughput feed, Flutter app with plant floor Canvas map and AGV/bin overlays, FCM push notifications for low bin and blocked AGV alerts, alert acknowledgment API, GKE Autopilot deployment, Firebase App Distribution to 20 pilot supervisors. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Corrective task assignment by floor managers, shift throughput analytics from InfluxDB aggregate API, BigQuery export for daily shift KPI reporting, supervisor area filter for multi-zone coverage, SVG map versioning in GCS for layout updates. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Predictive AGV maintenance alerts from battery degradation trend in InfluxDB, tablet layout optimization for control room display wall, quality escape tracking system alert integration, historical alert response time analytics for continuous improvement.
target_output.development_guidelines: Dart and Go strict linting enforced in CI: flutter analyze --fatal-infos, golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck. Flutter widget tests for FloorStateNotifier delta merge logic and CustomPainter dirty-region detection; unit tests for WebSocket reconnect with full state snapshot fetch. Go unit tests for WebSocket hub goroutine lifecycle, Redis pub/sub fan-out, and Firebase token claim extraction at 80% coverage. WebSocket delta message schema versioned with type field; hub rejects unknown types with log warning rather than panic. Plant floor SVG map fetched from GCS on startup and cached in Flutter app temporary directory; version hash in GCS object metadata compared to cached version on each app launch. AGV position parsing from telemetry feed validated with struct tags; malformed messages logged and skipped without crashing ingestor. No direct InfluxDB writes from websocket-hub or floor-api; only telemetry-ingestor writes to InfluxDB to preserve write path isolation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: factory Wi-Fi packet loss causes WebSocket disconnects during shift peak, supervisors miss alert notifications. Mitigation: FCM push notification as independent alert channel; WebSocket reconnect fetches full state snapshot ensuring no stale map state after reconnect. Risk: Redis Memorystore failover during primary/replica switchover causes brief loss of live state serving. Mitigation: websocket-hub falls back to InfluxDB last-value query for state snapshot during Redis unavailability; degraded latency acceptable for brief failover window. Tradeoff: InfluxDB over PostgreSQL with timescale for AGV telemetry chosen for native time-series write throughput at 200 panels/hour AGV update rate; adds operational complexity of InfluxDB StatefulSet management. Tradeoff: Flutter Canvas custom painter over flutter_map for plant floor visualization chosen for animated AGV overlay performance on 200 units; requires custom SVG parsing and path painting but avoids map tile latency and CDN dependency on factory network.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed MES integration via REST webhook push from AGV fleet management system rather than polling, removing telemetry-ingestor polling loop complexity. SVG plant floor map hosted in GCS with version hash for OTA updates resolved the app-release-free layout update requirement. Firebase Auth custom claims set by Cloud Function on account provisioning confirmed as the mechanism for area_id scoping. InfluxDB retention policy set to 90 days; BigQuery aggregate export via daily Cloud Function confirmed for long-term shift analytics.

metadata.notes: Consumer electronics factory plant floor mobile app with sub-1-second WebSocket AGV tracking, Flutter Canvas map rendering, and FCM alert push notifications on GKE Autopilot.

project_goal.value: Real-time acoustic emission monitoring of welding robots on automotive body-in-white line to detect cold welds, spatter, and arc instability mid-process and trigger MES rework flags within 150 ms of weld completion without halting the line
project_goal.rationale: Zero-line-stop constraint is non-negotiable for body shop cycle time

target_users.value: Quality engineers and MES integration consuming rework flags; weld process engineers tuning detection thresholds
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Core value is ML-based anomaly detection, not a served application

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, realtime, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Streaming inference, edge ML, ClickHouse analytics, MES integration

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-150ms latency requirement across 60+ welding robots, online learning pipeline

risk_level.rationale: Missed defects cause automotive recall exposure; confidential process parameters

data_sensitivity.rationale: Weld process parameters and defect rates are proprietary production IP

external_exposure.rationale: System runs in plant OT/IT network with no external access

access_model.value: Azure AD role-based access: quality_engineer (flag review, threshold tuning), process_engineer (read-only analytics), mlops_admin (model deployment), system_integration (MES API write)
access_model.rationale: Separation of duties between quality review and model operations

feature_scope.value: Edge inference on acoustic emission streams, rework flag emission to MES, online learning from confirmed/rejected flag outcomes, quality engineer review dashboard, threshold configuration UI, model deployment management
feature_scope.rationale: Complete scope approved in sprint planning

mvp_scope.value: Edge inference on 20 pilot robots with rework flag emission to MES; quality engineer review UI for false positive labeling
mvp_scope.rationale: Pilot scope sufficient to validate ROI before full body shop rollout

security_baseline.value: mTLS between edge nodes and AKS backend, Azure AD authentication, encrypted ClickHouse at rest, audit log for all model deployments and threshold changes
security_baseline.rationale: OT/IT boundary security requirements and confidential IP protection

frontend_stack.value: React with TypeScript served from AKS ingress
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; enables reuse of existing component library

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum for edge inference service; Python 3.12 with FastAPI for MLOps API
backend_stack.rationale: Rust for sub-ms edge processing; Python for ML toolchain compatibility

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for acoustic sample store and analytics; Redis 7 for edge state and deduplication
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse column store handles high-frequency weld event queries efficiently

hosting_target.value: Azure IoT Edge on plant-floor gateway nodes + AKS for backend and MLOps services
hosting_target.rationale: IoT Edge enables sub-150ms local inference; AKS for scalable backend

privacy_retention_policy.value: Acoustic samples retained 90 days hot in ClickHouse, 2 years cold in Azure Blob Storage; model versions retained indefinitely for traceability
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: IATF 16949 traceability and internal IP retention policy

future_scope.value: Extension to spot-welding and laser-welding cells; OEM audit data export interface; cross-plant model federation
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap after full body shop deployment

constraints.value: Edge inference must complete within 150 ms of weld completion; no cloud egress of raw acoustic data; plant network air-gap for OT zone
constraints.rationale: Line cycle time and data sovereignty constraints

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for edge node health and inference latency; ClickHouse audit tables for detection events; PagerDuty for edge node connectivity loss
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing plant monitoring stack; PagerDuty for on-call escalation

execution_preference.value: MLOps-driven iterative deployment with canary model promotions and automated rollback
execution_preference.rationale: Online learning pipeline requires controlled model lifecycle management

compliance_context.value: IATF 16949 weld quality traceability: every rework flag must reference robot ID, weld joint ID, model version, and confidence score; customer weld audit requirements from OEM audit programs
compliance_context.rationale: Automotive supply chain quality standard and OEM customer requirements

llm_integration.value: LLM-assisted anomaly explanation: GPT-4o generates natural-language descriptions of detected anomaly patterns (e.g., 'arc instability signature consistent with contaminated wire feed') for quality engineer review queue, using structured acoustic feature vectors as context
llm_integration.rationale: Quality engineers lack signal processing expertise; LLM narrows investigation time

project.goal: Real-time weld anomaly detection on body-in-white line with sub-150ms latency and MES integration
project.target_users: Quality engineers, process engineers, MES system
project.timeline: Pilot on 20 robots by Q2, full rollout Q4

backend.framework: Axum (edge), FastAPI (MLOps)
backend.api: REST for MLOps management, gRPC for MES flag delivery

security.auth: Azure AD with RBAC, mTLS for edge-to-backend
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest in ClickHouse, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.db: ClickHouse for acoustic data and analytics
data.cache: Redis 7 for edge deduplication and state
data.streaming: Azure IoT Hub for edge telemetry

constraints.data_residency: No raw acoustic data to cloud

reasoner_reviews.product: The rework flag latency SLA of 150 ms is achievable with edge-local inference but requires careful acoustic feature extraction pipeline design to keep pre-processing within 50 ms. The online learning loop with quality engineer labeling is the critical feedback mechanism for model accuracy improvement over time.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust Axum on Azure IoT Edge is the right choice for deterministic sub-150ms inference. The split architecture (Rust edge, Python MLOps) introduces deployment complexity; the canary promotion mechanism must handle the edge node fleet update atomically to avoid mixed model versions on the same weld cell.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS is mandatory for edge-to-AKS communication given the OT/IT boundary. Model deployment artifacts must be integrity-checked (SHA-256) before loading on edge nodes. Audit logging of every model version change and threshold adjustment is required for IATF 16949 traceability.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 150ms wall clock constraint leaves approximately 50ms for acoustic feature extraction, 60ms for 1D-CNN inference, and 40ms for flag emission to MES. This budget is tight and must be validated on production edge hardware. No cloud egress of raw acoustic data is a hard constraint given plant OT policy.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Previous rounds did not adequately specify the online learning pipeline's safeguards against label poisoning or systematic false positive injection by operators. The model update cadence and validation gate thresholds need explicit definition to prevent gradual accuracy degradation.

backend.service_design: Two-service split: EdgeInferenceService (Rust, IoT Edge module) handles acoustic feature extraction and 1D-CNN scoring per weld event; MLOpsAPI (Python FastAPI) manages model lifecycle, threshold configuration, and labeling ingestion. Services communicate via gRPC over mTLS.
backend.api_patterns: REST API on MLOpsAPI for quality engineer dashboard (flag review, threshold config, model deployment management); gRPC stream for MES rework flag delivery with guaranteed at-least-once delivery via Redis dedup.
backend.background_jobs: Weekly challenger model training job (Python, GPU node pool on AKS): pulls labeled outcomes from ClickHouse, trains 1D-CNN challenger, evaluates against 500-sample golden test set (min 0.92 F1 gate), promotes to edge fleet via canary if passing.
backend.failure_handling: Edge node inference timeout fallback: if Rust service exceeds 140ms, emit conservative rework flag and log timeout event to Prometheus. MES delivery retry with 3 attempts and dead-letter queue for undeliverable flags. Model load failure: retain previous model version and alert MLOps team.

security.auth_design: Azure AD with workload identity for AKS pods; IoT Edge modules authenticate via X.509 device certificates issued by plant CA. Quality engineers use Azure AD SSO with RBAC claims.
security.secrets_management: Model signing keys in Azure Key Vault; edge device certificates rotated every 90 days via SCEP. No hardcoded credentials in IoT Edge module manifests.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only audit table in ClickHouse: every rework flag (robot_id, weld_joint_id, model_version, confidence_score, timestamp); every model deployment event; every threshold change with actor identity. IATF 16949 traceability requirement.
security.security_risks: Insider label poisoning: quality engineers systematically marking true defects as false positives could degrade model. Mitigated by statistical drift detection on label acceptance rate per operator.

data.entities: welding_robots (id, cell_id, robot_type, acoustic_sensor_id, calibration_date), weld_joints (id, robot_id, joint_type, nominal_energy_joules, target_spec), acoustic_samples (id, weld_joint_id, sample_rate_hz, duration_ms, feature_vector_json, captured_at), anomaly_detections (id, weld_joint_id, model_version_id, anomaly_type ENUM cold_weld/spatter/arc_instability/nominal, confidence_score, inference_latency_ms, detected_at), rework_flags (id, anomaly_detection_id, mes_work_order_id, status ENUM pending/confirmed/rejected, reviewed_by, reviewed_at), model_deployments (id, model_version, checksum_sha256, deployed_to_count, promoted_at, promoted_by).
data.storage_design: ClickHouse MergeTree for acoustic_samples and anomaly_detections partitioned by toYYYYMM(captured_at); retention: 90 days local SSD, then lifecycle to Azure Blob cold tier. Redis HASH for edge deduplication keyed by weld_joint_id + weld_sequence_number.
data.analytics_events: Per-shift false positive rate by robot, anomaly type distribution per production day, model inference p50/p95/p99 latency histogram, MES flag delivery success rate.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure IoT Edge runtime on 4 gateway nodes (1 per body shop zone, 16 robots each); IoT Edge modules: EdgeInferenceService, AcousticCollector, RedisEdge. AKS cluster (3 node pools: system, mlops-cpu, mlops-gpu) for MLOpsAPI, dashboard, training jobs.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: on PR, run Rust unit tests and clippy, Python pytest with mock acoustic fixtures, integration test with simulated weld events. On merge to main, build and push Docker images to ACR, deploy to staging IoT Edge fleet (5 robots) with 30-min soak. Production deploy requires MLOps team approval.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus node exporters on IoT Edge gateway nodes; custom Rust metrics: inference_latency_ms histogram, weld_events_processed_total, flag_emission_success_rate. Grafana dashboards per robot cell. PagerDuty alert on edge node heartbeat loss >2 min or inference_latency p95 >130ms.
devops.rollback_strategy: IoT Edge module twin desired property controls active model version; rollback by updating twin to previous version ID, propagated to all assigned edge nodes within 60 seconds.

ISS-001.title: Online learning pipeline lacks label poisoning safeguards
ISS-001.detail: No statistical controls exist to detect operators systematically marking true defects as false positives, which would degrade model accuracy over weeks of production.

history.note: Identified by critic review
Mitigations described but not architecturally specified

ISS-002.title: Mixed model versions on same weld cell during canary rollout
ISS-002.detail: Canary deployment updates edge nodes sequentially; during rollout, robots on the same weld cell may run different model versions, producing inconsistent rework flag rates that confuse quality engineers.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner in round 2

ISS-003.title: Acoustic feature extraction latency budget not validated
ISS-003.detail: The 50ms feature extraction budget assumption has not been benchmarked on target IoT Edge gateway hardware, risking violation of the 150ms end-to-end SLA.

history.note: Flagged in constraints review
Benchmark plan described but not actioned

previous_plan.title: Weld Acoustic Anomaly Detection System - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Real-time 1D-CNN inference on acoustic emission signals from 60+ body-in-white welding robots on Azure IoT Edge with sub-150ms rework flag delivery to MES, online learning from quality engineer labels, and ClickHouse analytics backend.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Azure IoT Edge gateway nodes run Rust-based EdgeInferenceService per weld cell zone; acoustic signals processed locally and scored within 150ms; rework flags delivered via gRPC to MES. AKS hosts MLOpsAPI, React dashboard, and training job scheduler. ClickHouse on AKS stores all detection events and acoustic feature metadata.

previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS edge-to-backend, Azure AD RBAC, Key Vault for model signing keys, audit table for all flag and deployment events.
