Key decisions: EMR Serverless for cost-efficient Spark execution without cluster management overhead, three-tier medallion (bronze/silver/gold) for staged quality validation required by IATF audit defensibility, MWAA for managed Airflow orchestration, and Unity Catalog for table-level RBAC. The compliance_context field is required because data_sensitivity=confidential and risk_level=medium with IATF 16949 traceability requirements. No frontend specialist runs for a data pipeline project class.
target_output.title: Oracle MES to Delta Lake Migration Pipeline Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A batch data pipeline migrating 15 years of production and quality records from a legacy Oracle-based MES into a three-tier Delta Lake medallion architecture on AWS S3. PySpark jobs on EMR Serverless extract Oracle source tables in parallelized partitioned JDBC reads, normalize schema drift accumulated over 15 years, and enrich records with SAP S/4HANA material master data. Airflow on MWAA orchestrates extraction, transformation, and quality-gate promotion across bronze, silver, and gold tiers. Unity Catalog on Databricks governs table-level access by role. IATF 16949 traceability retention is met through 10-year silver/gold retention and a transformation_audit Delta table recording lineage for every job run.
target_output.architecture_overview: Medallion architecture on Delta Lake: Oracle MES is extracted via partitioned Spark JDBC reads into bronze Delta tables on S3, partitioned by plant_code and source_date. Silver tier applies schema normalization, deduplication of known Oracle ETL duplicates, and enrichment joins to SAP ERP material master and customer order data. Gold tier computes plant-level and product-level aggregations for dashboards. Airflow DAGs on MWAA control inter-tier promotion gates: a silver job only processes bronze partitions that have passed a data quality check (row count validation, null rate thresholds, referential integrity checks). EMR Serverless executes all Spark jobs with auto-scaling from 8 to 200 vCPUs to control cost during the one-time historical backfill. Databricks workspace in a customer-managed VPC provides SQL querying and Unity Catalog governance. Historical backfill estimated at 4-6 weeks; nightly incremental CDC jobs run until Oracle MES decommission.
target_output.technology_stack: Compute: Apache Spark 3.5 on AWS EMR Serverless. Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on MWAA. Storage: Delta Lake on S3 with SSE-KMS encryption. Governance: Databricks Unity Catalog with three-tier medallion catalog (bronze_catalog, silver_catalog, gold_catalog). Query: Databricks SQL Serverless. Languages: Python 3.12, PySpark. Extraction: Spark JDBC with Oracle JDBC driver 21c. ERP integration: SAP JDBC or daily CSV export from S3. CI: GitHub Actions with pytest and PySpark local mode. IaC: Terraform for S3 buckets, EMR Serverless application, MWAA environment, Unity Catalog schemas.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Bronze Ingestion: Parallelized JDBC extraction per source table (production_orders, operation_records, quality_inspections, material_lots, equipment_events) with partition column on date fields; mergeSchema=true to capture schema drift; raw Oracle rows written to Delta with source_mes_id and ingestion_timestamp. Silver Normalization: Deduplication by source_mes_id using ROW_NUMBER() window function; column rename standardization across Oracle version eras; ERP material master join on material_number; referential integrity validation (operations without parent orders flagged as orphans). Silver Quality Gate: Per-partition checks publishing pass/fail to migration_checkpoints table before silver promotion. Gold Aggregation: First-pass yield by plant/part/month, downtime hours by work_center/week, quality rejection rate by operation type. Transformation Audit: Every job writes rows_extracted, rows_rejected, schema_version, emr_job_run_id to transformation_audit Delta table for IATF lineage defense.
target_output.system_components: Oracle MES Source (read-only JDBC, 15-year dataset, estimated 800 GB to 1.2 TB). EMR Serverless Application (Spark 3.5, auto-scaling 8-200 vCPUs, job submission via MWAA SparkSubmitJobOperator). MWAA Environment (Airflow 2.8, DAGs in S3 DAG bucket, task logs in CloudWatch Logs). S3 Delta Lake Buckets (bronze/, silver/, gold/, jobs/, checkpoints/ prefixes, SSE-KMS, VPC endpoint access). Databricks Workspace (customer-managed VPC, Unity Catalog with bronze_catalog/silver_catalog/gold_catalog, SQL Serverless endpoints). AWS Secrets Manager (Oracle JDBC credentials, SAP credentials, 90-day rotation). CloudWatch (custom migration metrics namespace, Grafana integration). SAP S/4HANA Integration (JDBC or daily S3 CSV export for material master enrichment).
target_output.workflows: Historical Backfill: MWAA triggers backfill_dag on deployment. For each source table, SparkSubmitJobOperator launches extract_oracle job with table name, date partition range, and target S3 bronze prefix. After extraction, bronze_quality_check task validates row counts against Oracle source counts via JDBC query. On pass, transform_silver job runs dedup, normalization, and ERP join for the table. Silver quality gate checks referential integrity. On pass, silver partition marked ready. After all tables pass silver, aggregate_gold job computes summary tables. End-to-end backfill estimated 28 days for production_orders (350M rows). Nightly Incremental CDC: incremental_dag runs at 02:00 UTC. For each source table, Spark job queries Oracle for rows where updated_timestamp > high_watermark from migration_checkpoints. Delta MERGE upserts changed rows to bronze. Downstream silver and gold refresh triggered for affected date partitions. High-watermark updated on success.
target_output.data_model: production_orders (id UUID, plant_code CHAR(4), part_number VARCHAR(40), quantity_ordered DECIMAL, quantity_completed DECIMAL, scheduled_start TIMESTAMP, actual_start TIMESTAMP, actual_end TIMESTAMP, work_center_id VARCHAR(20), status ENUM(released/in_progress/completed/cancelled), source_mes_id VARCHAR(60), ingestion_ts TIMESTAMP, schema_version INT). operation_records (id UUID, production_order_id UUID FK, operation_number SMALLINT, work_center_id VARCHAR(20), setup_time_min DECIMAL, run_time_min DECIMAL, operator_badge VARCHAR(20), completed_at TIMESTAMP, source_mes_id VARCHAR(60)). quality_inspections (id UUID, production_order_id UUID FK, inspection_type ENUM(final/in_process/receiving), result ENUM(pass/fail/conditional), inspector_badge VARCHAR(20), measured_dimensions_json VARIANT, defect_codes_array ARRAY<STRING>, inspected_at TIMESTAMP). material_lots (id UUID, material_number VARCHAR(40), lot_number VARCHAR(30), quantity DECIMAL, unit_of_measure VARCHAR(10), received_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, supplier_id VARCHAR(30), certificate_number VARCHAR(60)). equipment_events (id UUID, equipment_id VARCHAR(30), event_type ENUM(planned_downtime/unplanned_downtime/maintenance/calibration), start_time TIMESTAMP, end_time TIMESTAMP, reason_code VARCHAR(20), technician_badge VARCHAR(20)). erp_materials (material_number VARCHAR(40), description VARCHAR(200), material_group VARCHAR(20), base_unit VARCHAR(10), customer_part_number VARCHAR(60), drawing_revision VARCHAR(10)). migration_checkpoints (job_name VARCHAR(100), table_name VARCHAR(100), high_watermark_ts TIMESTAMP, rows_processed BIGINT, status ENUM(success/failed/in_progress), executed_at TIMESTAMP). transformation_audit (job_id UUID, source_table VARCHAR(100), rows_extracted BIGINT, rows_loaded BIGINT, rows_rejected BIGINT, schema_version INT, executed_at TIMESTAMP, emr_job_run_id VARCHAR(100)).
target_output.api_design: No external API endpoints. Internal pipeline interfaces via Delta Lake table reads: silver jobs read bronze tables filtered on quality_gate_passed=true (boolean column written by bronze quality check task). Gold jobs read silver tables filtered on silver_tier_complete=true partition column. Databricks SQL endpoints exposed to analysts via Unity Catalog table grants (SELECT on silver_catalog.quality.quality_inspections for auditor role, SELECT on gold_catalog.analytics.* for analyst role). No REST endpoints exposed by this pipeline.
target_output.security_and_compliance: S3 buckets: SSE-KMS with CMK, bucket policy denying PutObject without kms:GenerateDataKey condition, VPC endpoint gateway for S3 access (no public internet egress from EMR). IAM roles: emr-bronze-role (s3:PutObject on s3://bucket/bronze/*, s3:GetObject on s3://bucket/jobs/*), emr-silver-role (s3:PutObject on silver/*, s3:GetObject on bronze/*), emr-gold-role (s3:PutObject on gold/*, s3:GetObject on silver/*). Oracle credentials in Secrets Manager with SecretString, retrieved via boto3.client('secretsmanager').get_secret_value() at Spark driver startup; never written to Spark config files or logs. Databricks Unity Catalog RBAC: analyst role GRANTED SELECT on gold_catalog.analytics.*; auditor role GRANTED SELECT on silver_catalog.quality.quality_inspections, silver_catalog.production.*; no GRANT on bronze_catalog to any end-user role. IATF 16949 compliance: transformation_audit table records every job execution with row counts and EMR job run ID, enabling auditors to trace any silver record back to its Oracle source row via source_mes_id.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI/CD: on merge to main, pipeline runs pytest (PySpark local mode unit tests for transform functions), black + flake8 lint, aws s3 sync src/jobs/ s3://bucket/jobs/, MWAA DAG validation (airflow dags test backfill_dag and incremental_dag). Terraform apply for infrastructure changes (S3 bucket policies, Unity Catalog schema grants) requires manual approval in production environment. Environments: dev (20 GB Oracle sample, single-AZ S3, EMR Serverless dev application), staging (1-year slice 100 GB, mirrors production IAM roles, full DAG run validation), production (full 15-year dataset, multi-AZ S3, EMR Serverless auto-scaling). EMR Serverless pre-initialized capacity: 4 workers warm during nightly incremental window (01:00-04:00 UTC) to eliminate cold-start latency on scheduled CDC jobs.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom namespace MES-Migration/Pipeline: rows_extracted_total (dimension: table_name), rows_rejected_total (dimension: table_name, rejection_reason), silver_promotion_lag_hours (time from bronze write to silver completion), incremental_cdc_rows_processed (nightly job throughput). MWAA task-level logs exported to CloudWatch Logs group /mwaa/mes-migration/tasks. EMR Spark job event logs pushed to S3 for post-job analysis in EMR console. Grafana dashboard panels: migration completion % per table, daily rejection rate trend, incremental lag vs. threshold (alert at >4 hours behind), EMR vCPU utilization vs. auto-scale limit. PagerDuty alert on: bronze extraction failure (any table), silver quality gate rejection rate >5%, incremental lag >6 hours.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated EMR Serverless cost: historical backfill $1800-2400 over 4-6 weeks (4000 vCPU-hours at $0.052/vCPU-hour). Nightly incremental: $15-30/night for 2-3 hours of CDC processing. S3 storage: $200/month for 1.2 TB Delta Lake. MWAA: $300/month for small environment class. Databricks SQL Serverless: $100-200/month for analyst query load. Total steady-state after backfill: $700-800/month, well under $3000/month constraint. Cost controls: EMR Serverless auto-termination after 15 minutes idle, no pre-provisioned capacity outside nightly CDC window, S3 Glacier lifecycle for bronze after 12 months post-migration.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Bronze Ingestion (sprint 1, 2 weeks): Oracle JDBC connection validation, extract_oracle PySpark job for all 5 source tables, bronze Delta table creation with SSE-KMS, backfill DAG scaffolding, bronze row count quality checks, GitHub Actions CI with pytest. Phase 2 — Silver Normalization (sprints 2-3, 4 weeks): deduplication transform for operation_records (2019 ETL bug), schema normalization across Oracle version eras, ERP material master join (JDBC or S3 CSV fallback), silver quality gate tasks in MWAA, referential integrity checks. Phase 3 — Gold Aggregations + Analytics (sprint 4, 2 weeks): first-pass yield aggregation, downtime aggregation, quality rejection rate gold tables, Databricks Unity Catalog schema grants, Grafana dashboard for migration progress. Phase 4 — Incremental CDC + Observability (sprint 5, 2 weeks): incremental_dag with high-watermark CDC, transformation_audit table, CloudWatch metrics, PagerDuty alerts, IATF audit documentation package.
target_output.development_guidelines: PySpark transform functions in src/transforms/ as pure functions (no SparkSession in function signatures) to enable pytest local-mode unit testing. Each transform module has a corresponding test_*.py file with at least 5 test cases covering normal path, schema drift, and duplicate input. No Oracle credentials or S3 paths hardcoded — all injected via Airflow variables or Secrets Manager at runtime. Delta table schema defined in src/schemas/ as StructType objects imported by both extraction and transformation jobs to ensure consistency. Black + flake8 enforced in CI; isort for import ordering. All Spark job scripts accept --env argument to select dev/staging/production configuration.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Oracle JDBC driver compatibility with Spark 3.5 — Oracle 11g source schema may require ojdbc8.jar with type 4 driver; validate in dev before production run. Mitigation: EMR Serverless bootstrap action installs Oracle driver from S3 at job startup. Risk: SAP S/4HANA JDBC blocked by SAP licensing or firewall policy. Mitigation: fallback to daily SAP CSV extract pushed to S3 by SAP basis team; silver enrichment job detects source type automatically. Risk: schema drift in 15-year Oracle source causes mergeSchema column explosion (>200 columns in bronze). Mitigation: bronze schema audit in dev using Oracle data dictionary queries before full backfill. Tradeoff: MWAA over self-hosted Airflow — higher cost ($300/month) but eliminates Airflow upgrade maintenance during a time-sensitive 6-month migration window. Tradeoff: EMR Serverless over Databricks Spark — lower cost for one-time backfill but lacks Delta Lake native optimizations; mitigated by using Delta Lake OSS library on EMR.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected EMR Serverless over Databricks Spark for extraction to minimize cost during the one-time historical backfill phase; Databricks used only for query access. Confirmed mergeSchema=true for bronze layer to handle 15-year Oracle schema evolution without manual schema migration scripts. ERP enrichment fallback confirmed as S3 CSV export if JDBC access is denied. Deduplication strategy for operation_records confirmed as ROW_NUMBER() keyed on source_mes_id with latest ingestion_ts as tiebreaker.

metadata.notes: Round 1 migration pipeline for Oracle MES to Delta Lake; IATF 16949 traceability compliance, three-tier medallion, no frontend specialist.

project_goal.value: Research prototype exposing a REST API for testing digital twin synchronization strategies for industrial robotic arm simulation, allowing researchers to push joint position and torque updates from a physics simulator and measure state divergence between the virtual and physical twin
project_goal.rationale: Lab group needs a controlled API environment to benchmark event-driven vs. fixed-rate synchronization algorithms for a conference publication

target_users.value: Robotics PhD researchers and simulation engineers in the lab group; simulation clients are ROS2 nodes and MATLAB scripts running on researcher workstations
target_users.rationale: Internal lab users; not a commercial product

project_class.rationale: Experimental API prototype for benchmarking synchronization strategies; not production-hardened

capabilities.rationale: FastAPI backend, InfluxDB time-series for joint state storage, REST API for sim client integration, SSE for real-time state push, ROS2 bridge integration

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-joint state synchronization, configurable sync strategies, divergence metric computation, and concurrent session management drive moderate complexity

risk_level.rationale: Research prototype with no safety-critical or financial consequences from failure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Simulated joint position and torque data has no sensitivity classification

external_exposure.rationale: API accessible to authenticated lab researchers and simulation clients only; not publicly indexed

access_model.value: JWT bearer tokens issued to researcher accounts and simulation client service accounts; session-scoped tokens that expire after 24 hours
access_model.rationale: Prevent unauthorized simulation clients from polluting the shared research dataset

feature_scope.value: Synchronization session management, joint state ingestion from simulator clients, physical twin state ingestion from robot controller, real-time divergence metric computation (RMSE over joint positions, torque delta), time-series query API for researchers, configurable sync strategy parameters (event-driven threshold vs. fixed-rate interval), dataset export for publication analysis
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for conference paper data collection

mvp_scope.value: Session creation, joint state push from ROS2 client, divergence computation (position RMSE), time-series query endpoint, dataset export to CSV
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to run the first round of synchronization experiments

security_baseline.value: JWT bearer auth for all endpoints, HTTPS on Cloud Run, no raw joint state data accessible without valid session token
security_baseline.rationale: Basic authentication to prevent unauthorized session pollution

frontend_stack.value: None -- REST API only; researchers use Jupyter notebooks, MATLAB, and Grafana for visualization
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI needed; researchers access data via API and standard analysis tools

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; asyncio for concurrent session handling; numpy for divergence metric computation
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI provides async I/O for concurrent sessions; numpy is standard in the lab analysis toolchain

data_platform.value: InfluxDB 2.x on GCP for joint state time-series; Redis 7 for active session state and real-time divergence cache
data_platform.rationale: InfluxDB suited for high-frequency joint state writes at sub-10ms resolution; Redis caches latest divergence metrics for SSE push

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for FastAPI service; InfluxDB on GCP Compute Engine e2-standard-4; Redis on GCP Memorystore
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run scales to zero between experiments, minimizing lab compute budget; InfluxDB on dedicated VM for consistent write latency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Joint state datasets retained for 12 months then deleted; researchers can export full datasets before expiry; no personal data collected
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Lab data management policy; 12-month window covers the publication cycle

future_scope.value: Multi-arm synchronization sessions, torque-based divergence metrics, ROS2 action server integration for command replay, ML-based adaptive sync strategy selection
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 research extensions beyond initial publication

constraints.value: Cloud Run budget under $100/month; InfluxDB VM under $80/month; API must support concurrent sessions from up to 10 simultaneous simulator clients
constraints.rationale: Lab research budget constraints; 10 concurrent sessions covers all lab workstations

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for Cloud Run request metrics and InfluxDB write latency; structured JSON logs in GCP Cloud Logging
observability_baseline.rationale: Lab standard monitoring stack; GCP Cloud Logging native integration

execution_preference.value: Iterative: implement session management and state ingestion first, then divergence computation, then export API; GitHub Actions CI with pytest
execution_preference.rationale: Incremental delivery allows experiments to begin before all features are complete

project.goal: Benchmark digital twin synchronization strategies via controlled API for robotic arm simulation research
project.target_users: PhD researchers, ROS2 simulation clients, MATLAB analysis scripts
project.timeline: Initial experiments in 8 weeks, publication export in 16 weeks

backend.framework: FastAPI with asyncio
backend.computation: numpy for RMSE and torque delta divergence metrics

security.auth: JWT bearer

data.cache: Redis 7 (GCP Memorystore)

open_questions.ros2_bridge: Direct HTTP from ROS2 node or dedicated ROS2 bridge node?

reasoner_reviews.product: Session-based architecture is the right model for benchmarking: each synchronization experiment is a named session with configurable strategy parameters (event-driven threshold or fixed-rate interval), enabling controlled A/B comparison of strategies within the same robot configuration. Divergence metrics (position RMSE, torque delta) should be computed incrementally as states arrive so researchers can observe convergence behavior in real time via Grafana during an active experiment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async handlers suit concurrent state ingestion from 10 simulator clients. Each active session should hold an in-memory SessionState object with the latest virtual and physical twin joint vectors, updated on each ingestion request and used to compute incremental divergence without reading InfluxDB on the hot path. InfluxDB writes should be fire-and-forget using the async WriteAPI -- divergence computation reads from in-memory state, not from InfluxDB, keeping latency under 10 ms per state push.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT scope should include session_id claim so a simulator client token issued for session-A cannot write states to session-B. This prevents cross-session pollution in multi-researcher lab environments. Token issuance should require a valid researcher account to prevent unauthorized session creation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 10 concurrent sessions at 100 Hz each = 1000 state updates/second. FastAPI on Cloud Run with min-instances=1 handles this on a 2-vCPU Cloud Run service. InfluxDB e2-standard-4 writes 1000 points/second comfortably. Redis Memorystore M1-basic is sufficient for 10 active session state objects under 100 KB each. Cloud Run ~$30/month, InfluxDB VM $80/month, Memorystore $20/month -- within $180 budget.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three routers: sessions (session lifecycle), states (virtual and physical twin state ingestion), metrics (divergence queries and dataset export). In-memory session registry (asyncio-safe dict keyed by session_id) holds active SessionState objects with latest joint positions, torques, and accumulated divergence history. Asyncio background task computes divergence and publishes to Redis on each state update.
backend.api_patterns: POST /sessions -- create session with sync strategy config. GET /sessions/{id} -- session status and current divergence. POST /sessions/{id}/states/virtual -- push joint state from simulator. POST /sessions/{id}/states/physical -- push joint state from robot controller. GET /sessions/{id}/divergence -- time-series divergence query with from/to params. GET /sessions/{id}/export -- CSV export. GET /sessions/{id}/stream -- SSE stream of real-time divergence metrics.
backend.failure_handling: Cloud Run instance restart loses in-memory session state. Mitigation: session state (latest joint positions, sync strategy config) written to Redis on each state update; on startup FastAPI restores active sessions from Redis. InfluxDB write failure: async write client logs error to Cloud Logging; state is not re-attempted (research prototype trade-off -- occasional write failures acceptable vs. adding retry queue complexity).
backend.backend_risks: NumPy RMSE computation on the request hot path adds ~0.2 ms for 6-DOF joint vectors -- acceptable at 100 Hz. If session count grows beyond 50, in-memory session registry may exceed Cloud Run memory; current 10-session constraint makes this a non-issue.

security.auth_design: FastAPI JWT middleware validates RS256 bearer tokens. Token claims: sub (researcher_id or client service account), session_id (scopes token to specific session or '*' for session creation), role (researcher/simulator_client). Researcher tokens allow session creation and metrics queries. Simulator client tokens are scoped to a specific session_id and only allow state push endpoints.
security.secrets_management: InfluxDB token, Redis URL, JWT RS256 private key stored in GCP Secret Manager. Cloud Run service account has secretmanager.versions.access IAM permission. Secrets loaded at startup via google-cloud-secret-manager SDK, never in environment variables or source code.
security.security_risks: In-memory session registry is not replicated across Cloud Run instances. Mitigation: Cloud Run min-instances=1 with max-instances=1 for this prototype (single-instance constraint acceptable for 10 concurrent sessions at lab scale). SSE stream connections tie to one instance naturally.

data.entities: robot_twins (session_id, robot_model ENUM(ur5/ur10/fanuc_m10), num_joints INT, sync_strategy ENUM(event_driven/fixed_rate), strategy_param FLOAT, created_at, status ENUM(active/completed/archived)). joint_states (InfluxDB measurement): tags: session_id, twin_type ENUM(virtual/physical), robot_model. Fields: q1_rad..q6_rad FLOAT, tau1_nm..tau6_nm FLOAT. Timestamp nanoseconds. divergence_metrics (InfluxDB measurement): tags: session_id, sync_strategy. Fields: position_rmse_rad, max_joint_delta_rad, torque_rmse_nm, virtual_sample_count, physical_sample_count. physics_sim_snapshots (Redis hash per session): latest virtual twin state (joint vectors, msgpack). synchronization_sessions (Redis hash per session): robot_model, sync_strategy, strategy_param, status. divergence_ring_buffer (Redis list per session): last 1000 divergence samples for SSE replay.
data.storage_design: InfluxDB bucket 'research_twin_sync' with 12-month retention. Measurements: joint_states and divergence_metrics. Redis Memorystore M1-basic for active session state (Redis hashes, msgpack serialization).
data.data_risks: InfluxDB field type conflicts if a simulator client pushes different joint count to a session. Mitigation: session creation validates robot_model and num_joints; state push endpoint validates field count matches session configuration before InfluxDB write.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service (Python 3.12, min-instances=1, max-instances=1, 2 vCPU, 2 GB memory). InfluxDB 2.7 on GCP Compute Engine e2-standard-4 (4 vCPU, 16 GB RAM, 100 GB SSD). Redis on GCP Memorystore M1-basic (1 GB). Cloud Run accesses InfluxDB and Redis via GCP internal VPC.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest (unit tests for divergence computation, session lifecycle, JWT validation), ruff lint, docker build, gcloud run deploy --min-instances 1 --max-instances 1 --no-allow-unauthenticated. Staging uses separate Cloud Run service with smaller InfluxDB bucket prefix.
devops.observability_stack: Grafana dashboard querying Prometheus for Cloud Run request rate, p99 latency, error rate, and InfluxDB write latency. GCP Cloud Logging for structured FastAPI access logs (session_id, endpoint, duration_ms, divergence_rmse). Custom Prometheus metrics: active_sessions_gauge, state_ingestion_rate_counter per session, divergence_computation_latency_histogram.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 1 first_pass for a FastAPI research prototype on GCP Cloud Run enabling digital twin synchronization benchmarking for robotic arm simulation. Key decisions: in-memory session registry with Redis persistence for Cloud Run statelessness, InfluxDB for joint state time-series at nanosecond resolution, divergence computed in-process with numpy to avoid latency overhead, session-scoped JWT to prevent cross-session data pollution across lab researchers. No frontend specialist needed for a research prototype with no UI. data_sensitivity=none means no compliance_context required.
target_output.title: Digital Twin Synchronization Benchmarking API -- Research Prototype
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 FastAPI research prototype on GCP Cloud Run enabling robotics researchers to benchmark event-driven vs. fixed-rate digital twin synchronization strategies for 6-DOF robotic arm simulations. ROS2 nodes and MATLAB scripts push virtual joint states (q1..q6 in radians, tau1..tau6 in Nm) and physical robot controller states to session-scoped endpoints; the service computes position RMSE and torque delta divergence in-process using numpy and persists joint state time-series to InfluxDB at nanosecond precision. Session-scoped JWT tokens prevent cross-session data pollution. Grafana dashboards visualize live divergence convergence during active experiments, and a CSV export endpoint produces datasets compatible with the lab's pandas and MATLAB analysis pipeline.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Cloud Run service (min-instances=1, max-instances=1, 2 vCPU) hosts the FastAPI application with three routers: sessions, states, and metrics. An in-memory session registry (asyncio-safe dict keyed by session_id) holds active SessionState objects containing the latest virtual and physical twin joint vectors, avoiding InfluxDB read latency on the state ingestion hot path. On each state push, an asyncio background task computes position RMSE between virtual and physical joint vectors using numpy, writes the divergence metric to a Redis Memorystore ring buffer for SSE streaming, and fires an async InfluxDB write for time-series persistence. Cloud Run connects to InfluxDB (GCP Compute Engine e2-standard-4) and Redis via GCP internal VPC. Researcher JWTs (role=researcher) allow session creation and metric queries; simulator client JWTs carry a session_id claim scoping all writes to a single session.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, Pydantic v2, numpy 1.26, influxdb-client-python 1.42 async WriteAPI, redis-py 5.x asyncio, python-jose 3.3 (JWT RS256). GCP: Cloud Run (us-central1), Compute Engine e2-standard-4 (InfluxDB 2.7), Memorystore for Redis M1-basic, Secret Manager, Cloud Logging, Artifact Registry. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus (co-hosted on InfluxDB VM). CI: GitHub Actions with pytest, ruff, docker build, gcloud CLI. IaC: Terraform for Cloud Run, Compute Engine, Memorystore, Secret Manager.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Session Management: POST /sessions creates a sync session with robot_model (ur5/ur10/fanuc_m10), num_joints, sync_strategy (event_driven with position_threshold_rad param or fixed_rate with interval_ms param), initializing the in-memory SessionState and Redis session hash. Virtual State Ingestion: POST /sessions/{id}/states/virtual accepts joint position array (q1..q6 radians) and torque array (tau1..tau6 Nm) from ROS2 client with session-scoped JWT; triggers background divergence task. Physical State Ingestion: POST /sessions/{id}/states/physical accepts identical schema from the robot controller client. Divergence Computation: numpy position RMSE over 6 joint deltas computed in background task; published to Redis ring buffer and InfluxDB. SSE Streaming: GET /sessions/{id}/stream pushes {position_rmse_rad, torque_rmse_nm, timestamp_utc} events as they are computed. Dataset Export: GET /sessions/{id}/export returns CSV with columns timestamp_utc, twin_type, q1_rad..q6_rad, tau1_nm..tau6_nm, position_rmse_rad, torque_rmse_nm for pandas/MATLAB import.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run Service (FastAPI, in-memory session registry, asyncio divergence task, SSE event publisher). InfluxDB 2.7 (GCP Compute Engine e2-standard-4, bucket 'research_twin_sync', measurements: joint_states and divergence_metrics, 12-month retention). Redis Memorystore M1-basic (active session state as Redis hashes keyed by session_id, divergence sample ring buffer as Redis list per session, last 1000 samples). GCP Secret Manager (InfluxDB token, Redis URL, JWT RS256 private key). Grafana + Prometheus (co-hosted on InfluxDB VM, dashboards for live divergence monitoring and state ingestion rate). GCP Cloud Logging (structured JSON access logs with session_id field). GitHub Actions CI runner.
target_output.workflows: Synchronization Experiment Run: Researcher POSTs to /sessions with body {robot_model:'ur5', num_joints:6, sync_strategy:'event_driven', strategy_param:0.005} (5 mrad position change threshold). Service creates session in registry, writes session record to InfluxDB, returns {session_id:'sess-20260320-001', client_token:'<JWT with session_id claim>'}. ROS2 node receives the JWT and begins POSTing to /sessions/sess-20260320-001/states/virtual at 100 Hz with joint_positions_rad:[q1..q6] and joint_torques_nm:[tau1..tau6] from the physics simulator. Robot controller client simultaneously POSTs physical joint states to /sessions/sess-20260320-001/states/physical at 100 Hz from encoder readings. FastAPI handler validates JWT session_id claim, updates in-memory SessionState.virtual_joints, triggers asyncio background task. Background task: numpy computes position_rmse = sqrt(mean((q_virt - q_phys)**2)) over 6 joints; publishes {position_rmse_rad:0.0023, torque_rmse_nm:0.12, timestamp_utc:'...'} to Redis ring buffer and SSE event queue; fires async InfluxDB write for both joint_states and divergence_metrics measurements. Dataset Export for Publication: After experiment completes, researcher GETs /sessions/sess-20260320-001/export?format=csv. FastAPI issues Flux queries to InfluxDB for all joint_states and divergence_metrics for the session time range, joins on timestamp, and streams a CSV response to the researcher's Jupyter notebook for pandas analysis and conference paper figures.
target_output.data_model: robot_twins (session metadata stored as InfluxDB tag set + fields): session_id VARCHAR(60), robot_model ENUM(ur5/ur10/fanuc_m10), num_joints SMALLINT, sync_strategy ENUM(event_driven/fixed_rate), strategy_param FLOAT, created_by VARCHAR(60), created_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM(active/completed/archived). joint_states (InfluxDB measurement 'joint_states'): tags: session_id, twin_type ENUM(virtual/physical), robot_model. Fields: q1_rad FLOAT, q2_rad FLOAT, q3_rad FLOAT, q4_rad FLOAT, q5_rad FLOAT, q6_rad FLOAT, tau1_nm FLOAT, tau2_nm FLOAT, tau3_nm FLOAT, tau4_nm FLOAT, tau5_nm FLOAT, tau6_nm FLOAT. Timestamp nanosecond precision from client-provided ISO8601 or server-assigned. divergence_metrics (InfluxDB measurement 'divergence_metrics'): tags: session_id, sync_strategy. Fields: position_rmse_rad FLOAT, max_joint_delta_rad FLOAT, torque_rmse_nm FLOAT, virtual_sample_count BIGINT, physical_sample_count BIGINT. Timestamp: server time of computation. physics_sim_snapshots (Redis hash, key session:{id}:virtual_state): q1..q6 float, tau1..tau6 float, updated_at Unix timestamp, all msgpack-serialized. synchronization_sessions (Redis hash, key session:{id}:meta): robot_model, num_joints, sync_strategy, strategy_param, status, researcher_id. divergence_ring_buffer (Redis list, key session:{id}:divergence): last 1000 {position_rmse_rad, torque_rmse_nm, timestamp_utc} items for SSE replay on reconnect.
target_output.api_design: Base path: /api/v1. POST /v1/sessions -- body: {robot_model, num_joints, sync_strategy, strategy_param}; returns {session_id, virtual_state_endpoint, physical_state_endpoint, stream_endpoint, client_token, expires_at}. GET /v1/sessions/{session_id} -- returns {session_id, robot_model, sync_strategy, strategy_param, status, virtual_sample_count, physical_sample_count, latest_position_rmse_rad, latest_torque_rmse_nm, created_at}. POST /v1/sessions/{session_id}/states/virtual -- body: {timestamp_utc, joint_positions_rad:[q1..q6], joint_torques_nm:[tau1..tau6]}; returns {accepted:true, computed_divergence:{position_rmse_rad, torque_rmse_nm}}. POST /v1/sessions/{session_id}/states/physical -- same schema. GET /v1/sessions/{session_id}/divergence -- params: from (ISO8601), to (ISO8601), resolution (1s/10s/1m); returns {session_id, data:[{timestamp_utc, position_rmse_rad, torque_rmse_nm}]}. GET /v1/sessions/{session_id}/export -- params: format (csv/json); streams CSV with columns timestamp_utc, twin_type, q1_rad..q6_rad, tau1_nm..tau6_nm, position_rmse_rad, torque_rmse_nm. GET /v1/sessions/{session_id}/stream -- Server-Sent Events pushing {position_rmse_rad, torque_rmse_nm, timestamp_utc} as events with Last-Event-ID for reconnect. All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer JWT. State push endpoints validate session_id path param matches token session_id claim.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256: private key in GCP Secret Manager, loaded at FastAPI startup. Researcher JWTs (role=researcher, session_id='*') issued by /v1/auth/token accepting researcher API key. Simulator client JWTs (role=simulator_client, session_id='sess-...') issued by the researcher after creating a session and distributed to ROS2 nodes. FastAPI dependency validate_jwt: verifies RS256 signature, checks expiry, extracts session_id claim. State push endpoints additionally verify request session_id path param matches token.session_id claim -- prevents cross-session state injection. HTTPS enforced on Cloud Run (HTTP to HTTPS redirect by default). GCP Secret Manager for InfluxDB token, Redis URL, JWT RS256 key -- loaded at startup via google-cloud-secret-manager SDK, not environment variables or os.getenv().
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run: gcloud run deploy digital-twin-api --image gcr.io/lab-project/digital-twin-api:tag --min-instances 1 --max-instances 1 --cpu 2 --memory 2Gi --no-allow-unauthenticated --vpc-connector lab-vpc-connector. min-instances=1 prevents cold start during active experiments; max-instances=1 keeps all session state on one instance (avoids distributed state complexity at prototype scale). InfluxDB on Compute Engine: startup script installs InfluxDB 2.7, creates bucket 'research_twin_sync' with 12-month retention. Grafana + Prometheus co-hosted on same VM. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: pytest (divergence computation edge cases: identical states RMSE=0, single-joint delta, NaN input validation; JWT scope validation; session lifecycle), ruff lint, docker build --platform linux/amd64, gcloud run deploy. Staging: separate Cloud Run service with InfluxDB bucket prefix 'staging_' and fakeredis in-process for tests.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics via prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator: request_duration_seconds histogram by endpoint, active_sessions_total gauge (updated on session create/complete), state_ingestion_rate_total counter by twin_type. Custom metrics: divergence_computation_latency_seconds histogram (numpy RMSE compute time per state update), influxdb_write_latency_seconds histogram, redis_publish_latency_seconds histogram. Grafana dashboard panels: live position_rmse_rad vs. time per active session (queries Redis ring buffer via Grafana Redis datasource), state ingestion rate per twin type, RMSE convergence comparison across sync strategies (Flux query on divergence_metrics grouped by sync_strategy tag for publication figures). GCP Cloud Logging: structured JSON logs per request with fields: session_id, twin_type, endpoint, duration_ms, position_rmse_rad.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run (1 instance, 2 vCPU, 2 GB, continuous operation during experiment windows): ~$30/month. InfluxDB VM (e2-standard-4): ~$80/month. Redis Memorystore M1-basic: ~$20/month. GCP networking: ~$5/month. Total: ~$135/month, within the $180 lab budget. Cloud Run scales to zero when no experiments are running (nights and weekends), reducing actual monthly cost. Single Cloud Run instance is deliberate for prototype session state locality; graduate to multi-instance with full Redis state if the experiment program grows beyond 10 concurrent sessions.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): FastAPI project scaffold, JWT authentication flows (researcher and simulator client token issuance), session creation and in-memory registry with Redis persistence, POST /states/virtual and /states/physical endpoints, numpy position RMSE divergence computation in asyncio background task, InfluxDB write for joint_states and divergence_metrics, GET /divergence query endpoint, GitHub Actions CI with pytest coverage for divergence and session logic. Phase 2 (weeks 5-8): SSE /stream endpoint with Redis ring buffer and Last-Event-ID reconnect support, GET /export CSV endpoint generating pandas-compatible output, Grafana dashboard with live position RMSE convergence panels, Cloud Run deployment to GCP with Terraform. Phase 3 (weeks 9-16): torque RMSE metric added to divergence computation, fixed-rate vs. event-driven A/B comparison experiment runner script, dataset archival workflow before session expiry, final API documentation for lab wiki and ROS2 client setup guide.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints on all function signatures; Pydantic v2 models for all request and response bodies. pytest coverage target 80% for divergence computation (edge cases: zero delta, NaN, single-joint), session lifecycle, and JWT validation. FastAPI async handlers: no synchronous blocking I/O in endpoint handlers -- InfluxDB writes use async WriteAPI, Redis operations use redis-py asyncio client. Numpy RMSE call is synchronous (sub-millisecond) and called via asyncio.create_task() to avoid blocking the request handler event loop. All secrets via GCP Secret Manager at startup -- no os.getenv() for credentials anywhere in the codebase. ROS2 client integration example (rclpy HTTP POST to state endpoint) provided as a Jupyter notebook in the repo root for researcher onboarding.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Cloud Run instance restart (GCP platform maintenance) during an active experiment loses in-memory session state for any sessions whose latest joint state has not yet been written to Redis. Mitigation: Redis write occurs synchronously before returning the HTTP 200 from each state push -- Redis persistence guarantees recovery within the next startup cycle. Risk: numpy RMSE is synchronous on the background task; future researchers adding computationally expensive metrics (e.g., dynamic time warping over 10-second windows) could block the asyncio event loop. Mitigation: document that expensive metrics must use asyncio.run_in_executor() with a thread pool. Tradeoff: max-instances=1 on Cloud Run for in-memory session state locality vs. multi-instance scalability -- correct for 10-session research prototype; documented in README for future maintainers. Tradeoff: InfluxDB over TimescaleDB for joint state time-series -- InfluxDB tag-based schema avoids relational joins for session+joint queries; trade-off is Flux query language unfamiliarity, mitigated by pre-built Flux query templates in the repo.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ROS2 integration confirmed as direct HTTPS POST from a rclpy HTTP client node using the requests library -- no dedicated ROS2 bridge service required, reducing deployment complexity. Confirmed max-instances=1 on Cloud Run as deliberate prototype constraint for in-memory session state co-location. Confirmed JWT session_id claim for simulator clients to prevent cross-session state pollution in multi-researcher lab environments. CSV export column order confirmed as timestamp_utc, twin_type, q1_rad..q6_rad, tau1_nm..tau6_nm, position_rmse_rad, torque_rmse_nm -- validated as compatible with the lab pandas analysis pipeline.

metadata.notes: Round 1 first_pass for digital twin sync benchmarking API; FastAPI on Cloud Run, InfluxDB time-series, robotics-domain entities (joint states, divergence metrics, synchronization sessions).

project_goal.value: Provide process control engineers a unified desktop HMI configurator supporting Modbus, OPC-UA, and EtherNet/IP protocols for tag database design, alarm setpoint configuration, and versioned configuration deployment to distributed control systems.
project_goal.rationale: Core engineering requirement replacing three separate vendor tools

target_users.value: Process control engineers and automation integrators at manufacturing facilities
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified during discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Requires local protocol connectivity to PLC hardware without cloud dependency

capabilities.rationale: Tauri shell wraps Rust backend with React UI plus local SQLite persistence

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-protocol adapters, versioned config packages, and deployment health checks create non-trivial state management

risk_level.rationale: IEC 62443 compliance required; misconfigured deployments can cause production control system failures

data_sensitivity.rationale: Tag databases and alarm setpoints represent proprietary process IP and safety limits

external_exposure.rationale: Application runs entirely on engineer workstation; no internet exposure

access_model.value: Custom JWT with local user store; operator sign-off required for deployment actions
access_model.rationale: IEC 62443 requires authenticated change records without external IdP dependency

feature_scope.value: Tag database designer, alarm setpoint editor, protocol adapter configurator (Modbus/OPC-UA/EtherNet/IP), deployment package builder, control node health check, change history viewer
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full configuration lifecycle from design to deployment

mvp_scope.value: OPC-UA and Modbus adapters, tag database with address validation, single-node deployment with sign-off, local change history
mvp_scope.rationale: EtherNet/IP can follow in v1.1 after core workflow is validated with pilot customers

security_baseline.value: AES-256 encryption for project files, custom JWT with RS256 for local sessions, signed deployment packages, audit log integrity via SHA-256 chaining
security_baseline.rationale: IEC 62443 SL-2 minimum controls for IACS configuration tools

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2 with React 18 and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Native Tauri shell gives direct OS file and serial port access while React provides rich UI

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum for local API bridge
backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides memory safety and high performance for protocol adapter threads

data_platform.value: SQLite via sqlx for local project and change history storage
data_platform.rationale: Embedded database eliminates external server dependency for a local desktop tool

hosting_target.value: Local Windows 10/11 workstation, distributed as signed MSI installer
hosting_target.rationale: Engineers operate on locked-down plant-floor workstations with no cloud access

privacy_retention_policy.value: Change history retained indefinitely in local SQLite; project files owned by engineer team; no telemetry sent externally
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No personal data processed; confidential process IP stays on-premises

future_scope.value: EtherNet/IP adapter, multi-node batch deployment, project file cloud backup for enterprise customers
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap pending pilot feedback

constraints.value: Must run offline on air-gapped workstations; no dependency on cloud services or internet connectivity; Windows 10+ compatibility required
constraints.rationale: Plant-floor workstations are air-gapped by security policy

observability_baseline.value: Structured local log files (JSON) rotated daily; application error reporting to local file only; no external APM
observability_baseline.rationale: Air-gapped constraint prohibits external monitoring services

execution_preference.value: Iterative 2-week sprints with feature-branch workflow and binary releases via GitHub Actions artifact uploads
execution_preference.rationale: Small team workflow with frequent testable builds

compliance_context.value: IEC 62443-3-3 SL-2 for industrial automation cybersecurity; change management procedures for safety-critical control system modifications
compliance_context.rationale: Risk level high and data sensitivity confidential trigger compliance requirement

project.goal: Unified HMI configurator for multi-protocol DCS tag and alarm management
project.target_users: Process control engineers and automation integrators
project.timeline: MVP in 16 weeks, EtherNet/IP in v1.1

frontend.state: Zustand for UI state, React Query for local Axum API calls

backend.framework: Axum for local IPC bridge
backend.protocol_libs: tokio-modbus, opcua crate, EtherNet/IP Rust binding (v1.1)

security.auth: Custom JWT RS256 with local user store
security.encryption: AES-256 for project files, SHA-256 chain for audit log

reasoner_reviews.product: The unified configurator addresses real pain where engineers juggle three vendor-specific tools for Modbus, OPC-UA, and EtherNet/IP. The versioned project file approach reduces deployment errors that currently cause unplanned downtime. MVP scoping to two protocols is pragmatic.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Tauri with Rust backend is well-suited: Rust protocol crates give direct TCP/serial access without JVM or Node overhead. SQLite is correct for a desktop tool. The Axum bridge pattern keeps React UI decoupled from Rust business logic, enabling unit testing of adapter logic independently. Change history chain integrity must be enforced at the Rust layer, not the UI.
reasoner_reviews.security: IEC 62443 SL-2 mandates authenticated change records. The SHA-256 chain for audit log integrity is the right approach but round 1 plan did not specify how the chain is initialized or how tampering is detected on load. This gap must be closed before deployment to safety-related systems.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Air-gap constraint is non-negotiable. No Sentry, no Datadog, no update checks to cloud URLs. The MSI auto-update mechanism must be removed or pointed to an internal WSUS server. Review all Tauri plugin defaults for network calls.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The round 1 plan described the deployment health check as a simple TCP ping. For OPC-UA this is insufficient - the tool should perform a CreateSession handshake to confirm the server is responsive and credentials are valid before pushing configuration. This should be explicitly called out.

backend.service_design: Rust Axum process spawned by Tauri as a local sidecar on a loopback port; exposes REST endpoints consumed by the React renderer process. Protocol adapter modules (ModbusAdapter, OpcUaAdapter) implement a common ControlNodeAdapter trait for address validation, health check, and config push operations.
backend.api_patterns: Internal REST on localhost:7432 with JSON payloads; not exposed to network interfaces. Endpoints: POST /adapters/validate, POST /nodes/:id/healthcheck, POST /nodes/:id/deploy, GET /projects, POST /projects, GET /history.
backend.failure_handling: Protocol connection errors return structured ErrorResponse with error_code and human-readable detail. Deployment failures trigger automatic rollback signal to the caller and write a failed_deployment record to change_history. OPC-UA CreateSession timeout treated as fatal pre-deployment error.
backend.backend_risks: Rust async runtime panics in protocol crates could crash the sidecar silently. Mitigation: wrap all adapter calls in catch_unwind and expose a /health endpoint polled by the Tauri main process every 5 seconds.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri 2 app with React 18 SPA rendered in WebView2. Feature folders: tag-database, alarm-editor, protocol-config, deployment, history. Tauri commands bridge file system and OS-level operations.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Tag Database Designer (address grid with inline validation), Alarm Setpoint Editor (threshold table with unit conversion), Protocol Adapter Setup wizard (Modbus RTU/TCP, OPC-UA endpoint URL + certificate), Deployment Manager (target node list, health check status, sign-off modal), Change History viewer (filterable timeline).
frontend.state_management: Zustand stores for active project, selected adapter config, and deployment status. React Query for all Axum API interactions with optimistic updates for tag edits.
frontend.frontend_risks: WebView2 rendering differences between Windows versions can cause subtle CSS layout bugs. Enforce WebView2 minimum version in installer.

security.auth_design: Local user accounts stored in SQLite with bcrypt-hashed passwords. JWT RS256 tokens issued by Rust sidecar on login, 8-hour expiry, stored in Tauri secure storage. No network auth flows.
security.authorization_model: Two roles: Engineer (read/write project files, initiate deployment) and Admin (manage local users, view full audit log). Deployment sign-off requires Engineer role plus re-authentication challenge.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: change_history table records every tag create/update/delete and every deployment event with user_id, timestamp, sha256 of payload, and prev_hash linking to prior record. Integrity verification on startup checks full chain from genesis record.
security.security_risks: Project files shared via USB or network share could be tampered with. Mitigation: AES-256 encrypt project file with key derived from installation-unique hardware fingerprint plus user passphrase.

data.entities: tag_databases (id, project_id, adapter_type, created_by, version), tags (id, tag_db_id, address, data_type, description, eu_range_low, eu_range_high), alarm_setpoints (id, tag_id, severity ENUM info/warning/critical, threshold, deadband, enabled), deployment_targets (id, project_id, node_name, protocol, endpoint, last_health_status), change_history (id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, payload_hash, prev_hash, timestamp)
data.storage_design: Single SQLite file per project stored in user-selected directory. sqlx migrations run at project open. Separate application.db for user accounts and global settings.
data.migration_notes: sqlx-cli generates versioned migration files embedded in the binary via include_str!. Project schema version tracked in project metadata; older project files are migrated on open with user confirmation.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions builds signed MSI via tauri-bundler on Windows Server runner. Code signing certificate stored in GitHub Actions secret as base64-encoded PFX. Release artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases for manual distribution to plant IT teams.
devops.ci_cd_design: On PR: cargo test, cargo clippy --deny warnings, vitest for React components, integration test suite launching sidecar against a local softPLC emulator. On tag push: full MSI build, smoke test installer on Windows Server runner, publish release.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous MSI installers retained in GitHub Releases. Plant IT performs manual rollback by running prior MSI. No auto-update to preserve air-gap compliance.
devops.devops_risks: Code signing certificate expiry can block releases. Track expiry in CI and alert 60 days before. Windows Defender SmartScreen may flag unsigned test builds - document workaround for QA team.

ISS-001.title: OPC-UA pre-deployment health check is a TCP ping only
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan specifies a TCP connectivity check before pushing configuration to OPC-UA nodes. A successful TCP connection does not confirm the OPC-UA server is operational or that credentials are valid. A CreateSession handshake is required to catch authentication failures and server initialization errors before deployment.

history.note: Identified by critic review

ISS-002.title: Audit log chain initialization and tamper detection on load not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentions SHA-256 chaining for change history integrity but does not specify how the genesis record is initialized, what happens when tampering is detected on application load, or how chain verification errors are surfaced to the user.

history.note: Identified by security review

ISS-003.title: Tauri default plugins may make network calls violating air-gap constraint
ISS-003.detail: Tauri updater plugin and some community plugins phone home by default. Round 1 plan does not specify which plugins are enabled and whether update check URLs are disabled. This must be explicitly inventoried and resolved.

history.note: Identified by constraints review

focus_issues.title: OPC-UA pre-deployment health check is a TCP ping only
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan specifies a TCP connectivity check before pushing configuration to OPC-UA nodes. A successful TCP connection does not confirm the OPC-UA server is operational or that credentials are valid. A CreateSession handshake is required.

focus_issues.title: Audit log chain initialization and tamper detection on load not specified
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan mentions SHA-256 chaining but does not define genesis record initialization or how chain break detection is surfaced to the user.

latest_recommendations: Specify OPC-UA CreateSession handshake as the required health check before deployment
Define audit log genesis record structure and chain integrity verification behavior on startup
Enumerate all Tauri plugins and confirm updater and network-calling plugins are disabled for air-gapped builds

previous_audits.summary: Solid architectural foundation with correct technology choices for an air-gapped desktop tool, but two high-severity gaps in OPC-UA health check depth and audit log integrity specification block approval.

SQLite project file per-project is appropriate for desktop tool
IEC 62443 compliance intent is present

Audit log chain initialization left unspecified
Tauri plugin network call inventory missing

Audit log tamper detection behavior on load not defined

Define genesis record: id=0, prev_hash=SHA256(project_id+created_at), verify full chain on project open
