previous_plan.title: HMI Protocol Configurator Desktop Tool - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Tauri+Rust desktop application providing a unified configurator for Modbus and OPC-UA tag databases with versioned deployment to DCS nodes, targeting air-gapped plant-floor workstations.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 shell with React 18 renderer process communicating via IPC to a Rust Axum sidecar. Sidecar handles protocol adapter logic, SQLite persistence, and JWT auth. Project files stored as encrypted SQLite databases on local filesystem.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: AES-256 project file encryption, SHA-256 chained audit log, IEC 62443 SL-2 intent. TCP connectivity check before deployment. JWT RS256 for local sessions.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions MSI build with tauri-bundler. Code-signed installer. Manual distribution to plant IT.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two high-severity issues: ISS-001 (OPC-UA health check must be a full CreateSession handshake, not TCP ping) and ISS-002 (audit log chain genesis record and tamper detection on project load must be fully specified). ISS-003 (Tauri plugin network calls) also addressed with explicit tauri.conf.json CSP and plugin inventory. The core Tauri+Rust architecture remains sound; these are specification gaps in the security and deployment sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced TCP ping pre-deployment check with OPC-UA CreateSession handshake. ControlNodeAdapter trait now requires a health_check() method that performs protocol-specific deep validation: for OPC-UA, creates and immediately closes a session; for Modbus, reads the configured coil address as a loopback check. Deployment Manager blocks the deploy button until health_check() returns Ok.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Engineers receive a meaningful error ('OPC-UA server unreachable or credentials invalid') before any configuration push, preventing silent deployment to unresponsive nodes.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined audit log genesis record: on project creation, change_history inserts record with id=1, prev_hash=SHA256(project_id || created_at_unix_ms), payload_hash=SHA256('genesis'). On every subsequent write, prev_hash = SHA256 of previous row's payload. On project open, Rust sidecar performs full chain walk from record 1 to tail; if any prev_hash mismatch is found, project opens in read-only mode and user sees a 'Audit log integrity violation detected at record N - project opened read-only' warning modal.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Tampered or corrupted project files are detected immediately on open with actionable user feedback, satisfying IEC 62443 change record integrity requirements.

target_output.title: HMI Protocol Configurator - Unified Multi-Protocol Desktop Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A Tauri 2 and Rust desktop application providing process control engineers a unified configurator for Modbus, OPC-UA, and EtherNet/IP tag databases and alarm setpoints, with versioned deployment packages pushed to distributed control nodes after protocol-level health verification. Runs fully offline on air-gapped Windows workstations with IEC 62443 SL-2 compliant change history protected by SHA-256 audit chain integrity.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 shell wraps a React 18 renderer process communicating over Tauri IPC with a Rust Axum sidecar spawned on localhost:7432. The sidecar owns all protocol adapter logic via a ControlNodeAdapter trait implemented for Modbus (tokio-modbus), OPC-UA (opcua crate), and future EtherNet/IP. SQLite via sqlx stores per-project tag databases, alarm setpoints, and the SHA-256 chained change_history. Project files are AES-256 encrypted at rest with a key derived from a hardware fingerprint and user passphrase. All operations are local; tauri.conf.json CSP blocks all external URLs and the Tauri updater plugin is explicitly disabled.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop shell: Tauri 2, WebView2. Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Zustand, React Query. Backend sidecar: Rust stable, Axum, tokio, tokio-modbus, opcua crate, sqlx. Database: SQLite (sqlx with embedded migrations). Build: GitHub Actions on Windows Server runner, tauri-bundler, code-signed MSI. Testing: cargo test, vitest, integration suite against softPLC emulator.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Tag Database Designer: grid editor for tag address, data type, EU range, and description with per-adapter address syntax validation (Modbus coil/register address format, OPC-UA NodeId). Alarm Setpoint Editor: threshold table per tag with info/warning/critical severity, deadband, and enable toggle. Protocol Adapter Wizard: guided setup for Modbus RTU/TCP (COM port or IP:port, unit ID) and OPC-UA (endpoint URL, certificate thumbprint, security policy). Deployment Manager: target node list with protocol-level health check status and operator sign-off modal requiring re-authentication. Change History Viewer: filterable audit trail with chain integrity indicator.
target_output.system_components: Tauri main process (window management, OS file dialogs, Tauri command routing), React renderer (UI, Zustand state, React Query), Rust Axum sidecar (protocol adapters, JWT issuance, SQLite reads/writes, audit chain management), SQLite project file (tag_databases, tags, alarm_setpoints, deployment_targets, change_history), SQLite application.db (local user accounts, global settings), Protocol adapter threads (tokio async tasks per active connection), tauri-bundler MSI (distribution artifact).
target_output.workflows: Tag Database Creation: Engineer opens project, adds a new tag database, selects Modbus TCP adapter, enters IP:port and unit ID, adds tags with address validation (Modbus 4x register range check), saves project triggering AES-256 re-encryption of SQLite file and appending change_history record with SHA-256 chain link. OPC-UA Deployment: Engineer opens Deployment Manager, selects a configured OPC-UA node, clicks 'Check Health' - sidecar calls opcua::Client::connect() performing CreateSession handshake, returns Ok or StructuredError. On Ok, engineer fills sign-off reason, re-authenticates, clicks Deploy - sidecar serializes configuration package, pushes via OPC-UA write operations, records deployment event in change_history with prev_hash link. Project Open Integrity Check: On project file open, sidecar reads change_history ordered by id, computes SHA-256(prev_record.payload_hash) and compares to current record.prev_hash for each row; any mismatch opens project read-only with integrity violation modal showing first failing record id.
target_output.data_model: tag_databases (id INTEGER PK, project_id TEXT, adapter_type ENUM modbus_tcp/modbus_rtu/opcua/ethernetip, name TEXT, version INTEGER, created_by TEXT, created_at INTEGER). tags (id INTEGER PK, tag_db_id INTEGER FK, address TEXT, data_type ENUM bool/int16/int32/float32/float64, description TEXT, eu_range_low REAL, eu_range_high REAL, scan_rate_ms INTEGER). alarm_setpoints (id INTEGER PK, tag_id INTEGER FK, severity ENUM info/warning/critical, threshold REAL, deadband REAL, enabled INTEGER). deployment_targets (id INTEGER PK, project_id TEXT, node_name TEXT, protocol TEXT, endpoint TEXT, last_health_status TEXT, last_checked_at INTEGER). change_history (id INTEGER PK, user_id TEXT, action TEXT, entity_type TEXT, entity_id INTEGER, payload_hash TEXT, prev_hash TEXT, timestamp INTEGER). Users stored in application.db: local_users (id TEXT PK, username TEXT UNIQUE, password_hash TEXT, role ENUM engineer/admin, created_at INTEGER).
target_output.api_design: Internal Axum REST on localhost:7432. POST /auth/login {username, password} -> {token, expires_at}. GET /projects -> [{id, name, version}]. POST /projects {name} -> {id}. GET /projects/:id/tag-databases -> [tag_database]. POST /projects/:id/tag-databases {adapter_type, name} -> {id}. PUT /projects/:id/tags/:tag_id {address, data_type, eu_range_low, eu_range_high} -> updated tag. POST /projects/:id/nodes/:node_id/healthcheck -> {status: ok|error, detail}. POST /projects/:id/nodes/:node_id/deploy {sign_off_note, re_auth_token} -> {deployment_id, status}. GET /projects/:id/history -> [{id, action, entity_type, timestamp, chain_valid}].
target_output.security_and_compliance: IEC 62443 SL-2 controls: AES-256-GCM encryption of SQLite project file with key = HKDF(hardware_fingerprint, user_passphrase). Custom JWT RS256 with 8-hour expiry stored in Tauri secure storage (OS keychain). Deployment sign-off requires re-authentication challenge. SHA-256 audit chain: genesis record prev_hash = SHA256(project_id || created_at_unix_ms); each subsequent record prev_hash = SHA256(prior payload_hash); full chain verified on project open - integrity failure triggers read-only mode with violation modal. tauri.conf.json: allowlist restricted to fs, dialog, and path APIs; CSP set to 'default-src self'; updater plugin disabled. All Tauri plugins audited against air-gap constraint before inclusion.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on Windows Server 2022 runner. PR gate: cargo test --all, cargo clippy --deny warnings, vitest --run, integration test suite launching sidecar against a Prosys OPC-UA simulation server and a Modbus softPLC emulator running in Docker. Tag push pipeline: cargo build --release, tauri-bundler generates MSI, code-signing step signs MSI with PFX from GitHub Actions secret (base64-encoded), smoke test installs MSI on fresh Windows Server VM runner and exercises login and tag creation flows. Release artifact uploaded to GitHub Releases. Distribution: plant IT downloads MSI from GitHub Releases and deploys via internal SCCM or manual install. No auto-update mechanism - Tauri updater plugin disabled in tauri.conf.json.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON log files written to %APPDATA%/HMI-Configurator/logs/ rotated daily at midnight, maximum 30 retained files. Log levels: ERROR (protocol failures, auth errors, chain integrity violations), WARN (deployment retries, validation warnings), INFO (project open/close, deployment success). Rust tracing crate with tracing-subscriber JSON formatter. React renderer errors caught by ErrorBoundary and surfaced in-app with log correlation ID. No external APM or telemetry consistent with air-gap constraint.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud infrastructure cost. Distribution via GitHub Actions artifact storage (GitHub Free tier sufficient for MSI artifacts). Engineering cost: ~8-week MVP build with 2 Rust engineers and 1 React engineer. Scaling dimension is concurrent engineers using the tool simultaneously, addressed by SQLite file locking; a future shared project server could use PostgreSQL if multi-user collaboration is required.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Tauri+Rust sidecar scaffolding, Modbus TCP and OPC-UA adapters with CreateSession health check, tag database designer with address validation, deployment manager with sign-off modal and SHA-256 chained change history, JWT local auth, MSI build pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Alarm setpoint editor, EtherNet/IP adapter, project file AES-256 encryption with hardware fingerprint key derivation, audit chain integrity verification on project open with read-only mode. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Multi-node batch deployment, change history diff viewer, export of tag database to CSV and vendor-specific formats for legacy tool migration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: enforce clippy::all + clippy::pedantic in CI; use thiserror for structured error types in all adapter modules; no unwrap() in production paths. TypeScript: strict mode, ESLint + Prettier enforced in CI. Protocol adapter logic tested with vitest mocks for the Tauri IPC layer and cargo integration tests against softPLC emulators. PR requires one senior review. ADR documents for any protocol library changes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: opcua Rust crate has had breaking API changes between minor versions. Mitigation: pin exact version in Cargo.lock, run integration tests against Prosys simulation server in CI. Risk: WebView2 version variance across plant workstations causing React rendering differences. Mitigation: enforce minimum WebView2 version in MSI installer prerequisites. Tradeoff: per-project SQLite file over a shared database simplifies air-gap distribution but prevents multi-engineer concurrent editing - acceptable for MVP single-user workflow. Tradeoff: hardware-fingerprint key derivation for project file encryption ties files to a specific workstation - documented limitation requiring key export procedure for workstation replacement.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: OPC-UA health check resolved: CreateSession handshake required before any deployment push. Audit log genesis record defined: SHA256(project_id || created_at_unix_ms) as initial prev_hash. Tauri network call audit completed: updater disabled, CSP blocks all external URLs. EtherNet/IP deferred to Phase 3 with protocol trait interface designed for easy addition.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision closing IEC 62443 health check and audit chain gaps for air-gapped HMI configurator desktop tool.

profile.domain: Manufacturing Quality Assurance - Internal Audit Mobile App

project_goal.value: Provide ISO 9001 internal auditors at manufacturing facilities a security-hardened mobile app for biometric-gated audit sessions, end-to-end encrypted finding submissions, and tamper-evident audit trail logging.
project_goal.rationale: Replaces paper-based audit forms with digital evidence capture and verifiable chain of custody

target_users.value: Certified internal quality auditors conducting ISO 9001 audits at manufacturing plant facilities
target_users.rationale: Primary user group identified in scoping workshop

project_class.rationale: Field auditors work on mobile devices; React Native provides cross-platform iOS and Android support

capabilities.rationale: Mobile frontend, Spring Boot backend API, PostgreSQL persistence, Azure AD auth, and export integrations

complexity_level.rationale: Standard CRUD workflows with added security hardening for biometrics, mTLS, and audit chain integrity

risk_level.rationale: Audit findings are legally sensitive; tampering or unauthorized disclosure could undermine regulatory certification

data_sensitivity.rationale: Audit findings contain nonconformance records and corrective action details that are confidential business information

external_exposure.rationale: All API access requires Azure AD authentication with MFA; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Azure AD with MFA plus on-device biometric (FaceID/fingerprint) for session unlock; RBAC with Auditor and Quality Manager roles
access_model.rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 requires strong access control for confidential audit data

feature_scope.value: Audit program selection, facility area checklist completion, finding logging with photo evidence, corrective action assignment, audit report generation, tamper-evident audit trail viewer
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full audit lifecycle from initiation to report

mvp_scope.value: Audit program selection, checklist-based finding logging with photo capture, server-side audit trail, basic report PDF export
mvp_scope.rationale: Corrective action tracking and trail viewer can follow in v1.1

security_baseline.value: On-device AES-256 encryption for cached findings, mTLS for all API connections, biometric gate for session unlock, server-side audit trail with SHA-256 record chaining, OWASP Mobile Top 10 mitigations
security_baseline.rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 and audit data integrity requirements

frontend_stack.value: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Single codebase for iOS and Android; team has existing React Native expertise

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.3
backend_stack.rationale: Enterprise Java aligns with existing backend standards; Spring Security for Azure AD integration

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
data_platform.rationale: Managed PostgreSQL with Azure AD authentication and encryption at rest

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps for Spring Boot API
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers on Azure align with existing Azure tenant

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit records retained 3 years per ISO 9001 clause 9.2.2; photo evidence stored in Azure Blob with same retention; no personal auditor data retained beyond active employment
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: ISO 9001 mandatory retention and company data policy

future_scope.value: Corrective action tracking with due-date reminders, CAPA integration, multi-site cross-audit trending dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap items

constraints.value: Must support offline operation for 4+ hours in connectivity-poor factory areas; iOS 16+ and Android 13+ minimum; all data encrypted at rest on device
constraints.rationale: Factory floor often has limited Wi-Fi coverage

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for API metrics; structured JSON logging from Spring Boot; on-device crash reporting via Sentry
observability_baseline.rationale: Operations team standard tooling

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD; TestFlight and internal Play Store track for beta distribution
execution_preference.rationale: Aligns with existing Azure DevOps project setup

compliance_context.value: ISO 9001:2015 clause 9.2 internal audit requirements; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 information security controls for confidential audit data
compliance_context.rationale: Risk level high and data sensitivity confidential trigger compliance requirement

project.goal: Biometric-secured mobile app for ISO 9001 internal quality audits with tamper-evident findings
project.target_users: Internal quality auditors at manufacturing plants
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks targeting Q3 pilot at 2 sites

frontend.state: Zustand for offline queue and session state, React Query for API sync
frontend.offline: WatermelonDB for local SQLite with sync protocol

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3.3 with Spring Security and Spring Data JPA

security.auth: Azure AD MSAL with MFA; biometric re-auth via react-native-biometrics
security.transport: mTLS with client certificate pinned in app bundle
security.encryption: AES-256-GCM on device for cached findings

data.storage: Azure Blob Storage for photo evidence
data.cache: None; WatermelonDB handles offline SQLite

reasoner_reviews.product: The biometric-gated audit workflow addresses a genuine certification risk: paper audit forms can be backdated or altered. Digital findings with timestamped photo evidence strengthen the ISO 9001 audit evidence trail. MVP scope of checklist-based finding capture plus PDF export is well-calibrated for a 12-week delivery.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: React Native with WatermelonDB for offline sync is the right call for factory floor connectivity gaps. Spring Boot on Azure Container Apps aligns with the Azure tenant. The mTLS requirement needs a client certificate provisioning workflow defined - how auditors receive and renew device certificates must be specified.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS with certificate pinning is strong but requires a certificate lifecycle management process. Client certificates embedded in the app bundle will be the same for all auditors; per-device certificates via a device enrollment flow would be stronger. Biometric gate is correct for session unlock but must fall back to Azure AD MFA if biometric fails, not to a simple PIN.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 4-hour offline window requires careful conflict resolution: if two auditors log findings for the same checklist item while offline, the sync protocol must define merge behavior. Recommend last-write-wins with auditor identity and timestamp preserved.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Photo evidence sync is a bandwidth risk in factory environments. Images should be compressed to <500KB on-device before upload. The audit trail chaining mechanism needs to be defined server-side - the plan should specify the chain structure clearly.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3.3 with domain modules: audit-programs, findings, evidence, audit-trail, reports. Each module has its own JPA repository and service layer. AuditTrailService handles SHA-256 chain writes atomically with the finding insert.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0 at /api/v1. JWT from Azure AD MSAL validated by Spring Security OAuth2 resource server. mTLS enforced at Azure Container Apps ingress.
backend.failure_handling: Idempotent finding submission using client-generated UUID as idempotency key. Duplicate submissions return 200 with existing record. Evidence upload failures return 202 with retry location header.
backend.backend_risks: Chain integrity write and finding insert must be atomic - use PostgreSQL transaction with SERIALIZABLE isolation for audit_trail table to prevent concurrent inserts from breaking chain.

frontend.app_structure: React Native app with tab navigation: Active Audits, Findings Queue, History. WatermelonDB local SQLite for offline persistence. Sync engine pushes queued findings when connectivity restored.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (Azure AD MSAL), Biometric Unlock, Audit Program List, Facility Area Checklist, Finding Entry (criteria, severity, description, photo capture), Offline Queue indicator, Sync status.
frontend.state_management: Zustand for session state and offline queue count. React Query for server-state sync when online. WatermelonDB observers for reactive UI updates from local DB.
frontend.frontend_risks: WatermelonDB migration between app versions requires careful schema versioning. Test migration path from v1 schema before each app store release.

security.auth_design: Azure AD MSAL library for React Native handles OAuth2 token acquisition. Biometric unlock via react-native-biometrics re-authenticates against locally stored encrypted session token. Biometric failure after 3 attempts forces full Azure AD re-login.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: Auditor (submit findings, view own audit programs), Quality Manager (view all findings, generate reports, manage audit programs). Enforced in Spring Security via @PreAuthorize on service methods.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_trails table: id UUID PK, finding_id FK, auditor_id, action, payload_sha256, prev_hash, server_timestamp. Chain initialized with genesis record on audit program creation. Full chain verified before report finalization.
security.security_risks: Client certificate shared across all devices is a risk if one device is compromised. Mitigation: implement MDM-based per-device certificate enrollment for production rollout.

data.entities: audit_programs (id, name, iso_clause_ref, facility_id, created_by, status), audit_findings (id, program_id, area_id, criteria_ref, severity ENUM observation/minor_nc/major_nc, description, auditor_id, submitted_at), evidence_attachments (id, finding_id, blob_url, content_hash, captured_at), corrective_actions (id, finding_id, assigned_to, due_date, status), audit_trails (id, finding_id, auditor_id, action, payload_sha256, prev_hash, server_timestamp)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL for relational data. Azure Blob Storage for evidence photos with SAS token access. Photos compressed to JPEG <500KB on-device before upload.
data.retention_and_deletion: 3-year retention for audit records per ISO 9001 clause 9.2.2. Azure Blob lifecycle policy moves photos to Cool tier after 1 year. Soft delete only; hard delete requires Quality Manager approval.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps for Spring Boot API (min 1, max 5 replicas). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Azure Blob Storage. App distributed via TestFlight (iOS) and internal Play Store track (Android).
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: PR gate runs mvn test, Detox E2E tests against staging API. Main branch triggers container build, push to ACR, deploy to staging, smoke test, promote to production with manual approval gate.
devops.observability_stack: Grafana + Prometheus for API metrics (finding submission rate, sync queue depth, p99 upload latency). Structured JSON logging from Spring Boot via Logback. Sentry for React Native crash reporting with breadcrumb trail.
devops.devops_risks: App store review delays can hold up urgent security patches. Maintain enterprise distribution as fallback for emergency fixes.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a security-hardened mobile audit app where the security constraints are the primary architectural drivers. Key decisions: mTLS at ingress layer with per-audit-session JWT claims; WatermelonDB for offline sync with last-write-wins conflict resolution preserving auditor identity; SHA-256 chained audit_trails table written atomically with finding inserts via SERIALIZABLE transactions; photo evidence compressed on-device to <500KB before upload to Azure Blob. Spring Boot on Azure Container Apps aligns with existing Azure tenant.
target_output.title: ISO 9001 Internal Audit Mobile Application
target_output.executive_summary: A security-hardened React Native mobile application for ISO 9001 internal quality auditors at manufacturing facilities, featuring biometric-gated sessions, on-device AES-256 encrypted finding storage, mTLS API transport, and server-side SHA-256 chained audit trail. Built on Java Spring Boot 3.3 with PostgreSQL on Azure, supporting 4-hour offline operation for factory floor use.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native mobile client using WatermelonDB for offline SQLite persistence syncs to a Spring Boot 3.3 REST API on Azure Container Apps via mTLS connections validated at ingress. Azure AD MSAL handles OAuth2 token acquisition; on-device biometric re-authentication gates each audit session using react-native-biometrics. Finding submissions are end-to-end encrypted on-device (AES-256-GCM) before transmission. The Spring Boot API writes findings and audit trail records atomically using SERIALIZABLE PostgreSQL transactions to maintain SHA-256 chain integrity. Photo evidence is compressed to JPEG <500KB on-device and uploaded to Azure Blob Storage with SAS tokens. Azure Container Apps enforces mTLS at ingress using client certificates distributed via MDM enrollment.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.74, TypeScript, Zustand, React Query, WatermelonDB, react-native-biometrics, react-native-camera-kit. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.3, Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server, Spring Data JPA, Hibernate. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server. Storage: Azure Blob Storage. Auth: Azure AD (Entra ID) MSAL. Infra: Azure Container Apps, Azure Container Registry, Azure DevOps Pipelines. Observability: Grafana, Prometheus, Sentry, Logback JSON.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Audit Session Management: auditor selects an active audit_program, authenticates with biometrics, and the app loads assigned facility_area checklists from local WatermelonDB cache. Finding Entry: auditor selects a checklist criterion, assigns severity (observation/minor_nc/major_nc), enters description, captures and compresses photo evidence; finding is stored encrypted in WatermelonDB offline queue. Sync Engine: when connectivity is restored, queued findings are submitted to /api/v1/findings with idempotency keys; server writes finding and audit_trail record atomically. Report Generation: Quality Manager requests PDF export; server assembles findings, verifies audit chain integrity, and generates signed PDF via JasperReports. Audit Trail Viewer: Quality Manager can view chain of custody for each finding with server timestamps and payload hashes.
target_output.system_components: React Native app (WatermelonDB offline store, AES-256-GCM encryption layer, MSAL auth, biometric gate, sync engine), Azure Container Apps ingress (mTLS termination, client cert validation), Spring Boot API (finding service, audit trail service, report service, Azure AD JWT validation), PostgreSQL 16 (audit_programs, audit_findings, evidence_attachments, audit_trails), Azure Blob Storage (compressed JPEG evidence with lifecycle policies), Azure AD tenant (MSAL app registration, user directory, MFA policies), Azure DevOps Pipelines (CI/CD), Grafana + Prometheus (API observability).
target_output.workflows: Biometric Audit Session: Auditor opens app, MSAL token refreshed silently; biometric prompt via react-native-biometrics required before audit program loads; 3 consecutive biometric failures force full Azure AD re-login. Finding Submission with Offline Queue: Auditor logs finding against checklist criterion, attaches JPEG compressed on-device to <500KB via react-native-image-resizer, finding encrypted with AES-256-GCM and stored in WatermelonDB; sync engine monitors network reachability and submits queued findings in FIFO order when online, using client-generated UUID for idempotency; server response updates WatermelonDB sync status. Report Finalization: Quality Manager requests report for an audit_program; AuditReportService walks audit_trail chain from genesis to tail verifying each prev_hash link; if chain is intact, JasperReports generates PDF with finding list, evidence thumbnails, and chain integrity attestation; if chain break detected, report generation is blocked with error logged.
target_output.data_model: audit_programs (id UUID PK, name TEXT, iso_clause_ref TEXT, facility_id UUID, created_by UUID, status ENUM draft/active/closed, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). facility_areas (id UUID PK, program_id UUID FK, name TEXT, checklist_criteria JSONB). audit_findings (id UUID PK, program_id UUID FK, area_id UUID FK, criteria_ref TEXT, severity ENUM observation/minor_nc/major_nc, description TEXT, auditor_id UUID, client_uuid UUID UNIQUE for idempotency, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). evidence_attachments (id UUID PK, finding_id UUID FK, blob_url TEXT, content_hash TEXT, file_size_bytes INTEGER, captured_at TIMESTAMPTZ). corrective_actions (id UUID PK, finding_id UUID FK, assigned_to UUID, due_date DATE, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed). audit_trails (id UUID PK, finding_id UUID FK, auditor_id UUID, action TEXT, payload_sha256 TEXT, prev_hash TEXT, server_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). Genesis record per audit program: prev_hash = SHA256(program_id || created_at epoch ms).
target_output.api_design: All endpoints at /api/v1, Azure AD JWT required, mTLS enforced at ingress. GET /audit-programs?status=active -> [{id, name, iso_clause_ref, facility_id}]. GET /audit-programs/:id/areas -> [{id, name, checklist_criteria}]. POST /findings {client_uuid, program_id, area_id, criteria_ref, severity, description, auditor_id} -> {id, status, chain_position}. POST /findings/:id/evidence (multipart JPEG, max 512KB) -> {evidence_id, blob_url}. GET /findings?program_id=:id -> paginated finding list. POST /reports/:program_id/generate -> {report_id, status: queued}. GET /reports/:report_id -> {status, download_url}. GET /audit-trail/:program_id -> [{id, finding_id, action, payload_sha256, server_timestamp, chain_valid}].
target_output.security_and_compliance: ISO/IEC 27001 controls: mTLS at Azure Container Apps ingress with client certificates distributed via MDM (Intune) per-device enrollment; Azure AD MFA mandatory for all users; biometric gate (Face ID/fingerprint) for audit session unlock with 3-failure fallback to full Azure AD re-auth. On-device: AES-256-GCM encryption for WatermelonDB cached findings with key stored in iOS Secure Enclave or Android Keystore; react-native-jailbreak-detection blocks launch on jailbroken/rooted devices. Server: SHA-256 chained audit_trails with SERIALIZABLE transaction isolation; genesis record prev_hash = SHA256(program_id || created_at_ms); chain verification before report generation. Evidence photos: content_hash stored server-side for integrity verification. 3-year retention per ISO 9001 clause 9.2.2; Azure Blob lifecycle to Cool tier after 12 months.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps: Spring Boot API min 1 / max 5 replicas, scale on HTTP request queue. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Business Critical tier for PITR. Azure Blob Storage with geo-redundant replication. Azure DevOps Pipelines: PR gate - mvn test (85% coverage), ktlint, Detox E2E against staging. Main branch - Docker build, push to ACR, deploy to staging via rolling update, smoke test POST /findings and GET /audit-trail, manual approval gate for production promotion. Mobile: TestFlight internal track for iOS QA, internal Play Store track for Android QA; production via public App Store and Google Play. Azure Key Vault for Spring Boot secrets (DB credentials, Blob SAS signing key).
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics via Micrometer: finding_submissions_total (by severity, program), sync_queue_depth (from mobile clients reported at sync), evidence_upload_duration_seconds (p50/p99), audit_chain_verifications_total (by result: passed/failed). Grafana dashboards per facility showing audit completion rates and finding severity distribution. Spring Boot Logback JSON to Azure Log Analytics. Sentry React Native for mobile crash reporting with audit session breadcrumbs (finding count, sync attempts). PagerDuty alert: audit_chain_verifications failed > 0 in 1-hour window (potential tampering indicator).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure Container Apps: ~$80/month at baseline, auto-scales for sync bursts post-shift. Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server Business Critical: ~$300/month. Azure Blob Storage: ~$20/month for photo evidence at 3-year retention for 500 audits/year. Total estimated: ~$450/month for 10-site pilot. Scale to 50 sites: ~$800/month with connection pooling via PgBouncer sidecar.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Azure AD MSAL auth with biometric gate, WatermelonDB offline finding queue, finding submission with idempotency, photo capture and compression, SHA-256 chained audit_trails, basic PDF report, mTLS ingress, Azure DevOps CI/CD, TestFlight + Play Store internal distribution. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Corrective action creation and assignment, audit trail viewer in app, chain integrity visualization, per-device MDM certificate enrollment replacing shared cert. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-site dashboard for Quality Manager, CAPA system integration webhook, finding trend analytics by ISO clause reference.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in React Native, no implicit any. Java: Spotless + Checkstyle enforced in Maven build. 85% test coverage gate for Spring Boot service layer via JUnit 5. Detox E2E tests for offline finding submission and sync flows run in CI against staging. WatermelonDB schema migrations must be backward compatible - test migration from previous version in CI before release. ADR required for any change to audit chain algorithm.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB sync conflict when two auditors log against the same checklist item offline simultaneously. Mitigation: last-write-wins with full auditor identity and device timestamp preserved in conflict record for Quality Manager review. Risk: MDM certificate enrollment adds onboarding friction. Mitigation: fall back to shared cert with certificate pinning for MVP, migrate to per-device certs in Phase 2. Tradeoff: JPEG compression to <500KB on-device trades photo quality for upload reliability on factory floor Wi-Fi - acceptable for audit evidence documentation. Tradeoff: SERIALIZABLE isolation for audit_trail writes reduces write throughput - acceptable at audit submission volumes (peak ~200 findings/hour per site).
target_output.open_questions_resolved: mTLS client certificate strategy: MDM per-device enrollment in Phase 2; shared cert with pinning for MVP. Photo size limit: 500KB JPEG after on-device compression. Offline conflict resolution: last-write-wins with full audit record preserved. Audit chain genesis: SHA256(program_id || created_at_ms) as initial prev_hash.

metadata.notes: Security-hardened ISO 9001 mobile audit app with biometric gate, mTLS, and SHA-256 chained audit trail on Azure.

profile.domain: Manufacturing IoT - Cloud Cost Optimization Infrastructure

project_goal.value: Right-size cloud spend for a plastics manufacturer IoT telemetry platform by migrating always-on EC2 telemetry processors to event-driven Lambda, implementing tiered S3 storage for cold historian data, and automating idle resource termination to reduce monthly AWS bill by 40%.
project_goal.rationale: Current platform costs $12,000/month with 60% of compute idle during off-shift hours

target_users.value: Plant operations team and cloud platform engineering team
target_users.rationale: Operations monitors the platform; engineering executes the migration

project_class.rationale: Pure infrastructure migration with no new application logic; Terraform and AWS tooling

capabilities.rationale: Lambda compute, DynamoDB data layer, IaC DevOps, and scheduled batch cost reporting

complexity_level.rationale: Established migration patterns with primary risk in Lambda cold-start latency for telemetry processing

risk_level.rationale: Telemetry data loss during migration window would impact quality monitoring; rollback plan required

data_sensitivity.rationale: Telemetry data is operational IP but not regulated; IAM-based access control sufficient

external_exposure.rationale: Platform serves internal applications only; no public endpoints

access_model.value: IAM roles for Lambda execution, Kinesis consumer, and S3 lifecycle management; no user-facing auth
access_model.rationale: Service-to-service platform with no human user access patterns

feature_scope.value: EC2-to-Lambda migration for telemetry processors, Kinesis event trigger wiring, S3 Intelligent-Tiering for hot data, S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval lifecycle for data >90 days, idle EC2 detection and automated termination, CloudWatch cost dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Covers all three cost reduction vectors identified in cost audit

mvp_scope.value: Lambda migration for two highest-volume telemetry processors, Kinesis trigger configuration, S3 lifecycle policy deployment
mvp_scope.rationale: Two processors account for 70% of EC2 spend; remaining processors migrate in subsequent sprints

security_baseline.value: IAM least-privilege roles per Lambda function, VPC-bound Lambda execution, S3 bucket policies blocking public access, CloudTrail enabled for all API actions
security_baseline.rationale: AWS security baseline for internal infrastructure

frontend_stack.value: none
frontend_stack.rationale: Infrastructure project; no frontend application

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 on AWS Lambda
backend_stack.rationale: Existing processor logic is Python; Lambda Python 3.12 runtime avoids rewrite

data_platform.value: DynamoDB for telemetry metadata, S3 Intelligent-Tiering for hot historian data, S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval for cold data
data_platform.rationale: DynamoDB replaces EC2-local state; S3 tiering automates cost reduction

hosting_target.value: AWS Lambda in eu-west-1, triggered by Kinesis Data Streams
hosting_target.rationale: eu-west-1 is the existing platform region

privacy_retention_policy.value: Telemetry data retained 12 months in hot storage, 3 years in cold storage, then deleted per data classification policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Operational data retention aligned with production record keeping policy

future_scope.value: Reserved capacity for predictable baseline Lambda invocations, Graviton3 Lambda runtime for additional cost reduction
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 cost optimizations after baseline migration is stable

constraints.value: Zero telemetry data loss during migration; rollback within 15 minutes if Lambda error rate exceeds 1%; migration must occur during off-shift window (Sunday 02:00-06:00 UTC)
constraints.rationale: Production telemetry is used for real-time quality monitoring - data loss could mask defects

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for Lambda metrics and Kinesis shard utilization; CloudWatch cost anomaly detection; existing Grafana dashboards updated to consume CloudWatch metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing CloudWatch investment; no new monitoring tooling budget

execution_preference.value: Terraform for all IaC; GitHub Actions for plan/apply pipeline; migrate processors one at a time with parallel-run validation before EC2 termination
execution_preference.rationale: Terraform already manages existing infrastructure; parallel-run reduces migration risk

project.goal: 40% AWS cost reduction via Lambda migration and S3 tiering for IoT telemetry platform
project.target_users: Plant operations and cloud engineering

backend.trigger: Kinesis Data Streams event source mapping
backend.state: DynamoDB for per-processor state (last processed sequence, error counts)

data.hot_storage: S3 Intelligent-Tiering for telemetry partitions <90 days
data.cold_storage: S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval via lifecycle policy for partitions >90 days

reasoner_reviews.product: The 40% cost reduction target is achievable: Lambda eliminates ~$7,000/month in idle EC2 during off-shift hours and S3 Intelligent-Tiering cuts storage costs for the 80% of historian data that is rarely accessed after 30 days. The parallel-run validation approach is the correct risk mitigation.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Lambda cold-start latency is the key risk: if Kinesis stream throughput spikes after a cold period, the first batch of invocations may take 800ms+ to initialize. The Lambda processor must be benchmarked for initialization time and provisioned concurrency configured for the two highest-volume streams. Round 1 plan did not address provisioned concurrency for cold-start mitigation.
reasoner_reviews.security: IAM least-privilege is correctly scoped per function. Ensure Lambda execution roles do not have s3:* - scope to specific bucket ARNs and required actions only. VPC-bound Lambda needs NAT Gateway for DynamoDB access if using private subnets without VPC endpoints. Recommend DynamoDB and S3 VPC endpoints to avoid NAT costs.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 15-minute rollback SLA is tight. The parallel-run approach (Lambda and EC2 both consuming from Kinesis with Lambda as shadow consumer) allows instant rollback by disabling the Lambda event source mapping. Ensure Kinesis stream retention is set to at least 24 hours during migration window to allow replay if needed.

backend.service_design: Two Lambda functions: TelemetryNormalizerLambda (replaces EC2 normalizer, processes Kinesis batches, writes to DynamoDB + S3) and TelemetryAggregatorLambda (replaces EC2 aggregator, triggered by DynamoDB Streams, computes 5-minute averages). Both packaged as Lambda container images using existing Python virtualenvs.
backend.api_patterns: No REST API. Event-driven: Kinesis event source mapping for normalizer (batch size 100, parallelization factor 1 per shard), DynamoDB Streams for aggregator.
backend.failure_handling: Lambda destination on failure: DLQ per function (SQS). Kinesis bisect-on-error enabled to isolate poison messages. CloudWatch alarm on DLQ depth > 0 triggers PagerDuty.
backend.backend_risks: Lambda container image cold starts can reach 1200ms for Python with heavy dependencies. Benchmark init time; if >500ms enable provisioned concurrency for 2 reserved instances per function.

data.entities: telemetry_partitions (partition_key: machine_id#date, sort_key: hour, s3_path, record_count, size_bytes, storage_class, created_at), processor_state (function_name PK, last_sequence_number, last_processed_at, error_count)
data.storage_design: S3 bucket with prefix structure machine_id/YYYY/MM/DD/HH/ enabling partition-level lifecycle policies. Intelligent-Tiering for prefixes with last_modified <90 days. Lifecycle rule transitions objects with last_modified >90 days to Glacier Instant Retrieval.
data.retention_and_deletion: S3 lifecycle: Intelligent-Tiering (0-90 days) -> Glacier Instant Retrieval (90 days - 3 years) -> expire. DynamoDB telemetry_partitions TTL set to 3 years.

devops.deployment_topology: Terraform modules: lambda-processors (function definitions, IAM roles, event source mappings), kinesis-streams (existing, minor updates), s3-historian (bucket, lifecycle policies, VPC endpoints), dynamodb-tables (telemetry_partitions, processor_state). Deployed to eu-west-1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR - terraform plan, pytest for Lambda unit tests, checkov for IaC security scan. On merge to main - terraform apply to staging, integration test (send 1000 synthetic Kinesis events, verify DynamoDB and S3 writes), manual approval, terraform apply to production.
devops.cost_controls: Lambda: function-level cost allocation tags. S3: storage lens dashboard for per-prefix storage class distribution. CloudWatch cost anomaly detection alert if Lambda invocation cost exceeds $200/month. Monthly cost report Lambda runs on 1st of month, writes summary to S3, posts to Slack.
devops.devops_risks: Terraform state locking in S3 + DynamoDB - ensure existing state backend is not shared with another team's apply during migration window.

ISS-001.title: Lambda cold-start latency not addressed for high-volume Kinesis streams
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan migrates telemetry processors to Lambda without addressing cold-start latency. During off-shift hours when Lambda scales to zero, the first post-shift Kinesis trigger will initialize cold containers. For the high-volume normalizer processing 8000 records/minute, cold starts causing >500ms init time will cause Kinesis iterator age to grow, potentially triggering back-pressure alarms. Provisioned concurrency configuration must be specified.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner review

ISS-002.title: VPC-bound Lambda NAT Gateway cost may offset compute savings
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan places Lambda in private subnets (correct for security) but does not specify VPC endpoints for DynamoDB and S3. Without VPC endpoints, all Lambda traffic to DynamoDB and S3 must route through NAT Gateway. At current telemetry volumes, NAT Gateway data processing charges could reach $800/month, offsetting 11% of the target savings.

history.note: Identified by security/constraints review

focus_issues.title: Lambda cold-start latency not addressed for high-volume Kinesis streams
focus_issues.detail: Provisioned concurrency not configured; cold starts at shift start will cause Kinesis iterator age growth and potential back-pressure.

focus_issues.title: VPC-bound Lambda NAT Gateway cost may offset compute savings
focus_issues.detail: No VPC endpoints for DynamoDB and S3 specified; NAT Gateway charges could reach $800/month.

latest_recommendations: Configure provisioned concurrency for TelemetryNormalizerLambda: 2 reserved instances on a schedule matching shift start times via Application Auto Scaling
Add DynamoDB Interface VPC Endpoint and S3 Gateway VPC Endpoint to Terraform lambda-processors module to eliminate NAT Gateway traffic
Document expected cold-start benchmark (target <200ms for provisioned, <1200ms for on-demand) in deployment spec
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: IoT Telemetry Platform Cost Reduction - Lambda Migration Round 1

convergence_state.reason: High-severity cold-start issue unresolved after round 1

previous_audits.summary: Solid migration architecture with correct parallel-run strategy, but Lambda cold-start latency for high-volume streams is a blocking gap that must be addressed before production cutover.
strengths: Parallel-run validation approach minimizes zero-data-loss risk
Terraform modular structure is well-organized
S3 lifecycle tiering correctly targets 90-day threshold
concerns: No provisioned concurrency specification for cold start mitigation
VPC endpoint absence creates hidden NAT Gateway cost
DLQ for Kinesis poison messages not mentioned in round 1
blocking_issues: Lambda cold starts at shift start could cause Kinesis iterator age accumulation beyond 1-hour alert threshold
recommendations: Add Application Auto Scaling provisioned concurrency schedule for shift start windows (05:45 UTC Mon-Sat)
Add aws_vpc_endpoint resources for com.amazonaws.eu-west-1.dynamodb and com.amazonaws.eu-west-1.s3 in Terraform
Add bisect-on-error and DLQ configuration to event source mapping Terraform resource

issue_updates.title: Lambda cold-start latency not addressed
issue_updates.detail: Lambda function definition in Terraform has no provisioned_concurrency_config block

issue_updates.title: VPC-bound Lambda NAT Gateway cost
