previous_plan.title: Industrial Machine Connectivity Gateway API — Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22 Gin-based REST and SSE gateway normalizing Modbus and OPC-UA telemetry into a unified JSON schema stored in InfluxDB, with JWT RS256 auth and Redis-backed rate limiting deployed on ECS Fargate.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Adapter goroutines maintain persistent protocol connections and feed a normalization pipeline that writes to InfluxDB; REST and SSE endpoints expose unified telemetry to internal consumers within VPC.
previous_plan.data_model: machine_assets, telemetry_streams (InfluxDB), schema_mappings, rate_limit_policies defined but write path between goroutines and InfluxDB not buffered.
previous_plan.workflows: Machine registration assigns protocol adapter; normalization goroutines transform raw protocol frames to unified JSON and write directly to InfluxDB batch client without overflow handling.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 focuses on resolving ISS-001 (InfluxDB write backpressure) and ISS-002 (schema versioning). Key decisions: introduce a bounded buffered channel (10,000 message capacity) between normalization goroutines and a dedicated InfluxDB batch writer goroutine with explicit overflow shedding and metrics; add schema_version field to unified JSON and X-Schema-Version negotiation header for consumer compatibility. The Go goroutine model makes the bounded channel pattern natural and memory-predictable under burst conditions.

fix_report.action_taken: Added bounded buffered channel of 10,000 normalized messages between adapter goroutines and InfluxDB batch writer. When channel exceeds 80% capacity, oldest messages are dropped and backpressure_drops_total metric incremented. Batch writer flushes every 500ms or when batch reaches 5,000 points.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Eliminates OOM goroutine pile-up under 2,000-machine burst load; overflow shedding is visible in Datadog dashboard enabling capacity planning alerts

fix_report.action_taken: Added schema_version field (current value 'v1') to all unified telemetry JSON messages. API returns X-Schema-Version response header. Consumer documentation specifies 6-month deprecation window when schema increments. PATCH /v1/schema-version endpoint allows consumers to pin to a supported schema version during migration.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Consumer integrations survive schema evolution without silent breakage; version negotiation header enables gradual migration

target_output.title: Industrial Machine Connectivity Gateway API
target_output.executive_summary: A high-throughput Go 1.22 REST and SSE API service normalizing telemetry from Modbus TCP, OPC-UA, and future industrial protocol devices into a versioned unified JSON schema consumed by internal MES, analytics, and data platform services. Deployed on ECS Fargate with InfluxDB time-series storage, Redis rate limiting, and JWT RS256 service-to-service authentication. Addresses round 2 gaps with bounded write buffering for OOM prevention and schema versioning for consumer stability.
target_output.architecture_overview: Protocol adapter goroutines (ModbusAdapter, OPCUAAdapter) maintain persistent device connections and push normalized messages onto a bounded buffered channel (capacity 10,000). A dedicated batch writer goroutine drains this channel and flushes batches of up to 5,000 points to InfluxDB every 500ms, providing explicit backpressure isolation between ingestion and persistence. Gin HTTP server handles REST endpoints for asset registration and telemetry queries, plus SSE upgrade for real-time streaming consumers. Redis cluster holds rate limit counters and SSE subscriber state shared across ECS tasks. All traffic travels over TLS 1.3 within the VPC ALB listener; JWT RS256 middleware validates consumer tokens on every request.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, asyncua-go for OPC-UA, gomodbus for Modbus TCP. Persistence: InfluxDB 2.7 (hot 90 days), S3 Parquet via continuous query downsampling for 2-year archive. Cache: Redis 7 cluster for rate limit token buckets and SSE subscriber ZSET. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with TLS 1.3 listener, ECR, AWS Secrets Manager. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with go test -race. Observability: Datadog APM, StatsD custom metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Machine Asset Registration: POST /v1/machines accepts protocol type, adapter config JSON, and asset group assignment; validates adapter config schema before persisting. Protocol Adapter Management: runtime adapter pool supervisor reconnects failed device connections with exponential backoff. Telemetry Normalization: each adapter's Normalize() method maps protocol-specific register values to unified JSON with machine_id, tag_name, value, unit, quality_code, schema_version. SSE Streaming: GET /v1/machines/:id/stream upgrades to SSE and pushes normalized messages for subscribed tags in real time. Historical Query: GET /v1/machines/:id/telemetry supports time range and tag_name filters with cursor-based pagination. Consumer Rate Limiting: token bucket per JWT subject enforced via Redis pipeline operations.
target_output.system_components: Protocol Adapter Pool: goroutine-per-connection supervisors for ModbusAdapter (gomodbus) and OPCUAAdapter (asyncua-go) with health check pings every 30s. Normalization Channel: buffered Go channel capacity 10,000 with 80% high-watermark overflow shedding. InfluxDB Batch Writer: goroutine flushing 5,000-point batches to InfluxDB measurement 'machine_telemetry' with circuit breaker on write failures. Gin API Server: JWT middleware, scope validator, rate limiter middleware, SSE handler with per-machine subscriber registry in Redis ZSET. Redis Cluster: token bucket counters (HASH), SSE subscriber tracking (ZSET), machine connection state (HASH). ALB: TLS 1.3 termination, target group health checks on /healthz.
target_output.workflows: Telemetry Normalization Flow: ModbusAdapter goroutine polls configured register addresses every 100ms, maps raw uint16 register values through schema_mapping scale/offset formulas, constructs unified JSON message {machine_id, tag_name, value, unit, quality_code, schema_version:'v1', timestamp}, and sends to normalization channel. Batch writer drains channel, accumulates InfluxDB line protocol batch, and flushes every 500ms. SSE Delivery Flow: consumer opens GET /v1/machines/cnc-42/stream; Gin handler registers SSE subscription in Redis ZSET for machine cnc-42; normalization batch writer also fans out to SSE subscriber goroutines for real-time push before InfluxDB write. Schema Version Negotiation: consumer sends Accept-Schema-Version: v1 header; if omitted, latest version served; PATCH /v1/schema-version allows pinning per consumer subject.
target_output.data_model: machine_assets (machine_id UUID PK, asset_name, protocol_type ENUM modbus_tcp/opc_ua/ethernet_ip, adapter_config_json JSONB, asset_group, status ENUM online/offline/degraded, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ) stored in PostgreSQL 16 as registry. telemetry (InfluxDB measurement 'machine_telemetry', tags: machine_id, tag_name, quality_code ENUM good/uncertain/bad; fields: value FLOAT, schema_version STRING; timestamp nanosecond precision). schema_mappings (mapping_id UUID, machine_id FK, source_address TEXT, target_tag_name, unit, scale_factor FLOAT, offset FLOAT, active BOOL). rate_limit_policies (consumer_subject, requests_per_min INT, max_sse_streams INT). connection_health_records (machine_id, last_seen_at, consecutive_failures INT, reconnect_count INT).
target_output.api_design: OpenAPI 3.1 contract published at /openapi.json. POST /v1/machines {protocol_type, adapter_config, asset_group} — registers asset, returns machine_id. GET /v1/machines?group=cnc-cell-3 — list assets with status. GET /v1/machines/:id/telemetry?tag=spindle_speed&from=2026-03-20T08:00:00Z&cursor=abc — paginated historical query. GET /v1/machines/:id/stream — SSE upgrade, emits unified JSON per tag update. PATCH /v1/machines/:id/adapter-config — hot-reload adapter parameters. PATCH /v1/schema-version {consumer_subject, pinned_version} — pin schema version per consumer. GET /healthz — liveness probe for ALB. All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer <JWT> header; scope validated per route.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256 validation: Gin middleware fetches JWKS from internal endpoint at startup, caches public key with 24h TTL, validates alg=RS256, exp, and iss claims. Scope enforcement: read:telemetry required for GET /v1/machines and streaming endpoints; write:machines for POST and PATCH operations; validated in per-route middleware before handler invocation. Rate limiting: Redis token bucket with MULTI/EXEC pipeline; 1,000 requests/min REST, 10 concurrent SSE streams per JWT subject. TLS 1.3: enforced at ALB listener; application receives plaintext within VPC. Secrets: InfluxDB token, Redis password, JWKS URL stored in AWS Secrets Manager; injected as environment variables via ECS task definition secrets block. Input validation: adapter_config_json validated against JSON Schema before persistence to prevent injection of malicious protocol addresses.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate service: 3 tasks baseline, auto-scaling policy triggers at active_connections_gauge > 1,500 per task (custom CloudWatch metric from Datadog). Task definition: Go API container + Datadog agent sidecar; secrets from Secrets Manager. ALB: TLS 1.3 listener on port 443, target group with /healthz health check (interval 10s, threshold 2). GitHub Actions pipeline: go vet, staticcheck, go test -race -cover, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with 50% minimum healthy and 5-minute stabilization window. ECS deployment circuit breaker enabled for automatic rollback on stabilization failure. Environments: dev (single task, dev InfluxDB), staging (3 tasks, load test with k6 validating p99 < 500ms and OOM-free 2,000-connection soak), production (3-6 tasks, multi-AZ Fargate). Migrations: PostgreSQL schema for machine_assets via Flyway in GitHub Actions pre-deploy step.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces for all HTTP handlers and InfluxDB write operations. Custom StatsD metrics: messages_normalized_total (by adapter_type label), backpressure_drops_total (critical alert if > 100/min), active_connections_gauge (by protocol_type), sse_subscribers_gauge, influxdb_write_duration_p99, circuit_breaker_state (0=closed,1=open). Datadog dashboard: gateway throughput panel, adapter connection health heatmap, backpressure drop rate, consumer rate limit hit rate. PagerDuty alerts: backpressure_drops_total > 100/min (high), circuit_breaker_state=1 for > 60s (critical), active_connections_gauge < expected floor (adapter pool crash indicator).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: 3 baseline ECS Fargate tasks (0.5 vCPU, 1GB RAM each) at ~$90/month. InfluxDB self-managed on EC2 m5.large at ~$70/month. Redis cluster (2 nodes cache.t3.small) at ~$50/month. S3 Parquet archive for 2-year telemetry history at ~$30/month. Total ~$240/month for 2,000 machines. Scaling ceiling: 6 Fargate tasks handling 4,000 connections at ~$420/month. Go's low memory footprint keeps per-task cost predictable under connection growth.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core MVP (6 weeks): Modbus TCP adapter, unified JSON schema v1 with schema_version field, InfluxDB persistence with bounded write channel, JWT RS256 auth, GET/POST/SSE endpoints, ECS Fargate deploy, GitHub Actions CI, Datadog metrics. Phase 2 — OPC-UA and Rate Limiting (4 weeks): OPC-UA adapter integration, Redis token bucket rate limiting, PATCH adapter-config hot-reload, schema version pinning endpoint, staging load test (2,000 connections, p99 validation). Phase 3 — Resilience and Archive (3 weeks): InfluxDB circuit breaker tuning, S3 Parquet continuous query export, ECS auto-scaling policy, consumer SDK documentation with schema migration guide.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with go.sum committed. All exported adapter interface methods documented with godoc. Table-driven unit tests for Normalize() on each adapter using raw protocol frame fixtures. Integration tests run against containerized InfluxDB and Redis in CI. go test -race mandatory in CI to catch goroutine data races in the normalization channel. Buffered channel depth and flush interval are configurable via environment variables (WRITE_BUFFER_DEPTH, BATCH_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS) — no hardcoded values in handler code. OpenAPI 3.1 spec generated from code annotations and committed to repo as source of truth for consumer teams.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: InfluxDB tag cardinality explosion if tag_name values include free-form device descriptions. Mitigation: tag_name allowlist validated at normalization layer against schema_mappings table; unmapped tags rejected with 422 and logged. Risk: SSE connection fan-out amplification — 10 SSE consumers per machine × 2,000 machines = 20,000 goroutines. Mitigation: per-machine SSE subscriber limit of 10, enforced via Redis ZSET count check. Tradeoff: bounded channel with overflow shedding means telemetry loss under extreme burst is acceptable to preserve service availability. Tradeoff: polling-based Modbus adapter versus interrupt-driven event model adds fixed latency (100ms poll interval) but simplifies connection lifecycle management.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Schema version pinning resolved via PATCH /v1/schema-version with consumer_subject scoping — each consumer service can pin independently during migration. PostgreSQL chosen for machine_assets registry over DynamoDB for JSONB adapter_config querying capability. Redis cluster over single-node Redis for rate limit state consistency across ECS task scale-out events.

metadata.notes: Round 3 weak-quality api_service: resolves backpressure and schema versioning issues from prior rounds; some sections at minimum word count reflecting weak quality distribution target.

profile.domain: Industrial Network OPC-UA Live Tag Monitor CLI

project_goal.rationale: Fills gap in network engineer tooling for PLC commissioning without requiring SCADA license

target_users.value: Industrial network engineers and automation commissioning technicians
target_users.rationale: Engineers connecting PLCs during factory automation startup and troubleshooting sessions

risk_level.rationale: Local-only tool with read-only OPC-UA access, no network exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: PLC tag values are internal operational data, not personal or financial

access_model.rationale: Local tool used by engineers who already have network access to the PLC

feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for commissioning troubleshooting workflow

mvp_scope.value: OPC-UA subscription to configurable node list, Textual TUI with live value/quality display, anomaly detection with SQLite logging
mvp_scope.rationale: Core monitoring workflow sufficient for initial release

security_baseline.value: OPC-UA connection supports Basic256Sha256 security policy when PLC requires it; SQLite log file stored in user home directory with OS-level permissions
security_baseline.rationale: Minimal security surface appropriate for local-only CLI tool

frontend_stack.value: Textual TUI framework for terminal dashboard rendering
frontend_stack.rationale: Textual provides rich terminal UI with reactive widgets suitable for live data display

backend_stack.rationale: asyncua is the de facto Python OPC-UA client; Typer for ergonomic CLI argument definition

data_platform.rationale: Zero-dependency embedded database appropriate for local log storage

project.goal: OPC-UA live tag monitoring CLI with terminal dashboard and anomaly logging
project.target_users: Industrial network engineers and automation commissioning technicians

backend.framework: asyncua for OPC-UA, Typer for CLI, Textual for TUI

security.auth: none required; OPC-UA Basic256Sha256 security policy support
security.transport: OPC-UA connection encrypted if PLC requires Basic256Sha256

data.db: SQLite via stdlib sqlite3 for anomaly_log_entries
data.cache: none

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with pytest and ruff lint
devops.hosting: pip package on PyPI or internal registry

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is tightly bounded and appropriate for the commissioning use case. The combination of live TUI dashboard and local anomaly logging covers both real-time awareness and post-session analysis. Anomaly band configuration should support both absolute range bounds and rate-of-change thresholds to cover common PLC startup failure modes.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Python asyncio fits naturally for concurrent OPC-UA subscription event handling and Textual TUI rendering. asyncua's subscription callback model maps cleanly to Textual's message-passing reactive model. SQLite writes should be deferred to a background thread to avoid blocking the asyncio event loop during anomaly log inserts.
reasoner_reviews.security: Local-only CLI with read-only OPC-UA access has minimal attack surface. OPC-UA Basic256Sha256 support should be documented but not required — anonymous mode is common on test PLCs. SQLite file path should default to user home directory to avoid requiring elevated permissions.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: pip install with pyproject.toml and no compiled C extensions ensures Windows/Linux compatibility. Textual dependency pulls in rich and asyncio which are standard Python 3.12 stdlib or PyPI packages. asyncua is pure Python OPC-UA implementation compatible with both platforms.

backend.service_design: Single async Python process: asyncio event loop drives OPC-UA subscription callbacks and Textual app concurrently using asyncio.run(). CLI entry point defined via Typer app with 'monitor' command accepting --endpoint, --nodes (multiple), --anomaly-low, --anomaly-high, --rate-threshold, --log-file flags.
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP API. CLI commands: opcua-monitor monitor --endpoint opc.tcp://192.168.1.10:4840 --nodes 'ns=2;i=1001' 'ns=2;i=1002' --anomaly-low 0 --anomaly-high 100 --log-file session.db; opcua-monitor export --log-file session.db --format csv
backend.failure_handling: asyncua subscription keeps-alive with 30s publish interval; on connection loss, CLI displays 'DISCONNECTED' banner in TUI and retries connection every 5s with exponential backoff up to 60s. On reconnect, re-subscribes to all configured node IDs.

data.entities: opc_ua_nodes (node_id TEXT, display_name TEXT, data_type TEXT, unit TEXT) — in-memory only. value_updates (node_id, value, quality_code, timestamp) — in-memory ring buffer of last 500 per node for TUI sparkline. anomaly_log_entries (id INTEGER PK, node_id TEXT, trigger_type ENUM range_violation/rate_violation, previous_value REAL, current_value REAL, quality_code TEXT, timestamp TEXT) — persisted to SQLite.
data.storage_design: SQLite database at configurable path (default ~/.opcua-monitor/sessions/<timestamp>.db); schema created on first run via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS. Writes via sqlite3 with WAL mode enabled for concurrent read access during session.
data.retention_and_deletion: SQLite files remain on local disk; no automatic cleanup. CLI emits warning if log file exceeds 100MB during session.

devops.deployment_topology: pip-installable package with pyproject.toml entry_points for opcua-monitor CLI command. Tested on Python 3.12 on ubuntu-latest and windows-latest GitHub Actions runners.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest unit tests (mocked asyncua subscription), build wheel, optionally publish to PyPI on tag push.
devops.devops_risks: asyncua and Textual version pinning required to avoid breaking API changes; pin in pyproject.toml with upper bounds.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a single-process Python asyncio CLI tool combining asyncua OPC-UA subscription callbacks with Textual TUI rendering. Key decisions: share the asyncio event loop between asyncua and Textual using asyncio.run() with Textual's async app model; defer SQLite writes to a thread pool executor to avoid blocking the event loop; use a ring buffer per node for sparkline display without unbounded memory growth.
target_output.title: OPC-UA Live Tag Monitor CLI
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 CLI tool for industrial network engineers that subscribes to OPC-UA monitored items on test PLCs, renders live tag values and quality codes in a Textual terminal dashboard, and logs anomalous value transitions (range violations and rate-of-change exceedances) to a local SQLite database for post-session analysis during automation startup troubleshooting.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Python asyncio application launched via 'opcua-monitor monitor' Typer command. The asyncua Client establishes a MonitoredItem subscription to the configured node list; subscription callbacks post TagUpdateMessage events to a Textual App message queue. The Textual TUI renders a live DataTable of current values, quality codes, and update rates per node alongside a per-node sparkline using an in-memory ring buffer of the last 500 readings. Anomaly detection runs inline in the callback handler; violations are written to SQLite via asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor() to keep writes off the main loop. On OPC-UA connection loss, an asyncio reconnect task retries every 5s with exponential backoff and displays a banner in the TUI status bar.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. OPC-UA client: asyncua 1.x (pure Python, Windows/Linux compatible). CLI parser: Typer 0.12 with click integration for --endpoint, --nodes, --anomaly-low, --anomaly-high, --rate-threshold flags. TUI: Textual 0.60 with DataTable and Sparkline widgets. Persistence: SQLite via Python stdlib sqlite3 in WAL mode. Distribution: pyproject.toml with Hatchling build backend, opcua-monitor console_scripts entry point. CI: GitHub Actions with pytest, ruff, mypy on ubuntu-latest and windows-latest.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Live Tag Dashboard: DataTable refreshed on every OPC-UA subscription callback showing node_id, display_name, current_value, quality_code, last_update_time, update_rate_hz per monitored item. Quality Code Coloring: good values shown in green, uncertain in yellow, bad in red using Textual Rich markup. Anomaly Detection: range violation triggered when value falls outside [anomaly_low, anomaly_high]; rate violation triggered when abs(delta/dt) exceeds rate_threshold per second. Anomaly Log View: toggleable panel listing recent anomaly_log_entries from SQLite with node ID, trigger type, and delta magnitude. Session Export: 'opcua-monitor export --log-file session.db --format csv' writes anomaly log to CSV for spreadsheet analysis.
target_output.system_components: Typer CLI Entry Point: parses flags and calls asyncio.run(monitor_app.run_async()). asyncua Subscription Handler: registers CreateMonitoredItemsRequest for all configured NodeIds; on_datachange callback posts TagUpdate to Textual message queue. Textual App: TagDashboard app with TagTableWidget (DataTable), AnomalyPanel (ListView), StatusBar (Label showing connection state and subscription count). In-Memory Ring Buffer: collections.deque(maxlen=500) per node_id for sparkline data. SQLite Anomaly Logger: ThreadPoolExecutor-backed writer inserting anomaly_log_entries with WAL-mode sqlite3 connection per worker thread. Reconnect Supervisor: asyncio Task that monitors subscription health and re-establishes connection on failure.
target_output.workflows: Startup and Subscription: engineer runs 'opcua-monitor monitor --endpoint opc.tcp://192.168.1.10:4840 --nodes ns=2;i=1001 ns=2;i=1002 --anomaly-low 0 --anomaly-high 100'; Typer parses args, asyncua client connects (anonymous or Basic256Sha256), creates subscription with 100ms publish interval and CreateMonitoredItems for each node; Textual app renders initial dashboard with 'CONNECTING' status. Live Update Flow: PLC publishes new tag value; asyncua on_datachange fires with NodeId, DataValue (value + quality StatusCode); callback appends to ring buffer, updates in-memory current_value dict, posts TagUpdate message to Textual; TagTableWidget on_message refreshes DataTable row for that node_id in <50ms. Anomaly Detection: same callback checks if new value is outside [anomaly_low, anomaly_high] or if abs(value - prev_value) / elapsed_ms * 1000 > rate_threshold; if violated, constructs AnomalyEntry and submits to ThreadPoolExecutor for SQLite INSERT; TUI highlights row in red and increments anomaly counter badge on AnomalyPanel tab.
target_output.data_model: In-memory: node_registry dict mapping node_id to {display_name, data_type, unit, last_value, last_quality, last_ts, update_count}. ring_buffers dict mapping node_id to deque(maxlen=500) of (timestamp, value) tuples for sparkline. SQLite (anomaly_log_entries): id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, node_id TEXT NOT NULL, trigger_type TEXT CHECK(trigger_type IN ('range_violation','rate_violation')), previous_value REAL, current_value REAL, quality_code TEXT, session_endpoint TEXT, timestamp TEXT NOT NULL. opc_ua_nodes are not persisted — loaded from CLI args each session.
target_output.api_design: CLI interface only — no HTTP API. Entry points: 'opcua-monitor monitor' with flags --endpoint TEXT (required, e.g. opc.tcp://192.168.1.10:4840), --nodes TEXT... (multiple NodeId strings, e.g. 'ns=2;i=1001'), --anomaly-low FLOAT (default -inf), --anomaly-high FLOAT (default inf), --rate-threshold FLOAT (default inf), --log-file PATH (default ~/.opcua-monitor/sessions/<iso_timestamp>.db), --security-policy [None|Basic256Sha256] (default None), --verbose / --no-verbose. 'opcua-monitor export' with --log-file PATH, --format [csv|json] (default csv), --output PATH.
target_output.security_and_compliance: OPC-UA security: Basic256Sha256 security policy support with client certificate generation on first run stored at ~/.opcua-monitor/certs/; anonymous mode default for test PLCs. No network server ports opened by this tool. SQLite file default path in user home directory requires no elevated permissions. No data transmitted to external services. stderr logging never includes OPC-UA values to prevent accidental log shipping of operational data when --verbose is used in shared terminal sessions.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: pyproject.toml with Hatchling build backend; requires-python = >=3.12; dependencies: asyncua>=1.0, textual>=0.60, typer>=0.12. GitHub Actions CI: matrix on ubuntu-latest and windows-latest, ruff check, mypy --strict, pytest tests/ with asyncua mocked via asynctest. Version tagged releases trigger 'publish' job to PyPI via trusted publisher OIDC. Installation: pip install opcua-monitor. No Docker, no service infrastructure.
target_output.observability: stderr structured logging with Python logging module at DEBUG/INFO/WARNING levels; --verbose flag enables DEBUG output including OPC-UA subscription status messages. TUI status bar displays: connection state, subscription ID, monitored item count, messages received per second, anomaly count for session. No remote telemetry or crash reporting.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost — runs entirely on engineer laptop. asyncua async subscription model handles up to ~500 monitored items per session within a single event loop without performance degradation. For sessions monitoring fewer than 50 nodes, Textual refresh latency stays under 50ms per update on standard hardware.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core MVP (3 weeks): asyncua subscription, Textual DataTable live display, quality code color coding, range and rate anomaly detection, SQLite anomaly log, CLI flags via Typer, GitHub Actions CI, pip-installable package on internal registry. Phase 2 — Polish and Export (1 week): AnomalyPanel tab with session log view, 'opcua-monitor export' command for CSV output, sparkline per node, reconnect supervisor with TUI status banner. Phase 3 — Protocol Expansion (future): MQTT subscriber mode, Modbus TCP polling mode, session replay from SQLite log.
target_output.development_guidelines: All async functions use asyncio; no threading except ThreadPoolExecutor for SQLite writes. mypy --strict enforced in CI. Unit tests mock asyncua.Client and subscription callbacks using pytest-asyncio. Textual components tested with Textual's App.run_test() context manager. ruff with isort and flake8-style rules as single lint tool. Node ID parsing validated with regex before passing to asyncua to provide clear user error messages for malformed node strings.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Textual and asyncua both use asyncio; event loop contention if subscription callbacks are slow. Mitigation: callbacks are non-blocking (ring buffer append + Textual post_message); all I/O (SQLite) deferred to executor. Risk: OPC-UA server certificate validation fails for self-signed PLC certs. Mitigation: --security-policy None default for test environments; Basic256Sha256 mode requires manual certificate acceptance on first connection. Tradeoff: SQLite WAL mode adds marginal write overhead but enables concurrent read access for 'export' command running against an active session log.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: asyncua chosen over opcua (opcua-asyncio) for Python 3.12 asyncio compatibility and active maintenance. Textual chosen over urwid or curses for reactive widget model compatible with asyncio message passing. SQLite WAL mode enabled to allow 'opcua-monitor export' to read log while a session is still active.

metadata.notes: Strong first-pass cli_tool: OPC-UA live monitoring with asyncio/Textual/SQLite; no FrontendAgent subplan per cli_tool specialist gating rules.

project_goal.value: Migrate preventive maintenance scheduling from spreadsheets to a centralized system with auto-generated work orders, technician task management, spare parts reservation, and compliance certificate tracking for semiconductor fab equipment
project_goal.rationale: Excel-based tracking causes missed PM events and certificate lapses on critical lithography and etch tools

target_users.value: Maintenance technicians, metrology engineers, fab supervisors, and maintenance managers at a semiconductor equipment manufacturer
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Requires Vue 3 SPA frontend plus FastAPI backend with complex scheduling and batch logic

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, admin_panel, integrations, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Scheduling, task dispatch, ERP integration, and nightly batch triggers all required

complexity_level.rationale: Complex scheduling logic and SAP ERP integration but bounded domain with established data model

risk_level.rationale: Internal system handling operational data; missed PM events have direct production impact

data_sensitivity.rationale: Equipment and maintenance records are proprietary operational data, not personal or financial

external_exposure.rationale: System deployed inside corporate network; no external partner or public access needed

access_model.value: RBAC with Cognito groups: technician, metrology_engineer, supervisor, manager, admin
access_model.rationale: Different roles require different action permissions on work orders, certificates, and schedules

feature_scope.rationale: Full scope to replace all spreadsheet workflows

mvp_scope.value: PM schedule management, nightly idempotent work order generation, technician task completion UI, basic spare parts reservation, compliance certificate expiry alerting within 30 days
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP eliminates highest-risk gaps: missed PM events and certificate lapses on metrology tools

security_baseline.value: Cognito authentication with RBAC groups, TLS 1.3 in transit, RDS encryption at rest, append-only audit log for certificate and work order mutations
security_baseline.rationale: Corporate security baseline for internal operational systems with ISO/IEC 17025 audit requirements

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript and Pinia
frontend_stack.rationale: Frontend team expertise; PrimeVue component library suits form-heavy admin and checklist UIs

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI and SQLAlchemy 2.0
backend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; async FastAPI suits mixed IO and batch scheduling workloads

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ with Redis 7 ElastiCache
data_platform.rationale: Relational model fits scheduling domain; Redis caches SAP parts data and session metadata

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-west-2
hosting_target.rationale: Existing AWS footprint; Fargate removes cluster management overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Maintenance records and calibration certificates retained 10 years per fab document control policy; application logs retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Equipment maintenance history required for process audit trails and regulatory inspections

constraints.rationale: SAP integration is non-negotiable for parts availability and inventory reservation data

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM, structured JSON logs to CloudWatch, PagerDuty alerts for batch job failures and certificate expiry anomalies
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; cert expiry failures need immediate on-call alerting

project.goal: Replace spreadsheet PM tracking with centralized scheduling and certificate management
project.target_users: Maintenance technicians, metrology engineers, fab supervisors, maintenance managers

backend.framework: FastAPI with SQLAlchemy 2.0

security.auth: Cognito user pools with RBAC groups
security.audit: append-only PostgreSQL audit_log for certificate and work order mutations

data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache

constraints.erp_integration: SAP RFC calls for spare parts availability

open_questions.sap_auth: Determine SAP RFC credential rotation approach

reasoner_reviews.product: The PM scheduling domain is well-scoped and the migration rationale is clear. Certificate expiry alerting addresses the most operationally critical risk since missed recertification on metrology tools triggers tool quarantine and production holds. The spare parts reservation flow must account for SAP downtime scenarios to avoid blocking technicians mid-task.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with APScheduler is appropriate for nightly batch work order generation. The critical gap from round 1 is idempotency: the batch job must use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING keyed on (equipment_id, schedule_id, trigger_date) to survive ECS task restarts without creating duplicate work orders. SAP RFC adapter needs tenacity retry and pybreaker circuit breaker with Redis fallback cache.
reasoner_reviews.security: RBAC groups in Cognito are sufficient for role gating. The audit_log table must be append-only for ISO/IEC 17025 traceability — a PostgreSQL trigger should prevent UPDATE and DELETE on audit rows. Certificate record mutations require full before/after snapshots in old_value_json and new_value_json columns.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: SAP RFC call latency can block the technician spare parts lookup path during peak PM windows. Caching SAP parts availability in Redis at 15-minute TTL resolves this. The $2,000/month budget is achievable at moderate scale with t3.medium RDS and 2-task Fargate baseline.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round-1 phased implementation described phases in one-sentence summaries without sprint-level feature assignments, making it unusable for sprint planning. Deployment section omitted Fargate task sizing and auto-scaling policy. These must both be corrected in round 2.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with domain modules: schedules (PM schedule CRUD and trigger evaluation), work_orders (generation with idempotent upsert, assignment, status state machine), tasks (technician checklist step execution and measurement recording), spare_parts (SAP RFC adapter with Redis cache and reservation lifecycle), certificates (ISO/IEC 17025 tracking, expiry scan, alert dispatch), admin (user management, equipment asset CRUD, CSV import)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0 at /api/v1; all endpoints require Cognito JWT; RBAC enforced via FastAPI RBACDepends dependency; idempotency enforced at database level via UNIQUE constraint on work_orders(equipment_id, schedule_id, trigger_date)
backend.background_jobs: APScheduler cron at 00:30 fab-local time for PM work order generation using INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (equipment_id, schedule_id, trigger_date) DO NOTHING; separate daily cron at 06:00 for certificate expiry scan querying compliance_certificates where expiry_date <= now() + 30 days and status = valid, enqueuing SNS alerts to metrology team
backend.failure_handling: SAP RFC adapter wrapped in tenacity retry with exponential backoff (3 retries, 30s cap); pybreaker circuit breaker transitions adapter to open state after 3 consecutive failures, returns Redis-cached parts data; failed SAP syncs inserted into retry_queue table with dead-letter PagerDuty alert after 3 exhausted retries
backend.scaling_notes: api service scales on CPU 60% target (min 2, max 4 Fargate tasks); scheduler service runs as single task with no auto-scaling; Redis SAP cache reduces RFC call volume by ~85% during normal operations

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Pinia store modules: auth (Cognito token state), workOrders (queue and status), schedules (PM schedule list and calendar), certificates (registry and expiry tracking), spareParts (SAP search and reservation); route guards enforce RBAC group membership
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (Cognito hosted UI redirect), Equipment Asset List, PM Schedule Calendar (trigger date timeline per asset), Work Order Queue (technician view filtered by assigned_technician_id), Task Checklist Stepper (step-by-step completion with measurement inputs and parts logging), Spare Parts Search Dialog (SAP-backed with availability indicators), Certificate Tracker (per-equipment with CertExpiryBadge urgency colors), Supervisor Dashboard (overdue count, upcoming PM calendar, cert expiry summary), Admin Panel (user management, equipment CRUD, CSV bulk import)
frontend.state_management: Pinia for work order and certificate state with optimistic updates on task completion; TanStack Query for server cache invalidation and background refresh on work order queue
frontend.ui_modules: PMScheduleCalendar showing trigger dates on equipment asset timelines, TaskChecklistStepper for guided technician workflows with conditional measurement input fields, CertExpiryBadge with color-coded urgency (green/amber/red), SAPPartsSearchDialog with cached availability indicators and reservation action

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with hosted UI redirect; five RBAC groups (technician, metrology_engineer, supervisor, manager, admin) with JWT custom claims carrying group membership; FastAPI RBACDepends validates group per endpoint at request time
security.authorization_model: Technicians: complete only their assigned tasks; Metrology engineers: manage compliance certificates; Supervisors: view all work orders, reassign tasks, approve completions; Managers: create and edit PM schedules; Admin: user management, equipment CRUD, CSV import
security.audit_and_logging_controls: PostgreSQL audit_log table (id UUID, user_id, action ENUM create/update/approve/reject/delete, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, old_value_json JSONB, new_value_json JSONB, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) with trigger BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE that raises exception to enforce append-only semantics required for ISO/IEC 17025 traceability
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager for SAP RFC credentials and RDS passwords; 30-day automatic rotation; secrets injected at ECS task startup via task definition secretsFrom references; no credentials in environment variables or source code

data.entities: equipment_assets (id UUID PK, asset_tag VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, tool_type ENUM lithography/etch/cvd/pvd/metrology/other, location, status ENUM active/quarantined/retired, secs_gem_id, created_at). pm_schedules (id UUID PK, equipment_id FK, pm_type ENUM calendar/runtime_hours, interval_days, interval_hours, procedure_id FK, last_completed_at, next_due_at). work_orders (id UUID PK, schedule_id FK, equipment_id FK, trigger_date DATE, status ENUM pending/assigned/in_progress/completed/cancelled, assigned_technician_id FK, generated_at, due_date, completed_at, UNIQUE(equipment_id, schedule_id, trigger_date)). technician_tasks (id UUID PK, work_order_id FK, step_number INT, description, requires_measurement BOOL, measurement_value NUMERIC, actual_duration_minutes INT, parts_used_json JSONB, completed_by FK, completed_at). spare_parts_reservations (id UUID PK, work_order_id FK, part_number, sap_material_id, quantity_reserved INT, reservation_status ENUM pending/confirmed/consumed/released, reserved_at). compliance_certificates (id UUID PK, equipment_id FK, cert_type, certificate_number, issuing_lab, calibration_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, status ENUM valid/expiring_soon/expired/renewed, document_s3_key, recorded_by FK, recorded_at). maintenance_history (id UUID PK, equipment_id FK, work_order_id FK, summary, technician_id FK, completed_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ for all relational data; S3 with SSE-KMS for calibration certificate PDF uploads with block-public-access enforced; Redis 7 ElastiCache for SAP parts availability cache (15-min TTL) and Cognito session metadata
data.retention_and_deletion: maintenance_history and compliance_certificates retained 10 years per fab document control; equipment_assets soft-deleted with retired status; S3 Glacier lifecycle transition for certificate PDFs older than 2 years; CloudWatch logs retained 1 year

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster us-west-2: api service (min 2 / max 4 tasks, 0.5 vCPU / 1024 MB each, CPU 60% scale-out target, scale-in cooldown 300s); scheduler service (1 task, 0.25 vCPU / 512 MB, no auto-scaling); ALB with HTTPS listener; RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ in private subnets; ElastiCache cache.t3.micro Redis 7 in private subnets; S3 + CloudFront for Vue 3 SPA
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy strict type check, pytest (80% coverage gate on routes and batch logic), Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deployment (minimum 100% healthy), Alembic migration as ECS one-off task before service update; Vue 3 build: ESLint, Vitest unit tests, npm build to S3
devops.environments: dev (single AZ, t3.micro RDS, shared Redis, mock SAP adapter), staging (Multi-AZ, full SAP sandbox RFC integration, complete security controls), production (Multi-AZ us-west-2a/2b, auto-scaling, New Relic APM, PagerDuty)
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS deployment circuit breaker enabled; previous task definition revision available for manual rollback within 30 minutes; Alembic downgrade script tested in staging before any production schema migration

ISS-001.title: Work order generation not idempotent — duplicate orders on ECS task restart
ISS-001.detail: Round-1 plan used a simple INSERT for nightly work order generation. If the APScheduler ECS task restarts mid-run, duplicate work orders are created for the same equipment/schedule/trigger_date combination, causing technicians to execute the same PM twice and corrupting maintenance history.

history.note: Identified during architect reasoner review of batch job design

ISS-002.title: Phased implementation lacks sprint-level feature breakdown
ISS-002.detail: Round-1 phased_implementation described three phases with one-sentence summaries. Fab supervisors and engineering leads cannot use this for sprint planning or stakeholder milestone reviews.

history.note: Identified by critic reasoner

ISS-003.detail: Round-1 deployment section did not specify vCPU/memory allocations for ECS Fargate tasks or the auto-scaling target metric, making infrastructure provisioning ambiguous and preventing accurate cost estimation.

history.note: Identified by critic reasoner

target_output.title: Preventive Maintenance Scheduling System — Semiconductor Fab Migration
target_output.executive_summary: A Vue 3 and FastAPI fullstack application migrating a semiconductor equipment manufacturer from Excel-based PM tracking to a centralized scheduling system on AWS ECS Fargate. Nightly batch jobs auto-generate idempotent work orders based on calendar and runtime-hour triggers, feeding technician task checklists with step-by-step guidance and measurement recording. Spare parts are reserved against SAP ERP inventory via a Redis-cached RFC adapter. Calibration certificates for metrology tools are tracked with 30-day expiry alerts to prevent ISO/IEC 17025 compliance lapses and tool quarantine events.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Vue 3 SPA served from S3 via CloudFront communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI REST API on ECS Fargate (api service, 2-4 tasks) behind an ALB. A dedicated APScheduler ECS service (scheduler, 1 task) runs the nightly PM work order generation and daily certificate expiry scan. The API connects to PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ for transactional data and Redis 7 on ElastiCache for SAP parts availability cache (15-min TTL) and Cognito session metadata. SAP ERP integration uses an RFC adapter module with tenacity retry and pybreaker circuit breaker. Cognito user pools with RBAC groups provide authentication; JWT custom claims encode group membership validated per endpoint via FastAPI RBACDepends. All certificate and work order mutations are captured in an append-only audit_log table enforced by a PostgreSQL trigger, satisfying ISO/IEC 17025 traceability requirements.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, TanStack Query, PrimeVue, Vue Router. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, APScheduler 3.x, tenacity, pybreaker, pyrfc (SAP RFC). Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ. Cache: Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (api + scheduler services), ALB, S3 + CloudFront (SPA + certificate PDFs), ECR, AWS Secrets Manager. Auth: AWS Cognito user pools. Monitoring: New Relic APM, CloudWatch Logs, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: PM Schedule Management: CRUD for calendar-trigger and runtime-hour-trigger schedules per equipment asset with configurable intervals and linked procedure documents. Idempotent Work Order Generation: nightly batch producing orders per triggered schedule using UNIQUE(equipment_id, schedule_id, trigger_date) and ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING upsert. Technician Task Execution: step-by-step checklist UI with conditional measurement input fields, labor time recording, parts consumption logging, and calibration certificate number entry. Spare Parts Reservation: SAP RFC lookup with Redis cache, reservation lifecycle (pending/confirmed/consumed/released). Certificate Tracker: per-equipment certificate registry with expiry countdown badges and automated 30-day SNS alert dispatch. Supervisor Dashboard: overdue work order count, upcoming PM calendar, certificate expiry summary by urgency tier. Admin Panel: user management, equipment asset CRUD, PM schedule CSV bulk import for spreadsheet migration.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution serving Vue 3 SPA from S3 origin. ALB with HTTPS listener routing to ECS Fargate api service (min 2 / max 4 tasks, 0.5 vCPU / 1024 MB each). APScheduler ECS scheduler service (1 task, 0.25 vCPU / 512 MB). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (db.t3.medium, private subnets). ElastiCache Redis 7 (cache.t3.micro, private subnets) for SAP cache and session metadata. AWS Secrets Manager for SAP RFC credentials and DB passwords (30-day rotation). S3 bucket with SSE-KMS for calibration certificate PDF uploads. SNS topic for certificate expiry and batch failure notifications routing to PagerDuty.
target_output.workflows: PM Work Order Generation: APScheduler cron at 00:30 fab-local time queries pm_schedules where next_due_at <= now() + 24h; for each triggered schedule executes INSERT INTO work_orders(equipment_id, schedule_id, trigger_date, status, due_date) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (equipment_id, schedule_id, trigger_date) DO NOTHING; assigns order to a certified technician based on skill lookup; emits SNS notification to assignee. Technician Task Completion: Technician opens assigned work order on Vue 3 SPA, steps through TaskChecklistStepper recording measurements and parts used, enters calibration certificate numbers for metrology steps, submits completion; API updates work_order status to completed, appends to maintenance_history, runs certificate expiry check and dispatches 30-day alert via SNS if expiry is within window.
target_output.data_model: equipment_assets (id UUID PK, asset_tag VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, tool_type ENUM lithography/etch/cvd/pvd/metrology/other, location, status ENUM active/quarantined/retired, secs_gem_id, created_at). pm_schedules (id UUID PK, equipment_id FK, pm_type ENUM calendar/runtime_hours, interval_days, interval_hours, procedure_id FK, last_completed_at, next_due_at). work_orders (id UUID PK, schedule_id FK, equipment_id FK, trigger_date DATE, status ENUM pending/assigned/in_progress/completed/cancelled, assigned_technician_id FK, generated_at, due_date, completed_at, UNIQUE(equipment_id, schedule_id, trigger_date)). technician_tasks (id UUID PK, work_order_id FK, step_number INT, description, requires_measurement BOOL, measurement_value NUMERIC, actual_duration_minutes INT, parts_used_json JSONB, completed_by FK, completed_at). spare_parts_reservations (id UUID PK, work_order_id FK, part_number, sap_material_id, quantity_reserved INT, reservation_status ENUM pending/confirmed/consumed/released, reserved_at). compliance_certificates (id UUID PK, equipment_id FK, cert_type, certificate_number, issuing_lab, calibration_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, status ENUM valid/expiring_soon/expired/renewed, document_s3_key, recorded_by FK, recorded_at). maintenance_history (id UUID PK, equipment_id FK, work_order_id FK, summary, technician_id FK, completed_at).
target_output.api_design: RESTful at /api/v1. GET /equipment?tool_type=metrology&status=active (list assets). POST /pm-schedules (create schedule). GET /work-orders?status=pending&technician_id=X (technician queue). PATCH /work-orders/:id/tasks/:step/complete (record step with measurement and parts). POST /work-orders/:id/complete (finalize order, update maintenance_history). GET /certificates?equipment_id=X (registry). POST /certificates (record new certificate with S3 upload). GET /certificates?expiring_within_days=30 (expiry report). POST /spare-parts/reserve (SAP lookup and reserve). GET /dashboard/supervisor (overdue count, upcoming PM, cert expiry by urgency). All endpoints require Cognito JWT; RBAC enforced per route.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito hosted UI redirect with user pool groups (technician, metrology_engineer, supervisor, manager, admin); JWT custom claims carry group; FastAPI RBACDepends validates group per endpoint. TLS 1.3 on ALB and CloudFront. Append-only audit_log table (user_id, action ENUM, resource_type, resource_id, old_value_json JSONB, new_value_json JSONB, timestamp) with PostgreSQL BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising exception to enforce immutability required for ISO/IEC 17025 audit traceability. SAP RFC credentials in Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. S3 certificate PDF bucket with SSE-KMS and block-public-access. CloudWatch log retention 1 year.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster us-west-2: api service (min 2 tasks, max 4 tasks, 0.5 vCPU / 1024 MB each, CPU utilization 60% scale-out target, scale-in cooldown 300s); scheduler service (1 task, 0.25 vCPU / 512 MB, no auto-scaling). RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.t3.medium Multi-AZ in private subnets (us-west-2a/2b). ElastiCache cache.t3.micro Redis 7 in private subnets. ALB with HTTPS listener, WAF with AWS managed rules. S3 + CloudFront for Vue 3 SPA. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest 80% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deployment (minimum 100% healthy during update), Alembic migration as ECS one-off task before service update. Vue 3 pipeline: ESLint, Vitest unit tests, Vite build, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation. Environments: dev (single AZ, t3.micro RDS, mock SAP adapter), staging (Multi-AZ, SAP sandbox RFC integration, full security controls), production. Rollback: ECS deployment circuit breaker; previous task definition revision for manual rollback; Alembic downgrade script validated in staging.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM agent injected via ECS task environment variable. Structured JSON logs (equipment_id, work_order_id, action, duration_ms, correlation_id) to CloudWatch Logs. Custom New Relic dashboards: nightly batch work order generation count and duration, certificate expiry counts by urgency tier (valid/expiring_soon/expired), SAP adapter circuit breaker state (open/closed/half-open), p95 API latency per endpoint, RDS connection pool utilization. PagerDuty alerts on: batch job failure, certificate expiry scan error, SAP circuit breaker open > 5 minutes, RDS connections > 80%, api service p99 latency > 3s.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1,400/month at 200 equipment assets and 500 work orders/month: ECS api service 2-task baseline ~$180, scheduler ~$25, RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ ~$120, ElastiCache cache.t3.micro ~$25, CloudFront + S3 ~$30, Secrets Manager + CloudWatch + SNS ~$20. Well within $2,000/month budget with ~$600 headroom. Scale path: increase api service max tasks to 8 and upgrade RDS to db.t3.large when monthly work order volume exceeds 2,000.
target_output.phased_implementation: MVP Phase (Sprints 1-5, weeks 1-10): Sprint 1 — project scaffold, Cognito RBAC integration, equipment_assets CRUD API + Vue list page, Alembic schema baseline; Sprint 2 — pm_schedules CRUD, PMScheduleCalendar component, APScheduler batch skeleton with idempotent upsert, unit tests for conflict resolution logic; Sprint 3 — work order generation end-to-end, technician work order queue UI, TaskChecklistStepper with measurement inputs; Sprint 4 — spare parts reservation with SAP RFC adapter (tenacity + pybreaker), Redis cache layer, SAPPartsSearchDialog; Sprint 5 — compliance_certificates CRUD, daily expiry scan batch, CertExpiryBadge component, SNS alert dispatch, append-only audit_log trigger. MVP acceptance criteria: technicians complete assigned work orders with parts and measurements logged; metrology team receives 30-day expiry alerts. Post-MVP Phase (Sprints 6-8, weeks 11-16): Sprint 6 — supervisor dashboard (overdue count, upcoming PM calendar, cert expiry summary), maintenance_history timeline view; Sprint 7 — admin panel (user management, equipment CRUD, CSV bulk import for spreadsheet migration); Sprint 8 — New Relic dashboards, PagerDuty alert tuning, load and failover testing, production cutover and spreadsheet decommission.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff linting, mypy strict mode, Black formatting enforced in CI. pytest with 80% line coverage gate on FastAPI route handlers and APScheduler batch job logic including idempotency edge cases. Vue 3: ESLint + Prettier, Vitest for Pinia store unit tests, Playwright E2E covering work order completion flow and certificate submission. PR requires two approvals for changes to work_orders schema, APScheduler batch logic, or audit_log trigger. Trunk-based development with feature flags for SAP integration toggle in staging.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SAP RFC call latency blocks technician spare parts lookup during peak PM windows. Mitigation: Redis cache at 15-min TTL with stale-while-revalidate; pybreaker circuit breaker returns cached data during SAP outage. Risk: CSV import from legacy spreadsheets produces data quality issues (malformed dates, duplicate asset tags). Mitigation: admin bulk import validates against asset_tag UNIQUE constraint and reports row-level errors before committing. Tradeoff: APScheduler single-task service vs. Celery distributed queue — simpler architecture acceptable at 500 orders/month; revisit when volume exceeds 5,000/month. Risk: certificate PDF uploads blocked if S3 unreachable during task completion. Mitigation: allow technicians to enter certificate number inline and attach PDF retrospectively.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose APScheduler over Celery Beat to avoid Redis broker dependency for low-frequency nightly jobs. SAP RFC adapter uses pyrfc library with connection pooling; credentials in Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Selected PrimeVue over Vuetify for stronger TypeScript support and better data table components for work order queues. Alembic chosen over Django ORM migrations for SQLAlchemy 2.0 compatibility in FastAPI.

profile.domain: Smart Factory Machine Catalog and Capability Registry API

project_goal.value: REST API service providing a standardized machine catalog and capability registry for a smart factory platform, enabling production scheduling systems to query machine availability, supported operations, tooling configurations, and real-time status before dispatching work orders
project_goal.rationale: Central source of truth for machine capabilities eliminates hardcoded machine knowledge in scheduling logic

target_users.value: Production scheduling systems, MES dispatchers, and shop-floor operator terminals consuming via REST
target_users.rationale: Service-to-service API; human users are indirect consumers via scheduling dashboards

project_class.rationale: Pure REST API with no UI; consumed by other internal platform services

capabilities.rationale: Internal API with auth, PostgreSQL persistence, Redis cache, and ERP integration for master data sync

complexity_level.rationale: Standard CRUD registry with caching and ERP sync; no ML or realtime streaming complexity

risk_level.rationale: Scheduling decisions depend on registry accuracy; stale data could cause work order mis-dispatch

data_sensitivity.rationale: Machine configuration and tooling data is proprietary operational data, not personal or financial

external_exposure.rationale: Registry API consumed only by internal scheduling and MES services within VPC

access_model.value: JWT bearer tokens per consumer service; read:machines scope for queries, write:machines scope for status updates
access_model.rationale: Fine-grained scope separation prevents scheduling systems from inadvertently mutating machine config

feature_scope.value: Machine catalog CRUD, operation capability queries, tooling configuration management, real-time status updates, capacity window queries, scheduling reservation hold
feature_scope.rationale: Full registry feature set for scheduling integration

mvp_scope.value: Machine catalog, operation capability queries filtered by operation code and shift window, status PATCH endpoint, Redis caching of capability queries
mvp_scope.rationale: Core query path used by scheduling system in MVP integration

security_baseline.value: JWT RS256 validation, TLS 1.3 in transit, input validation on all write endpoints, PostgreSQL parameterized queries
security_baseline.rationale: Standard internal service security baseline

frontend_stack.value: none - API service only
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI; consumed programmatically by internal services

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Hono framework
backend_stack.rationale: Hono provides lightweight request routing with TypeScript-first design and low overhead per request

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for machine registry persistence, Redis 7 for capability query caching
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational capability queries with JSONB for tooling configs; Redis for sub-10ms cached responses

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run with Cloud SQL PostgreSQL and Memorystore Redis
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run serverless scaling appropriate for scheduling query burst patterns; Cloud SQL for managed PostgreSQL

privacy_retention_policy.value: Machine configuration records retained indefinitely; status change audit log retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Machine configurations are operational master data; status audit needed for scheduling dispute resolution

future_scope.value: GraphQL query interface, real-time status push via Pub/Sub, scheduling reservation holds, ERP master data sync via scheduled job
future_scope.rationale: Enhanced query flexibility and bidirectional ERP integration planned for v2

constraints.value: Capability query p99 under 50ms with cache; must integrate with SAP ERP for machine master data import; no external cloud regions
constraints.rationale: Scheduling system SLA requires sub-50ms registry responses; SAP is source of truth for machine master data

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus metrics, structured JSON logging, cache hit rate dashboard
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing platform monitoring stack

execution_preference.value: OpenAPI 3.1 contract published before implementation; consumer teams integrate against mock server
execution_preference.rationale: Scheduling and MES teams need stable contract before backend is ready

project.goal: Machine catalog and capability registry REST API for smart factory scheduling integration
project.target_users: Production scheduling systems and MES services

backend.framework: Hono with TypeScript, zod for input validation

security.auth: JWT RS256 with scope-based authorization
security.transport: TLS 1.3 via Cloud Run managed TLS
security.validation: zod schema validation on all request bodies

data.cache: Memorystore Redis 7 for capability query results

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with TypeScript compile, eslint, vitest

constraints.latency: p99 capability query under 50ms with cache hit
constraints.integration: SAP ERP machine master data import

confirmed_decisions.status_invalidation: PATCH /machines/:id/status invalidates machine capability cache entries

reasoner_reviews.product: Registry design is well-scoped for the scheduling integration use case. The operation capability query with shift window filtering is the critical hot path — cache hit rate on this endpoint must be monitored closely. Status change invalidation of cache entries is correctly specified.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Hono on Node.js 22 is a good fit — lightweight and TypeScript-native. Previous round missed specifying the cache key structure for capability queries, which affects cache hit rate under varying operation code and shift window parameters. Need deterministic cache key generation from query parameters to maximize hit rate.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT RS256 scope separation between read:machines and write:machines is correct. Previous round did not specify how the SAP ERP integration authenticates — this import job needs its own service account JWT with write:machines scope and IP allowlisting to prevent external abuse of the machine registration endpoint.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cloud Run cold start latency could push p99 above 50ms for infrequent capability queries. Minimum instances = 1 setting in Cloud Run configuration required to eliminate cold starts for the scheduling integration path.

backend.service_design: Hono app with three route groups: /v1/machines (catalog CRUD), /v1/capabilities (operation capability queries), /v1/status (machine status updates). Middleware stack: JWT validation, scope enforcement, zod request validation, Prometheus request duration histogram. Repository pattern: MachineRepository (Cloud SQL), CapabilityCache (Redis).
backend.api_patterns: GET /v1/capabilities?operation_code=MILL_FACE&shift_window=2026-03-20T06:00:00Z/2026-03-20T14:00:00Z&min_capacity=1 — returns ranked list of eligible machines. GET /v1/machines/:id — full machine record with tooling config. PATCH /v1/machines/:id/status {status, reason} — updates status and invalidates cache. POST /v1/machines — registers new machine (write:machines scope). GET /v1/machines/:id/tooling-configs — returns available tooling configurations.
backend.failure_handling: Redis cache miss falls through to PostgreSQL query; if PostgreSQL query exceeds 200ms, response proceeds without caching and slow_query_total counter incremented. Circuit breaker on SAP ERP import job; if SAP unavailable, existing records retained with last_synced_at staleness warning in GET /v1/machines response.

data.entities: machines (machine_id UUID PK, asset_name, machine_class ENUM cnc/robot/press/conveyor, cell_location, status ENUM available/in_use/maintenance/offline, tooling_config_ids UUID[], last_status_at TIMESTAMPTZ, erp_asset_number), operation_capabilities (capability_id UUID PK, machine_id FK, operation_code TEXT, min_cycle_time_sec INT, setup_time_sec INT, supported_material_groups TEXT[], valid_from DATE, valid_until DATE), tooling_configurations (tooling_id UUID PK, machine_id FK, tool_set_name, tooling_spec_json JSONB, installed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, active BOOL), capacity_windows (window_id UUID PK, machine_id FK, shift_label, available_from TIMESTAMPTZ, available_until TIMESTAMPTZ, reserved_capacity INT), scheduling_reservations (reservation_id UUID PK, machine_id FK, work_order_id, reserved_from TIMESTAMPTZ, reserved_until TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM held/confirmed/released)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL with composite index on operation_capabilities (operation_code, machine_id) for capability lookup. Redis cache key pattern: 'cap:v1:{operation_code}:{shift_window_hash}:{min_capacity}' with 60s TTL; invalidated by PATCH /v1/machines/:id/status.
data.consistency_notes: Status updates and cache invalidation in same Hono handler: PATCH status persists to PostgreSQL then calls Redis DEL on affected capability cache keys. No distributed transaction needed — stale cache serving for max 60s is acceptable per product requirement.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service with min-instances=1, max-instances=10, concurrency=80. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (db-standard-2) in same GCP region. Memorystore Redis 7 (basic tier, 1GB). Secret Manager for JWT public key, database credentials, Redis password.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: tsc --noEmit, eslint, vitest unit tests, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy via gcloud run deploy. Integration tests run against Cloud SQL Auth Proxy + local Redis in CI via docker-compose.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus custom metrics via prom-client: http_request_duration_seconds histogram (by route and status_code), cache_hit_total / cache_miss_total by endpoint, machine_status_changes_total by new_status. Grafana dashboard with cache hit rate panel and capability query p99 latency.

ISS-001.title: Cache key structure not deterministic for capability queries
ISS-001.detail: Capability query endpoint caches results but cache key was derived from raw query string which varies by parameter order, causing near-zero cache hit rate in staging. Need normalized cache key from sorted canonical query parameters.

history.note: Identified in staging integration test: cache hit rate 2% due to parameter order variation in scheduling system requests

ISS-002.title: SAP ERP import service account not specified
ISS-002.detail: SAP ERP master data import job authentication path not defined in round 1 plan. Need dedicated service account JWT with write:machines scope and IP allowlist for SAP integration server.

history.note: Raised by security reviewer: import job auth gap leaves machine registration endpoint potentially open to unauthenticated calls

focus_issues.title: Cache key structure not deterministic for capability queries
focus_issues.detail: Near-zero cache hit rate due to non-canonical query parameter ordering in cache key derivation.

focus_issues.title: SAP ERP import service account not specified
focus_issues.detail: Import job authentication path undefined; machine registration endpoint has auth coverage gap.

latest_recommendations: Normalize capability query cache key by sorting and hashing canonical query parameters
Define SAP ERP import service account JWT with dedicated write:machines scope and IP allowlist enforcement
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Smart Factory Machine Registry API — Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two medium issues unresolved from round 1

previous_audits.summary: Registry design is solid and well-structured but two medium issues prevent production readiness: cache key non-determinism causes near-zero hit rate, and SAP ERP import auth is undefined.
strengths: Operation capability query design with shift window filtering maps well to scheduling use case
Redis cache invalidation on status PATCH is correctly specified
JWT scope separation between read and write operations is clean
concerns: ISS-001: Non-canonical cache key causing near-zero hit rate in staging — p99 latency will miss 50ms target
ISS-002: SAP ERP import job auth not specified — security gap on write:machines endpoint
blocking_issues: ISS-001: p99 capability query latency exceeds 50ms SLA without effective caching

Specify SAP import service account: dedicated JWT issuer, write:machines scope, Cloud Run ingress IP allowlist for SAP server IP range

issue_updates.title: Cache key structure not deterministic for capability queries
issue_updates.detail: Cache key derived from raw URL query string; parameter order from scheduling system varied, causing each unique request to miss cache

issue_updates.title: SAP ERP import service account not specified
