previous_plan.title: Smart Factory Machine Registry API — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Node.js 22 Hono REST API serving machine capability queries to production scheduling system; PostgreSQL 16 for registry persistence, Redis for caching, deployed on Cloud Run.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Hono request router with JWT middleware, capability query handler fetching from PostgreSQL with Redis cache fallback; status PATCH invalidates cache.
previous_plan.data_model: machines, operation_capabilities, tooling_configurations defined; cache key derived from raw query string without normalization.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256 validation and scope enforcement specified; SAP ERP import job authentication not addressed.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 (non-deterministic cache key) by implementing a canonical cache key function that sorts capability query parameters, serializes to stable JSON, and SHA256-hashes the result — guaranteeing identical keys regardless of parameter order. ISS-002 resolved by defining a dedicated SAP ERP import service account JWT with write:machines scope issued by an internal JWKS endpoint, combined with Cloud Run ingress IP allowlist for the SAP integration server IP range. Both fixes are straightforward Hono middleware changes with clear test coverage paths.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented canonicalCacheKey(params) function: extracts operation_code, shift_window_start, shift_window_end, and min_capacity from query params, sorts keys alphabetically, serializes to stable JSON string, and SHA256-hashes for 32-byte Redis key. Cache key format: 'cap:v1:{SHA256(canonical_json)}'. Applied in CapabilityQueryHandler before Redis lookup. Unit-tested against 10 parameter-order permutations asserting identical key output.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cache hit rate rises from ~2% to expected 70-80% in staging based on scheduling system query pattern analysis; p99 capability query latency under 50ms SLA met

fix_report.action_taken: Defined SAP ERP import service account: dedicated JWT issued by internal JWKS /auth/sap-import with sub='sap-erp-import', scope='write:machines', exp=1h rotated via scheduled token refresh. Cloud Run ingress configured with VPC Service Controls allowing only SAP integration server IP range (10.20.5.0/24). Import job authenticates with client credentials grant to /auth/sap-import before each sync run.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Machine registration endpoint protected; SAP import job has auditable service identity; unauthorized write:machines calls from non-SAP sources blocked at network layer

target_output.title: Smart Factory Machine Catalog and Capability Registry API
target_output.executive_summary: A Node.js 22 Hono REST API providing the authoritative machine catalog and operation capability registry for a smart factory's production scheduling and MES platform. Persists machine records, tooling configurations, and capacity windows in Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16; caches capability queries in Memorystore Redis 7 with a canonical SHA256-keyed cache for deterministic hit rates; deploys on GCP Cloud Run with min-instances=1 to eliminate cold-start latency on scheduling hot paths. Round 2 fixes canonical cache key derivation and SAP ERP import service account authentication.
target_output.architecture_overview: Hono HTTP server on Node.js 22 handles three route groups: machine catalog CRUD, operation capability queries, and status update endpoints. JWT RS256 middleware validates all requests; scope enforcement middleware gates write:machines endpoints. The critical capability query path: Hono handler invokes canonicalCacheKey() over sorted query params, checks Redis for a cached result (TTL 60s), falls through to PostgreSQL query with composite index on (operation_code, machine_id) on cache miss, stores result in Redis. Status PATCH handler persists to PostgreSQL and synchronously DELetes affected capability cache keys. SAP ERP import job authenticates with short-lived JWT and pushes machine master data via POST /v1/machines from within the allowlisted IP range. Cloud Run min-instances=1 keeps one warm instance ready for scheduling query bursts.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Node.js 22 LTS. Framework: Hono 4.x with TypeScript. Validation: zod 3.x for request body and query parameter schemas. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (db-standard-2) via node-postgres (pg). Cache: Memorystore Redis 7 via ioredis. Auth: jose library for JWT RS256 verification. Metrics: prom-client for Prometheus exposition. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run, Secret Manager, Artifact Registry, VPC Connector for Cloud SQL and Redis access. CI: GitHub Actions with vitest, eslint, tsc.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Machine Catalog: GET /v1/machines lists assets with status and tooling config IDs; POST /v1/machines registers new machine from SAP import or manual entry; GET /v1/machines/:id returns full record including operation_capabilities and tooling_configurations. Capability Query: GET /v1/capabilities?operation_code=MILL_FACE&shift_window_start=...&shift_window_end=...&min_capacity=1 returns ranked eligible machines; results cached with canonical SHA256 key. Status Management: PATCH /v1/machines/:id/status {status, reason} persists status change, writes to status_audit_log, invalidates capability cache entries for that machine_id. Tooling Config: GET /v1/machines/:id/tooling-configs returns active tooling configurations with installed_at and tooling_spec_json. Health: GET /healthz returns service and dependency status.
target_output.system_components: Hono App: route definitions, global error handler, Prometheus middleware for request duration histogram. JWT Middleware: fetches JWKS from internal endpoint at startup, verifies RS256 signature and exp/iss claims, attaches decoded payload to request context. Scope Middleware: reads required scope from route definition, checks against JWT claims array, returns 403 if absent. canonicalCacheKey(params): deterministic cache key generator — sorts {operation_code, shift_window_start, shift_window_end, min_capacity} keys, JSON.stringify, SHA256 hash via Node crypto. MachineRepository: pg Pool queries with parameterized SQL, typed return types via TypeScript interfaces. CapabilityCache: ioredis GET/SETEX/DEL wrappers with key prefix namespacing. Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar for secure database connection within Cloud Run service. Prometheus /metrics endpoint for Grafana scraping.
target_output.workflows: Capability Query Flow: scheduling system sends GET /v1/capabilities?operation_code=LATHE_TURN&shift_window_start=2026-03-20T06:00:00Z&shift_window_end=2026-03-20T14:00:00Z&min_capacity=1; Hono handler calls canonicalCacheKey({operation_code:'LATHE_TURN', shift_window_start:'2026-03-20T06:00:00Z', shift_window_end:'2026-03-20T14:00:00Z', min_capacity:'1'}) producing SHA256 hash 'a3f9...'; Redis GET 'cap:v1:a3f9...' returns cached JSON array; response served in <5ms. Status Update Flow: shop-floor operator terminal calls PATCH /v1/machines/mach-042/status {status:'maintenance', reason:'spindle bearing replacement'}; handler validates write:machines scope; pg INSERT into status_audit_log; pg UPDATE machines SET status='maintenance'; ioredis DEL all 'cap:v1:*' keys matching machine_id mach-042 via SCAN + DEL pipeline; response 200 with updated machine record. SAP ERP Import Flow: nightly cron job on SAP integration server requests short-lived JWT from /auth/sap-import; POSTs machine records with write:machines scope; Cloud Run ingress VPC Service Controls verify source IP in 10.20.5.0/24 allowlist before request reaches Hono.
target_output.data_model: machines (machine_id UUID PK, asset_name TEXT, machine_class TEXT CHECK IN ('cnc','robot','press','conveyor'), cell_location TEXT, status TEXT CHECK IN ('available','in_use','maintenance','offline'), erp_asset_number TEXT UNIQUE, last_status_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()). operation_capabilities (capability_id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK REFERENCES machines, operation_code TEXT NOT NULL, min_cycle_time_sec INT, setup_time_sec INT, supported_material_groups TEXT[], valid_from DATE, valid_until DATE). Composite index: CREATE INDEX ON operation_capabilities (operation_code, machine_id). tooling_configurations (tooling_id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK, tool_set_name TEXT, tooling_spec_json JSONB, installed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, active BOOL DEFAULT true). capacity_windows (window_id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK, shift_label TEXT, available_from TIMESTAMPTZ, available_until TIMESTAMPTZ). status_audit_log (log_id UUID PK, machine_id UUID, old_status TEXT, new_status TEXT, reason TEXT, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, changed_by TEXT).
target_output.api_design: OpenAPI 3.1 spec at /openapi.json. GET /v1/capabilities?operation_code=MILL_FACE&shift_window_start=ISO8601&shift_window_end=ISO8601&min_capacity=INT — returns [{machine_id, asset_name, status, setup_time_sec, tooling_config_ids, cache_hit: bool}]. GET /v1/machines?cell_location=cnc-cell-3&status=available — list with pagination cursor. GET /v1/machines/:id — full record. POST /v1/machines {asset_name, machine_class, cell_location, erp_asset_number} — requires write:machines. PATCH /v1/machines/:id/status {status: 'maintenance'|'available'|'offline', reason: string} — requires write:machines. GET /v1/machines/:id/tooling-configs — active tooling for machine. GET /healthz — {status:'ok', db:'ok', cache:'ok'}. GET /metrics — Prometheus exposition. All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer <JWT>.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256: jose library verifies alg, exp, iss claims; JWKS fetched from internal /auth/jwks at startup with 12h in-memory cache and automatic refresh on 401. Scope enforcement: read:machines for all GET endpoints, write:machines for POST and PATCH, enforced in per-route middleware before zod validation. SAP import service account: dedicated short-lived JWT (1h exp) issued by /auth/sap-import endpoint; write:machines scope; Cloud Run VPC Service Controls IP allowlist 10.20.5.0/24. Input validation: zod schemas on all request bodies and query parameters; invalid inputs return 422 with field-level error details. Parameterized SQL via node-postgres prevents injection. Secrets: database URL, Redis password, JWKS URL in Secret Manager; mounted as environment variables in Cloud Run service.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run service: min-instances=1 (eliminates cold starts for scheduling hot path), max-instances=10, concurrency=80, memory=512Mi, CPU=1. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-standard-2 with automated daily backups. Memorystore Redis 7 basic tier 1GB. Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar container in same Cloud Run service for secure DB connection. VPC Connector for Cloud SQL and Redis private IP access. GitHub Actions CI: tsc --noEmit, eslint --max-warnings 0, vitest run, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, gcloud run deploy --region us-central1. Flyway migrations in Cloud Run Jobs pre-deploy step. Rollback: previous Cloud Run revision reachable via traffic split for instant rollback. Environments: dev (Cloud Run dev service, dev Cloud SQL instance), staging (identical to production, load tested with scheduling system integration tests), production.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics via prom-client exposed at /metrics, scraped by Grafana Cloud SQL proxy sidecar. Custom metrics: capability_query_duration_seconds histogram (route, cache_hit label), cache_hit_total / cache_miss_total by endpoint, machine_status_changes_total by new_status, sap_import_records_total, slow_query_total. Grafana dashboard panels: cache hit rate (target >70%), capability query p99 latency (target <50ms), machine status distribution pie, SAP import success rate. Alerts: p99 latency > 80ms for 5 minutes, cache_hit_rate < 50% for 10 minutes, database connection pool exhaustion.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run: min-instances=1 at ~$15/month base, scales to ~$40/month under scheduling burst. Cloud SQL db-standard-2: ~$120/month. Memorystore Redis basic 1GB: ~$30/month. Total ~$165/month. Scaling path: Cloud Run auto-scales to 10 instances; Cloud SQL read replica adds ~$120/month when reporting load increases. Cache hit rate target 70%+ keeps Cloud SQL query rate manageable at scale.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core Registry MVP (4 weeks): machine catalog CRUD, operation capability query with canonical cache key, status PATCH with cache invalidation, JWT RS256 auth with read/write scopes, Cloud Run deploy, GitHub Actions CI, OpenAPI 3.1 spec published for consumer team mock server. Phase 2 — SAP Integration and Tooling (2 weeks): SAP ERP import job with service account JWT, tooling configuration endpoints, capacity window schema, Grafana dashboard with cache hit rate and latency panels, status audit log. Phase 3 — Resilience and Expansion (future): scheduling reservation holds, GraphQL interface, Pub/Sub status change events for real-time consumer notification.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode with no-any lint rule. zod schemas colocated with route handler files. canonicalCacheKey() has 100% unit test coverage with permutation fixtures. All PostgreSQL queries in MachineRepository use parameterized placeholders — no string interpolation in SQL. vitest integration tests run against testcontainers PostgreSQL and Redis. OpenAPI spec generated from Hono route definitions via @hono/zod-openapi and committed to /docs/openapi.json in CI. Conventional commits enforced via commitlint for changelog generation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis cache invalidation by machine_id requires SCAN across all cap:v1:* keys — O(N) on large keyspaces. Mitigation: store machine_id-to-cache-key index in Redis SET 'cap:machine:{machine_id}:keys' and DEL only indexed keys on status update. Risk: Cloud Run concurrency=80 with blocking PostgreSQL queries could saturate connection pool under scheduling burst. Mitigation: pg Pool maxConnections=20 with queue timeout; prom-client pool_waiting_requests_total alert. Tradeoff: 60s cache TTL chosen as balance between hit rate and status staleness; scheduling system accepts up to 60s delay in reflecting maintenance status changes per product agreement.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Canonical cache key SHA256 approach chosen over cache key template strings for collision resistance across operation code variants. Cloud Run min-instances=1 confirmed as acceptable cost ($15/month) to eliminate cold start latency on scheduling integration. Redis basic tier sufficient for <10k cache entries; standard tier with replication not required for this data sensitivity level.

metadata.notes: Moderate revision_round api_service: resolves cache key and SAP auth issues; some sections mid-weight reflecting moderate quality distribution target.

project_goal.value: Publish a TypeScript SDK providing composable client modules for a smart factory platform's microservice APIs (machine registry, production scheduling, quality management, energy monitoring), with built-in retry logic, circuit breakers, and OpenTelemetry tracing for use in distributed factory applications
project_goal.rationale: Consuming application teams need a typed, resilient client library that handles distributed systems concerns without reimplementing retry and circuit breaking per service

target_users.value: Application developers at smart factory customers integrating platform microservices into factory control systems, dashboards, and automation scripts using Node.js 22 or edge TypeScript runtimes
target_users.rationale: Primary SDK consumers identified in developer experience research

project_class.rationale: Distributed as npm package with no hosted runtime; pure TypeScript library with no server-side infrastructure

capabilities.rationale: SDK provides typed API client modules, request/response data models, and integration abstractions for microservice communication

complexity_level.rationale: Circuit breaker state machines, OpenTelemetry span propagation, and multi-module composability require careful abstraction design

risk_level.rationale: SDK is a client library with no server-side attack surface; risk is limited to dependency vulnerabilities and breaking API changes

data_sensitivity.rationale: SDK itself stores no data; caller applications control what data passes through client methods

external_exposure.rationale: Published to npm registry for public consumption by smart factory platform customers

project.goal: Publish composable TypeScript SDK client modules for smart factory platform microservices with resilience primitives and OpenTelemetry tracing
project.target_users: Node.js 22 and edge runtime application developers at smart factory customers
project.timeline: MVP (MachineRegistry + ProductionScheduling clients) in 6 weeks; full platform coverage in 12 weeks

backend.api: SDK client methods wrapping platform REST endpoints
backend.resilience: retry with exponential backoff, circuit breaker state machine

data.storage: none; in-memory circuit breaker state only
data.types: full TypeScript interface definitions for all request and response shapes

devops.publishing: npm with provenance attestation via OIDC

constraints.dependencies: zero production deps beyond fetch and @opentelemetry/api

open_questions.circuit_breaker_sharing: Should circuit breaker state be shared across all client module instances from the same SDK factory or isolated per instance?

reasoner_reviews.product: The four client module domains (machine registry, production scheduling, quality management, energy monitoring) map directly to the platform's core microservice topology, making this SDK immediately useful for integration teams. The composable client design — where each module is independently importable — is critical for edge runtime bundle size constraints. The circuit breaker circuit state change EventEmitter pattern is the right abstraction level: it lets calling apps observe degradation without coupling to SDK internals.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Dual ESM/CJS output via tsup is the correct build strategy for maximum runtime compatibility. The zero-production-dependency constraint is achievable by implementing retry and circuit breaker logic natively in TypeScript rather than pulling in 'axios-retry' or 'opossum'. OpenTelemetry integration should use the @opentelemetry/api peer dependency pattern — the SDK declares it as a peer, not a bundled dep — so calling applications control which OTel SDK version they ship.
reasoner_reviews.security: npm provenance attestation via GitHub Actions OIDC is the right supply chain control. The SDK must ensure no API tokens, base URLs, or request payloads are captured in OpenTelemetry span attributes without explicit caller configuration — default span attributes should include only method, URL pattern, and HTTP status code. Dependabot scanning should cover both runtime and devDependencies.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 15 KB gzipped bundle size limit per client module is achievable with zero production dependencies and tree-shaking via ESM output. Vitest is the right choice for unit testing in a TypeScript-first project. The shared vs. isolated circuit breaker question must be resolved — sharing state across client instances is the right default for accurate failure detection when multiple callers hit the same downstream service.

backend.service_design: SDK organized as a monorepo-style package with barrel exports: SmartFactorySDK factory function creates configured client instances; individual clients (MachineRegistryClient, ProductionSchedulingClient, QualityManagementClient, EnergyMonitoringClient) are composable and independently importable via subpath exports (/machine-registry, /production-scheduling, etc.) for tree-shaking
backend.api_patterns: Each client module exposes typed async methods wrapping platform REST endpoints; request config objects carry baseUrl, authToken, timeout, and optional trace context; responses are discriminated unions (success/error) with typed payload shapes; pagination handled via async generator methods for list endpoints
backend.failure_handling: Native TypeScript retry implementation with exponential backoff and jitter (configurable maxAttempts, baseDelayMs, maxDelayMs); circuit breaker implemented as a state machine class (closed/open/half-open) with configurable failureThreshold, successThreshold, and resetTimeoutMs; circuit breaker state changes emitted as typed events via EventEmitter for calling application observability
backend.backend_risks: Breaking change risk as platform microservice APIs evolve — mitigation: strict versioning of request/response TypeScript types with deprecation notices in JSDoc before removal; bundle size creep if OpenTelemetry API is inadvertently bundled rather than treated as peer dependency — mitigation: size-limit CI check on each PR

security.auth_design: No auth stored in SDK; callers supply authToken at client construction; SDK includes header in every request as Authorization: Bearer <token>; no token caching or introspection within SDK
security.secrets_management: No secrets in SDK source or npm package; npm publish token stored in GitHub Actions secret NPMJS_TOKEN; provenance attestation enabled via 'npm publish --provenance' with OIDC in Actions workflow
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limit headers (Retry-After, X-RateLimit-Remaining) surfaced as typed response metadata so calling applications can implement backpressure; SDK does not auto-retry on HTTP 429 by default to avoid masking rate limit violations
security.security_risks: Dependency supply chain risk despite zero production dependencies — devDependencies (tsup, Vitest, TypeScript) are potential vectors; mitigated by dependabot weekly scans and npm audit in CI

data.entities: RequestConfig (baseUrl, authToken, timeout, retryPolicy, circuitBreakerPolicy, traceContext). RetryPolicy (maxAttempts: number, baseDelayMs: number, maxDelayMs: number, retryableStatusCodes: number[]). CircuitBreakerPolicy (failureThreshold: number, successThreshold: number, resetTimeoutMs: number). CircuitBreakerState (enum: closed/open/half_open). MachineStatusResponse (machineId, name, status, lastHeartbeatAt, currentJobId). ProductionOrderResponse (orderId, partNumber, targetQuantity, completedQuantity, scheduledStart, scheduledEnd, lineId). API response discriminated union: ApiSuccess<T> | ApiError (errorCode, message, retryable: boolean).
data.schema_notes: All request and response types exported from subpath entry points; internal types not exported to keep public API surface minimal; strict TypeScript 5.4 with exactOptionalPropertyTypes and noUncheckedIndexedAccess enabled in tsconfig
data.consistency_notes: Circuit breaker state is per client instance by default; SDK factory function SmartFactorySDK({...}) can optionally accept a shared CircuitBreakerRegistry to coordinate state across multiple client modules targeting the same upstream host

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflows: (1) PR checks — tsc type check, ESLint, Vitest with 90% coverage gate, size-limit bundle size check per subpath export, Vitest integration tests against mock server; (2) Release — triggered on semver tag push, runs full checks, 'npm publish --provenance', creates GitHub release with auto-generated CHANGELOG from conventional commits
devops.environments: npm dist-tags: 'latest' for stable releases, 'next' for pre-release; local development uses 'npm link' or 'yalc' for consuming app integration testing before publish
devops.devops_risks: Accidental breaking change publish without major semver bump — mitigation: semantic-release enforces conventional commit parsing and blocks major bump bypass; npm provenance attestation failure if GitHub Actions OIDC permissions misconfigured — mitigation: provenance test in pre-release dry-run step

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a TypeScript SDK library, not a hosted platform — architecture decisions center on package structure, dual ESM/CJS build output, zero-production-dependency constraint, and correct OpenTelemetry peer dependency pattern. The circuit breaker state machine is the most complex internal component and needs clear design: closed/open/half-open states with configurable thresholds and EventEmitter state change events so calling applications can observe degradation without SDK coupling to any specific OTel implementation. Bundle size CI checks are essential given the 15 KB per-module constraint.
target_output.title: Smart Factory Platform SDK — TypeScript Client Library
target_output.executive_summary: A TypeScript SDK published to npm as @smart-factory/sdk providing four composable client modules (MachineRegistryClient, ProductionSchedulingClient, QualityManagementClient, EnergyMonitoringClient) for the smart factory platform's microservice APIs. Each client module includes native retry-with-jitter logic, a circuit breaker state machine, and OpenTelemetry span propagation via peer dependency. Distributed as dual ESM/CJS with subpath exports for tree-shaking; zero production dependencies beyond @opentelemetry/api as peer; per-module bundle target under 15 KB gzipped. Designed for use in Node.js 22 factory application servers and edge runtimes.
target_output.architecture_overview: SDK package organized with a SmartFactorySDK factory function as the primary entry point plus four independent subpath exports (/machine-registry, /production-scheduling, /quality-management, /energy-monitoring) for tree-shakeable imports. Each subpath exports a typed client class, its request/response interfaces, and its error types. Retry logic and circuit breaker are implemented natively in TypeScript with no external packages — the retry module uses exponential backoff with full jitter, and the circuit breaker implements a closed/open/half-open state machine emitting typed events via Node.js EventEmitter. OpenTelemetry integration uses @opentelemetry/api as a peer dependency, creating spans for each outbound request with method, URL pattern, and HTTP status attributes. Calling applications that do not configure an OTel SDK receive no-op spans at zero cost. A shared CircuitBreakerRegistry can be optionally injected at SDK factory construction to coordinate state across multiple client modules targeting the same upstream host.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: TypeScript 5.4 with strict mode (exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noUncheckedIndexedAccess). Build: tsup generating dual ESM (.mjs) and CJS (.cjs) output with declaration files (.d.ts). Testing: Vitest with msw (Mock Service Worker) for HTTP mocking, 90% coverage requirement. Linting: ESLint with @typescript-eslint/strict ruleset, Prettier formatting. Release: semantic-release with conventional commits for CHANGELOG generation and semver enforcement. Bundle analysis: size-limit CI check per subpath export. Publishing: npm with provenance attestation via GitHub Actions OIDC. Peer dependency: @opentelemetry/api ^1.x.
target_output.functional_feature_map: MachineRegistryClient: getMachineStatus(machineId), listMachines(filter), updateMachineState(machineId, state) — typed wrappers for machine registry microservice REST endpoints. ProductionSchedulingClient: getProductionOrder(orderId), listActiveOrders(lineId), scheduleOrder(request), completeOrder(orderId, result) — typed wrappers for scheduling microservice. QualityManagementClient: submitInspectionResult(result), getInspectionHistory(partSerialNumber), listOpenNonConformances() — typed wrappers for quality microservice. EnergyMonitoringClient: getCurrentConsumption(assetId), getConsumptionHistory(assetId, timeRange) — typed wrappers for energy microservice. SDK-wide: SmartFactorySDK factory function with SDKConfig builder, shared CircuitBreakerRegistry, RetryPolicy and CircuitBreakerPolicy configuration types.
target_output.system_components: SmartFactorySDK factory function (entry point, constructs configured clients, wires shared CircuitBreakerRegistry). HttpClient internal class (fetch wrapper with timeout, retry loop with exponential backoff and jitter, OTel span lifecycle). CircuitBreakerStateMachine class (closed/open/half-open state transitions, configurable failureThreshold and resetTimeoutMs, EventEmitter for state change events). CircuitBreakerRegistry (optional shared registry keyed by upstream host URL for cross-module state coordination). RetryExecutor (evaluates retryableStatusCodes, computes backoff with full jitter, respects Retry-After response header). TypeScript type definitions for all four microservice domains (request config, response payloads, error shapes as discriminated unions).
target_output.workflows: Standard Client Request: caller invokes getMachineStatus('MCH-001'); RetryExecutor checks circuit breaker state (closed — proceed); HttpClient creates OTel span 'machine-registry GET /machines/:id', injects traceparent header, calls fetch with timeout; on HTTP 200 response, span ends with status OK and typed MachineStatusResponse is returned; on transient HTTP 503, RetryExecutor computes backoff delay (exponential with jitter), records failure in CircuitBreakerStateMachine, retries up to maxAttempts. Circuit Breaker Open State: after failureThreshold consecutive failures within measurement window, CircuitBreakerStateMachine transitions to open and emits 'state_change' event with {from: closed, to: open, upstreamHost}; all requests to the affected client module immediately return CircuitOpenError without hitting the network; after resetTimeoutMs, state transitions to half-open and one probe request is attempted; on probe success, state returns to closed.
target_output.data_model: SDK TypeScript interfaces (compile-time only, no runtime storage). RequestConfig: {baseUrl: string; authToken: string; timeout?: number; retryPolicy?: RetryPolicy; circuitBreakerPolicy?: CircuitBreakerPolicy; traceContext?: Record<string,string>}. RetryPolicy: {maxAttempts: number; baseDelayMs: number; maxDelayMs: number; retryableStatusCodes: number[]}. CircuitBreakerPolicy: {failureThreshold: number; successThreshold: number; resetTimeoutMs: number}. MachineStatusResponse: {machineId: string; name: string; status: MachineStatus; lastHeartbeatAt: string; currentJobId: string | null}. ProductionOrderResponse: {orderId: string; partNumber: string; targetQuantity: number; completedQuantity: number; scheduledStart: string; scheduledEnd: string; lineId: string}. ApiResult<T>: ApiSuccess<T> | ApiError where ApiError includes {errorCode: string; message: string; retryable: boolean; upstreamStatus: number}.
target_output.api_design: SDK public API surface (not REST endpoints — client method signatures). import { MachineRegistryClient } from '@smart-factory/sdk/machine-registry'; const client = new MachineRegistryClient({baseUrl, authToken, retryPolicy, circuitBreakerPolicy}); await client.getMachineStatus(machineId): Promise<ApiResult<MachineStatusResponse>>; await client.listMachines({status?, lineId?, cursor?}): Promise<ApiResult<PagedResponse<MachineSummary>>>; import { ProductionSchedulingClient } from '@smart-factory/sdk/production-scheduling'; await client.scheduleOrder(req: ScheduleOrderRequest): Promise<ApiResult<ProductionOrderResponse>>; SDK factory: import { SmartFactorySDK } from '@smart-factory/sdk'; const sdk = SmartFactorySDK({baseUrl, authToken, sharedCircuitBreaker: true}); sdk.machineRegistry.getMachineStatus(id).
target_output.security_and_compliance: No secrets stored in SDK package or source. API tokens accepted at client construction and transmitted as Authorization: Bearer header; never logged, cached beyond request, or included in OTel span attributes by default. npm provenance attestation enabled via 'npm publish --provenance' in GitHub Actions with OIDC; generated provenance links publish event to sigstore transparency log for supply chain verification. Dependabot weekly scans on all dependencies including devDependencies. 'npm audit' enforced as CI gate — any high or critical advisory blocks publish. SDK internals use no 'eval', 'Function()', or dynamic require(). Rate limit response headers (Retry-After, X-RateLimit-Remaining) surfaced in ApiError metadata without auto-masking.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline on every PR: tsc --noEmit type check, ESLint with zero-warnings gate, Vitest unit and integration tests with 90% line coverage requirement, size-limit bundle size check (< 15 KB gzipped per subpath entry), npm audit for vulnerability gate. Release pipeline on semver tag push (e.g., v1.2.0): full CI suite re-run, tsup dual ESM/CJS build with declaration files, 'npm publish --provenance --tag latest', GitHub Release creation with semantic-release CHANGELOG. Pre-release flow: merge to 'next' branch triggers 'npm publish --tag next' for consumer testing before stable promotion. Local development integration: yalc for linking SDK into consuming factory app without npm publish round-trip. No infrastructure to provision or manage — pure package distribution.
target_output.observability: OpenTelemetry API spans on every outbound HTTP request: span name '{ServiceName} {HTTP_METHOD} {url_pattern}', attributes include http.method, http.url (pattern not interpolated path), http.status_code, retry.attempt_number. Circuit breaker state changes emitted as typed 'circuit_state_change' events on client EventEmitter: {clientModule, upstreamHost, previousState, newState, timestamp} for calling app metrics and alerting. No console.log or process.stdout writes in production SDK code — all diagnostic information surfaced via EventEmitter events or OTel span events. SDK includes exported TypeScript types for all emitted events enabling typed event listener registration in calling applications.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero runtime infrastructure cost — distributed as npm package with no hosted services. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI for open-source; private repo requires Actions minutes (estimated < 5 min per PR run). npm registry hosting is free for public packages. Scale is unlimited as consumers self-host; circuit breaker and retry configuration allows callers to tune for their specific microservice SLAs and traffic volumes. SDK memory footprint per client instance is minimal: circuit breaker state machine is a single object with 4 properties; no caches or persistent structures.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — MVP (weeks 1-6): Project scaffold with tsup config, ESLint, Vitest, size-limit; HttpClient with fetch, timeout, retry-with-jitter; CircuitBreakerStateMachine with closed/open/half-open states and EventEmitter; MachineRegistryClient (getMachineStatus, listMachines) with full TypeScript types; ProductionSchedulingClient (getProductionOrder, listActiveOrders, scheduleOrder); SmartFactorySDK factory function with shared CircuitBreakerRegistry; OTel span integration with peer dependency pattern; 90% test coverage; npm publish to 'next' tag for beta consumers. Phase 2 — Full Platform Coverage (weeks 7-12): QualityManagementClient (submitInspectionResult, getInspectionHistory, listOpenNonConformances); EnergyMonitoringClient (getCurrentConsumption, getConsumptionHistory); async generator pagination helpers for list endpoints; stable v1.0.0 publish to 'latest'. Phase 3 — Developer Experience (weeks 13-16): msw-based mock server package @smart-factory/sdk-mock for consumer integration testing; React hooks wrapper @smart-factory/sdk-react exposing useMachineStatus and useProductionOrders; WebSocket subscription client for real-time telemetry streams.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode with exactOptionalPropertyTypes and noUncheckedIndexedAccess; all public API types documented with JSDoc including @example snippets. ESLint @typescript-eslint/strict ruleset, zero warnings allowed in CI. Conventional Commits enforced via commitlint for automated CHANGELOG and semver bumping. Breaking changes marked with BREAKING CHANGE footer in commit message to trigger major version bump. Every exported public method has a corresponding Vitest unit test and at least one msw integration test. Bundle size gate: size-limit checks each subpath export in CI; any PR that increases a module above 15 KB gzipped is blocked until optimized. No production dependencies added without ADR documenting justification and bundle impact.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Platform microservice API changes cause SDK breaking changes requiring major version bumps, fragmenting consumer versions. Mitigation: strict TypeScript types catch response shape changes at compile time for consumers; deprecation notices via JSDoc @deprecated before removal in next major. Tradeoff: native retry and circuit breaker implementation vs. battle-tested libraries (opossum, p-retry) — chosen to meet zero-production-dependency constraint and 15 KB bundle limit; native implementation is fully tested with 90% coverage and simpler to audit. Risk: OTel peer dependency version mismatch if consumer uses @opentelemetry/api v2 while SDK declares ^1.x — mitigation: peer dependency range will be updated with each OTel API major release in a corresponding SDK major. Risk: circuit breaker half-open probe requests causing brief real traffic to degraded upstream — accepted tradeoff; probe interval configurable via resetTimeoutMs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Circuit breaker state sharing: SmartFactorySDK factory accepts 'sharedCircuitBreaker: true' to use a shared CircuitBreakerRegistry keyed by upstream host, correctly coordinating state when MachineRegistry and ProductionScheduling clients both target the same platform host. OTel integration: @opentelemetry/api declared as peer dependency (peerDependencies) with peerDependenciesMeta.optional=true so SDK works without OTel configured. Pagination: async generator pattern chosen over cursor callback for idiomatic TypeScript async iteration. CJS/ESM: tsup dual output with package.json exports map correctly routing require() to .cjs and import to .mjs.

metadata.notes: First-pass strong plan for TypeScript SDK with circuit breaker, retry, and OTel tracing for smart factory platform microservice APIs.

profile.domain: CMM GD&T Statistical Analysis Desktop Application

project_goal.value: Desktop application for R&D engineers at a precision machinery manufacturer that processes large-volume CMM output files, computes GD&T statistics across inspection batches, and renders 3D deviation color maps on part surfaces
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual Excel GD&T post-processing workflow too slow for batch sizes above 500 parts

target_users.value: R&D engineers and metrology specialists at a precision machinery manufacturer
target_users.rationale: Technical users comfortable with GD&T terminology and DMIS CMM output formats

project_class.rationale: Runs locally on engineer workstation with no server dependency; processes local CMM files

capabilities.rationale: Tauri desktop with React UI, Rust computation backend, DuckDB analytics, 3D deviation rendering

complexity_level.rationale: Parallel CMM file parsing, GD&T statistical computation, and 3D mesh rendering are each non-trivial

risk_level.rationale: Local-only desktop tool with no network exposure; CMM data is internal R&D data

data_sensitivity.rationale: CMM measurement data and GD&T results are proprietary R&D data, not personal or regulated

external_exposure.rationale: Desktop app with no server ports; all processing and storage local to workstation

access_model.value: No authentication; OS-level user access to application and local DuckDB database files
access_model.rationale: Single-user desktop tool operated by the engineer who owns the workstation

feature_scope.value: CMM output file import (DMIS, I++ DME formats), parallel GD&T computation (flatness, cylindricity, true position, perpendicularity), batch statistics across inspection runs, 3D deviation color map rendering on part mesh, CSV and PDF report export
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for metrology analysis workflow

mvp_scope.value: DMIS file import, flatness and true position computation, batch summary statistics in DuckDB, deviation color map on flat surface mesh, CSV export
mvp_scope.rationale: Most common GD&T characteristics for initial user validation

security_baseline.value: No network connections; local DuckDB file permissions managed by OS; no telemetry collection
security_baseline.rationale: Local-only tool with minimal security surface

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2.0 with React 18 and TypeScript for desktop application shell and UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native desktop packaging with Rust backend IPC; React for interactive dashboard

backend_stack.value: Rust with Tauri IPC commands for parallel CMM parsing and GD&T computation using rayon
backend_stack.rationale: Rust rayon parallelism critical for processing 10,000+ CMM measurement points within interactive response time

data_platform.value: DuckDB embedded for batch inspection result storage and aggregate statistical queries
data_platform.rationale: DuckDB columnar engine excels at analytical aggregation across large measurement datasets

hosting_target.value: Local workstation, distributed as Windows installer via internal software portal
hosting_target.rationale: Engineers install on their own workstations from corporate software portal

privacy_retention_policy.value: CMM data files and DuckDB database stored locally on engineer workstation; no server-side retention
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Local-only tool with no centralized data collection

future_scope.value: Cylindricity and perpendicularity GD&T computation, I++ DME format support, PDF report generation, batch comparison across part revisions
future_scope.rationale: Extended GD&T characteristics and output formats planned for v2

constraints.value: Must process a 500-part batch with 10,000 measurement points each within 30 seconds; must run on Windows 10/11 workstations with 16GB RAM
constraints.rationale: Engineer workflow tolerance for batch processing wait time

observability_baseline.value: Rust tracing crate with file-based log output; processing time metrics logged per batch; no remote telemetry
observability_baseline.rationale: Local diagnostic logging for debugging slow batch processing without remote monitoring

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with engineer feedback after each phase; GitHub Actions CI for Rust and TypeScript
execution_preference.rationale: R&D user feedback critical for GD&T algorithm correctness validation

project.goal: Desktop CMM GD&T statistical analysis tool with 3D deviation visualization
project.target_users: R&D engineers and metrology specialists
project.timeline: MVP for engineer validation in 6 weeks

backend.framework: Tauri IPC commands, rayon for parallel computation
backend.api: Tauri invoke() IPC -- no HTTP API

security.auth: none

data.cache: in-memory Rust HashMap for parsed CMM structures during batch

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with cargo test, cargo clippy, vitest

confirmed_decisions.gdt_library: Custom implementation -- no suitable Rust crate found
confirmed_decisions.visualization: Three.js with custom deviation colormap shader

reasoner_reviews.product: GD&T algorithm correctness is the highest risk area -- engineers will reject the tool immediately if computed flatness or true position values differ from their DMIS manual calculations. Recommend a test fixture of 20 known CMM reports with pre-computed expected values used as a CI regression suite to validate algorithm accuracy.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rayon parallel iterator over CMM files is the correct approach for throughput. Round 1 missed defining the DuckDB schema -- all measurement points cannot be loaded into a single flat table for 5M rows without query planning considerations. Columnar DuckDB handles this efficiently if partitioned by batch_id and part_id with a composite primary key.
reasoner_reviews.security: No network attack surface on this local-only tool. Primary concern is accidental data loss -- DuckDB write-ahead log and periodic checkpoint should be enabled so a crash during batch import does not corrupt the database file on the workstation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 30-second target for 500 parts x 10,000 points equals 5M measurement points, achievable with rayon on a modern 8-core workstation. The key bottleneck is likely DMIS file I/O, not computation -- parallel file reads via rayon plus std::fs::File should be benchmarked early in development.

backend.service_design: Tauri IPC command architecture: import_batch(folder_path) spawns rayon parallel iterator over .dmc files, each file parsed by DmisParser into MeasurementPoint structs, accumulated and batch-written to DuckDB transactionally. compute_gdt(batch_id, characteristic_type) queries DuckDB for batch points and runs GdtComputer algorithms: flatness via minimum-zone L-inf optimization, true position via RFS 3D Euclidean deviation.
backend.api_patterns: Tauri IPC commands only -- no HTTP: import_batch(folder_path: String) -> ImportResult, get_batches() -> Vec<BatchSummary>, compute_gdt(batch_id: Uuid, characteristic: String) -> Vec<GdtResult>, get_deviation_data(batch_id: Uuid, part_id: Uuid) -> Vec<DeviationPoint> capped at 50000 points, export_csv(batch_id: Uuid, output_path: String) -> ExportResult
backend.failure_handling: DMIS parse errors per file collected into ImportResult.parse_errors Vec; batch import continues on per-file failure. DuckDB transaction wraps full batch insert; rolled back on error with error message returned to React UI. compute_gdt returns Err variant for insufficient measurement points (fewer than 3 for flatness computation).

frontend.app_structure: Tauri React SPA with three main views: BatchImporter (file drag-and-drop with import progress bar), StatsDashboard (GD&T results table with pass/fail indicators and Cpk column), DeviationViewer (Three.js canvas with HSL deviation colormap and tolerance filter slider).
frontend.pages_and_flows: BatchImporter: engineer drags CMM output folder; progress bar shows files parsed per second; import summary displays part count, point count, parse error list. StatsDashboard: batch selector dropdown, sortable table of GD&T computed values vs tolerance, pass/fail badge, batch-level Cpk. DeviationViewer: Three.js BufferGeometry rendering part surface mesh colored by measurement residual magnitude; zoom, rotate, pan with mouse; tolerance band slider.
frontend.performance_notes: Three.js BufferGeometry with Float32Array for deviation vertex positions and color attributes; avoid per-point JavaScript objects. Cap deviation point rendering at 50,000 points with 1cm spatial grid decimation for larger datasets. Use useEffect cleanup to dispose BufferGeometry and avoid GPU memory leaks on view unmount.

data.entities: inspection_batches (batch_id UUID PK, batch_name TEXT, import_folder TEXT, part_count INT, total_points INT, imported_at TIMESTAMPTZ). cmm_reports (report_id UUID PK, batch_id UUID FK, part_serial TEXT, nominal_part_number TEXT, cmm_file_path TEXT, point_count INT). measurement_points (batch_id UUID NOT NULL, report_id UUID NOT NULL, point_seq INT NOT NULL, x_nominal DOUBLE, y_nominal DOUBLE, z_nominal DOUBLE, x_actual DOUBLE, y_actual DOUBLE, z_actual DOUBLE, feature_label TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (batch_id, report_id, point_seq)). gdt_results (result_id UUID PK, batch_id UUID, report_id UUID, characteristic_type TEXT, computed_value DOUBLE, tolerance DOUBLE, pass BOOL, cpk_index DOUBLE, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: DuckDB file at ~/cmm-analyzer/projects/<batch_name>.duckdb per batch project. WAL mode enabled via PRAGMA wal_mode=wal. measurement_points composite PK (batch_id, report_id, point_seq) enables efficient per-batch partition pruning. Estimated storage: 5M rows x 9 DOUBLE columns equals ~120MB compressed per 500-part batch.
data.data_risks: Loading all 5M measurement points into Three.js Float32Array would allocate ~160MB per batch view, acceptable on 16GB workstation. Risk: DuckDB file corruption on crash during large batch INSERT. Mitigation: WAL mode ensures transaction atomicity; incomplete batch insert is rolled back cleanly on restart.
