target_output.title: CMM GD&T Statistical Analysis Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: A Tauri 2.0 desktop application for precision machinery R&D engineers that imports DMIS-format CMM output files, computes flatness and true position GD&T statistics across 500-part inspection batches in parallel using Rust rayon, stores results in an embedded DuckDB columnar database, and renders 3D deviation color maps in a React Three.js viewer. Round 2 defines the complete DuckDB schema and Three.js memory management strategy. Note: GD&T regression test CI fixture suite not yet implemented -- algorithm correctness gap remains as an identified risk.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri 2.0 shell wraps a Rust backend exposing IPC commands to a React 18 TypeScript frontend. The import_batch command dispatches a rayon parallel iterator over .dmc DMIS files in the selected folder; each file is parsed by DmisParser into a Vec<MeasurementPoint>; the full batch is written transactionally to DuckDB via duckdb-rs. GD&T computation commands query DuckDB for batch measurement_points and run minimum-zone algorithms for flatness (L-inf linear programming via nalgebra) and true position (RFS 3D Euclidean deviation from nominal centroid). The deviation map command queries actual vs nominal coordinate residuals for a single part_id, downsamples to 50,000 points via 1cm spatial grid decimation if needed, and serializes as a Float32Array JSON payload for Three.js BufferGeometry rendering on the React side.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop shell: Tauri 2.0, React 18 TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Zustand. Rust backend: Rust 1.78, rayon 1.10 for parallel iteration, duckdb-rs 0.10 for DuckDB access, nalgebra 0.33 for geometric linear programming in flatness computation, tracing 0.1 with file appender for diagnostic logs. 3D visualization: Three.js r165 with custom GLSL HSL deviation colormap shader. Distribution: Tauri bundler generating NSIS Windows installer. CI: GitHub Actions on windows-latest with Cargo cache.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Batch Import: engineer drags CMM folder onto BatchImporter panel; rayon parses all .dmc files in parallel; import progress streamed via Tauri event emitter to React progress bar; import summary reports part_count, total_points, and parse_errors per file. GD&T Statistics: StatsDashboard renders sortable table of per-part computed_value, nominal tolerance from DMIS TOLS records, pass/fail badge, and Cpk index. Batch Aggregate Statistics: DuckDB aggregate queries compute batch-level mean, std_dev, Cpk, and out_of_tolerance_count per GD&T characteristic. Deviation Viewer: Three.js canvas with HSL colormap mapping residual magnitude to blue-to-red gradient; tolerance band slider highlights out-of-tolerance measurement points in red. CSV Export: export_csv IPC command writes gdt_results and batch statistics to engineer-specified output path.
target_output.system_components: DmisParser (Rust cmm-core crate): tokenizes DMIS line-by-line handling F(MEAS), MEAS/POINT, and TOLS records; returns Vec<MeasurementPoint> or Vec<ParseError> per file. Rayon Batch Processor: rayon::iter::IntoParallelRefIterator over directory entries; par_map to DmisParser per .dmc file; results collected into BatchImportResult. GdtComputer (Rust cmm-core): Flatness algorithm via minimum enclosing plane L-inf optimization using nalgebra simplex; TruePosition as 3D Euclidean distance from actual centroid to nominal centroid per DMIS TOLS nominal. DuckDB Manager: duckdb-rs Connection with WAL mode; transactional batch INSERT for inspection_batches, cmm_reports, measurement_points, gdt_results. Tauri IPC Layer: async fn annotated with tauri::command bridging React invoke() to Rust async task. React Frontend: Zustand store for selected batch_id and active GD&T characteristic; Three.js BufferGeometry with Float32Array for deviation vertex positions and vertex colors.
target_output.workflows: Batch Import Flow: engineer drags CMM export folder onto BatchImporter; Tauri invoke('import_batch') fires; Rust rayon iterator processes each .dmc file concurrently; DmisParser extracts MeasurementPoint structs per file; DuckDB transaction inserts inspection_batches, cmm_reports, and all measurement_points rows; Tauri emit('import_progress') fires per completed file updating React progress bar; final ImportResult returns part_count=500, total_points=4987320, errors=[]. GD&T Computation Flow: engineer selects batch and characteristic FLATNESS; Tauri invoke('compute_gdt') fires; Rust queries measurement_points WHERE batch_id=X and feature_label='DATUM_A'; runs minimum-zone flatness per report_id; inserts gdt_results; React StatsDashboard renders computed_value=0.014mm, tolerance=0.020mm, pass=true, Cpk=1.42. Deviation Map Flow: engineer clicks part row in StatsDashboard; Tauri invoke('get_deviation_data') queries residuals for that report_id; if row count exceeds 50,000, Rust applies 1cm grid decimation averaging residuals per spatial cell; Float32Array serialized to frontend; Three.js BufferGeometry renders HSL colormap on part mesh.
target_output.data_model: inspection_batches (batch_id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, batch_name TEXT NOT NULL, import_folder TEXT, part_count INT, total_points INT, imported_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()). cmm_reports (report_id UUID PRIMARY KEY, batch_id UUID REFERENCES inspection_batches, part_serial TEXT, nominal_part_number TEXT, cmm_file_path TEXT, point_count INT). measurement_points (batch_id UUID NOT NULL, report_id UUID NOT NULL, point_seq INT NOT NULL, x_nominal DOUBLE PRECISION, y_nominal DOUBLE PRECISION, z_nominal DOUBLE PRECISION, x_actual DOUBLE PRECISION, y_actual DOUBLE PRECISION, z_actual DOUBLE PRECISION, feature_label TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (batch_id, report_id, point_seq)). Estimated storage: 5M rows x 9 DOUBLE columns (8 bytes each) = 360MB raw; DuckDB columnar compression approximately 3x ratio giving roughly 120MB per 500-part batch. gdt_results (result_id UUID PRIMARY KEY, batch_id UUID, report_id UUID, characteristic_type TEXT CHECK (characteristic_type IN ('flatness','true_position','cylindricity','perpendicularity')), computed_value DOUBLE PRECISION, tolerance DOUBLE PRECISION, pass BOOL, cpk_index DOUBLE PRECISION, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Tauri IPC commands only -- no HTTP server or network ports opened. Rust tauri::command functions: import_batch(folder_path: String, window: tauri::Window) -> Result<ImportResult, String> emitting 'import_progress' events per file. get_batches() -> Result<Vec<BatchSummary>, String> querying inspection_batches. compute_gdt(batch_id: String, characteristic: String) -> Result<Vec<GdtResult>, String> returning per-part computed_value, tolerance, pass, cpk_index. get_batch_stats(batch_id: String, characteristic: String) -> Result<BatchStats, String> with mean, std_dev, cpk, out_of_tolerance_count. get_deviation_data(batch_id: String, report_id: String) -> Result<Vec<DeviationPoint>, String> capped at 50,000 points with decimation flag in response. export_csv(batch_id: String, output_path: String) -> Result<ExportSummary, String>.
target_output.security_and_compliance: No network connections -- Tauri allowlist configured with only filesystem and dialog permissions, no http or shell capabilities. DuckDB .duckdb files stored in %APPDATA%/cmm-analyzer requiring no elevated permissions. No telemetry collection. Rust tracing logs at %LOCALAPPDATA%/cmm-analyzer/logs contain processing durations and error counts only -- measurement values are never logged to prevent unintentional data exposure in shared log shipping tools. ASME Y14.5 GD&T standard compliance depends on algorithm correctness -- CI regression fixture suite is recommended but not yet implemented in this revision, which remains an identified correctness risk.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on windows-latest: cargo clippy --deny warnings, cargo test (unit tests for DmisParser tokenizer and GdtComputer flatness algorithm against synthetic point clouds), vitest for React components, tauri build --ci producing NSIS .exe installer artifact uploaded to GitHub Releases on tagged commits. Cargo workspace: cmm-core crate (parser plus GD&T algorithms, no Tauri dependency for unit testability), cmm-app crate (Tauri IPC commands plus DuckDB integration). Distribution: installer artifact linked in corporate software portal; no auto-update mechanism -- engineers download and install manually. App data directory: %APPDATA%/cmm-analyzer/projects for .duckdb files, %LOCALAPPDATA%/cmm-analyzer/logs for diagnostic logs.
target_output.observability: Rust tracing crate with INFO and DEBUG levels to rolling file appender at %LOCALAPPDATA%/cmm-analyzer/logs/app.log (10MB per file, 5 rolling files). Logged events include import_batch start and complete with duration_ms and point_count, DuckDB query durations for compute_gdt and deviation queries, and parse error details per file. React BatchImporter shows import progress percentage and elapsed seconds in UI. No Prometheus, no remote monitoring for this local tool.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost -- runs entirely on engineer workstation. Processing throughput benchmarks on 8-core i7 workstation: rayon parallel DMIS parse for 500 parts at 10,000 points each completes in approximately 18 seconds; DuckDB batch aggregate query across 5M measurement_points completes in approximately 1.2 seconds; Three.js deviation map rendering at 50,000 points renders in approximately 200ms. Memory peak during batch import is approximately 2GB for rayon Vec<MeasurementPoint> buffers, well within 16GB workstation budget.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Core MVP (5 weeks): DmisParser for DMIS point and tolerance records, rayon batch import with progress events, DuckDB schema with measurement_points as defined in this round, flatness and true position GD&T computation, StatsDashboard React view, CSV export, Windows installer via GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 -- Deviation Viewer and Batch Stats (3 weeks): Three.js deviation color map with 50,000-point cap and 1cm spatial grid decimation, batch-level Cpk DuckDB aggregate queries, Zustand batch selection and filter state. Phase 3 -- Extended Characteristics (future): cylindricity and perpendicularity algorithms, I++ DME format parser, PDF report generation, GD&T regression CI fixture suite.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: cargo clippy --deny warnings enforced in CI. cmm-core crate has zero Tauri dependency enabling isolated unit testing of parser and GD&T algorithms. GD&T algorithm unit tests in cmm-core/tests/ with synthetic geometric inputs and expected outputs -- currently 5 test cases only; full regression fixture suite with real DMIS files is recommended but not yet delivered. React: TypeScript strict mode, eslint, vitest component tests. Three.js BufferGeometry disposed in useEffect cleanup hook to prevent GPU memory leaks when DeviationViewer component unmounts. DuckDB Connection opened once at Tauri app startup and closed on exit -- not re-created per command to avoid connection overhead.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GD&T algorithm correctness -- minimum-zone flatness via L-inf optimization is numerically sensitive to point cloud conditioning; incorrect Rust implementation would produce values differing from DMIS manual calculation by >0.001mm, causing engineer rejection. Mitigation: CI regression fixture suite is recommended but not yet implemented -- this is an open correctness risk in this revision. Risk: DMIS format variation -- CMM software vendors produce non-standard DMIS dialects; DmisParser may fail on proprietary extensions. Mitigation: parse errors are collected per file and reported in ImportResult, allowing partial batch import to continue. Tradeoff: 1cm spatial grid decimation for deviation maps exceeding 50,000 points loses sub-centimeter measurement detail; acceptable for flatness and true position analysis but may require finer grid for surface roughness applications.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: DuckDB chosen over SQLite for measurement_points storage due to columnar compression efficiency on nine DOUBLE columns per row and built-in Cpk analytical computation via standard deviation aggregate function. Three.js 50,000-point rendering cap set based on GPU memory benchmark on target NVIDIA Quadro RTX 4000 workstation GPU. Spatial grid decimation cell size of 1cm determined appropriate for flatness and true position GD&T characteristics targeted in MVP.

metadata.notes: Flawed revision_round desktop_app: resolves DuckDB schema gap but intentionally omits GD&T regression CI fixture suite recommended by auditor -- algorithm correctness validation gap remains as intentional flaw.

project_goal.value: Go library providing high-throughput, protocol-agnostic adapter abstractions for industrial device communication (Modbus TCP/RTU, EtherNet/IP, OPC-UA, MQTT), designed for embedding in IoT gateway applications that must handle thousands of concurrent device connections with minimal memory overhead
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates duplicated protocol adapter code across multiple gateway implementations in the same organization

target_users.value: Go developers building industrial IoT gateway applications and device connectivity services
target_users.rationale: Library consumed programmatically by developers, not by end users

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a Go module with exported interfaces and types, no hosted runtime or UI

capabilities.rationale: Protocol adapter logic, register map data modeling, and multi-protocol integration are the core concerns

complexity_level.rationale: Concurrent protocol connection pooling, adaptive reconnect, and multi-protocol abstraction are architecturally non-trivial

risk_level.rationale: Library with no hosted surface; security risks are in how callers deploy it, not in the library itself

data_sensitivity.rationale: Library code contains no data; telemetry values are caller-owned

external_exposure.rationale: Published as open-source Go module on pkg.go.dev for community and internal use

access_model.value: No access control; open-source Go module importable by any Go application
access_model.rationale: Standard open-source library distribution model

feature_scope.value: Adapter interface with Connect/Read/Write/Subscribe/Close, Modbus TCP/RTU adapter, EtherNet/IP adapter, OPC-UA adapter, MQTT adapter, connection pool supervisor with adaptive reconnect, register map configuration, read request batching, write command queuing
feature_scope.rationale: Full multi-protocol adapter library feature set

mvp_scope.value: Adapter interface, Modbus TCP adapter with connection pooling, register map configuration, Read() and Write() with request batching, unit and integration test suite
mvp_scope.rationale: Modbus TCP covers the majority of legacy PLC devices; interface design is the critical MVP deliverable

security_baseline.value: OPC-UA adapter supports Basic256Sha256 security policy; MQTT adapter supports TLS 1.3 and client certificate authentication; Modbus and EtherNet/IP operate at transport layer of calling application
security_baseline.rationale: Library exposes security configuration hooks; enforcement is caller responsibility

frontend_stack.value: none - Go library with no UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Library_sdk project class with no frontend component

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with standard library concurrency primitives
backend_stack.rationale: Go goroutines and channels are idiomatic for high-concurrency connection management; no framework needed

data_platform.value: none - library operates in-memory; callers manage persistence
data_platform.rationale: Library passes normalized register values to caller callbacks; no embedded database

hosting_target.value: Go module registry (pkg.go.dev), distributed via go get
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Go module distribution; no hosted runtime

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retention in library; all values passed through to caller; no telemetry collection
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Library does not store any operational data

future_scope.value: PROFINET adapter, S7 (Siemens) adapter, hardware-in-the-loop integration test rig with physical PLCs, OpenTelemetry tracing hooks for adapter operations
future_scope.rationale: Protocol coverage expansion and observability integration planned for v2

constraints.value: Must handle 10,000 concurrent Modbus TCP device connections within 512MB memory on gateway hardware; no CGO -- pure Go only for cross-platform compilation
constraints.rationale: Edge gateway hardware memory budget and cross-compilation requirement for ARM targets

observability_baseline.value: Structured log hooks via slog-compatible interface; caller injects their own logger; library emits connection lifecycle events
observability_baseline.rationale: Library should not impose a specific logging implementation on callers

execution_preference.value: API-first design: exported interface types defined and stabilized before adapter implementations; semantic versioning with v1 stability guarantee
execution_preference.rationale: Stable interface contract critical for downstream gateway teams before implementing Modbus adapter

project.goal: Go library for multi-protocol industrial device communication with high-concurrency connection management
project.target_users: Go developers building IoT gateway applications
project.timeline: Modbus TCP MVP in 6 weeks; full multi-protocol in 16 weeks

backend.framework: stdlib only -- no external framework dependencies in library core
backend.api: Exported Go interfaces and types -- no HTTP API

security.auth: OPC-UA Basic256Sha256 security policy and MQTT TLS 1.3 as configurable adapter options
security.transport: Caller manages network transport; library provides TLS config hook

data.cache: in-memory connection pool state per adapter instance

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with go test -race -count=1, go vet, staticcheck, goleak

confirmed_decisions.interface_design: Adapter interface with Connect(ctx), Read(ctx, ReadRequest) ReadResult, Write(ctx, WriteCommand) WriteResult, Subscribe(ctx, SubscribeConfig) SubscribeHandle, Close() error
confirmed_decisions.versioning: semantic versioning with v1.x.x stability guarantee on exported interfaces

reasoner_reviews.product: The Adapter interface design is the most critical deliverable -- once downstream gateway teams import the library, the interface cannot change without a major version bump. Invest heavily in the interface design review before implementing the first adapter. The Read() batching and Subscribe() for push-based protocols covers both polling and event-driven device models.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go goroutine-per-connection is idiomatic but at 10,000 connections the goroutine stack memory becomes significant. Default goroutine stack is 8KB in Go 1.22, so 10,000 goroutines could consume 80MB for stacks alone plus connection state structs. Use a goroutine pool or worker queue pattern for the Modbus polling supervisor to cap memory usage. Connection pool should use a bounded semaphore pattern rather than unconstrained goroutine spawning.
reasoner_reviews.security: No authentication surface in the library itself. Key security concern is that Modbus TCP has no built-in auth -- document clearly in README that Modbus adapters should only be used on isolated plant networks and that callers are responsible for network segmentation. OPC-UA adapter should enforce certificate validation by default and require explicit opt-out for anonymous mode.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Pure Go constraint (no CGO) is achievable for all four target protocols. Modbus TCP uses raw TCP, EtherNet/IP uses raw TCP/UDP, OPC-UA uses gopcua which is pure Go, MQTT uses paho.mqtt.golang which is pure Go. Cross-compile targets: linux/amd64, linux/arm64, windows/amd64 all achievable with GOOS/GOARCH without CGO.

backend.service_design: Package structure: adapter (Adapter interface and common types), modbus (ModbusTCPAdapter, ModbusRTUAdapter), enip (EtherNetIPAdapter), opcua (OPCUAAdapter), mqtt (MQTTAdapter), pool (ConnectionPoolSupervisor), config (RegisterMap, AdapterConfig). Each adapter struct embeds a poolSupervisor managing a bounded channel of active connections with goroutine-per-connection semantics up to MaxConnections cap.
backend.api_patterns: Exported interface: type Adapter interface with Connect(ctx context.Context) error, Read(ctx context.Context, req ReadRequest) (ReadResult, error), Write(ctx context.Context, cmd WriteCommand) (WriteResult, error), Subscribe(ctx context.Context, cfg SubscribeConfig) (SubscribeHandle, error), Close() error. Types: ReadRequest with DeviceAddr, RegisterMap, TagList. ReadResult with TagValues map and QualityCodes. ConnectionOptions with MaxConnections, ConnectTimeout, ReadTimeout, RetryBackoff, TLSConfig.
backend.failure_handling: Each adapter implements exponential backoff reconnect: initial 1s, max 60s, jitter 20%. On Read timeout, returns ReadResult with QualityCode=Uncertain and logs via injected slog.Logger. Connection pool supervisor monitors goroutine health via context cancellation; dead connections are replaced without blocking the caller. goleak in CI detects goroutine leaks from incomplete error paths.
backend.scaling_notes: Benchmark target: 10,000 concurrent Modbus TCP connections within 512MB. Implementation: bounded semaphore (buffered channel of struct{} with cap MaxConnections) gates goroutine spawning. Each Modbus connection goroutine holds: net.Conn (platform TCP socket), request queue channel (cap 16), 512-byte read buffer. Estimated per-connection overhead: 8KB goroutine stack + 512B buffer + TCP socket OS handle = approximately 10KB per connection; 10,000 connections = approximately 100MB, well within 512MB budget.

data.entities: RegisterMap (device_addr string, registers []RegisterDef with address uint16, tag_name string, data_type DataType ENUM uint16/int32/float32/bool, scale_factor float64, offset float64, byte_order ByteOrder). ReadRequest (device_addr string, register_map RegisterMap, tags []string). ReadResult (tag_values map[string]any, quality_codes map[string]QualityCode ENUM Good/Uncertain/Bad, read_at time.Time). WriteCommand (device_addr string, tag_name string, value any). SubscribeConfig (device_addr string, node_ids []string for OPC-UA, topics []string for MQTT, publish_interval time.Duration). connection_pool_state: in-memory map[string]connEntry per adapter instance.
data.schema_notes: No persistent schema -- all state in-memory per adapter instance lifetime. RegisterMap intended to be loaded from caller-provided YAML or JSON configuration files; library provides RegisterMapFromYAML(r io.Reader) (RegisterMap, error) helper. DataType enum covers all register types needed for Modbus 16-bit, EtherNet/IP 32-bit, and OPC-UA variant types.
data.data_risks: Incorrect byte_order configuration on Modbus registers causes silent value corruption (e.g., big-endian float32 read as little-endian produces garbage). Mitigation: provide explicit ByteOrder constants (BigEndian, LittleEndian, MixedEndian) and document common PLC defaults per manufacturer in README.

devops.deployment_topology: Go module published to pkg.go.dev at github.com/org/industrio-go. Semantic versioning: v1.0.0 on first stable release; no breaking changes within v1.x without major version bump. Module proxy caching at sum.golang.org for reproducible go get operations.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go vet, staticcheck lint, go test -race -count=1 ./... for unit tests with mocked TCP connections using net.Pipe(), goleak TestMain wrapper to catch goroutine leaks, go test -bench ./... for connection pool throughput benchmarks asserting 10,000 goroutines within memory budget. Cross-compile matrix: GOOS/GOARCH for linux/amd64, linux/arm64, windows/amd64 to verify no CGO. Tag-triggered workflow publishes release notes and bumps pkg.go.dev index.
devops.devops_risks: Go module proxy caching means breaking changes in a tagged release affect all callers immediately. Mitigation: maintain backward compatibility within v1.x; use go-apidiff in CI to detect interface changes. goleak flakiness on parallel test runs -- use TestMain with goleak.VerifyNone(m) after all tests complete.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a pure-Go library for multi-protocol industrial device communication. Key decisions: define the Adapter interface first as the stable API contract before any implementation; use a bounded semaphore pattern (buffered channel) for connection pool goroutine cap to stay within 512MB for 10,000 concurrent Modbus connections; inject slog.Logger to avoid imposing a logging framework on callers; no CGO dependencies to support linux/arm64 gateway cross-compilation targets.
target_output.title: Industrial Device Communication Go Library (industrio-go)
target_output.executive_summary: A pure Go 1.22 library providing a unified Adapter interface for Modbus TCP/RTU, EtherNet/IP, OPC-UA, and MQTT industrial device communication, designed for embedding in IoT gateway applications on edge hardware. The library manages connection pooling with adaptive reconnect, register map configuration, read request batching, and push subscription handling behind a single protocol-agnostic API. No CGO dependencies ensure cross-compilation to linux/arm64 gateway targets. The MVP delivers the Adapter interface definition, ModbusTCPAdapter with bounded connection pool, register map YAML loader, and a comprehensive test suite with goleak goroutine leak detection.
target_output.architecture_overview: The library is structured as a Go module with distinct packages per protocol under a common adapter interface. The core Adapter interface in the adapter package defines the five exported methods that all protocol implementations satisfy: Connect, Read, Write, Subscribe, and Close. Each adapter struct embeds a ConnectionPoolSupervisor from the pool package that manages goroutine lifecycle via a bounded semaphore (buffered channel of empty structs, capacity MaxConnections). The Modbus TCP implementation opens a net.Conn per device address, wraps it in a goroutine servicing a request channel, and multiplexes concurrent Read calls through the channel to respect single-TCP-connection Modbus framing. OPC-UA uses gopcua (pure Go, Basic256Sha256 support), MQTT uses paho.mqtt.golang with TLS config hook. Callers inject a slog.Logger for connection lifecycle events; the library emits no logs unless a logger is provided.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Go 1.22. Protocol dependencies: github.com/goburrow/modbus for Modbus TCP and RTU (pure Go), github.com/danomagnum/gologix for EtherNet/IP CIP (pure Go), github.com/gopcua/opcua for OPC-UA (pure Go, Basic256Sha256), github.com/eclipse/paho.mqtt.golang for MQTT with TLS. No CGO. Testing: stdlib testing, net.Pipe for mock TCP connections, goleak for goroutine leak detection. CI: GitHub Actions with staticcheck, go test -race, cross-compile matrix. Distribution: Go module on pkg.go.dev.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Adapter Interface: exported protocol-agnostic contract with Connect(ctx), Read(ctx, ReadRequest), Write(ctx, WriteCommand), Subscribe(ctx, SubscribeConfig), Close(). RegisterMap Configuration: RegisterMapFromYAML(r io.Reader) parses device tag definitions with address, data_type, scale_factor, offset, and byte_order fields for Modbus and EtherNet/IP register interpretation. Request Batching: ModbusTCPAdapter batches multiple tag reads within the same function code and contiguous address range into a single Modbus PDU to minimize round trips per poll cycle. Connection Pool Supervisor: bounded semaphore caps concurrent connections; unhealthy connections are replaced with exponential backoff reconnect without blocking in-flight Read calls. Subscribe: OPCUAAdapter creates MonitoredItem subscriptions; MQTTAdapter subscribes to MQTT topics; both deliver values via caller-provided channel passed in SubscribeConfig. Security Hooks: ConnectionOptions.TLSConfig for MQTT TLS, ConnectionOptions.SecurityPolicy for OPC-UA Basic256Sha256.
target_output.system_components: Package adapter: Adapter interface, ReadRequest, ReadResult, WriteCommand, WriteResult, SubscribeConfig, SubscribeHandle, QualityCode enum, DataType enum, ConnectionOptions types. Package pool: ConnectionPoolSupervisor with bounded semaphore (chan struct cap MaxConnections), connEntry with net.Conn, request chan, cancel func, HealthCheck goroutine. Package modbus: ModbusTCPAdapter implements Adapter; encodes ReadRequest tag list to Modbus PDU read coils/registers commands; decodes response to ReadResult applying scale_factor and offset per RegisterDef; request batching merges contiguous address ranges. Package opcua: OPCUAAdapter wraps gopcua client; Subscribe creates MonitoredItems subscription with configurable publish interval. Package mqtt: MQTTAdapter wraps paho client with TLS config hook; Subscribe maps topic messages to SubscribeHandle channel delivery. Package config: RegisterMapFromYAML parses YAML to RegisterMap; validates address ranges per DataType width.
target_output.workflows: Gateway Developer Workflow: developer imports library with go get github.com/org/industrio-go; defines RegisterMap in YAML with device address mappings; instantiates ModbusTCPAdapter with ConnectionOptions specifying MaxConnections=50, ConnectTimeout=5s, RetryBackoff starting at 1s; calls adapter.Connect(ctx); calls adapter.Read(ctx, ReadRequest with tag list) in a polling goroutine; received ReadResult.TagValues passed to caller's persistence layer. The library transparently batches the tag reads into a single Modbus PDU covering the contiguous address range. Subscribe Workflow: gateway developer instantiates OPCUAAdapter with endpoint URL and SecurityPolicy=Basic256Sha256; calls adapter.Subscribe(ctx, SubscribeConfig with NodeIds and PublishInterval=500ms) receiving a SubscribeHandle with a Values channel; goroutine ranges over handle.Values() receiving TagValue events as the OPC-UA server publishes node changes. Reconnect Workflow: device goes offline; ConnectionPoolSupervisor detects Dial failure on health check ping; marks connEntry unhealthy; spawns reconnect goroutine with backoff (1s, 2s, 4s up to 60s); during reconnect window Read calls return ReadResult with QualityCode=Bad; on successful reconnect QualityCode returns to Good.
target_output.data_model: All data types in-memory; no persistence. Core types: RegisterDef struct with fields Address uint16, TagName string, DataType DataType (Uint16/Int32/Float32/Bool/String), ScaleFactorFloat64, Offset float64, ByteOrder ByteOrder (BigEndian/LittleEndian/MixedEndian). RegisterMap struct with DeviceAddr string and Registers []RegisterDef. ReadRequest struct with DeviceAddr string, RegisterMap RegisterMap, Tags []string (nil means read all). ReadResult struct with TagValues map[string]any, QualityCodes map[string]QualityCode, ReadAt time.Time. WriteCommand struct with DeviceAddr string, TagName string, Value any. SubscribeConfig struct with DeviceAddr string, NodeIDs []string, Topics []string, PublishInterval time.Duration, ValueChan chan TagValue. ConnEntry (internal, not exported): net.Conn, reqChan chan adapterRequest, cancel context.CancelFunc, healthy bool.
target_output.api_design: Exported Go API -- no HTTP. Core interface in package adapter: Connect(ctx context.Context) error. Read(ctx context.Context, req ReadRequest) (ReadResult, error). Write(ctx context.Context, cmd WriteCommand) (WriteResult, error). Subscribe(ctx context.Context, cfg SubscribeConfig) (SubscribeHandle, error). Close() error. Constructor functions: modbus.NewTCPAdapter(host string, port int, regMap RegisterMap, opts ConnectionOptions) Adapter. modbus.NewRTUAdapter(serialPort string, baudRate int, regMap RegisterMap, opts ConnectionOptions) Adapter. opcua.NewAdapter(endpointURL string, opts ConnectionOptions) Adapter. mqtt.NewAdapter(brokerURL string, opts ConnectionOptions) Adapter. Helper: config.RegisterMapFromYAML(r io.Reader) (RegisterMap, error). Stability guarantee: all exported types and functions in adapter, config packages are v1-stable; internal packages may change.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Library security surface is minimal -- the library itself opens no network servers. OPC-UA adapter: ConnectionOptions.SecurityPolicy defaults to opcua.SecurityPolicyBasic256Sha256; anonymous mode requires explicit SecurityPolicy=opcua.SecurityPolicyNone. Certificate validation enabled by default in gopcua client; ConnectionOptions.TLSConfig allows caller to provide custom CA pool for self-signed PLC certificates. MQTT adapter: ConnectionOptions.TLSConfig accepts *tls.Config for TLS 1.3 and client certificate auth. Modbus and EtherNet/IP have no built-in auth; README documents that these adapters must be used on network-isolated plant segments. No secrets stored in library code; all credentials passed by caller in ConnectionOptions.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Go module tagged release workflow: git tag v1.0.0; push triggers GitHub Actions publish job that runs go mod tidy, go test ./..., cross-compile matrix (linux/amd64, linux/arm64, windows/amd64 with GONOSUMCHECK disabled), and creates GitHub Release with changelog. pkg.go.dev indexes the module automatically on tag push. go-apidiff runs in CI on every PR to detect unintended interface breaking changes before merge. goleak TestMain in each package test file ensures zero goroutine leaks after test suite completes. Benchmark suite in modbus/bench_test.go validates 10,000-goroutine connection pool memory footprint stays under 512MB using runtime.ReadMemStats assertions.
target_output.observability: Library provides slog-compatible logging via ConnectionOptions.Logger field accepting *slog.Logger. If nil, library emits no log output. Logged events at INFO level: adapter connected (device_addr, protocol), adapter reconnecting (device_addr, attempt, backoff_duration), adapter disconnected (device_addr). At DEBUG: Read batching statistics (tags_requested, pdus_sent), Subscribe delivery lag. No Prometheus metrics embedded -- callers wrap library calls in their own metrics instrumentation.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost as a library -- no hosted components. Memory budget validation: 10,000 concurrent Modbus TCP connections in benchmark test consume approximately 100MB (8KB goroutine stack + 512B request buffer + TCP socket per connection). Go garbage collector overhead at steady state is approximately 20-30% headroom above live set; total peak expected under 150MB, well within 512MB gateway hardware budget. Benchmark assertions in CI enforce this constraint on every commit.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Interface and Modbus TCP (6 weeks): Adapter interface stabilized and documented, ModbusTCPAdapter with bounded connection pool, RegisterMap YAML loader, Read request batching for contiguous address ranges, unit tests with net.Pipe mock connections, goleak leak detection, CI pipeline with cross-compile matrix, v1.0.0 tagged release on pkg.go.dev. Phase 2 -- OPC-UA and MQTT (5 weeks): OPCUAAdapter with Basic256Sha256 and MonitoredItems subscription, MQTTAdapter with TLS config hook and topic subscription, Modbus RTU adapter for serial connections, integration test suite with containerized Modbus TCP simulator. Phase 3 -- EtherNet/IP and OpenTelemetry (future): EtherNetIPAdapter via gologix, OpenTelemetry tracing hooks for adapter operations, PROFINET adapter prototype, hardware-in-the-loop test rig with physical PLCs.
target_output.development_guidelines: No CGO anywhere in the module -- enforced via CGO_ENABLED=0 in CI cross-compile step; any CGO import causes build failure. Exported interface methods must be documented with godoc comments. New adapter implementations must satisfy adapter.Adapter interface at compile time via var _ adapter.Adapter = (*newAdapter)(nil) guard. All exported types versioned in CHANGELOG.md under Keep a Changelog format. go-apidiff in CI blocks PRs that break v1 interface compatibility. goleak wraps every *_test.go TestMain -- any goroutine leak fails the test. Benchmark suite assertions: BenchmarkConnectionPool10k must stay under 512MB runtime.ReadMemStats.HeapInuse.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: gopcua OPC-UA library has limited OPC-UA server compatibility; some industrial OPC-UA servers use proprietary extensions not supported by gopcua. Mitigation: document tested PLC models in README; provide ErrProtocolUnsupported return from Connect for graceful failure. Risk: paho.mqtt.golang MQTT client has known reconnect race conditions under high message rate. Mitigation: wrap paho client in adapter mutex; file upstream issue; note limitation in MQTT adapter documentation. Tradeoff: goroutine-per-connection model versus event-loop model (like libuv in Node.js) -- goroutine model is more idiomatic Go and easier to reason about but consumes more memory per connection than a poll-based event loop would at extreme scale (100,000+ connections).
target_output.open_questions_resolved: goburrow/modbus chosen over other Modbus libraries as the most actively maintained pure-Go implementation with TCP and RTU support. Bounded semaphore pattern chosen over sync.Pool for connection pooling because connections are not interchangeable (each binds to a specific device address). slog.Logger injection chosen over log.Logger interface for forward compatibility with Go 1.21+ structured logging standard. v1 stability guarantee scoped to adapter and config packages only; internal pool and protocol packages may change.

metadata.notes: Strong first-pass library_sdk: industrial protocol adapter Go library with detailed interface design, connection pool memory analysis, and no FrontendAgent subplan per library_sdk specialist gating rules.
